title: AMD Presents Latest High-Performance Computing Technologies in 2022 Product Premiere Livestream
summary: AMD announced over 30 new products in its 2022 Product Premiere livestream, including Ryzen 6000 Series processors for notebooks, new Radeon RX 6000 Series GPUs for laptops and desktops, and the Ryzen 7 5800X3D desktop processor. The company also previewed its forthcoming Ryzen 7000 Series desktop processors based on the new "Zen 4" architecture. These releases aim to deliver leadership performance for productivity, content creation, and gaming experiences across various PC segments.

title: An Under the Radar High Quality ETF
summary: The Fidelity Quality Factor ETF (FQAL) received a five-star rating from CFRA, outperforming its peers and the S&P 500 in 2021 despite its smaller asset size. The fund focuses on free cash flow and return on invested capital with a sector-neutral approach, and CFRA expects it to continue outperforming. Eight of its top-10 holdings are considered undervalued by CFRA analysts.

title: John Deere’s New Autonomous Tractor Offers Timeliness, Efficiency, Quality of Life
summary: John Deere has unveiled a fully autonomous tractor at CES 2022, set for production this year, to address labor shortages, improve farming efficiency, and enhance farmers' quality of life. The machine integrates advanced technologies like AI, machine learning, and GPS to allow 24/7 operation without an in-cab operator. Farmers can remotely monitor and manage the tractor via a mobile device, facilitating greater productivity and sustainability in agriculture.

title: Qualcomm unveils ‘digital chassis’ tech at CES as it diversifies beyond smartphones
summary: Qualcomm introduced its "digital chassis" strategy at CES, aiming to expand its mobile technology expertise beyond smartphones and into the automotive industry. This includes a new Snapdragon Ride Vision System for advanced driver assistance and infotainment deals with major car manufacturers like Volvo, Honda, and Renault. The company projects its automotive revenue to reach $3.5 billion annually in five years, signaling a significant diversification effort.

title: HP OMEN reveals products for 2022, including the unique cooling device, the OMEN Cryo Chamber
summary: HP OMEN has unveiled its 2022 gaming lineup, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with the innovative OMEN Cryo Chamber CPU cooling solution, which significantly lowers temperatures. The lineup also includes the OMEN 25L Desktop, the compact Victus by HP 15L Desktop, and the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles. These new products offer advanced performance, cooling, and customization options for a diverse gaming audience.

title: Nvidia’s Omniverse ‘metaverse for engineers’ emerges from beta
summary: Nvidia's Omniverse, a "metaverse for engineers" designed for 3D simulation and design collaboration, is now generally available for free to Nvidia Studio creators with GeForce RTX gear after a successful public beta. The platform offers new creative features, including AI-enabled 3D face modeling animation and is being utilized by companies like Ericsson to optimize 5G network buildouts by modeling signal propagation in virtual cities. Omniverse leverages Nvidia's RTX GPU technology and Pixar's Universal Scene Description for real-time collaboration and simulation across various industries.

title: JVAL Stock Price | JPMorgan U.S. Value Factor ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information about the JPMorgan U.S. Value Factor ETF (JVAL), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and comprehensive financial metrics such as dividend yield, expense ratio, and market capitalization. It also offers technical analysis insights and lists the ETF's top holdings, outlining its investment strategy as a value-focused equity fund targeting the U.S. market.

title: Innoviz Technologies unveils third-generation LiDAR sensor
summary: Innoviz Technologies has introduced its third-generation LiDAR sensor, the Innoviz360, building on its previous successful products. This new sensor offers high-resolution, a wide field of view, and reduced cost, aiming to overcome existing limitations in fully autonomous vehicles (L4-L5) like robotaxis and trucks. The company also expects the Innoviz360 to open new market opportunities beyond automotive, including logistics, mapping, and smart infrastructure, with samples anticipated in Q4 2022.

title: Leading HPC Software Company Bright Computing Joins NVIDIA
summary: Bright Computing, a leading HPC software company with over 700 customers, has been acquired by NVIDIA. This acquisition integrates Bright Cluster Manager into NVIDIA's accelerated computing software stack, aiming to simplify the building and operation of HPC data centers. The partnership will enhance NVIDIA's DGX and data center businesses and support the growing trend of industrial HPC by making advanced computing more accessible.

title: Nvidia buys high-performance computing cluster management software provider Bright Computing
summary: Nvidia has acquired Bright Computing Inc., a company specializing in software for managing high-performance computing (HPC) systems. This acquisition aims to enhance Nvidia's offerings in the HPC market, integrating Bright's Cluster Manager into Nvidia's software stack to provide more complete infrastructure platforms for accelerated computing. The move is seen as another step in Nvidia's evolution from a chip company to a comprehensive solution provider for data centers, particularly as HPC and AI workloads converge.

title: Stellar December gives TSMC record year
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported record sales for the previous year, driven by strong demand for its advanced 5-nanometer process used in emerging sectors like 5G and high-performance computing. The company's consolidated sales reached NT$1.59 trillion ($57.47 billion USD), an 18.5 percent increase from 2020. TSMC is expected to announce a significant capital expenditure budget for the current year to further expand production capacity and maintain its technological advantage.

title: NVIDIA Named America’s Best Place to Work on Latest Glassdoor List
summary: NVIDIA has been ranked as America's best place to work on Glassdoor’s 14th annual Best Places to Work list for large U.S. companies in 2022. The ranking is based on anonymous employee feedback, with 95% of NVIDIA employees recommending the company and 98% approving of CEO Jensen Huang. This recognition highlights NVIDIA's commitment to employee satisfaction amid evolving workplace expectations.

title: Get started on your AI learning today – NVIDIA Jetson Introduction and Application Sharing
summary: This article introduces an NVIDIA Jetson webinar focusing on the growing AI edge market and applications. It highlights how NVIDIA's hardware, software, and ecosystem can help in developing AI products, featuring embedded systems with Jetson modules. The webinar includes presentations from experts at EDOM Technology, ELCOM, and Weston Robot, covering topics like traffic surveillance and robotic applications.

title: 投资指南：2022年的5只精选区块链ETF
summary: 这篇文章介绍了2022年值得关注的五只精选区块链ETF，为普通投资者提供了投资区块链和加密货币类上市公司的途径。文章详细分析了每只ETF的资产规模、持股重点、费用率以及成立以来的业绩表现，旨在帮助投资者根据自身需求选择合适的区块链投资产品。

title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
summary: The article suggests that the semiconductor sector remains a strong investment area, even with rising interest rates impacting other tech stocks. It highlights the strong performance of chip exchange-traded funds (ETFs) over the past 15 years, outperforming both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq. This positive outlook is further supported by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive fourth-quarter results and significant capital expenditure plans for 2022.

title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
summary: This article highlights three ETFs that allow investors to gain exposure to the rapidly growing semiconductor sector, which includes companies like Nvidia, Micron, and AMD. Despite concerns about rising interest rates, the semiconductor industry continues to show strong growth, as evidenced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive Q4 sales and earnings report. The chip-focused exchange-traded funds have significantly outperformed both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq over the past 15 years.

title: Chip giant Nvidia expands R&D operations in Israel
summary: Nvidia is expanding its R&D operations in Israel by establishing a new design and engineering group focused on next-generation CPUs for AI, robotics, and autonomous vehicles. The company, which acquired Israel's Mellanox Technologies in 2020, plans to hire additional engineers in Israel, further solidifying the country's role as Nvidia's largest R&D hub outside the US. This expansion intensifies competition for tech talent in Israel, where both Nvidia and Intel have significant R&D presence, and Google is also entering the chip-making sector.

title: Nvidia expands Israel R&D operations
summary: Nvidia is expanding its R&D operations in Israel by establishing a new design and engineering group to develop next-generation CPUs. The company plans to hire hundreds of Israelis for this initiative, adding to its existing 2,800 employees across seven locations in the country. This expansion reinforces Israel's role as a key player in Nvidia's global tech ecosystem, including its DPU development and AI research, and builds upon its acquisition of Mellanox.

title: Israeli quantum cryptographic solutions provider QuantLR integrates with NVIDIA
summary: Israeli company QuantLR, a provider of quantum cryptographic solutions, has integrated its technology with NVIDIA's networking suite. This collaboration aims to develop quantum-secured data centers and accelerate the deployment of affordable quantum encryption solutions. QuantLR's CEO, Shlomi Cohen, highlighted the importance of NVIDIA's support in advancing their technology against eavesdropping and hacking attempts, anticipating significant growth in the quantum encryption market.

title: Van, Go: Pony.ai Unveils Next-Gen Robotaxi Fleet Built on NVIDIA DRIVE Orin
summary: Pony.ai, a self-driving startup, has announced its next-generation autonomous computing platform built on NVIDIA DRIVE Orin. This platform will power a robotaxi fleet of Toyota Sienna MPVs, marking a significant step for the company in developing level 4 self-driving vehicles. The high-performance, energy-efficient AI compute platform is designed for scalable autonomous driving development, with road testing beginning in China this year and commercial rollout planned for 2023.

title: In Its Next Significant Milestone for L4 Robotaxis, Pony.ai Debuts Its 6th Generation Autonomous Driving System Design
summary: Pony.ai has unveiled its 6th generation autonomous driving system, designed for L4 automotive-grade mass production fleets, featuring advanced sensors and NVIDIA DRIVE computing platforms. The first vehicle equipped with this system, a Toyota Sienna Autono-MaaS (S-AM), will begin road testing in China in 2022 and join Pony.ai's public robotaxi operations in early 2023. This system aims to enhance safety, reliability, and cost-effectiveness for large-scale robotaxi deployment, incorporating a 23-sensor suite and an NVIDIA DRIVE Orin-powered computing unit.

title: Intel is building a $20 billion computer chip facility in Ohio amid a global shortage
summary: Intel announced a $20 billion investment to construct two computer chip factories in central Ohio, aiming to alleviate the ongoing global semiconductor shortage. This move is expected to create thousands of jobs and enhance U.S. chip manufacturing capacity, reducing reliance on Asian producers. The project is Ohio's largest single private-sector investment, with production anticipated to begin by the end of 2025.

title: Nvidia powers latest Pony.ai autonomous car platform
summary: Pony.ai, an autonomous driving technology company, has chosen Nvidia's Drive Orin SoC for its sixth-generation autonomous driving system. This platform is designed to enable mass production of Level-4 autonomous vehicles, with trials set to begin in China this year using Toyota's S-AM vehicle, followed by deployment in Pony.ai’s robotaxi operations in H1 2023. Nvidia's chip can process 254 trillion operations per second.

title: Low-power AI computer chip startup Ceremorphic exits stealth with $50M funding
summary: Ceremorphic Inc., a startup focused on energy-efficient AI supercomputing chips, has emerged from stealth mode with a $50 million Series A funding round. The company, founded by tech veteran Dr. Venkat Mattela, aims to develop a "complete silicon system" incorporating a 5-nanometer processor with a unique architecture and patented multithread technology (ThreadArch) to address reliability, security, and energy consumption challenges in high-performance AI, HPC, and metaverse applications. The funding for the Series A round was entirely provided by the company's founders, many of whom have a background with Redpine Signals Inc., known for ultralow-power wireless chipsets.

title: CES 2022 Recap: Tech Becoming More Accessible and Interoperable
summary: CES 2022 showcased significant advancements making technology more accessible and interoperable. Key trends included enhanced immersion in the metaverse with new AR/VR products, traditional auto OEMs committing to mass-market electric vehicles, and the emergence of a unified Internet of Things ecosystem through initiatives like Matter. The event highlighted a shift towards practical, everyday applications for emerging technologies, signaling potential disruptions in various sectors.

title: What a Bristol-Myers project to monitor off-label prescribing says about future uses of A.I.
summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb collaborated with Cortical.io to utilize AI for monitoring off-label drug prescribing by analyzing social media data. The pilot project successfully identified off-label uses of drugs, including an unexpected finding for Suboxone, demonstrating the potential of natural language processing and less data-intensive AI methods for post-market surveillance. This initiative highlights the growing importance of AI in patient safety and the value of brain-inspired AI research.

title: Is chip giant Nvidia going to scrap its $40BN bid for Arm?
summary: Nvidia is reportedly preparing to abandon its $40 billion acquisition of Arm Ltd. due to fierce regulatory backlash and little progress in securing approvals. Regulators and customers, including the U.S. FTC, have expressed concerns that the deal would make Nvidia too powerful. SoftBank, Arm's current owner, is stepping up preparations for an Arm initial public offering as an alternative.

title: Nearly 80 Percent of Financial Firms Use AI to Improve Services, Reduce Fraud
summary: A survey by NVIDIA reveals that 78% of financial services professionals use accelerated computing for AI-enabled applications, driving innovation across the industry. Fraud detection, conversational AI, and algorithmic trading are key use cases, with significant year-over-year growth in AI adoption for various applications. Despite challenges like budget constraints and a shortage of data scientists, executives view AI as critical for future business success, with focuses on hiring AI experts, identifying new use cases, and investing in infrastructure.

title: NVIDIA Corp: the red flags for this stock investors need to know about
summary: This article discusses the trajectory of NVIDIA Corp.'s stock, highlighting its strong performance due to high demand for GPUs, driven by the global semiconductor shortage and boosted by record third-quarter earnings in November 2021. However, it also suggests that there are "red flags" that investors should be aware of, although the details of these flags are withheld for members only.

title: Corsair One i300 Review: Power Packed In
summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact gaming PC that offers powerful performance with high-end components like an Intel Core i9-12900K and Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti in a 12-liter chassis. While it provides big performance in a small package, making it ideal for limited desk space, its small size sacrifices some upgradeability and comes at a premium price, starting at $3,799.99 and reaching $4,999.99 as tested. The review highlights its strong gaming and productivity performance, outperforming larger systems in some benchmarks.

title: Chip shortage to last six months, one year or 18 months-- depending on who’s talking
summary: The article discusses the varying predictions for how long the global chip shortage will last, with estimates ranging from six months to 18 months or longer. It highlights views from IDC, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, and Texas Instruments, alongside ongoing efforts by the U.S. Congress and Commerce Secretary to address the issue through funding and incentives for domestic chip production. The consensus indicates that while some improvements might be seen, the shortage is expected to persist through 2022 and potentially into 2023 for various sectors.

title: TSMC: Can anything stop Taiwan's chipmaking giant?
summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chip manufacturer, reported bumper financial results in late 2021, with revenue of $15.7bn and profits of $6bn. The company plans to invest $40-$44bn into capital expenditure to maintain its market dominance, dwarfing its rivals. Despite its strong position, TSMC faces challenges from competitors like Intel and Samsung, as well as geopolitical risks and the complexities of global expansion.

title: AMD's stellar report fuels chip stock rally
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong quarterly demand for its semiconductors, boosting its annual revenue forecast and surpassing analysts' expectations, which led to an almost 11% surge in its stock. This positive report ignited a rally in other chip stocks, with Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Micron Technology also seeing jumps in their share prices, despite ongoing global supply snags and volatility in the semiconductor sector. Intel, however, saw a decline in its stock.

title: Metaverse stocks Nvidia and Roblox fall as Facebook parent Meta's Web3 business reports massive loss
summary: Shares of metaverse-related companies like Roblox, Nvidia, and Matterport fell after Meta Platforms reported a significant operating loss of $10 billion in its Web3 division, Reality Labs, for 2021. The news led to a market value wipeout of over $200 billion for Meta and a sell-off across various tech stocks connected to the metaverse concept. This downturn highlights concerns following Meta's aggressive push into the metaverse space.

title: Chip giants are ramping up spending by the billions as semiconductor demand booms
summary: Major semiconductor companies like TSMC and Intel are investing billions into new fabrication plants and research to meet the booming global demand for chips, despite the ongoing chip shortage. TSMC plans to spend $100 billion over three years, while Intel announced a $20 billion investment in Arizona. Analysts expect a "choppy" recovery from the shortage in the short term, but demand is projected to continue increasing, benefiting suppliers like ASML and Applied Materials.

title: Chip Startup Ceremorphic Seeks AI, HPC Edge With New 5nm Design
summary: Ceremorphic, a chip startup, has emerged from stealth mode with plans to produce a scalable, low-energy processor for AI and high-performance computing using TSMC's 5-nanometer process node. The company's CEO, Venkat Mattela, emphasizes that access to this advanced manufacturing process, secured through his team's prior success, as well as Ceremorphic's proprietary technologies for efficiency, reliability, and security, will set its Hierarchical Learning Processor apart from competitors. The startup aims to deliver samples to customers in 2023 and begin production in 2024, following test chip validation in March.

title: รีวิวกองทุน UHERO: กองทุนเรื่องเล่น ๆ แต่เติบโตไม่ใช่เล่น ๆ
summary: The article reviews the UHERO fund, which invests in the growing global gaming industry through the "The Global X Video Games & Esports ETF (HERO)". It details the industry's massive growth, exceeding music and film combined, and outlines UHERO's investment focus on companies like Nvidia, Electronic Arts, NetEase, Blizzard, and Nintendo, primarily in the US, Japan, and Korea. The fund is presented as a high-growth, high-volatility option for investors interested in alternative industries.

title: WEKA and Cisco funding contribution: Now you see it, now you don’t
summary: WEKA initially announced Cisco as an investor in its $73 million funding round, but Cisco's name was later replaced by Digital Alpha in a corrected release. Digital Alpha, founded by a former Cisco managing director, describes itself as an alternative asset manager with a strategic collaboration agreement with Cisco Systems and preferred access to Cisco’s commercial opportunities. This change highlights the close ties between Digital Alpha and Cisco, effectively positioning Digital Alpha as a Cisco investment vehicle.

title: Nvidia becomes 7th-largest U.S. company as it passes Facebook owner Meta for the first time
summary: Nvidia Corp. has surpassed Meta Platforms Inc. to become the seventh-largest U.S. company by market value. This shift highlights Nvidia's rapid growth over the past few years and Meta's recent decline, which saw its market value drop by $267 billion since Wednesday's close.

title: The biggest chip merger ever is dead. Here's why Nvidia dropped its bid for Arm and what's next.
summary: Nvidia's $40 billion planned acquisition of Arm has been called off due to significant concerns and opposition from its announcement. As a result, SoftBank now intends to take Arm public through an IPO. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that the company will continue to license architecture from Arm despite the deal's collapse.

title: Nvidia’s Arm deal: Largest-ever chip merger terminated over ‘significant regulatory challenges’
summary: Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Limited, which would have been the largest chip merger in history, has been officially terminated due to significant regulatory challenges globally. SoftBank, Arm's parent company, will retain a $1.25 billion breakup fee from Nvidia and plans to take Arm public by March 2023. Analysts suggest the termination is a "plus" for Arm, allowing it to maintain impartiality as an IP purveyor, and while a "setback" for Nvidia, it won't derail its progress in AI.

title: We should never have sold Arm in the first place, says ALEX BRUMMER
summary: Alex Brummer argues that the 2016 sale of British chip designer Arm Holdings to Japan's Softbank for £23 billion was a "serious betrayal" of British technology. He criticizes former Prime Minister Theresa May's government and Cabinet Secretary Jeremy Heywood for facilitating the deal, which he views as short-sighted given Softbank founder Masayoshi Son's intent to use Arm as a bargaining chip rather than for long-term investment. Brummer suggests that following the collapse of Nvidia's £50 billion bid for Arm, the British government has an opportunity to correct past mistakes and bring Arm back to the UK as a public company.

title: Nvidia, SoftBank Call Off Blockbuster Arm Deal - WSJ
summary: Nvidia and SoftBank have abandoned their $40 billion deal for Nvidia to acquire chip-design specialist Arm due to significant regulatory antitrust concerns. SoftBank, which owns Arm, now plans to pursue a public listing for the U.K.-based chip business. Nvidia will take a $1.36 billion charge against earnings related to the deal's termination, and SoftBank will retain the $1.25 billion prepaid by Nvidia.

title: ETF of the month: VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF (SMGB)
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF (SMGB) has been named ETF of the month, highlighting its impressive performance in 2021 by returning 44.4% and gathering $859m in assets since its December 2020 launch. The ETF has benefited from increased demand for semiconductor chips driven by global digitalization, the reopening of economies, and emerging technologies like electric vehicles and 5G. Despite some recent market concerns from hedge fund selling, the semiconductor industry is projected to see continued revenue growth in 2022.

title: AMD Completes $49B Xilinx Acquisition, Largest Chip Deal In History
summary: AMD has finalized its $49 billion acquisition of Xilinx, making it the largest chip deal in history and transforming AMD into an "industry’s high-performance and adaptive computing leader." This strategic move expands AMD's offerings beyond CPUs and GPUs into reprogrammable FPGAs, significantly boosting its presence in data centers, embedded computing, and telecommunications. The deal will also enhance AMD's total addressable market and is expected to be accretive to margins, earnings per share, and free cash flow in the first year.

title: AMD Closes Record Chip Industry Deal with about $50-Bln Purchase of Xilinx
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) has finalized its acquisition of Xilinx Inc. for approximately $50 billion, making it a record deal in the chip industry. This acquisition allows AMD to expand into the cloud, edge, and intelligent devices market, intensifying its competition with Intel Corp. in the data center chip sector. The combined entity will be led by AMD CEO Lisa Su and aims to leverage an outsourced manufacturing strategy, primarily with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (TSMC).

title: Intel Buying Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion to Boost Supply Chain Capacity
summary: Intel is acquiring Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity and strengthen its Intel Foundry Services (IFS) division. This acquisition is a key part of Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy, aiming to boost supply chain resilience and compete with leading chip manufacturers like TSMC. Tower Semiconductor will provide Intel with access to high-growth markets and enhance the scale of its foundry operations, which Intel believes is crucial for accelerating the growth of IFS.

title: Intel to Buy Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion
summary: Intel Corp. has agreed to acquire Israel’s Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion as part of CEO Pat Gelsinger’s strategy to expand into the outsourced chip-manufacturing business. This acquisition aims to enhance Intel's expertise and customer base in the chip-foundry market, directly competing with industry leaders like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal is expected to close in about 12 months and is a key part of Intel's broader investment cycle to modernize its factories and rebuild manufacturing capabilities.

title: Nvidia Posts Record Sales Amid Broad Chip Demand
summary: Nvidia Corp. reported record quarterly sales of $7.64 billion, a 53% increase from the previous year, and projected further growth. The graphics-chip maker also more than doubled its net income to $3 billion, surpassing Wall Street expectations for both profit and sales. This strong performance comes amidst broad chip demand, despite the company moving past its failed takeover bid for Arm Ltd.

title: Nvidia provides strong revenue outlook on demand for computer chips
summary: Nvidia reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter earnings and a robust outlook for the current quarter, driven by exceptional demand for its chips in AI and other intensive applications. The company exceeded analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, with data center sales up 71% and gaming up 37%. CEO Jensen Huang noted easing supply constraints and a projected substantial increase in product supply in the second half of 2022, despite the automotive business experiencing a decline and the Arm acquisition falling through previously.

title: Nvidia reports record Q4, FY21 revenue on strong data center growth
summary: Nvidia reported record quarterly revenue of $7.64 billion for Q4 FY21, a 53% increase year-over-year, and record fiscal year 2021 revenue of $26.91 billion, up 61% YoY, primarily driven by strong growth in its Data Center, Gaming, and Professional Visualization businesses. Data Center revenue alone saw a 71% YoY increase due to increased AI adoption, with the company anticipating continued growth in this sector, particularly with its new AGX Orin processor for autonomous vehicles and robotics. While acknowledging ongoing supply chain issues, Nvidia expects improvements and has expanded its supply chain for FY23, despite recently terminating its $40 billion acquisition deal with Arm Limited due to regulatory resistance.

title: The Processor Chip Industry Is Another Ugly Capitalist Oligopoly
summary: This article criticizes the semiconductor industry, labeling it as an "ugly capitalist oligopoly" due to the extreme concentration of chip manufacturing among a few companies, primarily Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). It highlights how this concentration, driven by high capital costs for fabrication plants and state subsidies, leads to supply chain vulnerabilities, inflation, and limited competition. The author argues that even efforts by governments like the US to subsidize domestic chip production won't fundamentally alter the industry's oligopolistic structure or shift its profit-driven nature.

title: New Chips from Infineon, TI, and Toshiba Drive Advances in Autonomous Driving
summary: As the autonomous vehicle market grows more competitive, new microcontroller units (MCUs) and integrated circuits (ICs) from Infineon, Texas Instruments (TI), and Toshiba are emerging to support advanced driver-assist systems (ADAS) functionalities. Infineon's AURIX TC4x series offers a heterogeneous architecture for automotive E/E systems and AI, while TI's AWR2944 radar sensor meets NCAP standards for improved traffic safety. Toshiba's TC9563XBG Ethernet bridge IC enables high-speed communication up to 10 Gbps and supports Zone Architectures in next-generation vehicles.

title: Nvidia is set to spend at least $10B to secure its share of limited 5nm chip supply
summary: Nvidia is reportedly planning to spend at least $10 billion to secure a significant share of TSMC's limited 5nm chip supply for its upcoming Ada Lovelace GPUs (RTX 40 series). This substantial investment is crucial for Nvidia to potentially achieve higher clock speeds and better power efficiency, giving them a competitive edge over rivals like AMD. The move highlights the intense competition among fabless companies for advanced chip manufacturing capacity, with Nvidia having already spent $9 billion on inventory and prepayments in a recent quarter.

title: Industry's 1st Automotive-Qualified Switches Enable Autonomous Driving Ecosystem
summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has launched the market's first Gen 4 automotive-qualified PCIe switches, including Switchtec PFX, PSX, and PAX solutions. These switches are designed to provide high-speed, low-latency connectivity for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), which are crucial for next-generation autonomous driving applications. This development, a result of collaboration with technology partners like NVIDIA, addresses the need for robust interconnect capabilities in distributed computing systems within the automotive industry.

title: Like building the world's tallest tower every two years
summary: This article delves into the critical role of Intel Israel's Core Group in developing the most essential parts of Intel's processors: the cores. It explains the evolution of processor cores from single-core to multi-core designs, highlighting the challenges and innovations, including the creation of hybrid architectures with Performance and Efficiency Cores in the Alder Lake processor. The piece emphasizes the immense complexity and continuous innovation required in core development, likening it to building and constantly improving the world's tallest tower.

title: Texas Instruments Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Texas Instruments Inc. stock experienced a slight drop of 0.03% on a generally grim trading day for the market. Despite this loss, the company's shares outperformed major indices like the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, which fell more significantly. Texas Instruments also performed better than several of its competitors, with a trading volume exceeding its 50-day average.

title: Veritas NetBackup 10, IBM FlashSystem Cyber Vault And Rambus HBM3
summary: This article discusses key announcements from Veritas, IBM, and Rambus in the technology sector. Veritas introduced NetBackup 10, an evolution in cloud-based data protection with enhanced features. IBM unveiled its FlashSystem Cyber Vault and new FlashSystems, while Rambus detailed its HBM3 high-performance memory.

title: An Interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger
summary: This article features an interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, discussing his IDM 2.0 strategy, the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, and Intel's role in the semiconductor industry. Gelsinger explains why Intel is committed to manufacturing its own chips despite the success of fabless competitors, emphasizing the geopolitical importance of a Western technology provider and the benefits of integrating foundry operations. He also addresses concerns about potential overcapacity in the industry and the future of x86 architecture.

title: Behind Intel’s HPC Chip that Will Pierce the Exascale Barrier
summary: Intel has unveiled new details about its Ponte Vecchio processor, which will power the Aurora supercomputer, aiming to achieve exascale performance. The chip utilizes advanced 2D and 3D chiplet integration technologies, combining over 100 billion transistors across 47 pieces of silicon using various manufacturing processes, including TSMC's N5 and Intel 7. This heterogeneous integration and sophisticated thermal management are crucial for achieving the targeted 2 exaflops for the Aurora supercomputer.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) stock rose 1.76% on Friday, closing at $170.93, despite underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw larger gains. Qualcomm's stock remains below its 52-week high and showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Alphabet Inc. Cl A, NVIDIA Corp., and Cisco Systems Inc.

title: Semiconductor sales to Russia banned, but that shouldn’t hurt Intel, AMD and other chip makers
summary: Semiconductor companies are banning sales to Russia in response to its invasion of Ukraine. Despite a broader market sell-off and sanctions against Russia, an analyst believes these bans will have little financial impact on major chip makers like Intel and AMD because Russia's semiconductor market is likely too small to significantly affect their revenue.

title: Use WGMI to Enter the Crypto Economy
summary: The crypto economy is diversifying beyond direct Bitcoin ownership, with new ETFs like the Valkyrie Bitcoin Miners ETF (WGMI) offering access. WGMI focuses on bitcoin mining equities and is actively managed, allowing it to adapt to a nascent asset class with high growth potential. The ETF also incorporates a renewable energy overlay for its mining components, addressing environmental concerns associated with bitcoin mining.

title: Nvidia says employee, company information leaked online after cyber attack
summary: U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp reported a cyber attack that led to the leak of employee credentials and some proprietary company information online. The company became aware of the breach on February 23rd and is analyzing the leaked data, stating it does not anticipate business disruption. A ransomware group named "Lapsus$" has reportedly claimed responsibility for the leak, which is said to include schematics, drivers, and firmware for Nvidia's graphics chips.

title: Nvidia Confirms Company Data Was Stolen in Hack
summary: Nvidia has confirmed that data, including employee credentials and proprietary information, was stolen during a recent hack by the group LAPSUS$. While Nvidia states there's no evidence of ransomware or connection to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, LAPSUS$ claims to have acquired 1TB of data, including source code for DLSS technology, which they are now leaking online and attempting to sell. Nvidia does not anticipate business disruption as a result of the incident.

title: Microsoft Corp Chief Satya Nadella’s son passes away
summary: Zain Nadella, the 26-year-old son of Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and his wife Anu, passed away on Monday morning. Born with cerebral palsy, Zain's journey profoundly influenced Satya Nadella's focus on designing accessible products at Microsoft. The Children's Hospital, where Zain received treatment, collaborated with the Nadella family to establish the Zain Nadella Endowed Chair in Pediatric Neurosciences.

title: Nvidia hackers allegedly attempting to blackmail company into open-sourcing GPU drivers
summary: The Lapsus$ ransomware gang, responsible for a recent attack on Nvidia, is allegedly attempting to blackmail the company into open-sourcing its GPU drivers. The attackers have threatened to leak sensitive GPU files if Nvidia does not comply with their demands by a set deadline, which also included a previous request to remove mining hashrate limiters. Nvidia has confirmed a cybersecurity incident where employee credentials and proprietary information were stolen, but denies ransomware deployment.

title: ST claims first 0.5-MP depth image sensor
summary: STMicroelectronics has launched VD55H1, the first product in its new family of 0.5-megapixel indirect time-of-flight (iToF) sensors. This sensor offers high-resolution 3D depth imaging, targeting applications in smartphones for enhanced camera features and face authentication, as well as robotics and AR/VR for precise 3D scene mapping. The VD55H1 boasts low power consumption and high measurement accuracy, leveraging ST's unique pixel architecture and 40-nm stacked wafer technology.

title: GLOBALFOUNDRIES ANNOUNCES NEXT GENERATION IN SILICON PHOTONICS SOLUTIONS AND COLLABORATES WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS TO ADVANCE A NEW ERA OF MORE IN THE DATA CENTER
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has announced GF Fotonix™, its next-generation silicon photonics platform, aimed at addressing the explosive growth of data and reducing power consumption in data centers. GF is collaborating with industry leaders including Broadcom, Cisco Systems, Inc., Marvell, and NVIDIA, along with photonic innovators like Ayar Labs and PsiQuantum, to deliver innovative and feature-rich solutions. This monolithic platform, combining advanced photonics features and RF-CMOS on a single silicon wafer, is designed to enable faster, more energy-efficient data transmission and supports various high-growth markets.

title: Nvidia invests $10M in sidewalk robot delivery company Serve Robotics
summary: Nvidia has invested $10 million in Serve Robotics, an Uber spinout specializing in sidewalk delivery robots. This investment marks Nvidia's first foray into the sidewalk delivery sector and is part of a broader collaboration to advance robotic technology. Serve Robotics will use the funds to expand its service beyond Los Angeles and San Francisco, leveraging Nvidia's Jetson AI platform, perception, and mapping tools to enhance its fully autonomous delivery robots.

title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split, $10 bln share buyback
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has announced a 20-for-1 stock split and authorized a $10 billion share buyback plan, causing its shares to rise by 7% in extended trading. This marks Amazon's first stock split since 1999, aimed at making shares more accessible to investors and increasing flexibility for employees managing equity. Trading with the new share price will commence on June 6.

title: Why Nvidia sees a future in software and services: Recurring revenue
summary: Nvidia is shifting its business model to focus on software and services to generate recurring revenue, moving beyond its traditional hardware sales. CFO Colette Kress highlighted opportunities in autonomous vehicles, where Nvidia's software will be monetized over the life of the car, and the metaverse, envisioning annual revenues of $1,000 per creative user and per digital twin bot. This strategic pivot aims to create more predictable income streams, leveraging existing hardware with new software offerings and expanding into new markets.

title: Nvidia invests USD 10 million in Serve Robotics
summary: Nvidia has invested $10 million in Serve Robotics, a company specializing in sidewalk delivery robots. This investment follows Serve Robotics' expanded seed round in December 2021 and will help the startup broaden its services beyond Los Angeles and San Francisco. Serve's robots utilize Nvidia's Jetson edge AI platform for autonomous movement and will integrate Nvidia's perception and mapping tools, alongside synthetic data generation for testing.

title: Nvidia investing $10M in last-mile company Serve Robotics
summary: Nvidia is investing $10M in Serve Robotics, a last-mile delivery company that spun out of Uber. Serve Robotics utilizes Nvidia's Jetson edge compute module for its autonomous mobile robots, making this investment a continuation of their long-standing partnership. The funding will support Serve Robotics' expansion and fleet building in existing and new markets.

title: Metaverse Technology Redefining Digital Experience
summary: The metaverse is emerging as a significant technological advancement, moving beyond gaming to redefine digital experiences across various industries. Companies like Facebook (Meta), Nike, and Microsoft are investing heavily, with projections indicating global metaverse revenue could approach $800 billion by 2024. While offering potential for retail, enterprise solutions, and new markets like virtual real estate and entertainment, the metaverse also faces challenges related to data security, legal frameworks, skilled resources, VR device limitations, and potential negative societal impacts.

title: 2 Bullish Stocks in this Weak Market from The Professor!!!
summary: In a weak market characterized by tech stock sell-offs due to inflation and interest rate concerns, "The Professor" highlights two bullish stocks: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) and Rambus Inc. (RMBS). JNJ is favored for its stability as a provider of essential goods, while RMBS is noted for its unusual relative strength in the semiconductor sector. Both stocks are presented with technical analysis setups, suggesting potential breakouts if the broader market stabilizes.

title: Aptos raises $200M to bring the Diem blockchain back to life
summary: Aptos Labs, a startup founded by former employees of Meta's Diem blockchain project, has raised $200 million in strategic funding led by Andreessen Horowitz. The company aims to develop a safe and scalable blockchain network, learning from Diem's regulatory challenges. Aptos is building its own open-source blockchain for Web3 applications and expects to launch its public mainnet in the third quarter of 2022.

title: Goldman Sachs ActiveBeta World Low Vol Plus Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:GLOV)
summary: This article provides details on the Goldman Sachs ActiveBeta World Low Vol Plus Equity ETF (GLOV), including its stock price, key statistics, and holdings. GLOV invests in large- and mid-cap stocks in developed markets, focusing on value, momentum, quality, and low volatility factors. The ETF was launched on March 15, 2022, and is managed by Goldman Sachs.

title: Serve, Nvidia Formalize Partnership with $10M Investment
summary: Serve Robotics announced a $10 million strategic investment from chipmaker Nvidia, deepening their partnership and accelerating Serve's growth and development. This investment is significant as it's Nvidia's largest direct investment in a partner company in recent years and its first in the food robotics space. The collaboration aims to speed up the adoption and development of robots, especially as Serve Robotics recently achieved Level 4 automation, allowing robots to operate routinely without human intervention.

title: New Investments Ready to Propel AV Technology Forward
summary: New investments are rapidly advancing autonomous vehicle (AV) technology, with major automakers and tech companies pouring billions into R&D to enhance safety and performance. While full autonomy and mass adoption are long-term goals due to testing, regulatory, and infrastructure challenges, early commercial applications like robotaxis present immediate growth opportunities. The article details technological advancements, investment trends, regulatory hurdles, consumer acceptance, and the potential for AVs to revolutionize transportation.

title: When the world runs low on neon gas
summary: The Russia-Ukraine war has significantly impacted the global supply of neon gas, which is crucial for semiconductor manufacturing. Ukraine, home to two major neon purification companies, has halted production due to the conflict, threatening to exacerbate the existing worldwide chip shortage. This situation highlights the precarity of specialized global supply chains, where the disruption of a single component can have far-reaching consequences for the technology industry, leading to higher prices, delivery delays, and potential product unavailability.

title: Is It Finally Time to Start Buying Intel Stock?
summary: Intel, a pioneer in micro-processing, has seen its stock underperform competitors for years, but an ambitious makeover and new leadership may signal an attractive entry point for long-term investors. CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans for a massive $20 billion semiconductor plant in Ohio and other investments aim to restore Intel's market leadership. Despite investor skepticism reflected in its depressed stock price, Morningstar analysts see an appealing opportunity with a 27% discount to its fair value estimate and a 3.11% dividend yield.

title: NVIDIA DRIVE Continues Industry Momentum With $11 Billion Pipeline as DRIVE Orin Enters Production
summary: NVIDIA's DRIVE Hyperion platform, powered by DRIVE Orin, is gaining significant industry momentum with an $11 billion automotive pipeline. Leading EV maker BYD and several self-driving startups have selected the DRIVE Hyperion architecture for their next-generation software-defined vehicles, with DRIVE Orin now in production. The platform enables advanced autonomous driving capabilities and supports the development of new energy vehicles.

title: Introducing NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion 9: Next-Generation Platform for Software-Defined Autonomous Vehicle Fleets
summary: NVIDIA announced DRIVE Hyperion 9, its next-generation open platform for automated and autonomous vehicles, set to launch in 2027. This scalable and programmable architecture will leverage the DRIVE Thor central computer, offering 8x the performance of current platforms. DRIVE Hyperion 9 features heightened sensing capabilities with a rich array of high-resolution cameras, radars, lidars, and ultrasonics, designed for Level 3 urban and Level 4 highway driving.

title: This tech group is leading the charge in sustainable Web 3.0 development
summary: Atlas Technology Management, a Singapore-headquartered technology group, is expanding its Web 3.0 computing infrastructure and committing to sustainable development. The company aims to achieve 100% green energy adoption by 2024 and plans to expand its global coverage to over 30 countries in the next five years. Atlas is also exploring advanced technologies like underwater data centers to minimize environmental impact.

title: Nvidia CEO says interested in exploring chip manufacturing with Intel
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed interest in exploring Intel's chip manufacturing services. Intel, which aims to expand its foundry business, confirmed ongoing discussions with Nvidia. This potential collaboration could be significant for Intel's efforts to attract external chip design companies to its factories.

title: Kroger partners with Nvidia to boost freshness efforts using AI
summary: Kroger has announced a strategic collaboration with Nvidia to enhance its fresh food initiatives using artificial intelligence and online simulations. The partnership will establish a lab and demonstration center at Kroger's headquarters to develop AI-driven methods for identifying food spoilage, optimizing delivery routes, and improving in-store efficiency. This move aims to leverage technology to extend the shelf life of fresh produce and improve customer service, aligning with Kroger's "Fresh for Everyone" branding.

title: Nvidia considers teaming up with Intel on chip manufacturing
summary: Nvidia is in discussions with Intel about potentially using Intel as a foundry for manufacturing its chips. This consideration comes amid a global semiconductor chip shortage and Intel's efforts to become a major U.S. foundry supplier, despite the challenges of aligning technologies and business models between the companies. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed openness to the idea, noting existing close collaboration between the firms.

title: WeRide zooms to $400m
summary: China-based autonomous driving technology producer WeRide has raised $400 million from investors including GAC and Robert Bosch, bringing its valuation to $4.4 billion. This latest funding round increases the company's total funding to over $1.2 billion and follows previous rounds with notable investors such as Alliance Ventures and Nvidia GPU Ventures. WeRide, founded in 2017, develops software and hardware for level 4 autonomous driving.

title: TIME100 Most Influential Companies 2022: NVIDIA
summary: NVIDIA, under CEO Jensen Huang, has become critical for various technologies from cryptocurrency to self-driving cars, earning it a spot on the 2022 TIME100 Most Influential Companies list. Despite abandoning a major acquisition, NVIDIA's investments in AI and experimental technology are profoundly shaping the future, with expectations for its processors to make human effort obsolete in many sectors.

title: TIME100 Most Influential Companies 2022: NVIDIA
summary: NVIDIA has been recognized as one of TIME's 100 Most Influential Companies of 2022 due to its essential role in advancing technology. Under CEO Jensen Huang, NVIDIA's processors are crucial for cryptocurrency, NFTs, and self-driving cars. The company's investments in AI and experimental technology are significantly shaping the future, despite abandoning a major acquisition this year.

title: The semiconductor decade: A trillion-dollar industry
summary: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach a trillion-dollar valuation by 2030, driven by an average annual growth rate of 6 to 8 percent. Key growth areas include automotive, computation and data storage, and wireless technologies, with automotive expected to triple demand. Industry leaders are advised to strategically focus on R&D, manufacturing, and sourcing to capitalize on this expansion.

title: TSMC To Become NVIDIA’s Sole GPU Supplier In 2022 – Report
summary: A report from Business Korea suggests that TSMC is poised to become NVIDIA's sole GPU supplier by the end of 2022 due to ongoing issues with Samsung Foundry's production yields. NVIDIA, which previously diversified its suppliers, is now reportedly moving all its GPU manufacturing to TSMC, including its new Hopper data center GPUs. This shift follows earlier rumors of a rift between Samsung and NVIDIA and significant investments by NVIDIA into TSMC's advanced manufacturing processes.

title: Corsair One i300 review
summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact and stylish small form factor gaming PC that offers extremely high-end performance with components like the Intel Core i9-12900K and Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti. While it excels in performance for 4K gaming and also serves as a powerful workstation for content creators, its major drawbacks are its exorbitant price tag ($4,999 / £4,399) and limited upgradeability due to its small chassis. The review concludes that while it's a top-tier machine for those who can afford it and don't plan on tinkering, it's overkill and too expensive for most casual gamers.

title: Investing in esports in the context of recent events
summary: This article discusses the growth of esports, significant investment it has received, and current challenges due to global events like the COVID-19 pandemic and the Russia-Ukraine conflict. It identifies investment opportunities in publicly traded tech companies, specialized esports firms, and esports-focused ETFs, while also raising questions about future growth amidst potential new pandemics and the integration of NFTs.

title: Technology ETF
summary: VanEck offers a suite of Technology ETFs that allow investors to gain exposure to disruptive and transformative megatrends such as semiconductors, spatial technology, hydrogen, esports, and cryptocurrency. These ETFs provide diversified exposure, potential for future winners through rules-based selection, regular rebalancing, and lower fees compared to actively managed funds. The article also details various risks associated with investing in Technology ETFs, including industry concentration, market risk, and liquidity risks.

title: Hello FPGA – Getting Started with Microchip FPGAs
summary: The "Hello FPGA" webinar introduces Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) and Microchip's low-power FPGA portfolio, drawing from a Microchip University course. It covers fundamental FPGA technology, provides background information, and features videos from the course, guided by Tim McCarthy (Microchip) and Clemens Valens (Elektor). The webinar aims to make FPGAs more accessible, despite their perceived complexity requiring hardware description languages like VHDL or Verilog.

title: Experts Details: Lillian Currens
summary: This page provides details about Lillian Currens, the deputy editor at Schaeffer's Investment Research. It highlights her professional background, experience in writing and editing digital content, and her role in managing Schaeffer's social media. The article also lists several of her published articles discussing stock analyses for companies like Netflix, Vertex Pharmaceuticals, Nvidia, and Home Depot.

title: Meta (FB) Plans to Introduce Virtual Coins to Diversify Income
summary: Meta Platforms (FB) is exploring the introduction of virtual coins, tokens, and lending services within its applications to reduce its reliance on advertising revenue. The company plans to create in-app tokens, similar to those in games like Roblox, and also social tokens and "creator coins" for influencers, rather than a blockchain-based cryptocurrency like its failed Diem project. This strategic shift aims to diversify its income streams and engage users amidst increasing competition and regulatory scrutiny.

title: Which is the bigger company: AMD, Nvidia, or Intel?
summary: This article analyzes Intel, AMD, and Nvidia to determine which is the "bigger" company, looking beyond market capitalization to factors like employee count, revenue, product diversity, and market share. While Nvidia leads in market cap, sustained analysis reveals Intel to be the largest overall due to its revenue and employee numbers, despite AMD and Nvidia's significant recent growth. The article also touches on the unique advantage Intel holds with its chip manufacturing capabilities.

title: Metaverse Market Size [2022-2029] Worth USD 1527.55 Billion | Exhibiting a CAGR of 47.6%
summary: The metaverse market is projected to grow significantly from USD 100.27 billion in 2022 to USD 1,527.55 billion by 2029, showcasing a CAGR of 47.6%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing penetration of online shopping and gaming, alongside advancements in blockchain technology and VR devices. Key companies leading this expansion include Tencent Holdings Ltd., NVIDIA Corporation, Meta, and Microsoft Corporation, who are focusing on product portfolio expansion and technological innovation.

title: NVIDIA enters Top 5 of Fortune's '100 Best Companies to Work For'
summary: NVIDIA has been ranked #5 on Fortune's 2022 list of the "100 Best Companies to Work For," placing ahead of major financial institutions like Capital One and American Express. Cisco secured the top spot for the second consecutive year, followed by Hilton and Wegmans Food Markets, Inc., with Salesforce coming in fourth. The recognition highlights the importance of strong workplace culture during a period of significant workforce changes.

title: The end of one-chip wonders: Why Nvidia, Intel and AMD’s valuations have experienced massive upheaval
summary: The article discusses the dramatic shift in valuations among semiconductor companies, specifically Nvidia, Intel, and AMD. Five years ago, Intel's market capitalization surpassed Nvidia and AMD combined, but now Nvidia alone is worth more than the other two combined, reflecting significant changes in the semiconductor industry. This upheaval is attributed to evolving technology consumption patterns and companies specializing in new areas.

title: With Vast Data, Nvidia is in the company of a storage unicorn
summary: Nvidia has deepened its relationship with storage unicorn Vast Data, with Nvidia Bluefield DPUs now powering Vast Data's new Ceres storage platform. This collaboration aims to redefine data center storage for the AI era by leveraging advanced flash drives and DPU technology for improved performance and cost efficiency. The partnership evolved through Mellanox, which Nvidia acquired and had previously invested in Vast Data, and both companies are also developing Zero Trust access to data storage and GPU direct storage.

title: Broadcom Inc. stock falls Thursday, underperforms market
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) stock fell 3.15% on Thursday, closing at $573.85, a day when the broader market also saw declines. This drop ended a two-day winning streak for the company, leaving its stock $103.91 short of its 52-week high. Competitors such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Ltd. ADR, NVIDIA Corp., and Intel Corp. also experienced declines.

title: Synopsys Introduces Industry's Highest Performance Neural Processor IP
summary: Synopsys has launched its DesignWare ARC NPX6 and NPX6FS NPU IP, along with the MetaWare MX Development Toolkit, to meet the increasing performance demands of AI systems-on-chip. This new NPU IP delivers the industry's highest performance, offering up to 3,500 TOPS for AI applications and support for complex neural network models, while also addressing functional safety requirements for automotive use. The comprehensive software environment aims to accelerate application development and ensure compliance with standards like ISO 26262.

title: Nvidia's GPU-powered AI is creating chips with 'better than human design'
summary: Nvidia is leveraging its GPU-powered AI to develop and design new computer chips, including its own GPUs, leading to designs that are "better than human." The company's chief scientist, Bill Dally, explained how AI is used to accelerate chip design processes like power estimation, parasitic prediction, and automated standard cell migration. This approach not only significantly reduces design time and labor but also results in more efficient and smaller chip components, showcasing a win-win scenario for Nvidia's internal development and its AI hardware offerings.

title: Lapsus$ Cyberattack Victim List: Globant, Microsoft, Nvidia, Okta, Samsung, T-Mobile -
summary: The Lapsus$ hacker group continued to target high-profile companies even after some alleged members were arrested in March 2022. This article lists several major companies, including Globant, Microsoft, Nvidia, Okta, Samsung, and T-Mobile, that have been victims of Lapsus$ cyberattacks. It also provides guidance on how to protect against and respond to ransomware attacks, emphasizing data backups, multi-factor authentication, and prompt incident reporting.

title: Leaked Chats Show LAPSUS$ Stole T-Mobile Source Code – Krebs on Security
summary: Leaked private chat messages from the LAPSUS$ cybercrime group reveal they breached T-Mobile multiple times in March 2022, stealing source code for various projects. Despite T-Mobile's claim that no customer or government information was compromised, the chats detail LAPSUS$'s methods, including purchasing access, social engineering employees, and utilizing internal tools like Atlas to look up sensitive accounts. The article also highlights the group's internal conflicts, their leader's fixation on stealing source code, and their eventual undoing due to compromised servers and arrests.

title: Chipmaker Texas Instruments expects revenue hit as China lockdowns dent demand
summary: Texas Instruments (TXN.O) forecast second-quarter revenue below Wall Street estimates due to reduced demand caused by COVID-19 lockdowns in China, sending its shares down 7% in extended trading. The company noted that the impact was primarily due to disruptions at its customers' factories. Despite this, Texas Instruments reported a 14% revenue increase to $4.91 billion for the first quarter, exceeding analyst expectations.

title: Fidelity Enters the Metaverse with New Financial Education Experience: Invest Quest at The Fidelity Stack
summary: Fidelity Investments has launched a new gamified financial education experience called "The Fidelity Stack" in Decentraland, an eight-story virtual building designed to teach users about ETF investing through an "Invest Quest" challenge. This initiative aims to engage younger audiences, particularly the 18-35 age range, following the company's recent introduction of a metaverse-themed ETF, FMET. Fidelity views this as a significant step in providing financial education in decentralized, virtual communities.

title: HPE Creates Its Own AI Stack For Large Enterprises
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is expanding its AI software portfolio and leveraging its HPC hardware expertise to provide a comprehensive AI stack for large enterprises. This initiative includes a Machine Learning Development System that simplifies infrastructure management and facilitates collaboration for training models at scale, addressing the complexities faced by data scientists. Furthermore, HPE is promoting "swarm learning" to enable AI model training where data resides, crucial for privacy and compliance in industries like healthcare and banking.

title: HCL Technologies brings All Access Media Marketplace Platform
summary: HCL Technologies has launched its All Access Media Marketplace Platform at NAB Las Vegas, aiming to streamline content creation and distribution processes. Developed in coordination with Microsoft and CoreSite, the platform addresses workflow, application, and vendor discovery challenges for media creators. It features HCL Workflow Creator for building workflows, leverages Microsoft 365 for communication, and utilizes Azure Cloud for cybersecurity, aligning with MovieLabs 2030 Vision.

title: Nvidia’s CFO on how they retain tech talent —even as the Great Resignation continues
summary: Colette Kress, Nvidia's CFO, discusses the company's strategies for retaining top tech talent amidst the Great Resignation. She emphasizes that beyond competitive salaries, a strong culture focused on continuous learning, challenging work, and an egalitarian leadership approach are key factors. Nvidia provides unique upskilling opportunities, offers benefits like "Free Days" and an employee stock purchase program, and fosters an environment where all employees are valued and have opportunities to lead, regardless of title.

title: US-based Applied Materials’ India arm buys office space, land in Bangalore for Rs 338 crore
summary: Applied Materials India, the subsidiary of the US-based semiconductor equipment manufacturer, has acquired nearly 10 acres of land and 2.6 lakh sq ft of office space in Bangalore for Rs 338 crore. This acquisition is part of the company's plan to invest over Rs 1,800 crore in India to expand its engineering infrastructure and support its growth. India is projected to become the fastest-growing semiconductor consumption market globally, with demand expected to exceed $110 billion by the end of the decade.

title: “There must be a new approach to the design of chips for high-performance computing applications”
summary: Joe Costello, former CEO of Cadence, has joined Anari AI as a Board Advisor to guide the startup in revolutionizing the hardware AI industry. Costello discusses the future of semiconductors, emphasizing the transition from general-purpose to domain-specific architectures, the critical role of cloud computing, and the transformative impact of AI/ML. He highlights the necessity of new chip design approaches for high-performance computing and the importance of fostering strong ecosystems.

title: Nvidia: transforming industries with pioneering AI tech
summary: Nvidia, a leader in AI and accelerated computing, is transforming industries through its pioneering technology. The company designs GPUs and APIs, supporting over 2.5 million developers globally and expanding its presence in gaming with RTX technology. Nvidia has also developed the Omniverse platform for virtual world simulation and is rapidly advancing supercomputing with its CUDA programming model and new H100 GPU and Grace CPU Superchip processors.

title: FMET Is a New, Fabulous Metaverse Idea
summary: Fidelity has launched the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), which tracks the Fidelity Metaverse Index, to capitalize on the nascent but expanding metaverse investment theme. The ETF mixes established tech giants with innovative smaller companies, including Meta Platforms, Apple, NVIDIA, Tencent, Baidu, and JD.com. Investors are advised that patience will be key for metaverse investing due to anticipated volatility, though the long-term growth outlook is considered bright with billions in projected revenue.

title: Toyota to apply artificial intelligence to boost efficiency in its factories
summary: Toyota Motor North America (TMNA) is partnering with Invisible AI to implement artificial intelligence in its factories to improve efficiency and safety. This AI system, using computer vision, will be deployed in 14 North American factories to analyze manufacturing operations, detect issues, and enhance processes. The initiative aims to reduce inefficiencies and ensures data privacy by analyzing information in-house and anonymizing videos to protect workers.

title: Nvidia to pay $5.5 million penalty for 'inadequate disclosures' about cryptomining
summary: Nvidia Corporation has agreed to pay a $5.5 million civil penalty to settle charges from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The SEC alleged that Nvidia failed to adequately disclose the significant impact of cryptomining on its gaming revenue growth in fiscal 2018. The company did not admit or deny the findings but agreed to the settlement, which highlights its omission of information crucial for investors and analysts.

title: Nvidia pays $5.5m SEC fine over crypto-mining disclosures
summary: Nvidia agreed to pay a $5.5 million fine to the SEC for failing to adequately disclose revenue from crypto mining during two quarters in 2018. The SEC stated that Nvidia omitted information about surging demand from crypto miners while discussing how digital assets impacted other business lines. This lack of disclosure made Nvidia's financial results less predictable for investors.

title: NVIDIA (NVDA) Fined $5.5M for Failing to Disclose Crypto Mining Impact
summary: NVIDIA was fined $5.5 million by the SEC for failing to disclose the significant impact of cryptocurrency mining demand on its graphics card sales during fiscal year 2018. The SEC stated that this omission deprived investors of crucial information regarding the company's business in a key market. Following the settlement, NVIDIA's stock experienced a decline, reflecting the underperformance of its crypto mining processor (CMP) chips amid a downturn in cryptocurrency prices.

title: NetApp Teams with NVIDIA to Accelerate HPC and AI with Turnkey Supercomputing Infrastructure
summary: NetApp and NVIDIA announced an expanded AI portfolio, including the certification of NetApp EF600 all-flash NVMe storage with BeeGFS file system for NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD. This partnership aims to simplify AI and HPC infrastructure, offering solutions like ONTAP AI and NVIDIA DGX Foundry to provide turnkey supercomputing capabilities. The collaboration provides organizations with flexible, high-performance infrastructure for AI development, whether on-premises or as a hosted service.

title: Intel launches new AI chips, challenging Nvidia's market
summary: Intel Corp launched new AI chips, Gaudi2 and Greco, to challenge Nvidia's dominance in the AI chip market. The company is focusing on developing its open-standard software platform to differentiate itself and aims to capture a significant share of the rapidly growing AI chip market, estimated to reach $50 billion in the next five years.

title: Absci Reports First Quarter 2022 Financial and Operating Results
summary: Absci Corporation announced its first-quarter 2022 financial and operating results, highlighting significant progress in advancing partnered programs, securing new collaborations, and enhancing its AI-driven technology platform. The company reported a net loss of $29.5 million and cash and cash equivalents of $226.0 million, sufficient to fund operations through the end of 2024, while reiterating its guidance for at least eight new Active Programs in 2022. Key achievements include a research collaboration with Merck, machine learning breakthroughs for antibody discovery, and a partnership with NVIDIA.

title: Improving PPA With AI
summary: This article discusses how Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are increasingly being used in Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools to enhance chip design by improving Power, Performance, and Area (PPA), reducing design errors, and accelerating time to market. It highlights that while AI can't replace the need for human designers to make critical decisions, it significantly streamlines tedious processes and optimizes designs that would be too complex for humans alone. The integration of AI promises greater efficiency, cost reduction, and the ability to design highly complex chips, as demonstrated by examples from leading technology companies.

title: Z by HP Powers Today’s Professionals to Create without Limitation
summary: HP announced new Z by HP solutions, including ZBook mobile workstations, Z displays, a Thunderbolt G4 Dock, and tools for data scientists, aimed at empowering creative and technical professionals. These products offer enhanced performance, seamless collaboration, and improved productivity for those working in diverse environments, from on-the-go to hybrid setups. Key features include powerful CPUs/GPUs, high-refresh-rate displays, secure docking, and a new Data Science Stack Manager to streamline workflows.

title: Gina Sanchez explains why these two mega cap stocks are a buy
summary: Gina Sanchez, chief market strategist at Lido Advisors, recommends buying Walmart Inc (NYSE: WMT) and Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA) stocks. She believes Walmart is a strong reopening stock capable of handling supply chain issues, while Nvidia offers a deep discount opportunity as a leader in the semiconductor space, essential for the growing Cloud trend. Both companies are scheduled to report their quarterly results later this month.

title: Broom, Broom: WeRide Revs Up Self-Driving Street Sweepers Powered by NVIDIA
summary: WeRide has launched a public pilot of 50 self-driving Robo Street Sweepers in Guangzhou, China, powered by NVIDIA technology. These vehicles perform essential municipal cleaning services autonomously, leveraging NVIDIA's high-performance AI compute platform for real-time data processing and safety. This deployment marks WeRide's second mass-produced self-driving vehicle and expands its diverse fleet of autonomous solutions.

title: Top blockchain stocks to explore: COIN, SQ, NVDA, MARA & RIOT
summary: This article highlights several top blockchain stocks, including Coinbase Global, NVIDIA Corporation, and Riot Blockchain. It mentions Coinbase's Q1 FY22 net revenue and NVIDIA's upcoming Q1 FY23 financial results. The content provides a glimpse into the financial performance of these key players in the blockchain sector.

title: Understanding Syfe’s Disruptive Technology portfolio
summary: This article explains Syfe's Disruptive Technology portfolio, detailing why investors should consider disruptive tech and how Syfe approaches this investment theme through direct stock holdings rather than ETFs. It outlines six key themes within the portfolio—Blockchain and crypto, Robotics and Artificial Intelligence, Fintech, Next-gen internet, Cloud-computing and cybersecurity, and Video games and e-sports—highlighting top holdings for each. The article emphasizes cost savings for investors through Syfe's direct investment model.

title: SEIV ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:SEIV
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the SEI Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Value Factor ETF (SEIV), focusing on its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It highlights SEIV as an actively managed fund targeting US large-cap value stocks using a quantitative-based selection process. The analysis covers AUM, expense ratio, holdings breakdown by sector and region, and dividend history, including a 24.49% increase in its last reported dividend.

title: HP Envy 34 All-in-One Desktop review: A big-screen AIO for power users and creators
summary: HP has updated its Envy All-in-One desktop, featuring a 34-inch 5K wide-screen display, making it a strong alternative to the discontinued 27-inch iMac for creative users and power users. This review highlights its impressive design, versatile features including accessible RAM/SSD slots, and strong performance, especially in graphics, though it notes some bloatware and a lack of display swivel. The Envy 34 offers various configurations, from an entry-level model to a high-end option with a Core i9 processor and RTX 3080 graphics.

title: TI Breaks Ground on New Semiconductor Wafer Plants in Sherman with Potential $30B Investment
summary: Texas Instruments has broken ground on new 300-mm semiconductor wafer fabrication plants in Sherman, Texas, representing a potential $30 billion investment. This project is the largest private-sector economic development in Texas history, aiming to create up to 3,000 jobs and produce millions of analog and embedded processing chips daily. Production from the first fab is expected to begin in 2025, contributing to long-term manufacturing capacity for semiconductors in the U.S.

title: Wall Street ends sharply lower as Target and growth stocks sink
summary: Wall Street closed sharply lower on Wednesday after Target's stock plummeted by about a quarter of its value, exposing concerns about the U.S. economy due to surging prices. This marked the worst one-day loss for the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average since June 2020, as inflation continued to erode consumer purchasing power, impacting retailers and prompting a market sell-off. Megacap growth stocks also contributed to the decline, with the Nasdaq falling significantly, amidst broader economic worries including rising inflation, supply chain issues, and central bank tightening.

title: 上海汽車、「純中国産」に固執せず自動運転タクシー開発
summary: 中国の上海汽車集団（SAIC）が、自動運転レベル4のロボタクシー車両開発において、アメリカ企業Cerenceの音声AI技術を採用することを発表しました。SAICは「純中国産」にこだわらず、Cerence Mobility PlatformとSpeech Signal Enhancementを導入し、乗客が音声で車内機能を操作できる高精度のロボタクシー実用化を目指しています。これは、NVIDIAの自動運転ソリューション採用に続く、海外技術の積極的な取り入れの一環です。

title: Clorox Stock (CLX): Anything But A Value Stock
summary: This article challenges the traditional classification of Clorox as a "value stock" and Nvidia as a "growth stock" by analyzing their fundamentals and valuations. The author argues that despite Clorox's low-growth industry, its valuation, when considering metrics like the PEG ratio and a proprietary model, makes it less of a value than commonly perceived. Conversely, Nvidia, a high-growth company, appears more reasonably valued than Clorox when growth prospects are factored in.

title: Semiconductor Competition: Opportunities for Taiwan
summary: The article discusses how Taiwan, particularly TSMC, dominates the global semiconductor industry despite intensifying geopolitical competition and efforts by countries like the US, EU, and China to achieve self-sufficiency in chip manufacturing. It highlights Taiwan's current technological edge and suggests that rather than threats, these global shifts present opportunities for Taiwanese firms to expand into new markets, enhance R&D, and diversify their economic reliance on manufacturing through strategic international partnerships.

title: Taiwan’s Tech Titans Adopt World’s First NVIDIA Grace CPU-Powered System Designs
summary: NVIDIA announced that major Taiwanese computer makers like ASUS, Foxconn Industrial Internet, GIGABYTE, QCT, Supermicro, and Wiwynn will release the first systems powered by the NVIDIA Grace CPU Superchip and Grace Hopper Superchip in the first half of 2023. These systems are designed for various demanding workloads including digital twins, AI, high-performance computing, cloud graphics, and gaming. The Grace Superchips deliver advanced performance and energy efficiency, supporting NVIDIA's vision of "AI factories" in data centers.

title: Nvidia Q1 FY2023 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
summary: Nvidia (NVDA) is set to report Q1 FY 2023 earnings, with analysts expecting growth in adjusted EPS and revenue, albeit at a slower pace. Investors will closely watch data center revenue, which is projected to accelerate and potentially surpass gaming revenue for the first time in FY 2023. The company is investing in AI chips to continue its growth trajectory, building on demand fueled by the pandemic.

title: Semiconductor ETF | ESG-screened investment in tech
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor UCITS ETF offers investors exposure to the semiconductor sector, targeting pure-play companies with at least half their revenues from semiconductors. It is Europe's first Semiconductor ETF, designed to capture growth from the "fourth industrial revolution" driven by semiconductor advancements, and is classified as SFDR Article 8 for promoting environmental and social characteristics. The ETF focuses on liquid semiconductor stocks globally and has a strategy that has been successful in the US since 2011, with $25 billion in AUM.

title: The Next $1 Trillion US Tech Stock Is Far Away From Joining Exclusive Club
summary: Nvidia's (NVDA) stock slump has put its $1 trillion valuation on hold, with the company’s stock giving back nearly all its gains from the past year. Investors are keenly awaiting first-quarter earnings to gauge the company's immediate future. The stock's performance has been pressured by factors including lockdowns in China and a general rout in high-valuation stocks.

title: Nvidia stock falls on light guidance, CFO says company will slow hiring
summary: Nvidia's stock dropped after providing a light forecast for the current quarter, despite beating analyst expectations for sales and earnings in Q1 2023.
