The company announced it would slow hiring and control expenses due to a "challenging macro environment." Revenue for the current quarter is projected to be $8.1 billion, lower than analyst expectations, with the CFO citing the war in Ukraine and Covid lockdowns in China as contributing factors.

title: Nvidia says video gaming market slowing; shares drop 7%
summary: Nvidia projects a decline in video game chip sales for the current quarter and attributes new supply-chain challenges to China's COVID-19 lockdowns. CEO Jensen Huang stated the gaming market is slowing, leading to a reduction in sales to China. Despite beating first-quarter revenue and earnings estimates, the company's shares dropped 6.7% due to the softer outlook and ongoing market concerns.

title: Nvidia Sees Explosive Growth In Data Center, Professional Visualization
summary: Nvidia reported impressive first fiscal quarter 2023 results, with revenue up 46% year-over-year to $8.3 billion, driven significantly by an 83% increase in data center revenue and a 67% increase in professional visualization revenue. CEO Jensen Huang highlighted four key initiatives: ramping next-generation AI infrastructure, launching the Grace CPU, expanding the ORIN robotics processor, and growing software platforms like Nvidia AI and Omniverse. Despite strong earnings that beat analyst expectations, the stock saw a dip in after-hours trading.

title: Ampere Roadmap Has Four Future Arm Server Chips
summary: Ampere Computing has outlined its future Arm server chip roadmap, revealing plans for three new processors beyond the recently announced AmpereOne ("Siryn") chip, extending through 2025. The company emphasizes its agile development approach and customer-driven roadmap, focusing on a continuous cadence of releases rather than yearly changes, with hyperscalers influencing chip features and lifespan. The roadmap details expected advancements in core count, process technology (moving to 5nm and 3nm), memory (DDR5, DDR6), and PCI-Express versions for these upcoming chips, while also hinting at a future shift to chiplet architectures.

title: Top Global Systems Makers Accelerate Adoption of NVIDIA Grace and Grace Hopper
summary: NVIDIA announced that top global computer makers including Atos, Dell Technologies, GIGABYTE, HPE, Inspur, Lenovo, and Supermicro are adopting its new Grace and Grace Hopper superchips to build next-generation servers for AI and HPC workloads. These systems, utilizing the NVIDIA HGX platform, aim to deliver high performance and energy efficiency. Leading supercomputing centers like Los Alamos National Laboratory and the Swiss National Computing Center are among the first to implement these superchips, with systems like Venado expected to exceed 10 exaflops of AI performance.

title: Los Alamos National Laboratory Announces Details Of Supercomputer Collaboration With Nvidia
summary: Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) announced new details of its supercomputer collaboration with Nvidia and HPE, resulting in the "Venado" system. Venado will be the first U.S. customer to receive an Nvidia Grace central processing unit and will utilize a balanced architecture optimized for diverse scientific research, from materials science to power-grid modeling. It represents a significant step in developing a robust software ecosystem and leadership-class computing capabilities for the Laboratory, with delivery expected in 2023.

title: Solving the World’s Biggest Challenges, Together
summary: NVIDIA is launching a new campaign to connect its diverse impacts across various fields, from gaming and AI to scientific research and business innovation. The campaign aims to highlight how NVIDIA's core GPU technology and platforms like CUDA underpin advancements in seemingly disparate areas, fostering a coherent understanding of the company's broad influence. It emphasizes that these technological spillovers are intentional and represent the essence of NVIDIA's mission to solve global challenges collaboratively.

title: Adas/Connected Car - June 2022 v2
summary: The "ADAS/Connected Car - June 2022" report provides an extensive overview of advancements and challenges in advanced driver-assistance systems and connected vehicle technologies. It emphasizes the critical role of edge computing, the integration of AI with radar systems, and the importance of connectivity for enhancing vehicle safety and performance. The report serves as a valuable resource for understanding the current landscape and future evolution of autonomous and connected automotive technology.

title: 6 High-Quality, Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
summary: Semiconductor stocks have seen significant declines in 2022, creating a strong buying opportunity for long-term investors. Morningstar analysts highlight several high-quality, undervalued semiconductor companies with strong competitive advantages, benefiting from attractive valuations and a long-term demand surge for chips across various industries. The article discusses when the chip shortage might end and the positive long-term outlook for the sector despite potential short-term slowdowns.

title: Nvidia TAO Toolkit update aims to simplify deep learning model development
summary: Nvidia has updated its TAO Toolkit platform to simplify and accelerate deep learning model creation for enterprise applications, making AI development more accessible and less time-consuming. The toolkit now includes new capabilities like "bring your own model weights" and integration with TensorBoard for better visualization, along with the option to deploy as-a-service with REST APIs. While other companies have also worked on simplifying AI pipelines, analysts note that Nvidia's TAO Toolkit, with its low-code nature and pre-trained models, aims to speed up time-to-market for AI models and reduce the need for extensive data science expertise.

title: Vision in the Making: Andrew Ng’s Startup Automates Factory Inspection
summary: Andrew Ng's startup, Landing AI, is leveraging his data-centric AI philosophy and NVIDIA GPUs to automate factory inspections, improving efficiency and reducing costs for manufacturers. Their flagship product, LandingLens, helps classify defects in manufacturing processes, as demonstrated by a chip maker client who significantly reduced costs. The company is also developing LandingEdge to bring AI inference directly to the factory floor.

title: Festo Develops With Isaac Sim to Drive Its Industrial Automation
summary: Festo, a German industrial automation company, is using NVIDIA's Isaac Sim, a robotics simulation application built on NVIDIA Omniverse, to develop AI for its future collaborative robots (cobots). The company's AI lab, led by Dionysios Satikidis, is applying principles from game development to robotics simulation, focusing on narrowing the sim-to-real gap and improving perception for real robots by training AI models on synthetic data. Festo is also utilizing Isaac Cortex within Isaac Sim to simplify programming for cobot skills, aiming to make task-aware robot programming as intuitive as programming gaming AIs.

title: TSMC Chairman Mark Liu on the revolution in semiconductors
summary: TSMC Chairman Mark Liu discusses how 5G, A.I., and high-performance computing (HPC) are ushering in a "golden era" for the semiconductor industry. He highlights how continuous innovation, exemplified by the pure-play foundry model pioneered by TSMC, has driven down costs and enabled widespread technological adoption. Liu projects significant growth for the industry, emphasizing the increasing value and omnipresence of semiconductors in modern society and foreseeing their critical role in future applications like VR/AR and personalized medicine.

title: Nvidia’s Data Center Segment Becomes its Biggest Revenue Contributor for Q1 2022
summary: Nvidia's data center segment has surpassed its gaming segment to become the largest revenue contributor for the company in Q1 2022. This shift signifies the growing importance of AI and cloud computing in Nvidia's business model. The article highlights this significant financial performance change for Nvidia.

title: Fortinet (FTNT) to Split Stock in 5 to Attract More Investors
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) announced a five-for-one forward stock split, effective June 22, aiming to make its shares more accessible to employees and retail investors. This move is expected to boost demand and further increase share prices, building on its impressive 710% return over the past five years. Other tech giants like Alphabet, Amazon, and NVIDIA have also recently undertaken stock splits for similar reasons.

title: Is the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) Right for You?
summary: The Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) offers investors diversified exposure to companies benefiting from the metaverse's growth. This article examines METV's top holdings, including Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Roblox, Microsoft, and Autodesk, highlighting their roles in the metaverse ecosystem. It also discusses the fund's expense ratio and the potential market size of the metaverse industry.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell 8.26% on Monday, closing at $86.99, amidst a general market downturn with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also dropping. The stock recorded its seventh consecutive day of losses and underperformed against competitors such as NVIDIA Corp., Intel Corp., and Texas Instruments Inc. Trading volume for AMD was also below its 50-day average.

title: 7 Founder-Led Stocks to Buy and Hold Forever
summary: This article highlights seven founder-led stocks, including Tesla, Nvidia, Salesforce, Blackstone, Steel Dynamics, Block, and Apollo Global Management, that are considered strong long-term buys due to their history of outperformance. The author emphasizes the sustained engagement of founders in their companies and provides a brief overview of each company's financial health, leadership, and recent performance. The piece suggests that founder-led companies tend to outperform those without a founder in the CEO role.

title: Who's growing faster than Nvidia and AMD? Rising datacenter star Marvell
summary: Marvell Technology significantly outpaced other major fabless chip designers like AMD, Nvidia, and Qualcomm in the first quarter, achieving 72% semiconductor revenue growth. This rapid growth was largely driven by its acquisition of Innovium, a switch ASIC business catering to hyperscale data centers. Marvell is strategically shifting its focus from consumer markets to data infrastructure, with datacenter products now accounting for 44% of its total revenue, reinforcing its long-term growth strategy in cloud, 5G, and automotive sectors.

title: All-In-One Financial Services? Vietnam’s MoMo Has a Super-App for That
summary: Vietnam-based fintech company MoMo has developed a super-app providing all-in-one financial services to over 30 million users. The company leverages NVIDIA GPUs and AI, specifically NVIDIA DGX systems, TensorRT, and Triton Inference Server, to enhance its chatbots, eKYC systems, recommendation engines, and credit scoring models. MoMo's use of AI has significantly sped up development, improved performance, and allowed for more accurate risk assessment, with plans to expand into graph neural networks and potentially make traditional credit scores obsolete.

title: $6.5B Texas Instruments Fab Could Be First of Four at Site Totaling $30B
summary: Texas Instruments plans to build up to four semiconductor chip fabrication plants in Sherman, Texas, with the first $6.5 billion fab already under construction and expected to begin production in 2025. The total investment for all four plants could reach nearly $30 billion, supported by significant local incentives. This development is part of a broader U.S. push to increase domestic semiconductor manufacturing amidst global shortages.

title: The King’s Swedish: AI Rewrites the Book in Scandinavia
summary: AI Sweden, in collaboration with NVIDIA, is developing a large language model called GPT-SW3 for Scandinavian languages. This initiative aims to provide a strategic digital asset, enabling citizens, companies, and government agencies to utilize advanced AI for various linguistic tasks. The model, trained on NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD using NVIDIA NeMo Megatron, is intended to inspire entrepreneurship and enhance services across the Nordic region.

title: Power GaN market to hit $2bn in 2027
summary: The power GaN market is projected to reach $2 billion by 2027, driven significantly by consumer products, which are expected to account for 48% of the market. Key growth areas also include datacom/telecom due to stricter regulations and increasing adoption of 48V-Point-Of-Load systems, as well as the automotive sector with GaN finding its way into DC-DC converters and OBCs. The market is also seeing new players, increased investments, and efforts to reduce manufacturing costs through 8-inch wafer capacity expansion and supply chain consolidation.

title: Opinion | Semiconductor Dependency Imperils American Security
summary: The article argues that the U.S.'s heavy reliance on Taiwan for advanced semiconductor manufacturing poses a significant national security risk, particularly in light of potential Chinese dominance. While some question the effectiveness of U.S. subsidies to foster domestic chip leadership, the authors emphasize that reducing dependence on TSMC is crucial to protect America's technology sector and military capabilities. A disruption in Taiwan's chip production could severely impact the U.S.

title: AI in the Big Easy: NVIDIA Research Lets Content Creators Improvise With 3D Objects
summary: NVIDIA Research has developed a new method called NVIDIA 3D MoMa, which allows content creators to quickly import 3D objects into graphics engines for improvisation and modification. This inverse rendering pipeline converts 2D images into editable 3D triangle mesh models within an hour on a single NVIDIA Tensor Core GPU, making them compatible with existing design tools. The technology was showcased using jazz instruments, highlighting its ability to reconstruct objects with accurate materials and lighting for creative workflows in various industries.

title: BITI Chart | ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF
summary: This page provides a live, interactive chart for the ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI), allowing users to analyze its performance. The chart offers various customization options and technical indicators to help investors understand price movements. It also includes current market data for BITI, such as its price, daily range, and 52-week range.

title: Teledyne FLIR Object Detection and Tracking Software Accelerates Thermal Camera Integration for ADAS and Autonomous Vehicles
summary: Teledyne FLIR has released Prism AI, a new software framework designed to accelerate the integration of thermal cameras into advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicles (AV). Prism AI provides classification, object detection, and tracking, featuring visible-and-thermal image fusion and advanced thermal image processing for superior pedestrian and animal detection, especially at night. This software, compatible with Teledyne FLIR thermal cameras, aims to help automotive OEMs improve safety, reduce development costs, and meet regulatory requirements for nighttime pedestrian crash prevention.

title: 7 Top Metaverse Stocks with Massive Potential
summary: The metaverse market is projected to reach $5 trillion by 2030, with a significant majority of consumers and industry leaders anticipating positive impacts. This article highlights seven top metaverse stocks and ETFs, including Global X Metaverse ETF (VR), ProShares Metaverse ETF (VERS), Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV), Meta Platforms (META), Nvidia (NVDA), Roblox (RBLX), and Unity Software (U), recommending them as key investment opportunities in this emerging sector. It notes that many of these stocks are currently oversold, presenting potential for future gains as the metaverse concept gains traction.

title: Finding NeMo: Sensory Taps NVIDIA AI for Voice and Vision Applications
summary: Sensory, a pioneer in compact AI models for consumer electronics, is leveraging NVIDIA's NeMo toolkit and Triton Inference Server to enhance its voice and vision AI applications. This hybrid computing approach improves features like wake words and speech-to-text accuracy across billions of devices. The collaboration allows for more powerful and adaptable on-device models through cloud connections and over-the-air updates, supporting 17 languages and achieving superior word error rates.

title: DDR5: How Faster Memory Speeds Shape The Future
summary: This article discusses the advancements and challenges brought by DDR5 memory, which doubles the data rate of its predecessor, DDR4. It highlights how DDR5's increased speeds and power efficiency enable emerging technologies like IoT and V2X autonomous vehicles. The article also touches upon the future of DDR6, anticipating further speed increases and new signaling innovations to overcome physical limitations.

title: Inferencing made simple with the NetApp DataOps Toolkit
summary: NetApp has introduced new capabilities in its DataOps Toolkit for Kubernetes to simplify the deployment of Triton Inference Server with NetApp persistent storage. This advancement addresses the complexities of AI model inferencing, allowing data scientists and MLOps engineers to deploy Triton Inference Server instances with one simple command, leveraging NetApp's data fabric for seamless synchronization and enterprise-class data protection features like Snapshot copies. The integration aims to streamline the AI lifecycle, particularly for on-premises deployments or those requiring specific storage efficiencies and data sovereignty.

title: Siemens and NVIDIA to Enable Industrial Metaverse
summary: Siemens and NVIDIA announced an expanded partnership to create an industrial metaverse by connecting Siemens Xcelerator and NVIDIA Omniverse platforms. This collaboration aims to transform the manufacturing industry by enabling full-fidelity digital twins and integrating AI systems from edge to cloud. The goal is to accelerate the use of digital twins, improve productivity, and facilitate immersive, physics-based simulations for faster and more confident decision-making across product lifecycles.

title: Building a Silicon Bulwark: How the United States and Taiwan Can Retain Joint Leadership of the Global Semiconductor Industry
summary: This article examines how the United States and Taiwan can jointly maintain their leadership in the global semiconductor industry against challenges, particularly from China's talent poaching. It proposes three key areas for cooperation: strengthening mutual legal assistance, harmonizing export control approaches, and pooling investments in semiconductor R&D and fabrication. The goal is to fortify Taiwan's "silicon shield" into a broader transnational bulwark.

title: Driving Data Center Innovation Through Ecosystem Partners
summary: NVIDIA is fostering data center innovation by collaborating with ecosystem partners through its DOCA Software Framework for BlueField DPUs. DOCA is an open SDK that allows developers to create DPU-accelerated applications for networking, storage, and security. Leading vendors like VMWare, Red Hat, and Juniper Networks are integrating their solutions, with Palo Alto Networks already developing a DPU-accelerated firewall.

title: Direxion SOXL ETF: How To Trade This Leveraged Play On Semis (NYSEARCA:SOXL)
summary: This article discusses the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares ETF (SOXL), a 3X leveraged ETF on the semiconductor index. Despite SOXL being down 85% this year, the author advises against buying it immediately. The recommendation is to wait for signs of a bottom in the broader semiconductor index (SOXX) before initiating a position in SOXL.

title: WNS acquires Vuram for $165Mn
summary: WNS (Holdings) Limited has acquired Chennai-based enterprise automation services provider Vuram for $165 million. This acquisition will expand WNS' intelligent automation capabilities and is expected to add approximately 2 percent to WNS’ net revenue in fiscal 2023, while also being accretive to adjusted earnings for the same fiscal year. Both companies anticipate enabling clients to accelerate automation, optimization, and transformation of their business models.

title: Artificial Intelligence at Nvidia – Two Current Use Cases
summary: This article examines two current use cases of Artificial Intelligence at Nvidia. The first use case details how Nvidia uses machine learning to enhance video resolution and audio quality during real-time video calls. The second use case explains how Nvidia, in collaboration with Adobe, uses AI algorithms to analyze marketing campaign effectiveness and optimize sales for a new GPU line.

title: Intel’s Dave Guzzi On The Big Channel Bet, Emerging Technologies And GPU Strategy
summary: Dave Guzzi, General Manager for Systems and Components Partners at Intel, discusses the company's significant commitment to its channel partners, driven by a massive manufacturing investment aimed at shifting chip production away from Asia. He emphasizes the importance of providing partners with secure supply chains and resources like Partner University to help them develop solutions with Intel's emerging technologies, including AI. Guzzi also highlights Intel's ambition to compete in the GPU market, offering partners more choice and leveraging Intel's extensive software development expertise.

title: In a Tough Quarter, Sustainable Strategies Struggled But Stayed Competitive
summary: In the second quarter, sustainable investing strategies faced headwinds as technology stocks, prominent in many ESG portfolios, declined, and energy stocks, often excluded, performed strongly. Despite this, many sustainable equity strategies, particularly those leveraging healthcare and consumer staples, managed to stay competitive and, in some cases, even outperformed the broader market. Over the long term, ESG-focused strategies continue to show strong performance relative to the market, indicating resilience despite short-term market shifts.

title: Teledyne FLIR Introduces Prism AI, Software for the Development of Automotive Thermal Perception Systems
summary: Teledyne FLIR has introduced Prism AI, a software framework designed to help automotive engineers integrate thermal cameras into advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicle (AV) systems. Prism AI offers features like visible and thermal image fusion, enhancing detection of pedestrians and animals, especially in low-light conditions. This development aims to improve road safety and assist OEMs in meeting upcoming regulatory requirements for nighttime collision prevention.

title: Nvidia QODA platform integrates quantum, classical computing
summary: Nvidia has launched the Quantum Optimized Device Architecture (QODA) platform, a hybrid environment that merges quantum and classical computing. This platform allows developers to create applications that leverage both systems, optimizing performance for scientific and quantum research and accelerating classical applications for quantum environments. QODA aims to bridge the gap between these two computing paradigms, facilitating quantum applications that utilize existing GPU and AI technologies.

title: Nvidia unveils QODA platform to accelerate hybrid quantum-classical computing research
summary: Nvidia has launched its Quantum Optimized Device Architecture (QODA) platform, designed to accelerate hybrid quantum-classical computing research. QODA aims to streamline development by providing a unified environment for supercomputers and quantum processors. This platform allows developers to integrate classical computing resources with quantum hardware from various partners, fostering breakthroughs in areas like chemistry and drug discovery.

title: Chip Shortage Turns into Surplus
summary: The global chip shortage, exacerbated by the coronavirus pandemic and high demand for electronics, has unexpectedly turned into a surplus due to high inflation, China's COVID restrictions, and the war in Ukraine, which reduced consumer spending. This shift has severely impacted semiconductor stocks, with companies like Micron Technology reducing production and manufacturers, especially in computing and smartphones, now facing an oversupply. The situation is worsened by hoarding practices, though some sectors like Apple suppliers and, for now, automobile chipmakers, are less affected.

title: First US Single-Stock ETFs Debut
summary: AXS Investments has become the first company to launch single-stock ETFs in the U.S. market, providing leveraged exposure to the daily performance of actively traded stocks like NVIDIA and Tesla. While these funds offer potential for significant short-term gains, AXS explicitly states they are not suitable for long-term investors due to the extreme performance volatility associated with leveraged and inverse products. SEC officials like Caroline Crenshaw have also voiced concerns about the risks these products pose to investors and markets.

title: Nancy Pelosi's husband buys millions of dollars’ worth of Nvidia stock ahead of vote on chip-manufacturing bill
summary: Paul Pelosi, husband of House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, purchased between $1 million and $5 million in Nvidia stock in June. This transaction occurred ahead of an anticipated congressional vote on a bill aimed at boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing. The disclosure shows the investment in the software and computer-chip company Nvidia.

title: Rescale integrates Nvidia’s AI software to power digital twins for the industrial metaverse
summary: Rescale Inc. has integrated Nvidia's AI software into its platform to simplify the creation of digital twins for industrial applications, supporting the development of the "industrial metaverse." This partnership allows Rescale customers to access GPU-optimized software, including Nvidia's NGC Catalog and Modulus neural network platform, enhancing the physical accuracy of digital twins with AI-assisted engineering. The collaboration aims to accelerate innovation by providing engineers and scientists a robust foundation for building and testing designs virtually, as exemplified by Hankook Tire's use of Rescale's technology.

title: Chip designers warm to U.S. bill despite big benefits to Intel
summary: Major U.S. chip firms are largely supporting a legislative package designed to boost the U.S. computer chip industry, despite earlier concerns that the subsidies disproportionately benefit manufacturers like Intel Corp. The legislation includes $52 billion in subsidies and an investment tax credit, primarily aiding companies that both design and manufacture chips. While chip designers like AMD and Qualcomm initially worried about not receiving direct benefits, they have reportedly agreed not to oppose the bill.

title: TSMC: Life’s A Beach, And Then You Make Dies
summary: TSMC reported strong Q2 2022 results with $18.16 billion in revenue and a 44.4% profit margin despite a slowdown in PC and smartphone sales, driven by high-performance computing (HPC) demand. The company is advancing its 3nm and 2nm process nodes, with 3nm on track for volume production in H2 2022 and 2nm for risk production in 2024. The article highlights TSMC's industry leadership compared to Intel's struggles and discusses the implications of the CHIPS Act for domestic chip manufacturing.

title: Nvidia leased a 100,000-square-foot building in Santa Clara
summary: Nvidia has leased a 100,000-square-foot building in Santa Clara's newly renovated Mission Technology Park, further expanding its footprint in the area. This lease follows another building acquisition by Nvidia in May, located next door to the recently leased structure. The move highlights the semiconductor giant's continued growth and investment in Silicon Valley.

title: NVDS Share Price & Live Chart | AXS 1.25X NVDA Bear Daily ETF
summary: This article provides live share price data, historical performance charts, and technical analysis for the AXS 1.25X NVDA Bear Daily ETF (NVDS). It details key metrics such as dividend yield, expense ratio, holdings, and long-term performance trends. Investors can access current share price, market data, and evaluate the ETF's suitability for their portfolio through InvestingPro's WarrenAI.

title: What’s New in NVIDIA AI Enterprise 2.1 | NVIDIA Technical Blog
summary: NVIDIA AI Enterprise 2.1 is now generally available, offering an optimized and supported software suite for deploying and scaling AI applications across various environments. Key updates include advanced data science capabilities with NVIDIA RAPIDS 22.04, low-code AI model development using NVIDIA TAO Toolkit 22.05 with new features like REST APIs and pre-trained models, and expanded public cloud support for Red Hat OpenShift and Microsoft Azure NVads A10 v5 series. These enhancements enable affordable GPU sharing, optimized performance for deep learning inferencing, and flexibility for enterprises in managing AI infrastructure.

title: Meet the 75-year-old propelling China’s AI ambitions forward
summary: Andrew Chi-Chih Yao, a Harvard-trained, China-born computer scientist and Turing Award recipient, is a pivotal figure in China's AI development. Through his "Yao Class" at Tsinghua University, he has inspired and educated a generation of tech entrepreneurs, leading to the creation of start-ups like Megvii and Pony.ai, valued at over US$12 billion. Yao emphasizes the critical importance of AI for China's future and advocates for talent cultivation and research to ensure the country leads in this field.

title: How’s That? Startup Ups Game for Cricket, Football and More With Vision AI
summary: TVConal, a deep-tech startup based in Singapore, is leveraging NVIDIA AI and computer vision to develop a sports video analytics platform. This platform automatically flags in-game events and provides performance insights for various sports like cricket, football, tennis, and badminton. By using NVIDIA Metropolis, TAO toolkit, and SDKs, TVConal's solution helps teams, leagues, and broadcasters analyze vast amounts of video data in real time, making sports analysis more efficient and accurate.

title: Pony.ai and SANY Establish Joint Venture to Develop Next Generation of Autonomous Trucks for Mass Production
summary: Pony.ai and SANY Heavy Truck have formed a strategic joint venture to develop and mass-produce next-generation Level 4 autonomous trucks. The collaboration aims to integrate Pony.ai's autonomous driving technology with SANY's heavy truck manufacturing expertise, with small-scale deliveries planned for 2022-2023 and mass production targeting 10,000 units annually by 2024. This partnership is expected to create a sophisticated intelligent logistics ecosystem, enhance road safety, reduce costs, and significantly lower greenhouse gas emissions by focusing on new energy vehicles.

title: Pony.ai Express: New Autonomous Trucking Collaboration Powered by NVIDIA DRIVE Orin
summary: Pony.ai and SANY Heavy Truck, China's largest heavy equipment manufacturer, have partnered to develop level 4 autonomous trucks using NVIDIA DRIVE Orin. The collaboration aims to address the rapidly growing package delivery market and driver shortages, with small-scale deliveries starting this year and mass production by 2024. The venture plans to produce approximately 10,000 trucks annually, leveraging NVIDIA DRIVE Orin's high-performance compute capabilities for safe and efficient autonomous operations.

title: Pony.ai autonomous driving controller built on NVIDIA DRIVE Orin, set for mass production
summary: Pony.ai announced that its Autonomous Driving Controller (ADC), powered by the NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion compute architecture (DRIVE Orin), has been sampled to customers with plans for mass production starting in Q4 2022. The ADC, featuring NVIDIA DRIVE Orin system-on-chip, offers configurations delivering up to 508 TOPS and is designed for safe, highly advanced intelligent driving across various road conditions. This development marks a significant step towards mass-producing robust autonomous vehicle platforms for both self-driving trucks and robotaxis.

title: With new federal aid, Minnesota chipmaker expects to speed up expansions
summary: SkyWater Technology, a Minnesota-based semiconductor manufacturer, anticipates accelerating its expansion projects in Minnesota, Indiana, and Florida thanks to the newly approved $52 billion CHIPS Act. While the exact amount of aid SkyWater will receive is unknown, CEO Tom Sonderman believes the funding package will help reduce risks associated with their investments. The CHIPS Act aims to bolster the U.S. chip industry and counter foreign government assistance, primarily from China, by providing direct aid and a 25% tax credit for domestic plant investments.

title: The Take: $52 Billion for Chip Plants… Better Than Nothing
summary: The US House of Representatives passed a $280 billion bill, the Chips and Science Act of 2022, primarily aimed at boosting US semiconductor manufacturing with $52.7 billion in subsidies for new plants. While the legislation is a step towards increasing domestic chip production, SAP global vice president Mike Lackey suggests that this funding might only support about 10 new fabrication facilities, which take years to build. The bill's effectiveness is further questioned by its lack of provisions for crucial capital equipment and the broader supply chain, amidst rapidly growing global demand for semiconductors.

title: Schwab Crypto Thematic ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:STCE)
summary: The Schwab Crypto Thematic ETF (NYSEARCA:STCE) tracks an index of global companies involved in the cryptocurrency ecosystem, utilizing a proprietary NLP algorithm for selection and weighting. It was launched on August 4, 2022, and is managed by Charles Schwab. The ETF's stock price experienced a decrease of 5.6% early in 2026, with top institutional shareholders including Financial Engines Advisors and Breakthru Advisory Services.

title: Micron 232-Layer TLC NAND in Volume Production
summary: Micron Technology, Inc. has begun volume production of its first 232-layer NAND, which offers the highest areal density, increased capacity, and improved energy efficiency compared to previous generations. This new NAND technology delivers a 50% faster data transfer rate and up to 100% higher write bandwidth, making it suitable for data-intensive applications from client to cloud, including AI, ML, and cloud computing. The 232-layer NAND is produced in Micron's Singapore fab and is initially shipping to customers for component use and in its Crucial SSD consumer product line.

title: UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium Announces Incorporation and New Board Members; Open for Membership
summary: The UCIe™ (Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express™) Consortium has officially incorporated and announced new board members, including Alibaba and NVIDIA, joining founding members like ASE, AMD, Intel, and Samsung. The consortium aims to advance the UCIe specification to create a universal and interoperable chiplet ecosystem, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already available for download. They are actively seeking new members to contribute to the development of future specifications.

title: Pelosi’s Taiwan trip puts the world's biggest chipmaker back in the spotlight of U.S.-China rivalry
summary: Nancy Pelosi's visit to Taiwan brought renewed attention to the island's critical role in the global chip supply chain, specifically highlighting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), a key manufacturer for tech giants like Apple and Nvidia. The visit underscored the strategic importance of semiconductors to U.S. national security and its rivalry with China. TSMC's dominance in the foundry market and the ongoing U.S. efforts to boost domestic chip manufacturing and secure supply chains were central themes, with concerns about China's potential actions towards Taiwan posing a significant risk to the global chip market.

title: Expanding the Possibilities
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) is challenging conventional semiconductor manufacturing by focusing on feature-rich process technology for 70% of the market. The company emphasizes innovation in RF communications, power management, and precision analog, working with industry leaders to deliver solutions across various high-growth markets. GF's efforts, including the GF Connex RF portfolio, GF Fotonix silicon photonics platform, and GF Labs, aim to drive product innovation and accelerate time-to-market.

title: Arista acquires Pluribus Networks for cloud fabric product
summary: Arista Networks has acquired Pluribus Networks to enhance its Unified Cloud Fabric product and gain entry into the telco market. The acquisition will integrate Pluribus's technology to create a software-defined network across various hardware components, targeting 4G/5G telco operators and leveraging relationships with partners like Ericsson and Nvidia. This move follows Arista's strategy of acquiring SDN vendors to bolster its networking capabilities.

title: Turbocharging Multi-Cloud Security and Application Delivery with VirtIO Offloading
summary: This article details how F5 and NVIDIA are addressing inefficiencies in data centers caused by increased virtualization and software-defined architectures by offloading VirtIO to the NVIDIA BlueField-2 DPU. This approach provides optimal acceleration, domain isolation, and dynamic composability, significantly reducing CPU reliance while maintaining flexibility. A demonstration showcased a 320% improvement in throughput and a 66% reduction in processing time when using hardware acceleration with the NVIDIA BlueField-2 DPU compared to running VirtIO on the host.

title: Nvidia is hit by another ‘crypto hangover,’ and that isn’t the only problem
summary: Nvidia is facing a "crypto hangover" and other issues as the pandemic-era booms in PC sales, videogames, and cryptocurrency decline. The company warned of a $1.4 billion revenue shortfall due to weak gaming equipment sales and excess inventory. Nvidia's stock fell 6.3% on the news, reflecting broader concerns about multiple challenges impacting its core business.

title: Nvidia warns of lower second-quarter revenue on gaming weakness
summary: Nvidia Corp warned of a 19% drop in second-quarter revenue from the prior quarter due to weakness in its gaming business, sending its shares down by about 8%. This follows similar warnings from other chipmakers and is attributed to consumers weighing purchases of discretionary items amidst economic concerns, as well as continued supply chain issues. The company expects to report overall Q2 revenue of approximately $6.70 billion, significantly below analyst estimates.

title: Cathie Wood sells Block shares to double down on Nvidia
summary: Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, sold over $20 million worth of Block (SQ) shares and simultaneously purchased over $65 million worth of Nvidia (NVDA) shares across three of its funds. This move follows Ark's previous decision to exit PayPal (PYPL) to bet on Block, and comes amidst macroeconomic turbulence causing significant losses in various asset classes, including Block and Bitcoin. Ark also recently sold Coinbase (COIN) shares due to regulatory uncertainty.

title: NVIDIA Announces Major Release of Omniverse With New USD Connectors and Tools, Simulation Technologies and Developer Frameworks
summary: NVIDIA has announced a major update to its Omniverse platform, introducing new developer frameworks, tools, apps, and plugins designed to build and connect metaverse worlds. These enhancements include AI-powered tools like Omniverse Avatar Cloud Engine, DeepSearch, and NeuralVDB, along with 11 new USD Connectors to integrate with leading 3D applications and CAD software. The updates aim to simplify virtual world creation and foster collaboration across industries, bridging the physical and virtual realms.

title: Chipmakers tumble on fears of worst downturn in 10 years
summary: Semiconductor stocks experienced a significant downturn following Micron Technology Inc.'s warning of slowing demand, raising concerns about the industry heading into a prolonged slump. Analysts believe the sector is entering its worst downturn in a decade, affecting every company and end market. Despite recent shortages and investments, the rapid evaporation of demand has taken experts by surprise, with some companies like Micron already cutting future spending.

title: AMD Stock Is a Bargain
summary: Morningstar considers AMD stock a bargain with a fair value estimate of $130 per share, giving it a 4-star rating and a narrow economic moat. The company is praised for its impressive growth, market share gains against Intel, and strong execution in new chip designs, especially in the data center and PC markets. However, high uncertainty remains due to the cyclical semiconductor industry and AMD's smaller scale compared to Intel and Nvidia.

title: Thematic Times: Semiconductors Power Thematic Indexes
summary: The article discusses how semiconductor technology drives many thematic investment indexes, particularly after the increased recognition of their importance due to pandemic-related supply issues. It highlights the impact of the newly signed CHIPS and Science Act, which provides significant subsidies to boost domestic chip manufacturing. The piece also explains how various thematic indexes, such as those for electric vehicles, streaming, gaming, and even space technology, are directly or indirectly exposed to the semiconductor industry and stand to benefit from these developments.

title: Thematic Times: Semiconductors Power Thematic Indexes
summary: The article discusses how semiconductor stocks are crucial to many thematic indexes, particularly after the CHIPS and Science Act. It highlights direct and indirect exposure of various thematic indexes to semiconductors and the long-term benefits of the CHIPS Act for the industry and end users. The growth of the semiconductor market is primarily driven by the automotive sector, data storage, and wireless communications.

title: Desktop CPU Sales Lowest in 30 Years, AMD Gains Market Share Anyway (Updated)
summary: Desktop CPU shipments hit a nearly 30-year low in Q2 2022, marking the largest year-over-year decline in Mercury Research's records. Despite the overall market slump, AMD significantly increased its market share and revenue in desktop, mobile, and server segments, setting new records for x86 processor share and mobile unit share. Intel, in contrast, reported a rare loss and delayed key products, indicating a challenging period for the long-standing leader.

title: Smart Devices, Smart Manufacturing: Pegatron Taps AI, Digital Twins
summary: Pegatron, a major manufacturer of tech devices, is integrating AI and digital twin technology to enhance its manufacturing processes. The company built an AI platform, Cambrian, using NVIDIA technologies to significantly improve product inspection, reducing defects and improving efficiency. Pegatron is now piloting NVIDIA Omniverse for developing digital twins of inspection machines and envisions creating a full virtual factory to further streamline operations and predict potential issues.

title: A message to prospectors of the semiconductor gold rush
summary: Governments worldwide are heavily investing in semiconductor manufacturing, recognizing their critical role in future technologies like AI, IoT, and 5G/6G, and their impact on national security and economic growth. While India is making efforts with a significant budgetary allocation, the article argues for a more strategic approach, focusing on building a comprehensive semiconductor ecosystem starting with simpler devices, rather than just pouring money into the industry, to avoid past mistakes observed in China and the USSR. The author emphasizes the long-term commitment needed for India to become a semiconductor superpower.

title: Lam Research to open its second R&D centre in India
summary: US-based semiconductor equipment supplier Lam Research is establishing its second R&D center in India in September, aiming to enable local design completion and reduce reliance on its US counterparts. The new facility will feature an advanced hardware engineering lab for deposition, etching, and wet processing, alongside AR/VR capabilities. This expansion underscores India's growing importance in the semiconductor design ecosystem, despite the manufacturing sector being in its early stages.

title: Startup Digs Into Public Filings With GPU-Driven Machine Learning to Serve Up Alternative Financial Data Services
summary: Intrinio, a startup based in St. Petersburg, Fla., uses GPU-driven machine learning to parse publicly available financial data, offering an alternative to traditional, bundled financial data products. Co-founded by Rachel Carpenter and Joseph French, Intrinio provides à la carte subscription services for equities, options, estimates, ETFs, and ESG data, making it more affordable and accessible for fintech innovators and startups. The company's technology significantly reduces errors and streamlines data cleansing and standardization, allowing clients like Robinhood, FTX, and Aiera to integrate high-quality financial data into their platforms.

title: Startup’s Vision AI Software Trains Itself — in One Hour — to Detect Manufacturing Defects in Real Time
summary: Covision Quality, an NVIDIA Metropolis member, has developed AI-based visual inspection software that can train itself in one hour to detect manufacturing defects. This software, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX A5000 GPUs, significantly reduces false-negative rates by up to 90% and pseudo-scrap rates, improving efficiency and sustainability in industrial production. Covision's solution allows for real-time defect detection without extensive coding or image labeling, addressing a major challenge in automated visual inspection scaling.

title: Intel shares new details about upcoming Meteor Lake processor series
summary: Intel has revealed new technical details about its upcoming Meteor Lake processor series, which will utilize chiplet technology for desktop and laptop CPUs. These processors will feature five chiplets, with the primary CPU module manufactured by Intel and other vital components like the GPU and I/O module produced by TSMC. This chiplet architecture allows for greater flexibility in design, improved manufacturing efficiency, and better performance through 3D stacking with Intel's Foveros technology.

title: Making AI Run At Any Scale But Not At All Costs
summary: This article discusses the challenges and solutions for scaling AI applications, highlighting how companies like HPE and Lenovo are developing accessible AI products. It focuses on Anyscale, a company founded by UC Berkeley researchers, which commercializes the open-source Ray framework to simplify distributed AI computing and make it easier for enterprises to develop and deploy AI models without extensive infrastructure expertise.

title: Nvidia sales forecast falls about $1 billion short of expectations, stock falls
summary: Nvidia's sales forecast for the current quarter fell approximately $1 billion short of analyst expectations, leading to a drop in its stock. The company confirmed a sharp reduction in profit and is working through inventory before releasing its new chip architecture. For the fiscal third quarter, Nvidia predicted revenue between $5.78 billion to $6.02 billion, while analysts had projected $6.91 billion.

title: Why Supermicro for On-Prem Data Centers
summary: Supermicro offers compelling advantages for on-premise data centers, enabling organizations to deploy the latest CPU and GPU technologies quickly due to Supermicro's close relationships with suppliers. The company provides a broad range of application and workload-optimized server and storage configurations, including liquid cooling options, to meet specific performance requirements and reduce TCO. Supermicro also offers comprehensive IT solutions, from single nodes to large clusters, with full assembly, testing, and rack integration services to ensure optimal performance and customer satisfaction.

title: AMD Set To Become TSMC’s Second Largest 5nm Customer Courtesy Of Ryzen 7000
summary: AMD is projected to become TSMC's second-largest customer for 5nm technology, primarily driven by its upcoming Ryzen 7000 series processors. These new CPUs, including desktop, notebook, and datacenter variants, will extensively utilize TSMC's 5nm process node. This increased demand will solidify AMD's position as a major client for the Taiwanese chip manufacturer, second only to Apple.

title: Details Emerge On Nvidia’s “Grace” Arm CPU
summary: Nvidia recently revealed new details about its "Grace" Arm server CPU at the Hot Chips 34 conference, confirming it will use Armv9 specification cores and SVE2 vector math units. The article explores the potential design, core count, and the decision to use LPDDR5 memory, highlighting Grace's integration into Grace-Grace or Grace-Hopper superchip complexes and its performance estimates for memory-intensive AI workloads like GPT-3.

title: AMD launching what it claims is ‘fastest processer in the world for gaming’ in September
summary: AMD announced the launch of its new Ryzen 7000 series gaming processors, touted as the "fastest processor in the world for gaming" by CEO Lisa Su. The processors are based on a 5-nanometer Zen 4 core architecture. This release comes amidst a challenging period for PC sales, with the low-end Ryzen 5 7600x model priced at $299.

title: Gaming industry advancing development of virtual ecosystem: Co-Founder of Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF
summary: Matthew Ball, CEO and Co-Founder of the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF, discusses the progress of the gaming industry in developing a virtual ecosystem. The discussion covers the future of virtual real estate companies, the capabilities of 3D rendering, and the practical aspects of building a virtual world.

title: U.S. Restricts Sales of Sophisticated Chips to China and Russia
summary: The Biden administration has imposed new restrictions on the sale of sophisticated computer chips, specifically high-end graphics processing units (GPUs), to China and Russia. This move, affecting companies like Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices, aims to prevent These chips, crucial for supercomputers and artificial intelligence used in applications like weapons development and intelligence gathering, from being used for military purposes. Nvidia estimates this restriction will impact approximately $400 million in revenue.

title: Nvidia Warns of Sales Hit From New U.S. Chip Licensing Requirements for China
summary: Nvidia Corp. announced that new U.S. licensing requirements for shipping advanced chips to China could result in a loss of up to $400 million in quarterly sales. The company was notified on Friday that it would need a license from the U.S. government for certain cutting-edge chip sales to China and Russia. This development follows President Biden's recent signing of the Chips and Science Act of 2022.

title: New U.S. curbs on sales of Nvidia AI chips to China spark selloff
summary: New U.S. restrictions on exports of advanced AI chips from Nvidia and AMD to China have caused Nvidia's stock to fall over 11%. The curbs, aimed at limiting China's technological prowess, specifically target Nvidia's H100 and A100 chips which are crucial for AI and machine learning applications. While potentially impacting Nvidia's business in China, the U.S. government has made some allowances for the completion and support of the H100 chip.

title: Nvidia Halting Chip Production to China, Fiber in Hawaii, Broadband Data Deadline Today
summary: The US government has instructed Nvidia to stop exporting two advanced computing chips to China, citing concerns over potential military use and impact on China's AI advancements. Meanwhile, Hawaiian Telecom is expanding its fiber internet service to nearly 10,000 homes and businesses on Kaua'i and participating in the Affordable Connectivity Program. Additionally, Thursday marks the deadline for providers to submit their broadband availability data for FCC maps as part of the Broadband Data Collection.

title: Texas Instruments Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Texas Instruments Inc. (TXN) fell 1.90% to $163.00 on Friday, despite an overall dismal trading session for the stock market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declined. The stock closed $39.26 shy of its 52-week high, but still outperformed some competitors like NVIDIA Corp., Intel Corp., and Qualcomm Inc., which experienced larger percentage drops. Trading volume for Texas Instruments Inc. was below its 50-day average.

title: Major stock averages slide for third week, Nasdaq posts six-day losing streak
summary: U.S. equities concluded their third consecutive weekly decline, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all falling. The Nasdaq recorded its first six-day losing streak since 2019. Despite a solid August jobs report, fears persist that the Federal Reserve will continue aggressive interest rate hikes to combat inflation, leading to market nervousness and concerns about retesting June lows.

title: 7 Growth Stocks That Could 10X by 2027
summary: This article identifies seven growth stocks that have the potential to appreciate tenfold by 2027, despite some experiencing declines in 2022. The author selected these stocks based on strong analyst ratings and their performance in previous years, aiming to provide a diversified list from various MarketWatch categories. Each stock, including Nvidia, Dick's Sporting Goods, Perficient, Moderna, Apple, O'Reilly Automotive, and Cross Country Healthcare, is analyzed for its growth prospects, financial health, and analyst sentiment.

title: Game on: Why esports and gaming could take it to the next level
summary: The esports and gaming sector, a multi-billion-dollar industry, continues to grow despite recent slowdowns attributed to supply chain delays and shifting consumer choices post-pandemic. Analysts and industry experts are confident in long-term growth, citing upcoming title launches and easing parts shortages. Mobile gaming remains the primary driver of revenue growth globally, while esports is gaining mainstream traction with significant investment and increasing popularity in emerging markets.

title: Chip Delivery Times Shrink in August
summary: Global chip delivery times shortened in August to an average of 26.8 weeks, a slight improvement signifying easing supply shortages due to slowing demand for products like phones and PCs. Despite this, certain semiconductors such as power management and microcontrollers remain hard to acquire, and the industry struggles to match supply with demand, leading to concerns about over-ordering and inventory build-up. The Philadelphia Stock Market Semiconductor Index has seen a significant downturn, reflecting investor apprehension.

title: Meme stocks to watch: top trending Reddit shares in September 2022
summary: This article identifies the top ten trending meme stocks on Reddit for September 2022, explaining what meme stocks are and their inherent benefits and risks, primarily high volatility. It then provides a detailed look into each of the ten companies, including their recent stock performance, significant events, and relevant analyst commentary, noting that all data is from a 30-day period prior to September 8, 2022.

title: US-China chip war is overheating
summary: The Biden administration plans to expand restrictions on U.S. semiconductor and chipmaking tool exports to China, targeting advanced AI chips and manufacturing equipment. These new regulations will codify earlier limitations imposed on companies like KLA Corp, Lam Research Corp, Applied Materials Inc, Nvidia Corp, and Advanced Micro Devices. The move is part of the U.S. strategy to curb China's technological advancements and strengthen its domestic semiconductor industry through initiatives like the $50 billion CHIPS Act.

title: Can the West wean itself off Taiwan’s semiconductors?
summary: The article discusses the West's reliance on Taiwan, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), for semiconductors and the efforts by the US and EU to onshore chip production due to geopolitical tensions with China. Despite significant investments like the US Chips Act and the European Chips Act, experts believe completely weaning off Taiwanese semiconductors would be costly and time-consuming, possibly creating new supply chain problems. The piece also highlights the complex nature of the global semiconductor supply chain, as US-made chips have been found in Russian weaponry despite sanctions.

title: MicroVision's Lidar Solution Supported on NVIDIA DRIVE Autonomous Vehicle Platform
summary: MicroVision announced that its MAVIN DR lidar system is now supported on the NVIDIA DRIVE AGX platform, enabling true highway-pilot functionality for OEMs. This integration aims to accelerate the adoption of advanced safety features in vehicles by providing high-resolution sensor technology with low latency. The MAVIN DR system is also supported on the NVIDIA DRIVE Sim simulation platform for development and validation.

title: Supermicro Announces 8U H100 HGX Server
summary: Supermicro has announced its new 8U Universal GPU Server, incorporating eight NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPUs, designed for large-scale AI training, NVIDIA Omniverse, and Metaverse applications. This server increases AI performance with improved thermal density, enabling lower power consumption and operation at higher data center temperatures, while supporting both current and next-generation CPUs and GPUs. The company is expanding its leading GPU server lineup with this addition, offering flexibility with front and rear I/O, AC or DC power support, and liquid cooling options.

title: Arm unveils updated Neoverse CPU roadmap, targeting cloud, hyperscale and HPC workloads
summary: Arm has announced an updated Neoverse CPU roadmap to provide a foundation for various workloads in cloud, hyperscale, and high-performance computing. The new Neoverse V2, codenamed Demeter, aims to deliver top per-thread performance and power efficiency, with partners like Nvidia already integrating it. Additionally, Arm detailed upcoming N-series and E-series chip designs set for a 2023 launch, catering to different compute needs prioritizing thread count or data plane processing.

title: To Infinity and Beyond!
summary: This article explores the futuristic concept of space-based data centers, following the current trend of underwater data centers. It discusses the potential advantages of such an endeavor, including cost reduction, natural cooling, and improved disk drive performance in zero-gravity environments. However, it also highlights the significant challenges, primarily the need for completely maintenance-free systems and the risks associated with the harsh space environment.

title: Strive 500 ETF (STRV) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Strive 500 ETF (STRV), including its current price, holdings, and news. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, expense ratio, top holdings like NVIDIA and Apple, and recent news headlines related to its performance and capital attraction. The piece serves as a comprehensive resource for potential investors interested in the STRV ETF.

title: Why Liz Truss is desperate for a Cambridge chipmaker to list in London
summary: British Prime Minister Liz Truss and Chancellor Kwasi Kwarteng are making a final attempt to persuade Cambridge-based chipmaker Arm Holdings to list on the London Stock Exchange. Arm, currently owned by Japanese conglomerate SoftBank, had previously indicated a preference for listing in New York, posing a significant challenge for London's markets which have struggled to attract IPOs. The UK government hopes a London listing would re-energize the City and boost confidence in the capital, following Arm's previous presence in the FTSE 100 before its acquisition.

title: Rigetti Announces New Partnerships and Details Multi-Year Roadmap Designed to Reach Quantum Advantage
summary: Rigetti Computing, Inc. announced an updated multi-year roadmap and key partnerships at its inaugural investor day, focusing on achieving Quantum Advantage. The roadmap includes the upcoming Ankaa™ 84-qubit system and Lyra™ 336-qubit system, alongside collaborations with companies like Keysight, NVIDIA, Bluefors, and Microsoft Azure to enhance hardware, software, and climate modeling applications. Rigetti is also expanding its Fab-1 facility and developing next-generation cryogenic platforms.

title: The making of TSMC and its ecosystem
summary: TSMC has solidified its dominant position in the global wafer foundry market due to its advanced technology and strategic ecosystem development, securing orders from major IC designers worldwide. Despite global economic headwinds, TSMC projects significant revenue growth and continues massive expansion projects, fostering extensive collaborations with its supply chain partners through initiatives like the Open Innovation Platform. The company faces competition in advanced process nodes but maintains a leading edge through strong client relationships and continuous investment in technological advancements.

title: HOLO Stock Price and Chart — NASDAQ:HOLO
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of MicroCloud Hologram Inc. (HOLO) stock, including its current price, historical performance, key financial statistics, and company information. It also features a collection of trading ideas and technical analysis indicators for investors interested in HOLO. The company specializes in holographic technology and is headquartered in New York, NY.

title: NVIDIA Delivers Quantum Leap in Performance, Introduces New Era of Neural Rendering With GeForce RTX 40 Series
summary: NVIDIA unveiled its new GeForce RTX 40 Series GPUs, powered by the Ada Lovelace architecture and new DLSS 3 technology, promising up to 4x performance improvement for gamers and creators. The flagship RTX 4090 and two configurations of the RTX 4080 were introduced, alongside new features like neural rendering and RTX Remix for classic games. These advancements aim to redefine real-time ray tracing, AI-powered graphics, and creative workflows.

title: NVIDIA Unveils DRIVE Thor — Centralized Car Computer Unifying Cluster, Infotainment, Automated Driving, and Parking in a Single, Cost-Saving System
summary: NVIDIA announced DRIVE Thor, its next-generation centralized computer for autonomous vehicles, delivering up to 2,000 teraflops of performance. This superchip unifies intelligent functions like automated driving, parking, and infotainment into a single architecture, significantly reducing system cost and complexity. ZEEKR, a Geely-owned automaker, is the first customer, planning to integrate DRIVE Thor into its intelligent electric vehicles starting in early 2025.

title: Nvidia CEO sees 'large space' for China sales despite U.S. restrictions
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that he still sees a large market for Nvidia's data center chips in China, despite U.S. export restrictions on two of its top chips (A100 and H100). Huang noted that the restrictions have specific performance thresholds that leave a "large space" for Nvidia's other products in the Chinese market, as the majority of their customers are not affected. He also indicated that Nvidia would seek licenses from the U.S. government for Chinese customers desiring the restricted high-end chips.

title: ‘Moore’s Law’s dead,’ Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says in justifying gaming-card price hike
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that "Moore's Law is dead" as he defended the price hike for the company's next-generation gaming cards, Ada Lovelace. He explained that silicon wafers are significantly more expensive to produce. Despite a softening in consumer demand, Huang anticipates a strong Q4 for Ada, citing high demand for their new chips.

title: 7 Meme Stocks to Buy During a Stock Market Crash
summary: This article identifies seven meme stocks that aggressive investors might consider buying during a stock market crash for long-term potential. The selection includes companies like Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), FedEx (FDX), GameStop (GME), NVIDIA (NVDA), Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT), and Tesla (TSLA). Each stock is discussed with reasons for its meme stock appeal and potential for recovery despite current market downturns.

title: An Interview With Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang About Building the Omniverse Cloud
summary: This article features an interview with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, discussing the company's new Ada Lovelace GPU architecture and its broader implications. Huang explains Nvidia's shift towards cloud services with Omniverse, focusing on its role in democratizing AI, foundational database systems, and real-time simulation for industrial and gaming applications. The interview also touches on the challenges of GPU supply and demand, geopolitical concerns regarding China, and how Nvidia measures its progress in expanding accelerated computing platforms into new markets.

title: Nvidia founder says country major growth market
summary: Jensen Huang, CEO of Nvidia Corp, stated that China remains a significant growth market despite US restrictions on high-end AI chip exports like the A100 and H100. Experts note that China's large market is crucial for semiconductor companies. The restrictions are expected to accelerate the development of domestic AI chip companies in China as they seek alternatives and build their own ecosystems.

title: How Yale New Haven Health transformed their data infrastructure
summary: Yale New Haven Health, the largest healthcare provider in Connecticut, transformed its data infrastructure to support AI and analytics for clinicians and patients. Facing limitations with their existing Hadoop-based system, they partnered with NetApp to create a flexible, cloud-ready computational health platform, significantly reducing licensing costs and enhancing research capabilities. This new platform allows Yale to centralize and integrate data, improve security, and efficiently utilize technologies like GPUs for advanced research, particularly in areas like COVID-19.

title: Nvidia, Rigetti Team on Quantum Hybrid Climate Modeling
summary: Rigetti Computing announced new partnerships and progress on its product roadmap at its inaugural investor day. Notable collaborations include a partnership with Nvidia for quantum hybrid climate modeling using quantum machine learning, integration with Microsoft Azure Quantum for its processors, and a collaboration with Keysight for error mitigation software. Rigetti also detailed its advancements in modular dilution fridges and released new 80-qubit and 40-qubit processors.

title: How Arm conquered the chip market without making a single chip, with CEO Rene Haas
summary: In this interview, Arm CEO Rene Haas discusses how Arm has become a central player in the tech ecosystem by designing chip instruction sets without manufacturing chips themselves. He explains Arm's licensing and royalty business model, its broad customer base including giants like Apple and Qualcomm, and how it is diversifying into cloud, automotive, and IoT markets. Haas also addresses the company's decision to go public, its role in navigating the chip shortage, and its competitive stance against x86 and RISC-V architectures.

title: ARK Invest CEO Cathie Wood’s tech bargain hunt
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest continued its strategy of investing in tech stocks in September 2022, acquiring shares in companies like Nvidia and Adobe despite significant market downturns for these firms. The ARK Innovation ETF has seen substantial losses and outflows, but the firm is betting on a rebound in battered tech stocks. Additionally, there has been a management reshuffle within ARK Invest, with William Scherer taking over two ETFs and Brett Winton promoted to chief futurist.

title: China chip start-ups rush to tout AI chip breakthroughs after Nvidia sales ban
summary: Chinese semiconductor start-ups are claiming breakthroughs in AI chips following US restrictions on Nvidia and AMD exports to China. Chongqing-based Xiangdixian Computing Technology unveiled Tianjun No. 1, a 12-nanometre GPU, while Shenzhen-based Moffett AI introduced an AI inference accelerator, S30, claiming superior performance to Nvidia's banned A100 and H100 chips. Analysts remain skeptical, noting the significant lag behind leading international chipmakers like Nvidia.

title: Why These Chip Design Stocks Just Got Fresh Buy Ratings
summary: A Deutsche Bank analyst has initiated buy ratings for chip design stocks Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) and Synopsys (SNPS). The analyst believes that ongoing technological advancements in semiconductors will drive strong and sustained demand for their chip design tools. Cadence and Synopsys collectively control a significant portion, about two-thirds, of the electronic design automation market.

title: As NVIDIA Corporation's (NASDAQ:NVDA)) market cap dropped by US$9.4b, insiders who sold US$156m worth of stock were able to offset their losses
summary: Insiders at NVIDIA Corporation sold US$156 million worth of stock over the last year at an average price of US$212 per share, effectively offsetting some losses as the company's market cap dropped by US$9.4 billion. While insider selling occurred, the notable sale by Independent Director Mark Stevens was at a price well above the current stock price, making strong conclusions difficult. Insiders collectively own about 4.0% of the company, valued at US$12 billion, which is seen as a positive sign of alignment with shareholders despite a lack of recent insider buying.

title: Meet the Harvard Law School graduate helping China cut its reliance on US AI chips
summary: Michael Zhang Wen, a Harvard Law School graduate and chairman of Biren Technology, is leading China's efforts to reduce its dependence on US artificial intelligence (AI) chips. His Shanghai-based start-up recently released the BR100, a 7-nanometre GPU, which some believe could position Biren to become "China's Nvidia" amidst escalating US export restrictions on advanced chips. Zhang faces pressure to deliver, as his company has raised a substantial US$655 million from major venture capital funds.

title: SoftBank’s Son expected to meet Samsung's Lee for Arm M&A discussion
summary: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son is anticipated to meet with Samsung Electronics de facto leader Jay Y. Lee to discuss a potential sale or strategic alliance for British chip designer Arm. This meeting follows a collapsed deal to sell Arm to Nvidia due to regulatory hurdles and a broader industry consensus that a single company acquisition might be difficult. Various scenarios are being considered, including a consortium of chipmakers or a partial acquisition by Samsung, though some industry watchers express skepticism about significant outcomes given Arm's high valuation and SoftBank's financial state.

title: Nvidia to close its offices and cease operations in Russia
summary: Nvidia Corp. announced it would cease operations and close its offices in Russia, citing its inability to operate effectively due to the invasion of Ukraine. The chip maker had previously suspended shipments to Russia in March and now joins many other companies in fully exiting the country. Nvidia had maintained a presence to support its employees and their families until this decision.

title: Nvidia says it is ceasing all activities in Russia, relocating employees
summary: U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp (NVDA.O) announced it is ceasing all activities in Russia and offering employees there the option to continue their jobs in other countries. The decision comes after previously suspending shipments to Russia, stating that recent developments make it no longer possible to operate effectively in the country.

title: Nvidia closes Russian offices, will fly relocating employees out of the country, report says
summary: Nvidia is ceasing all activities and closing its offices in Russia, offering its employees the opportunity to relocate out of the country. The company had previously suspended shipments to Russia but maintained an office to support employees and their families. Nvidia stated that recent developments have made it impossible to operate effectively there.

title: Ethereum Merge Not Yet Boosting Crypto Stocks
summary: The Ethereum merge has not yet boosted crypto-correlated stocks, including holdings in ETFs like SATO and BLKC, partly because most public crypto miners focus on Bitcoin. However, the switch to staking could benefit companies like Coinbase through increased revenue from staking services, potentially offering longer-term upsides for crypto equities and related ETFs. The article notes that ETH mining equipment is cheaper and more flexible than BTC mining equipment, using GPUs, which could benefit companies like Nvidia and AMD.

title: The Chips That Make Taiwan the Center of the World
summary: This article examines Taiwan's critical role in the global semiconductor industry, particularly through TSMC, and the geopolitical risks associated with its dependence on the island amid rising U.S.-China tensions. It explores the potential catastrophic economic consequences for the world if military action or political pressure disrupts Taiwan's chip manufacturing capabilities. The author argues that while Taiwan's chip industry provides a "silicon shield," it also makes the island a crucial target in any conflict.

title: MICLEDI Microdisplays and Kura Technologies Announce Partnership on Custom Micro-LED Display for Ultra High-Performance AR glasses, Scaling with Partners Including GlobalFoundries and TSMC
summary: MICLEDI Microdisplays and Kura Technologies have partnered to develop custom micro-LED displays for high-performance AR glasses. Kura's next-generation AR glasses will incorporate MICLEDI's 300mm microLED displays, with both companies leveraging expertise from partners like GlobalFoundries and TSMC for scaled production. This collaboration aims to bring advanced 8K AR glasses to market sooner by overcoming typical manufacturing challenges.

title: 10 Best Robotics Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies 10 leading robotics stocks and several robotics-focused ETFs for investors interested in the growing automation industry. It details key companies like Intuitive Surgical, Nvidia, and Rockwell Automation, highlighting their contributions to medical, AI, industrial, and defense robotics. The article also provides guidance on where to invest in these stocks and explains why robotic technology is a worthwhile investment.

title: Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:SHOC)
summary: The Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF (SHOC) invests in the 30 largest U.S. semiconductor companies and was launched on October 6, 2022. The ETF has current assets under management of $90.85 million and a net expense ratio of 0.40%. Its top holdings include NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Micron Technology.

title: 10 Best Laptops For Data Science And Data Analysis
summary: This article identifies the key specifications required for laptops used in data science and data analysis, such as ample RAM, fast processors, SSD storage, and CUDA-capable graphics cards. It then lists and describes 10 HP laptop models, including the HP ENVY 17t, HP Pavilion, and HP ZBook 15 G5, highlighting their suitability for various data-intensive tasks and user budgets. The guide emphasizes that while cloud solutions are common, local machine power remains crucial for data professionals.

title: U.S. aims to hobble China's chip industry with sweeping new export rules
summary: The Biden administration has enacted broad new export controls to impede China's chip industry, specifically targeting its access to advanced semiconductors made with U.S. equipment. These measures expand prior restrictions and are designed to slow Beijing's technological and military advancements, although their effectiveness relies on international cooperation. The rules are expected to significantly impact China's chip manufacturing capabilities, potentially setting the industry back years.

title: Intel Plans to Lay Off Thousands of Employees As the Chipmaker Looks to Trim Costs
summary: Intel Corp. is preparing for significant cost-cutting layoffs, potentially affecting thousands of employees, in response to a struggling personal-computer market and declining demand for its processors. The reductions are expected to be announced around the time of its third-quarter earnings report on October 27, with some divisions facing cuts of up to 20% of staff. This move reflects Intel's efforts to trim expenses, improve profit margins, and regain its competitive edge amidst a broader tech spending downturn.

title: Polestar 3 is the SUV for the electric age
summary: Polestar officially unveiled the Polestar 3, an electric performance SUV designed with Scandinavian minimalism and powerful dynamics, targeting the electric vehicle market. It features a dual-motor configuration with up to 517 hp, a 111 kWh battery offering a 300-mile EPA range, and advanced safety and infotainment systems. Production is planned in China starting mid-2023, with additional manufacturing in the US by mid-2024, and initial pricing starts at USD 83,900.

title: 3DMark Speed Way Benchmark Now Available, Test Your PCs Prowess In DX12 Ultimate & Ray Tracing
summary: UL Solutions has released the 3DMark Speed Way benchmark, allowing users to test their PC's performance in DirectX 12 Ultimate and Ray Tracing environments. The new benchmark integrates into the 3DMark suite, which now sees a price increase from $29.99 to $34.99, though existing owners can upgrade for $4.99. Users can download Speed Way from the UL Solutions website or Steam.

title: 7 Things to Know About the God Bless America (YALL) ETF
summary: The God Bless America ETF (YALL) is a new exchange-traded fund designed to invest in "anti-woke" companies that avoid "left-leaning statements" unrelated to their businesses. The ETF prioritizes valuation metrics like P/E ratios and job growth, with Tesla (TSLA) and Nvidia (NVDA) as its top holdings. It will have an expense ratio of 0.65% and hold 30-40 U.S.-listed companies with market capitalizations over $1 billion.

title: ASML, Lam Research rush to pull US engineers out of China amid new regulations
summary: ASML Holdings and Lam Research are quickly withdrawing American engineers from their operations in China to comply with new US trade restrictions targeting China's semiconductor industry. ASML, a monopolist in EUV lithography systems, has restricted US employees from serving Chinese clients, while Lam Research and KLA Corp have pulled US engineers from supporting equipment in China. These new regulations are expected to severely disrupt China's chip industry due to the crucial role US personnel play within it.

title: Kroger, Albertsons spin-off is extra ammunition in regulatory battle
