summary: Kroger and Albertsons are proposing to divest up to 650 stores, potentially through a spin-off to Albertsons shareholders, to gain regulatory approval for their $24.6 billion merger. This unusual structure aims to expedite divestitures amidst regulatory scrutiny and challenges in finding buyers for unionized stores, especially after a previous failed divestiture involving Albertsons and Safeway. The FTC, under Chair Lina Khan, is expected to closely examine the proposal given her skepticism towards large mergers impacting consumers.

title: U.S. Speaker Pelosi's husband sold Nvidia, Micron options at a loss
summary: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi's husband, Paul Pelosi, sold call options in chipmakers Micron Technology and Nvidia, incurring losses close to $1 million. The transactions were disclosed in a transparency filing, which also showed he exercised call options for Alphabet shares and allowed Walt Disney call options to expire at a loss. This comes amid scrutiny over stock trading by lawmakers and their families, leading to legislative efforts to restrict such activities.

title: Oracle Cloud adding tens of thousands of Nvidia chips for AI
summary: Oracle Corp and Nvidia Corp are expanding their partnership, with Oracle Cloud adding tens of thousands of Nvidia's A100 and H100 GPUs to boost AI-related computational work. This expansion addresses the growing demand for AI infrastructure as AI models become more complex. Nvidia currently dominates the accelerator chip market for AI, holding an 85% share among the six largest cloud providers.

title: Oracle and NVIDIA Partner to Speed AI Adoption for Enterprises
summary: Oracle and NVIDIA have expanded their long-standing alliance through a multi-year partnership aimed at accelerating AI adoption for enterprises. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) will significantly increase its GPU capacity with NVIDIA's A100 and upcoming H100 GPUs, supporting the full stack of NVIDIA AI software and services. This collaboration will provide enterprises with a broad, accessible portfolio for AI training and deep learning inference, integrating NVIDIA AI platforms into OCI to drive innovation across various industries.

title: US chip war with China creates 'serious challenges' for industry, says TSMC, but 'not us' says ASML
summary: The US chip war with China is creating "serious challenges" for the semiconductor industry, according to TSMC, due to new US restrictions on supplying Chinese tech companies with advanced chipmaking equipment. However, ASML Holding NV, a European chip equipment manufacturer, claims it will not be significantly impacted by these restrictions. ASML's CEO stated that the US rules do not amend regulations for lithography equipment shipped from the Netherlands, and the company expects limited direct impact on its 2023 shipment plan, with demand for its machines remaining strong.

title: Oracle Cloud adds NVIDIA chips, software to speed enterprise AI uptake
summary: Oracle has expanded its partnership with NVIDIA to integrate tens of thousands of NVIDIA's AI-processing graphics chips and software tools into Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This aims to accelerate enterprise AI adoption across industries like banking, healthcare, and manufacturing by offering powerful A100 and upcoming H100 GPUs. The collaboration will also provide access to NVIDIA software like RAPIDS Accelerator and Clara for enhanced AI performance, medical imaging, and drug discovery.

title: Why Are Semiconductor Stocks Getting Crushed?
summary: Semiconductor stocks have experienced significant losses in 2022 due to growing recession fears, oversupply from pandemic-driven demand, and new strict U.S. export restrictions on sales to China. These restrictions, particularly impacting high-performance chips and manufacturing equipment, are expected to cost several firms hundreds of millions to billions in revenue. While the near-term outlook is dim, the long-term growth expectation for the semiconductor market remains intact, despite anticipated cyclical downturns.

title: 7 Virtual Reality Stocks to Buy to Bet on the Coming VR Boom
summary: The virtual reality market is projected to grow significantly to over $227 billion by 2029, presenting a massive opportunity for investors, especially as many top VR stocks are currently oversold. This article highlights seven virtual reality stocks to consider, including the ProShares Metaverse ETF (VERS) for diversification, Roblox (RBLX) as a leading social metaverse platform, and tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT) and Apple (AAPL) that are heavily investing in VR/AR. Other recommendations include Nvidia (NVDA) for its Omniverse platform, Matterport (MTTR) for its exposure to "digital twins," and Unity Software (U) which recently received a "buy" rating.

title: 2020s–Today
summary: The article details SLB's (formerly Schlumberger) strategic evolution from the 2020s to today, focusing on its commitment to decarbonization and a balanced planet. It outlines the company's shift towards new energy ventures like geothermal, clean hydrogen, and lithium while continuing to innovate in oil and gas with digital solutions and emissions reduction technologies. Key events include brand changes, major collaborations, and advancements in AI, energy storage, and sustainability, emphasizing SLB's net-zero emissions goal by 2050 and its push for diversity.

title: China’s Biren GPUs Can’t Be Made at TSMC Due To US Regulations & Competition With NVIDIA
summary: Birentech, a Chinese chip startup, is reportedly barred from manufacturing its Biren GPUs at TSMC due to US regulations aimed at restricting China's access to advanced AI and datacenter chips. This decision is influenced by the Biren BR100's potential to outperform NVIDIA's A100 chips, which are already banned for the Chinese market, and could severely impact Birentech's manufacturing plans.

title: Rambus Announces PCIe 6.0: Powering Next-Gen High-Performance Data Center & AI Solutions
summary: Rambus has unveiled its new PCIe 6.0 interface subsystem, an advanced solution designed for the next generation of data centers and AI applications. This subsystem, comprising PHY and controller IP, delivers data rates up to 64 GT/s and supports the latest CXL 3.0 specification for enhanced memory sharing and pooling. It features an Integrity and Data Encryption (IDE) engine for data protection and is backward compatible with previous PCIe generations.

title: Rambus announces next-gen PCIe 6.0 interface for data centers, AI systems
summary: Rambus has unveiled its next-generation PCIe 6.0 Interface Subsystem, designed for high-performance data centers and AI SoCs, offering speeds up to 64 GT/s. This subsystem includes PHY and controller IP, supports CXL 3.0, and is optimized for advanced heterogeneous computing architectures with features like an Integrity and Data Encryption engine. It ensures compatibility with previous PCIe generations while introducing innovations like low-latency Forward Error Correction.

title: Lotus Eletre to debut ECARX's next-generation digital head unit
summary: ECARX's next-generation digital head unit (DHU) will debut on the new Lotus Eletre Hyper-SUV, featuring a dual-chip system for advanced processing and an immersive multi-screen user experience with customisable displays. The DHU utilizes Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8155 chipset and Unreal Engine for graphic rendering, ensuring a seamless user experience with real-time data from the car's Advanced Driver Assistance System. This collaboration between ECARX and Lotus is set to redefine in-car technology, furthering Lotus' transition into a global performance car business with smart electric vehicles.

title: Why Are Semiconductor Shares Being Crushed?
summary: Semiconductor stocks are facing significant declines in 2022 due to multiple factors, including recession fears, weak PC sales, and strict US export restrictions on sales to China. These restrictions are expected to heavily impact major companies like Nvidia and AMD, although the long-term growth outlook for the overall semiconductor market remains positive, albeit with near-term uncertainties.

title: Silicon Labs CEO Transforms Company to IoT Chip Designer
summary: Silicon Labs CEO Matt Johnson is transforming the company into a "pure-play" IoT chip designer, focusing solely on wireless connectivity and IoT devices. The company aims to become the undisputed leader in this space, having doubled its revenue in two years and being an early adopter in unifying various IoT standards like Matter, Zigbee, and Zwave. Despite facing competition from larger players and a recent softening of demand, Johnson sees significant growth potential, particularly in areas like digital retail labels and secure hardware.

title: Intel's Mobileye unit valued at $22 bln as shares cruise higher
summary: Mobileye Global Inc., Intel's self-driving technology unit, debuted strongly on the U.S. stock market, reaching a valuation of $21.8 billion despite earlier IPO target reductions. The company's chief communications officer highlighted the importance of gaining a public platform and product visibility, especially for its SuperVision product, amid a challenging year for U.S. IPOs. Mobileye, which was previously listed before its acquisition by Intel in 2017, competes in the fiercely competitive assisted driving market, projecting deployment in 270 million cars by 2030.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) fell 2.89% on Thursday, closing at $114.70, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the S&P 500 Index fell. This marked the stock's second consecutive day of losses and positioned it below its 52-week high. Qualcomm underperformed some competitors like NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc., though it fared slightly better than Alphabet Inc. Class A.

title: AMD Issues Performance Advisory for Ryzen 7000 Processors
summary: AMD has released a performance advisory for its new Ryzen 7000 processors, acknowledging reports of unexpected performance variations in certain games. The company is actively investigating these issues and plans to collaborate with game developers and hardware partners for optimization. AMD also addressed concerns about Windows 11 performance, stating that their testing showed no significant differences compared to Windows 10.

title: Arm Could Change Licensing Model to Charge OEMs Directly
summary: Arm might change its licensing model by requiring OEMs to license almost all of its IP if they wish to use its CPU IP, directly challenging existing deals and potentially pushing chipmakers toward alternatives like RISC-V. This potential shift, revealed through Qualcomm's counterclaim in a legal dispute, would prevent mixing Arm's IP with other companies' designs (e.g., AMD GPUs in Samsung chips). The change is expected to take effect in 2025, with exemptions for long-term licensees like NVIDIA and Apple.

title: At NetApp INSIGHT 2022, Dive Into NVIDIA DGX BasePOD and NetApp ONTAP AI
summary: This article announces NVIDIA's participation as a sponsor at NetApp INSIGHT 2022, highlighting solutions for operationalizing AI use cases. It introduces NVIDIA DGX BasePOD as a foundation for AI infrastructure, including NetApp ONTAP AI, and discusses how these solutions help businesses deploy proven models faster. The piece also outlines NVIDIA DGX-Ready Managed Services for managing AI infrastructure and filling IT skills gaps.

title: Micron Ships World's Most Advanced DRAM Technology With 1-Beta Node
summary: Micron Technology has announced it is shipping qualification samples of its 1β (1-beta) DRAM technology to select smartphone manufacturers and chipset partners, achieving mass production readiness for the world's most advanced DRAM technology node. This LPDDR5X mobile memory delivers top speeds of 8.5 gigabits per second, with significant gains in power efficiency (15% improvement) and bit density (over 35% improvement) at 16Gb per die capacity. The 1β node is poised to support highly responsive applications from mobile to data centers and intelligent vehicles, stemming from Micron's pioneering R&D and nanomanufacturing capabilities.

title: Jaguar Land Rover Secures Semi Conductor Chips For Future Electric Cars
summary: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) has formed a strategic partnership with Wolfspeed, Inc. to secure Silicon Carbide semiconductors for its next-generation electric vehicles. This collaboration aims to enhance powertrain efficiency and extend driving range for JLR's upcoming electric models, with the first Range Rover vehicles featuring this technology expected in 2024. The partnership is a key part of JLR's "Reimagine" strategy to become carbon net zero by 2039.

title: Lotus’ Super SUV Targets Self-Driving Fast Laps at Racetrack
summary: Lotus' new electric super SUV, the Eletre, aims to be the first car to complete a self-driving lap of Germany's Nürburgring racetrack. This ambitious goal is supported by the vehicle's standard-equipped ROBO Galaxy autonomous driving platform, which includes deployable Lidar technology and two Nvidia Drive Orin systems processing 508 trillion operations per second. While current regulations prevent full autonomous driving on public roads, Lotus plans to demonstrate the Eletre's capabilities at private facilities like the Nürburgring.

title: How Taiwan won the semiconductor race
summary: This article explains how Taiwan became a dominant force in the global semiconductor industry, despite starting as an agricultural economy. It attributes Taiwan's success to globalization's impact on electronics, East Asia's skilled workforce and infrastructure, and a unique business model focused on contract manufacturing rather than brand development. The piece highlights TSMC's role in supplying advanced chips globally, creating a significant dependency on Taiwan.

title: Intel Says Its On Track With “Aggressive” 2030 Foundry Goals, Aiming The 2nd Spot By The End of The Decade
summary: Intel is aggressively pursuing its 2030 foundry goals, aiming to become the world's second-largest chip manufacturer, surpassing Samsung and closing in on TSMC. The company is investing significantly in new fabrication plants globally, developing next-gen process technologies, and building capacity to attract clients and diversify the semiconductor supply chain. Intel plans to achieve this through strategic investments, global expansion, and strong competition against its rivals.

title: Chaos Engineering: Unleashing havoc in production
summary: Chaos engineering, a practice pioneered by Netflix, involves intentionally disrupting production environments to identify weaknesses and build system resilience. This approach, likened to "Bootcamp for DevOps teams," helps organizations prepare for inevitable service failures in complex cloud infrastructures. Tools like Chaos Monkey and Chaos Mesh are used to simulate outages, allowing teams to develop robust systems that can recover quickly from disruptions.

title: Tower Semiconductor: Opportunity or risk?
summary: Intel agreed to acquire Tower Semiconductor for $53 per share, but the market fears regulatory hurdles, especially from China and Japan, causing Tower's share price to drop significantly. The deal, valued at $5.4 billion at the time of agreement, needs approvals from multiple countries, including the US, China, Germany, Israel, Japan, the UK, and Italy. Analysts suggest that while regulatory risk exists, particularly with China due to US-China tensions, previous Israeli semiconductor acquisitions ultimately received Chinese approval, and Japan's hesitancy regarding M&A deals might be overcome as Intel plans to maintain operations there.

title: Exclusive: Nvidia offers new advanced chip for China that meets U.S. export controls
summary: Nvidia has developed a new advanced chip, the A800, specifically for the Chinese market, which complies with recent U.S. export control rules designed to restrict cutting-edge technology to China. This move by Nvidia (NVDA.O) is a strategic effort to maintain its presence in the significant Chinese market after U.S. regulations impacted sales of its A100 chip, with the A800 featuring a reduced data transfer rate to meet the export requirements. Analysts suggest this is a "repackaged A100 GPU" demonstrating Nvidia's adaptability to trade restrictions.

title: Nvidia’s new chip is tailored for Chinese market and meets US export regulations
summary: Nvidia has introduced a new A800 GPU specifically designed for the Chinese market to comply with US export regulations, which previously banned sales of its A100 and H100 chips without a license. This move allows Nvidia to continue serving its Chinese customers, who represent about a quarter of its gross sales, while adhering to US government’s restrictions on advanced chip technology. The A800 is an alternative product engineered to meet reduced export control requirements and cannot be programmed to exceed them.

title: Intel debuts powerful Max Series CPUs and GPUs for high-performance computing and AI
summary: Intel has launched its new Max Series CPUs and GPUs, designed to compete in the high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) markets against rivals like AMD and Nvidia. These new chips will power the upcoming Aurora supercomputer at Argonne National Laboratory, aiming to make HPC and AI more accessible for solving complex global challenges. Intel claims its Max Series offers significant performance advantages and better power efficiency compared to competitive silicon.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Extends Supercomputing to the Enterprise with New HPE Cray Portfolio
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is expanding its supercomputing portfolio with new HPE Cray EX and HPE Cray XD supercomputers, making exascale-class technology more accessible and affordable for enterprises. These systems are designed to help businesses across various industries, including energy, finance, healthcare, and manufacturing, accelerate R&D, AI, and machine learning initiatives. The new offerings provide significant performance and AI-at-scale capabilities in smaller form factors, supporting advanced modeling, simulation, and data-intensive workloads in traditional data centers.

title: AMD launches data center CPU 'Genoa', taps Google, Microsoft as customers (Nov. 10)
summary: AMD has launched its fourth-generation EPYC processor, codenamed "Genoa," for data centers, securing major customers like Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle. This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

title: Reza Partners with Perfect Corp. to Enable Virtual Try On of Jewelry
summary: French jewelry brand Reza has collaborated with Perfect Corp. to integrate AI and AR virtual try-on technology into its new eCommerce website. This technology, featuring Perfect Corp.'s AgileHand, allows customers to virtually try on rings, earrings, and bracelets in real-time digital renderings. The partnership aims to enhance the luxury accessories shopping experience by providing an immersive and impactful virtual try-on solution.

title: HPE's low-end supercomputers take aim at the AI market
summary: HPE and Dell are introducing new supercomputing and high-performance computing (HPC) systems designed to meet the growing demands of AI-driven workloads. HPE is focusing on smaller, lower-cost supercomputers with advanced cooling, while Dell is enhancing its HPC offerings with powerful GPUs, aiming to make supercomputing capabilities more accessible to a broader range of enterprise users. This convergence of supercomputing and HPC is blurring the lines between the two, as both companies compete to provide infrastructure that can efficiently handle complex AI, modeling, and simulation tasks.

title: NVIDIA Omniverse Opens Portals for Scientists to Explore Our Universe
summary: NVIDIA announced that its Omniverse platform now supports leading scientific computing visualization software and new batch-rendering workloads on NVIDIA A100 and H100 Tensor Core GPUs. This expansion enables scientists and engineers to use Omniverse for advanced simulations, visualize complex datasets, and create real-time digital twins for various scientific applications. Organizations like Argonne National Laboratory and Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory are already leveraging Omniverse to enhance their research workflows and foster collaboration across global teams.

title: AMD announces its reprogrammable Versal AI chips are ready for space-based deployments
summary: AMD's reprogrammable Versal AI Core chip has achieved Class B qualification, making it suitable for space-based deployments in commercial satellites. This radiation-resistant SoC enables significant advancements in onboard AI processing for satellites, offering greater performance and reconfigurability compared to traditional ASICs. Raytheon Co. is an early adopter, planning to use these chips for its next-generation space processors.

title: Buffett’s Taiwan Semiconductor Stake Highlights Beaten-Down Semi Stocks
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested $4.1 billion in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, drawing attention to the semiconductor industry, which Morningstar analysts consider largely undervalued. Following a significant selloff in 2022 due to recession fears and U.S. restrictions on sales to China, many semiconductor companies, including Skyworks Solutions, Teradyne, and Taiwan Semiconductor, are rated as significantly undervalued. The article details Morningstar's perspectives on Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, ASML, Broadcom, and Texas Instruments, highlighting investment opportunities despite industry challenges.

title: Nvidia says it is working with Microsoft to build 'massive' cloud AI computer
summary: Nvidia Corp is partnering with Microsoft Corp to build a "massive" cloud AI computer on Microsoft's Azure cloud, utilizing thousands of Nvidia's powerful H100 and A100 GPUs. This collaboration aims to handle intense artificial intelligence computing work, driven by the increasing demand for faster processing from AI models. Nvidia will also be a customer of Microsoft's AI cloud computer, developing AI applications for its own services.

title: Nvidia revenue falls 17% but data center growth remains strong
summary: Nvidia's fiscal third-quarter revenue fell 17% overall but its data center business saw significant growth, up 31% year-over-year. The company's gaming division sales were down 51% due to slowing demand and inventory issues. Despite beating revenue expectations, earnings per share were light, and Nvidia expects gross margin to recover in the current quarter.

title: Lockheed Martin, NVIDIA to Build Digital Twin of Current Global Weather Conditions for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
summary: Lockheed Martin and NVIDIA are collaborating to create an AI-driven Earth Observations Digital Twin for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). This system will efficiently monitor global environmental conditions, including extreme weather, by fusing data from various space and Earth-based sensors. The project aims to provide NOAA with a high-resolution, accurate, and timely depiction of global conditions to improve weather predictions and understanding of environmental changes.

title: OSS, TMGcore debut a liquid-immersion cooled transportable supercomputer featuring Nvidia GPUs
summary: One Stop Systems (OSS) and TMGcore have collaborated to launch a new two-phase liquid immersion-cooled version of the OSS Rigel Edge Supercomputer, designed for AI Transportable applications. This supercomputer, featuring Nvidia GPUs, offers efficiency, compactness, ruggedness, and high performance by using a unique immersion cooling technology that removes heat through liquid boiling, eliminating the need for fans and heatsinks. This innovation results in a smaller, more robust system with reduced power consumption and noise, ideal for autonomous vehicles, edge computing, and robotic process automation.

title: Nvidia beats revenue estimates, reassures investors
summary: Nvidia reported better-than-expected revenue for the fiscal third quarter, driven by strong demand for its AI and data-center chips, despite a slowdown in the PC market and challenges in China. The company's data center unit saw a 31% sales increase, offsetting a 51% drop in its gaming business. Although the fourth-quarter outlook was slightly below some estimates, it was sufficient to alleviate investor concerns, with CEO Jensen Huang acknowledging ongoing macroeconomic difficulties and COVID-19 restrictions in China.

title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

title: NVIDIA Revenue Beats Estimates, Fueled by Data Center Growth
summary: NVIDIA reported Q3 FY23 revenue of $5.9 billion, down 17% year-over-year but still beating analyst estimates due to strong data center sales. While gaming revenue declined significantly from post-pandemic highs, the company saw substantial growth in its data center and automotive segments, indicating a strategic shift and resilience. NVIDIA provided Q4 FY23 revenue guidance of $6 billion, showing confidence in its future despite current macroeconomic challenges and adjustments in the gaming market.

title: Exxact Corp. Works with NVIDIA to Support Omniverse Enterprise
summary: Exxact Corporation, a high-performance computing provider, is now offering integrated solutions to the media and entertainment industry to support NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise. This collaboration provides studios with workstations powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs and Omniverse Enterprise, enabling real-time collaborative 3D scene creation and simulation. Exxact aims to enhance productivity, communication, and creativity for M&E clients, particularly as remote production models become more prevalent.

title: These 2 ETFs show why owning small-cap stocks is key to beating the market at times like this
summary: Small-cap stocks within the S&P 500 have significantly outperformed larger-cap stocks this year, with the eight largest market cap stocks experiencing an average loss of 40.4% compared to a 9.5% average loss for the remaining stocks. This highlights how an equal-weight strategy can be more beneficial for S&P 500 investors when the biggest names are struggling. The article suggests that owning small-cap stocks is crucial for beating the market in such conditions.

title: Ciena Deepens Foray Into Access with Two Acquisitions
summary: Ciena Corp. is expanding its broadband access portfolio with two key acquisitions: Tibit Communications and Benu Networks. Tibit, acquired for approximately $210 million, specializes in pluggable optical line terminals for PONs, while Benu Networks, purchased for an undisclosed sum, offers a cloud-native platform for edge functions including virtual Broadband Network Gateways. These acquisitions aim to strengthen Ciena's presence in the lucrative broadband access market, which is a growing segment of its overall sales.

title: Samsung Secures 3-nm Advanced Chip Orders From NVIDIA, Qualcomm – Report
summary: Samsung Foundry has reportedly secured orders from major firms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, IBM, and Baidu to manufacture advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move, leveraging Samsung's gate-all-around (GAA) transistor technology developed with IBM, aims to position Samsung ahead of TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and diversify the semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions. Deliveries in large quantities are expected to begin in early 2024.

title: A Rising Star in the Biden Administration Faces a $100 Billion Test
summary: Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo is spearheading a massive industrial policy initiative, distributing nearly $100 billion to bolster the U.S. semiconductor industry and expand broadband access. This effort aims to enhance American competitiveness in future technologies like AI and secure chip supplies, while also navigating a growing economic rivalry with China. Her success in this endeavor will significantly impact the future of the U.S. economy.

title: Qubit Pharmaceuticals Accelerates Drug Discovery With Hybrid Quantum Computing
summary: Qubit Pharmaceuticals is leveraging hybrid quantum computing, combining classical and quantum computing, to significantly accelerate drug discovery. The company uses NVIDIA's QODA programming model and their Atlas software to simulate drug molecules, aiming to reduce the time and cost of identifying promising treatments for various diseases. This approach seeks to bring down the number of drug compounds needed for preclinical testing, saving substantial time and money in the pharmaceutical development process.

title: Monarch Launches Production of Electric, Driver-Optional Tractor
summary: Monarch has begun production of its MK-V, a driver-optional, all-electric tractor, with the first models slated for Constellation Brands. After four years of development and testing, the MK-V will begin shipping by mid-2023 at a price of $68,000, utilizing NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX chips for its AI and autonomous capabilities. While other companies are also developing autonomous tractors, Monarch aims to be the first with a driver-optional electric model, entering a growing robot-powered agriculture market.

title: PCI-SIG States That Partners Are Responsible For User Safety Following 16-Pin 12VHPWR Cable Lawsuit
summary: PCI-SIG has issued a statement regarding the 16-Pin 12VHPWR cable, emphasizing that its members are responsible for the design, manufacturing, and safety testing of products that use the specification. This response comes after a class-action lawsuit was filed against NVIDIA concerning melting cables on the RTX 4090, which the lawsuit alleges pose a serious electrical and fire hazard. PCI-SIG stresses the importance for all members to take appropriate measures to ensure end-user safety.

title: TSMC to make higher-end chips in US: sources
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) plans to produce advanced 4-nanometer chips at its new Arizona plant when it opens in 2024, an upgrade from initial plans, following pressure from US customers like Apple. The company is also expected to commit to a second plant for even more advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move aligns with US efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though Taiwan intends to keep its latest technology at home.

title: Intel says it will squeeze 1 trillion transistors onto a chip package by 2030
summary: Intel's researchers have revealed innovations in chip packaging that could lead to chips with over a trillion transistors by 2030, significantly extending Moore's Law. This development would result in computer chips ten times more powerful than current advanced silicon. The company aims to address the increasing demand for computing power driven by data consumption and AI.

title: Lattice Extends Low Power Leadership with New Lattice Avant FPGA Platform
summary: Lattice Semiconductor has launched Lattice Avant, a new FPGA platform designed to extend its low-power leadership into mid-range FPGAs. This platform offers enhanced power efficiency, advanced connectivity, and optimized computing capabilities, targeting applications in Communications, Computing, Industrial, and Automotive markets. The Avant platform, beginning with the Avant-E family, aims to double Lattice's addressable market and accelerate customer designs with superior power efficiency and performance.

title: How suite of dividend growth ETFs brings quality to the fore
summary: In an environment of market volatility and economic uncertainty, CI WisdomTree ETFs offer a quality-focused core portfolio solution that has outperformed benchmarks. These dividend growth ETFs, managed by CI Global Asset Management, employ a unique methodology to identify companies with strong fundamentals, sustainable earnings, and resilient business models, screening for quality and growth metrics. This strategy aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure and long-term stability by avoiding overvalued companies and focusing on profitable dividend payers, proving particularly effective during periods of economic contraction.

title: Artificial Intelligence and the Renaissance of Sports
summary: This article explores how artificial intelligence is revolutionizing the world of sports across various domains. It highlights AI's role in personalized training and diet plans, performance analysis, and player scouting and recruiting. The author provides examples such as LaLiga's Granada CF using AI for nutritional planning and professional leagues like the NBA and NFL utilizing sensors for performance tracking.

title: Deutsche Bank Partners with NVIDIA to embed AI into Financial Services
summary: Deutsche Bank announced a multi-year innovation partnership with NVIDIA to integrate AI and machine learning into financial services. This collaboration aims to accelerate the use of AI for improved risk management, enhanced customer service through interactive avatars, and better extraction of insights from unstructured data. The partnership will support Deutsche Bank's cloud transformation and expand its internal AI center of excellence, focusing on explainable and responsible AI.

title: Nvidia and Deutsche Bank team up to deliver enhanced, AI-powered financial services
summary: Nvidia and Deutsche Bank have announced a multiyear "innovation partnership" to accelerate the use of AI and machine learning in financial services. This collaboration aims to develop regulatory-compliant, AI-powered services, enhance risk model development, and accelerate Deutsche Bank's digital transformation. They will also explore AI for immersive metaverse experiences and advanced data analytics using "Finformers" to identify risks like fraud.

title: First Trust/abrdn Emerging Opportunity Fund Announces Termination and Liquidation
summary: The First Trust/abrdn Emerging Opportunity Fund (NYSE: FEO) completed its termination and liquidation on December 7, 2022, as consistent with its plan announced on September 20, 2022. Shareholders received a liquidating distribution of $9.635572 per common share. Investors are advised to consult a tax advisor regarding the tax implications of this liquidation.

title: The 10 Hottest Semiconductor Startup Companies Of 2022
summary: Despite a turbulent year for the semiconductor industry, driven by macroeconomic hurdles, the market for AI, ML, high-power computing, cloud, and intelligent edge business is thriving. This article highlights 10 semiconductor startups making significant strides in 2022. These companies are attracting substantial investments and developing innovative solutions for next-generation technologies.

title: BlackRock seeks to capitalise on metaverse through new ETF
summary: BlackRock has launched a new exchange-traded fund (ETF) called iShare Metaverse UCITS to allow European clients exposure to companies developing metaverse technologies. This ETF tracks the STOXX Global Metaverse Index, which identifies market leaders and innovators through patent data analysis. The initiative aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing metaverse market, predicted to reach US$4-5 trillion by 2030, offering investors a diversified portfolio in this emerging technological space.

title: 5 Charts on Big Tech Stocks’ Collapse
summary: In 2022, Big Tech stocks collectively lost nearly $4 trillion in market value, with the Morningstar US Technology Index down 28.7%. This collapse significantly impacted overall market returns, contributing 7 percentage points to the U.S. market index's 18.7% loss. Factors like recession fears, advertising slowdowns, and changes in consumer spending led to substantial declines for companies such as Meta Platforms, Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Nvidia.

title: GraniteShares goes live with three leveraged single stock ETFs on NASDAQ
summary: GraniteShares has launched three new leveraged single-stock ETFs on the NASDAQ Stock Exchange, offering sophisticated investors 1.5x to 1.75x long exposure to Meta, Alibaba, and Nvidia. This expansion builds on GraniteShares' existing leveraged ETF offerings for Tesla, Coinbase, and Apple and aims to make leveraged single-company exposure, previously confined to specialists, accessible to a broader range of investors. Having pioneered the single-stock ETF market in Europe, GraniteShares is now bringing its expertise and popular product range to the U.S. market.

title: Uber Eats' latest destination for autonomous food deliveries is Miami
summary: Uber Eats has launched autonomous food deliveries in Miami through a partnership with robotics company Cartken, starting in Dadeland and planning further expansion in 2023. This initiative follows previous pilots in Los Angeles and a deal with Nuro, signaling Uber Eats' commitment to expanding robot delivery services. Cartken's six-wheeled robots, built by Magna, can carry the equivalent of two grocery bags and travel at up to six miles per hour.

title: Best Large Workplaces in Technology
summary: Fortune partnered with Great Place to Work to identify the Best Workplaces in Technology for 2025 by analyzing survey feedback from over 149,000 U.S. employees. The article lists the top 40 companies in this ranking, with Cisco Systems, Nvidia, and World Wide Technology leading the list.

title: New Intel oneAPI 2023 Tools Maximize Value of Upcoming Intel Hardware
summary: Intel has released its oneAPI 2023 tools, designed to maximize the value of upcoming Intel hardware such as 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors, Intel Xeon CPU Max Series, and Intel Data Center GPUs. These tools offer enhanced performance and productivity, cross-platform compatibility through SYCL code for non-Intel GPUs, and improved support for AI and machine learning frameworks. The update aims to address the growing need for efficient multi-architecture programming in a developer landscape increasingly targeting heterogeneous systems.

title: NVDL Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:NVDL
summary: This article provides detailed information about the GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL), including its current price, performance metrics, key statistics like AUM and expense ratio, and issuer details. It also offers insights into the fund's investment strategy, classification, and answers frequently asked questions about ETFs. summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of HP ENVY laptops, highlighting their blend of style and functionality. It details key features such as design, performance specifications, entertainment capabilities, connectivity options, battery life, and security features. The review also offers a closer look at specific models like the 13-inch, 15-inch, and x360 convertible HP ENVY laptops, helping consumers choose the best fit for their needs.

title: Supermicro targets cloud-native workloads with new Arm-based Mt. Hamilton servers
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. has announced an expanded lineup of Arm-based servers, dubbed Mt. Hamilton, powered by Ampere Computing LLC’s Altra and Altra Max processors. These new MegaDC servers are designed for cloud-native workloads, offering high performance per watt for applications like cloud gaming, video-on-demand, and infrastructure-as-a-service. Leveraging a single motherboard design and featuring Ampere's Arm-based CPUs with up to 128 cores, Supermicro aims to provide customers with more choices for scalable, low-latency applications.

title: Despite Billions in Federal Subsidies, Chipmakers Are Laying Off Thousands of Workers
summary: Despite significant federal subsidies from the CHIPS Act, major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron Technology, Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and Qualcomm are implementing layoffs or hiring freezes due to slowing demand in key markets. Micron, one of the largest memory chip makers, plans to cut 10% of its workforce, impacting 4,800 employees, citing a "dramatic drop in demand" for PC and smartphone chips. While these companies invest heavily in new facilities to leverage tax breaks, government incentives are not alleviating their short-term financial challenges exacerbated by reduced consumer spending.

title: The bright side of the chip glut: Nvidia, AMD and Intel gaming cards are cheap and very available for Christmas
summary: The article highlights that due to a chip glut, gaming cards from Nvidia, AMD, and Intel are now readily available and significantly discounted, making them an affordable option for Christmas shoppers. This marks a significant change from the previous two years when these items were scarce and expensive. The availability addresses past disappointments for gamers hoping for these cards during the holidays.

title: How the industrial metaverse will transform manufacturing
summary: The industrial metaverse, leveraging virtual and augmented reality, is transforming manufacturing by enhancing training, collaboration, and the use of digital twins. Companies like Lincoln Electric are using VR for faster and more efficient welder training, while automotive manufacturers like Kia and Volvo are using metaverse technologies for remote design collaboration and virtual prototyping, significantly cutting down development time and costs. Despite challenges like high equipment costs and technical difficulties, the industrial metaverse is gaining traction, with its applications expected to expand beyond initial consumer-focused visions.

summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It details the fund's portfolio as of July 31, 2025, highlighting major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple among its top constituents. The report also includes performance metrics and historical data for ACWI.

title: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Bests An RTX 3090 Ti In Octanebench Leak
summary: A leaked Octanebench result for the NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti shows it outperforming the RTX 3090 Ti. While the RTX 4070 Ti's FP32 compute is similar to the RTX 3090 Ti, its improved efficiency likely contributes to the better performance in this specific benchmark. However, the article suggests that gaming performance might not see the same lead due to the RTX 4070 Ti's significantly lower memory bandwidth compared to the RTX 3090 Ti.

title: Micron Technology Sees Chip Recovery by the End of 2023
summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock has fallen significantly in 2022 due to weak demand for DRAM chips and an oversupply in the market. Despite a challenging fiscal Q1 2023 with missed earnings and lowered guidance, Micron expects improving customer inventories to lead to increased revenue in the fiscal second half of 2023. Analysts have revised their ratings and price targets, with some maintaining overweight or buy ratings despite the current downturn.

title: Stock Market Outlook 2023 for the Worst Performing S&P 500 Sectors
summary: This article provides an outlook for the worst-performing S&P 500 sectors in 2022, including Technology, Real Estate, Consumer Discretionary, and Communication Services. It analyzes the performance of key holdings within each sector, their outlook for 2023, and analyst sentiment with options market probabilities for recovery. The forecast indicates that a full recovery to previous highs for these sectors and their major constituents is unlikely in the short term.

title: Sales of computer graphics cards slump to lowest level in two decades
summary: Sales of computer graphics cards hit a 20-year low in Q3 2022 due to a global economic slowdown and the impact of the Ethereum merge on cryptocurrency mining. Major chipmakers like AMD and Nvidia experienced significant declines in discrete desktop GPU shipments, with AMD particularly hard hit. Despite the slump, new high-end GPU launches and Intel's entry into the market (grabbing a 4% share) offer potential for future sales boosts.

title: TSMC ramps up 3nm chip production in Taiwan
summary: TSMC has officially begun mass production of 3nm chips at its facilities in southern Taiwan, a process expected to offer substantial improvements in density, performance, and power efficiency compared to its 5nm node. This move comes as TSMC balances global expansion, including new fabs in the US, with a continued commitment to developing its most advanced technology in Taiwan, navigating complex geopolitical trade relations.

title: WisdomTree U.S. Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the WisdomTree U.S. Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW), including its stock price, holdings, and dividend yield. As of the latest N-PORT filing on September 30, 2025, the fund holds 301 stocks with a total value of $16.30 billion, with top holdings being Microsoft, Apple, NVIDIA, Exxon Mobil, and Chevron. The article also presents key statistics, performance data, and historical breakdowns of the fund's portfolio.

title: Foxconn to use Nvidia chips to build self-driving vehicle platforms
summary: Electronics manufacturer Foxconn and chipmaker Nvidia Corp have partnered to develop autonomous vehicle platforms. Foxconn will manufacture electronic control units (ECUs) based on Nvidia's DRIVE Orin chip for self-driving cars, aiming to address challenges in the global automotive market. Nvidia views this collaboration as a way to scale its efforts and meet the increasing demand for chips in autonomous and connected vehicles, projecting a $300 billion market opportunity in the automotive sector.

title: Nvidia unveils advances in autonomous robotics simulation using AI
summary: Nvidia has announced significant improvements to its Isaac Sim robotics simulation tool at CES 2023, catering to the increasing demand for autonomous robots in complex supply chains. These updates allow engineers to design and test virtual robots in realistic environments, leveraging Nvidia Omniverse for global collaboration. The new features enable realistic human-robot interaction simulations, improved sensor fidelity with RTX technology, and expanded libraries for rapid environment building, ultimately aiming for a higher ratio of virtual to physical robots for better AI training and design.

title: Hyundai, BYD, Polestar adopt Nvidia’s cloud gaming service
summary: Hyundai Motor Group, BYD, and Polestar have adopted Nvidia's cloud-based gaming service, GeForce NOW, for their vehicles. This integration allows drivers and passengers to stream games, with plans to expand the number of available titles in Hyundai's electric vehicles. Hyundai has collaborated with Nvidia since 2020 on AI-powered connected car technology and previously adopted the Nvidia Drive platform.

title: CES 2023: AMD seeks to attract Nvidia’s customers with advanced new AI chips
summary: AMD announced its new Instinct MI300 chip at CES 2023, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the AI market. The chip combines a CPU and GPU to accelerate the intensive process of developing artificial intelligence models in data centers. AMD also highlighted new energy-efficient laptops capable of extended video playback.

title: Tipping Point: NVIDIA DRIVE Scales AI-Powered Transportation at CES 2023
summary: At CES 2023, NVIDIA DRIVE showcased significant advancements in AI-powered transportation, indicating a shift towards global deployment of autonomous vehicles. Major announcements included Foxconn joining the NVIDIA DRIVE ecosystem to build EVs on the DRIVE Hyperion platform, and Polestar and Volvo Cars debuting new vehicles featuring NVIDIA DRIVE Orin for enhanced safety and autonomy. The article highlights the growing industry focus on in-cabin safety features through AI and the broader application of NVIDIA DRIVE in public transit, logistics, and active lighting solutions.

title: Nvidia upgrades robotics simulation tool
summary: Nvidia has significantly updated its Isaac Sim robotics simulation tool at CES 2023, introducing new people simulation, AI capabilities, and cloud access. These enhancements are designed to accelerate the development and optimization of AI robots for various applications, allowing for realistic testing in diverse environments. Key improvements include tools for warehouses, improved sensor performance, expanded ROS support, new research tools, and pre-integrated optimization features, all accessible via the cloud for better collaboration.

title: CES 2023: AMD, Nvidia, auto applications get the hype, but analysts say this one chip maker ruled
summary: At CES 2023, while companies like Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. received significant attention, analysts suggest that a lower-profile chip maker is better positioned post-convention. Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted ongoing caution regarding overall chip demand, particularly due to softness in China, but highlighted autos as a strong theme at the event.

title: Synaptics CEO Says Chip Shortages Could Return Sooner Than You Think
summary: The CEO of Synaptics suggests that chip shortages, similar to those experienced in recent years, could resurface sooner than many industry observers anticipate. This article from Barron's highlights a technology CEO's perspective on the potential for renewed challenges in the semiconductor supply chain.

title: Taiwan Launches Its Own Chip Fab Incentives to Rival US Japan and EU
summary: Taiwanese lawmakers have approved new tax incentives for local chip firms, allowing them to turn 25% of annual R&D expenses and 5% of new equipment costs into tax credits. This move aims to keep advanced semiconductor technologies within Taiwan and enhance the global competitiveness of its chip industry. The incentives are a response to similar programs in the US, Japan, South Korea, and the EU, and are designed to counter the trend of chipmakers like TSMC building facilities abroad.

title: Intel Launches 4th Gen Xeon Scalable Processors, Max Series CPUs and GPUs
summary: Intel has launched its 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors, along with the Intel Xeon CPU Max Series and Intel Data Center GPU Max Series. These new processors aim to deliver significant improvements in performance, efficiency, and capabilities for AI, cloud, network, edge, and high-performance computing, emphasizing built-in accelerators and sustainability features. The company highlights broad industry adoption and performance gains across various key workloads.

title: Nvidia debuts new DGX H100 systems powered by Intel’s 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable chips
summary: Nvidia has unveiled new DGX H100 systems that integrate its H100 Tensor Core GPUs with Intel's recently launched 4th Gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors. These systems, along with upcoming server offerings from Nvidia's partners, promise significantly enhanced efficiency and performance for AI workloads, boasting up to 25 times more efficiency and 3.5 times better AI training/inference efficiency compared to previous generation machines. The new hardware leverages PCIe Gen 5 for faster data transfer and includes a free license for Nvidia Enterprise AI software.

title: Google, Nvidia Express Concerns to FTC About Microsoft’s Activision Deal
summary: Google and Nvidia have joined Sony in expressing concerns to the Federal Trade Commission regarding Microsoft's proposed $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard. These concerns further support the FTC's lawsuit to block the deal, citing potential harm to competition in the video-game industry. The FTC has scheduled an in-house trial for August to address these issues.

title: How Both Taiwan and the US Can Benefit from TSMC's Investment
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is increasing its investment in the U.S. to $40 billion, building a second factory in Phoenix, Arizona. This move, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to strengthen the U.S. domestic semiconductor industry and diversify global supply chains. Experts suggest that while complete self-sufficiency for the U.S. might be unrealistic, this investment helps address critical dependencies, enhances national security, and fosters innovation in chip manufacturing.

title: Taiwan ASE plant listed as 'lighthouse factory' by WEF
summary: Advanced Semiconductor Engineering Inc (ASE) has been recognized as a "lighthouse factory" by the World Economic Forum (WEF) for its Kaohsiung bumping factory. The factory was inducted into the WEF's Global Lighthouse Network due to its adoption of Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) technologies, specifically AI-enabled processes. These innovations led to a 67% increase in output and a 39% reduction in order lead time.

title: Spot On: Texas Instruments Announces First Chipset That Enables Cameras and Sensors to Clean Themselves
summary: Texas Instruments has introduced the first semiconductors with ultrasonic lens cleaning (ULC) technology, enabling camera systems to self-clean dirt, ice, and water using microscopic vibrations. This innovation addresses the problem of dirty camera lenses in various applications, including autonomous vehicles, by offering a cost-effective and compact solution that improves system accuracy and reduces maintenance. The chipset includes the ULC1001 digital signal processor and DRV2901 piezo transducer driver, featuring proprietary algorithms for automatic sensing and cleaning.

title: Dell announces new PowerEdge servers based on Intel's 4th Gen Xeon Scalable processors
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. has refreshed its PowerEdge server lineup with 13 new models featuring Intel Corp.’s 4th Generation Xeon Scalable processors. These new servers, including rack, tower, and multi-node options, boast improved performance, reliability, and energy efficiency for various computing environments, from data centers to the network edge. Dell also highlighted enhanced server management software and introduced these servers as part of an expanded Dell APEX "as-a-service" offering.

title: Global X S&p 500 Covered Call Etf Share Price
summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the Global X S&P 500 Covered Call ETF (XYLD), including its current share price, performance metrics, historical returns, and fundamental data like expense ratio and AUM. title: Outrider Closes $73 Million in Series C Funding
summary: Outrider, a Golden, CO-based software company specializing in autonomous yard operations for logistics hubs, has secured $73 million in Series C funding, bringing its total raised to $191 million. The round was led by FM Capital with participation from several new and existing investors. The new capital will be used to expand its workforce in the U.S. and internationally and to scale commercial operations of its autonomous systems.

title: The Williams Companies Stock: Buy This Strong 5.4% Midstream Yield
summary: The Williams Companies (WMB) is presented as an attractive midstream investment due to its stable to growing EBITDA and robust cash flows, supported by a strong natural gas business and strategic acquisitions. The article highlights WMB's recent acquisition of MountainWest's natural gas pipeline system for $1.5 billion and emphasizes the company's strong dividend coverage, which fuels its dividend growth.

title: Add Exposure to Semiconductor Stocks with These 2 Invesco ETFs
summary: As semiconductor stocks experience a surge, investors can gain exposure to the industry through two Invesco ETFs: the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) and the Invesco Dynamic Semiconductors ETF (PSI). Both ETFs were up over 4% on the day and approximately 16% year-to-date, driven by positive analyst sentiment and significant gains in companies like Nvidia and AMD. SOXQ and PSI offer exposure to 30 U.S. semiconductor companies, with PSI focusing on investment merit criteria and SOXQ tracking the performance of the largest U.S.-listed semiconductor firms.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang: AI is creating tools that will need regulation
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang believes that the powerful new tools developed through artificial intelligence will necessitate regulation. This statement highlights the growing concern among technology leaders about the societal impact of AI innovation. The article briefly touches upon the challenges governments and societies face in adapting to this rapid technological advancement.

title: Is Augmented Reality safe?
summary: Augmented Reality (AR) blends digital information with the real world, raising significant safety concerns despite its benefits. The article highlights potential dangers in privacy, security, and mental health, including data collection, cyber-attacks, and the addictive nature of AR. It emphasizes the need for careful consideration and control as AR technology becomes more integrated into daily life.

title: Intel's 'historic collapse' erases $8 billion from market value
summary: Intel Corp saw its market value drop by about $8 billion after it issued dismal earnings projections and a surprise loss forecast for the first quarter, exacerbating fears about a slump in the personal-computer market and slowing data center growth. Analysts view this as a "historic collapse," with Intel losing market share to rivals like AMD due to superior chip technology and price-performance advantages. CEO Pat Gelsinger faces significant challenges in his efforts to restore Intel's dominance, with some analysts suggesting the company may need to cut its dividend due to high capital expenditures.

title: An open-source option is shaking up the microchip industry
summary: RISC-V, an open-standard instruction set architecture developed at UC Berkeley, is rapidly gaining traction in the microchip industry. Unlike proprietary ISAs like x86 and ARM, RISC-V offers flexibility, scalability, and extensibility, allowing companies to design custom, optimized chips for various devices, from laptops to autonomous car systems. This open approach is saving tech companies time and money, and its adoption is expected to continue growing significantly in the coming years.

title: Points of light for Intel Israel
summary: Intel's recent financial results were poor, and its acquisition of Tower Semiconductor faces delays due to US-China tensions. Despite these challenges, there are positive developments for Intel Israel, including the continued success of Mobileye, positive reception for the Sapphire Rapids processor, and the upcoming Lunar Lake processor and a new Kiryat Gat fab.

title: 2 Chip Stocks to Buy Instead of NVIDIA In 2023
summary: Despite macroeconomic headwinds and regulatory actions impacting the chip industry, technological advancements and government support are poised to drive long-term growth. This article suggests two fundamentally strong semiconductor stocks, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and Photronics (PLAB), as solid buys for 2023, offering an alternative to NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) due to their robust financials and promising market outlook.

title: India’s First NVIDIA DGX-Ready Data Center Colocation Provider
summary: Sify Technologies Limited (NASDAQ: SIFY) has been recognized as India's first NVIDIA DGX-Ready data center colocation provider, enabling customers to host NVIDIA DGX A100 systems. This partnership allows Sify to offer high-compute density, performance, and flexible infrastructure for AI workloads. Enterprises will benefit from AI-powered insights, efficient ROI on AI infrastructure, and scalable AI clusters for private cloud deployments.

summary: This article highlights the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending January 29, 2023. Key drivers for these strong performances include positive Tesla earnings, growth in electric and autonomous vehicles, and hopes for a slower pace of interest rate hikes from the Federal Reserve, boosting technology and consumer discretionary sectors. The list provides specific ETF tickers, their names, and weekly returns, along with brief explanations for their performance.

title: CEO longevity pays off for JPMorgan Chase’s Jamie Dimon and other chiefs of World’s Most Admired Companies
summary: This article highlights how CEO longevity, exemplified by Jamie Dimon of JPMorgan Chase, contributes to a company's success and reputation, as seen in Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies list. Long-tenured CEOs develop deep pattern recognition, build strong internal cultures, and foster innovation, leading to greater efficiency and financial performance. The article also touches on other long-serving CEOs like Warren Buffett and Jensen Huang, and notes Elon Musk's recent "most overrated CEO" distinction.

title: Rima Alameddine Joins IonQ to Expand Enterprise Sales
summary: Rima Alameddine, a former NVIDIA sales executive, has joined IonQ as Chief Revenue Officer to expand its commercial business. She will focus on helping enterprise customers identify critical workloads for quantum computers and fostering an ecosystem of partnerships. Alameddine aims to leverage her experience in technology adoption and her passion for diversity to drive quantum enterprise adoption forward, drawing parallels to her previous role at Nvidia.

title: Brutal Earnings From Intel Signal Changing of Guard in Chips
summary: Intel's recent devastating earnings report highlights a significant shift in the semiconductor industry, with Asian giants Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) and Samsung Electronics Co. demonstrating greater resilience amidst a market downturn. While Intel and SK Hynix faced record losses and cut spending, TSMC and Samsung maintained capital expenditure plans, showcasing their emerging dominance. This signals a changing of the guard, as Intel, once an industry leader, struggles to keep pace.

title: The 3 Best Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy for February 2023
summary: This article highlights the three best quantum computing stocks to consider for February 2023: IBM, IonQ, and Nvidia. It discusses how quantum computing is a rapidly growing field with diverse applications and a projected market value of $1.77 billion annually by 2026. The author provides reasons why each company is a strong investment in this emerging technology sector.

title: Equinix uses two-phase cooling on live Metal cloud servers
summary: Equinix has implemented two-phase liquid cooling on live production servers for its Metal infrastructure-as-a-service offering, using ZutaCore technology. This system, which involves a fluid boiling and condensing to remove heat more efficiently, has been running stably for six months in its NY5 data center. The company is testing this on standard hardware to prepare for higher thermal design power (TDP) servers and address potential future industry needs for more efficient cooling solutions, despite current PUE metrics not fully capturing its benefits.

title: Wall Street ends sharply higher as investors eye inflation data
summary: Wall Street closed sharply higher as investors awaited key inflation data, which is expected to influence the Federal Reserve's future interest rate decisions. Tech-related heavyweights, including Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon, significantly contributed to the S&P 500's gains, with Meta jumping after news of potential further layoffs. Fidelity National Information Services slumped after announcing a spinoff of its merchant payments business.

title: Here's A Simple Way To Bet On Semiconductor Plays Like NVDA Stock
summary: The semiconductor sector is experiencing a resurgence fueled by a new Nasdaq "power trend," offering investors opportunities in stocks like Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) provides a way to capitalize on these rallies. The article highlights the SMH ETF as a simple investment vehicle for those looking to profit from the recovery and growth in semiconductor stocks.

title: Quantum Brilliance raises $18M to develop miniaturized quantum machines that run at room temperature
summary: Quantum Brilliance GmbH, a startup developing miniaturized quantum computers that operate at room temperature using synthetic diamonds, has successfully closed an $18 million funding round. This novel approach avoids the need for cryogenics or complex laser systems, making their quantum machines more portable and power-efficient compared to competitors like IBM and Google. The company plans to use the funds to expand international operations, enhance manufacturing, and further improve performance, aiming to make quantum computing an everyday technology.

title: How enterprises can use ChatGPT and GPT-3
summary: This article explores how enterprises can leverage chatbot platforms like ChatGPT and GPT-3 for various functions, from automating tasks and generating creative ideas to aiding in code development and customer support. It highlights the differences between ChatGPT and GPT-3, emphasizing GPT-3's broader capabilities and customizability for specific business needs through prompt engineering. The piece also addresses the critical risks associated with using these technologies, such as data security concerns and the potential for inaccurate or biased outputs, urging caution and the implementation of clear governance guidelines.

title: These Stocks Are Leading the Growth Rebound
summary: Growth stocks, particularly in the tech sector, have seen a significant rebound in early 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming value stocks. This surge is likely driven by investor expectations that the Federal Reserve may soon pause interest rate hikes. Despite strong recent gains, the Morningstar US Growth Index still needs a substantial rally to recover fully from last year's losses.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) fell by 0.90% to $85.18 on Wednesday, despite a generally positive trading session for the stock market. The decline broke a two-day winning streak for AMD, which closed significantly below its 52-week high. Compared to competitors, AMD's performance was mixed, with NVIDIA Corp. also falling, while Intel Corp. and Applied Materials Inc. saw gains.

title: Long AI Short Hype for CME_MINI:MNQM2023 by mintdotfinance
summary: This article analyzes the current hype surrounding generative AI, using the iShares Exponential Technologies ETF (XT) as a proxy for innovation. It proposes a spread trade—long on CME Micro E-Mini Nasdaq Futures (MNQ) and short on XT—to capitalize on the expected normalization after the initial hype cycle, aiming for a 1.49x reward-to-risk ratio. The strategy is designed to generate alpha by neutralizing beta in an uncertain market.

title: Microsoft teams up with Nvidia and Nintendo in 10-year deal to stream Xbox games
summary: Microsoft has secured 10-year agreements with Nvidia and Nintendo to stream Xbox games as part of its strategy to address antitrust concerns regarding its proposed acquisition of Activision Blizzard. This partnership aims to make Xbox and potential Activision titles available on Nvidia's GeForce Now cloud gaming service and Nintendo platforms, covering various devices. The move is intended to placate regulators scrutinizing the $69 billion deal.

title: Nvidia earnings overshadowed by Microsoft, ChatGPT, OpenAI, 10-year gaming partnership
summary: Nvidia's upcoming earnings report is largely overshadowed by its new 10-year partnership with Microsoft to bring Activision Blizzard and Xbox games to GeForce Now. This partnership also highlights Nvidia's role in powering AI technologies like OpenAI's ChatGPT, especially given Microsoft's recent investment in OpenAI and plans to integrate its AI across services. The focus is now on Nvidia's data-center sales and its contribution from public cloud providers such as Microsoft Azure.

title: How to invest in the right AI stocks
summary: Investing directly in AI stocks is not straightforward, as AI's impact on company revenues is still emerging, with semiconductor companies like Nvidia being the primary direct beneficiaries. Many companies integrate AI to enhance existing products and operations rather than create new revenue streams. Investors should consider "picks and shovels" suppliers in the semiconductor industry, such as AMD and Intel, which are currently undervalued, while Nvidia is fairly valued.

title: Nvidia adds to AI hype with new cloud-based service, stock jumps on forecast
summary: Nvidia's shares surged after the company announced strong fiscal fourth-quarter results and plans to make its AI software available on major cloud platforms like Oracle, Microsoft, and Alphabet. CEO Jensen Huang highlighted the potential of their new browser-based DGX Cloud AI supercomputer service to accelerate adoption of higher-margin data-center software, capitalizing on the booming interest in artificial intelligence. This move is expected to expand the chip maker's revenue streams beyond hardware.

title: Meet the $10,000 Nvidia chip powering the race for A.I.
summary: The Nvidia A100 chip, costing around $10,000, has become a critical tool in the artificial intelligence industry, powering applications like ChatGPT and Stable Diffusion. Nvidia dominates the market for GPUs used in machine learning, with the A100 serving as a "workhorse" for AI professionals and requiring substantial investment for companies to train and deploy AI models. Despite the high costs and emerging competition, Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang asserts that their GPUs offer a much more affordable solution for developing sophisticated AI models compared to traditional CPU-based data centers.

title: As chatbots boom, Nvidia sales outlook beats Wall Street expectations
summary: Nvidia Corp (NVDA.O) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by a surge in demand for its chips used in AI services like chatbots. The company announced a new cloud computing service to help businesses develop generative AI, positioning itself to significantly benefit from the growing AI sector despite a broader downturn in the PC market. Analysts project this boom in generative AI could add an additional $14 billion to Nvidia's revenue by 2027.

title: Mercedes enlists Google’s help in hunt for billion-dollar software windfall
summary: Mercedes-Benz is partnering with Google to develop a new operating system for its vehicles, aiming to generate approximately 10 billion euros (US$10.6 billion) in additional software-based revenue by the end of the decade. The luxury automaker plans to debut this system in 2025 across its entire lineup, focusing on retaining control over the technology while leveraging partnerships with companies like Alphabet, Nvidia, and Luminar. Mercedes also expects 1 billion euros in earnings before interest and taxes from software sales by 2025 and is significantly increasing its software R&D budget and hiring software engineers.

title: This Week in the Metaverse: BlackRock's new ETF, Titanic artifacts as NFTs, and a Snow Crash Sotheby’s auction
summary: BlackRock has launched a new metaverse ETF, allowing investors to engage with companies contributing to virtual platforms, gaming, and AR/VR. In other news, never-before-seen photos of rapper Tupac Shakur are being released as NFTs, while artifacts from the Titanic will also be preserved and sold as NFTs. Additionally, Sotheby's is holding an auction to celebrate 30 years of Neal Stephenson's "Snow Crash," featuring physical and digital items tied to the seminal metaverse novel.

title: Microchip Technology, microchip manufacturer with capital of $50 billion, coming to Armenia
summary: American microchip manufacturer Microchip Technology, with a market capital of $50 billion, is expanding its operations into Armenia. This follows a two-year collaborative agreement with Instigate company, which culminated in Microchip Technology acquiring Instigate Semiconductor. The move is part of a larger trend of major tech and energy companies, including NVIDIA, AMD, and Shell, establishing a presence in Armenia, aiming to boost the country's productivity, innovation, and expertise in high-tech fields.

title: A Comprehensive Guide to Video Gaming ETFs
summary: The video game industry, despite a post-pandemic slowdown in momentum, continues to show significant growth potential, driven by innovation, eSports, and the metaverse. Consumer spending on video games totaled $56.6 billion in 2022, with eSports projected to expand by $3.5 billion by 2027 and the metaverse gaming market estimated to reach $663.8 billion by 2030. The article highlights several video-gaming ETFs for investors looking to capitalize on this growing market, including NERD, ESPO, HERO, and GAMR.

title: Rockwell Automation acquires Knowledge Lens
summary: Rockwell Automation has acquired Bengaluru-based enterprise insights provider Knowledge Lens for an undisclosed sum. This acquisition aims to boost Rockwell's capabilities in data utilization, autonomous manufacturing, and continuous optimization for manufacturers worldwide. Knowledge Lens will join Rockwell's digital services business, Kalypso, to accelerate transformational outcomes for its global clientele, aligning with Rockwell's strategy to expand capabilities and explore early-stage investments in India.

title: Exclusive: Nvidia's plans for sales to Huawei imperiled if U.S. tightens Huawei curbs-draft
summary: Nvidia's plans to sell technology to Huawei could be thwarted if the U.S. government implements stricter restrictions on shipments to the blacklisted Chinese company, according to a draft government contractor report. The Biden administration is currently evaluating the economic impact of potential new rules on U.S. companies before proceeding. The report indicates a "high economic impact" on Nvidia and a "moderate economic impact" on Qualcomm if the proposed policy changes are enacted.

title: TSMC customer billed NT$529bn
summary: TSMC's largest customer, widely believed to be Apple Inc., generated NT$529.65 billion (US$17.32 billion) in revenue for the company last year, marking the first time a single customer contributed over NT$500 billion. Despite this record revenue, the customer's share of TSMC's total sales decreased from 26% in 2021 to 23% in 2022. This shift is attributed to significant growth in TSMC's automotive chip sales, which outperformed those for high-performance computing and smartphones.

title: Buy Nvidia (NVDA) & Broadcom (AVGO) Stock for More Upside?

title: ASML, China customers haunted by uncertainty on new Dutch chip export rules
summary: The Dutch government's newly announced restrictions on chip-technology exports to China have left ASML Holding NV and its Chinese customers uncertain about crucial details, including whether ASML can service existing chip-printing machines in China. While potentially aligning with U.S. goals, the lack of clarity on servicing limits and affected machine models poses a significant challenge for ASML's outlook and its substantial sales and service agreements in the Chinese market. The situation could lead Chinese chipmakers to focus on older technology, shifting ASML's demand to other regions.

title: Top Companies for Employee Engagement and Development

This ranking, developed by the Drucker Institute, assesses corporate effectiveness across various metrics including customer satisfaction, social responsibility, innovation, and financial strength. Adobe also ranks in the top 10 for social responsibility.

title: Ssangyong E&C wins contract for building ASML Korea campus
summary: Ssangyong Engineering & Construction secured a 200-billion-won ($170-million) contract to build a new campus for ASML Holding NV in Hwaseong, South Korea. The campus, with two buildings totaling 74,418 square meters, will house offices and research facilities for ASML, specializing in extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography equipment. This project aims to expand ASML's local supply chain and customer support by enabling more parts procurement in Korea and expanding its partner firm base.

title: Deal Roundup: WeRide files for $500m US listing

title: Demo of China’s Answer to ChatGPT Disappoints Investors
summary: Baidu Inc.'s CEO Robin Li unveiled "Ernie Bot," China's answer to ChatGPT, in a recorded video demonstration that disappointed investors, leading to a 6.4% drop in Baidu's shares. The pre-recorded nature of the demo and lack of real-time interaction raised questions about Ernie's capabilities compared to OpenAI's ChatGPT. Despite the initial disappointment, Baidu is considered a frontrunner in China's AI race, planning to integrate Ernie Bot across its services while navigating concerns about US tech restrictions and Chinese regulatory oversight.

title: Raja Koduri, Main Driver Of Intel’s GPU Efforts, To Leave Company
summary: Raja Koduri, Intel's chief architect and the main driver behind its discrete GPU efforts, is leaving the company at the end of March to start a new software company focused on generative AI for gaming, media, and entertainment. Koduri had been with Intel for over five years, leading the buildout of its graphics capabilities and serving as chief architect for most of his tenure. His departure comes as Intel announced cuts to its data center GPU roadmap and delays to Falcon Shores.
