title: Intel co-founder and philanthropist Gordon Moore has died at 94
summary: Gordon Moore, the visionary co-founder of Intel and profound philanthropist, has passed away at the age of 94. He was widely recognized for his groundbreaking "Moore's Law," which accurately predicted the exponential growth of computing power, significantly influencing the trajectory of the digital age. Beyond his technological contributions, Moore, alongside his wife, established a foundation that has donated over $5.1 billion to environmental conservation, science, and patient care.

title: Cryptocurrencies add nothing useful to society, says chip-maker Nvidia
summary: Nvidia's chief technology officer, Michael Kagan, stated that cryptocurrencies bring nothing useful to society, despite the high demand for Nvidia's processors for crypto mining. He contrasted this with the value of AI applications like ChatGPT, which he believes represent a more worthwhile use of processing power. Nvidia had previously taken steps to limit the use of its graphics cards for crypto mining to prioritize supply for AI researchers and gamers.

title: Beamr teams with NVIDIA to accelerate Beamr technology on NVIDIA GPUs
summary: Beamr Imaging Ltd. announced that its video encoding technology now supports NVIDIA GPU acceleration, achieving up to 10x faster encoding speeds compared to CPUs. This advancement enables real-time optimization for live video streams, reducing storage and bitrate while maintaining high visual quality. Beamr is collaborating with NVIDIA to develop a cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering, with a beta launch planned for Q3 2023, aiming to attract new customers with competitive pricing.

title: Micron Could Report Largest Ever Loss in Q2
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is projected to report its largest-ever loss in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, with an estimated net loss of nearly $1.2 billion and EPS of -$1.06, a significant drop from the prior year. This financial struggle is attributed to weak consumer demand, high inflation, inventory issues, and industry-wide supply constraints. Analysts predict negative gross margins for the first time in 15 years, with an expected inventory write-down in the range of a billion dollars.

The article highlights the efficiency benefits of liquid cooling over traditional air cooling and the broader industry trend towards these advanced thermal management solutions.

title: The Netherlands Joins US Effort To Deny China Semiconductor Technology
summary: The Netherlands has announced plans to ban the export of semiconductor technology to China, aligning with US efforts to limit China's access to advanced chipmaking capabilities due to national security concerns. This move is significant as Dutch suppliers, particularly ASML, are crucial for essential chipmaking tools. While ASML states the ban will not materially affect its 2023 financial outlook, China has condemned these "bullying acts" as harmful to market principles and global supply chains.

title: Synopsys Launches Full-Stack, AI-Driven EDA Suite

This suite enables engineers to leverage AI at every stage and access tools via the cloud, significantly reducing product development time and improving silicon performance. Leading semiconductor companies, including Renesas, MediaTek, IBM Research, and TSMC, are already utilizing Synopsys.ai to optimize design, verification, testing, and manufacturing, demonstrating the transformative potential of AI in the semiconductor industry.

title: Cadence Launches Cloud AI Tool to Shrink PCB Placement and Routing Times
summary: Cadence Design Systems has introduced its new Allegro X AI technology, a cloud-based tool designed to automate PCB placement and routing. Early customer testing has shown substantial time savings and improved wire lengths in PCB designs.

title: Qualcomm, Nvidia spar for top spot in AI chip efficiency tests
summary: Qualcomm's AI 100 chips outperformed Nvidia's H100 chips in two out of three power efficiency categories for AI inference tasks, specifically image classification and object detection. Nvidia, however, took the lead in natural language processing performance and power efficiency. This highlights the competitive landscape in the growing market for data center inference chips, where power consumption is a key cost factor.

title: Alibaba's Hong Kong shares slump on SoftBank's stake sale report
summary: Alibaba's Hong Kong-listed shares fell significantly following reports that SoftBank Group Corp is selling almost all of its remaining stake in the Chinese e-commerce giant. This move comes as valuations for China's big tech firms are recovering after a period of heightened regulatory scrutiny and as SoftBank seeks to monetize profitable holdings amidst efforts to shore up cash reserves for its Vision Fund losses. SoftBank has been shifting to a "defensive mode" and had previously booked a substantial gain by cutting its Alibaba stake last year.

title: TSMC seeks $15B in U.S. support despite ‘unacceptable’ conditions
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is seeking $15 billion in U.S. government aid for its Arizona chip plants but finds some of the conditions attached to the CHIPS Act funding "unacceptable." The primary concern involves rules requiring profit-sharing if returns exceed projections, which TSMC chairman Mark Liu fears could complicate profit calculations and make the Arizona investment less attractive. Other conditions, like providing affordable childcare and restrictions on expanding manufacturing in "countries of concern" such as China, also pose challenges for chipmakers.

title: Prime Minister Pashinyan receives the President and CEO of Nvidia company Jensen Huang
summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan met with Jensen Huang, President and CEO of Nvidia, a leader in the high-tech sector. The discussion focused on the development of engineering education, the advancement of the high-tech sector, and continued collaboration between the Armenian government and Nvidia. Nvidia had established a scientific research center in Armenia the previous year, with both parties expressing commitment to further development in the country's IT sector.

title: PM Pashinyan receives the President and CEO of Nvidia company Jensen Huang
summary: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan met with Jensen Huang, President and CEO of Nvidia, discussing the development of Armenia's high-tech sector. Pashinyan highlighted Nvidia's scientific research center in Armenia and the government's commitment to attracting IT companies. Huang expressed his impression of Armenia's potential and human capital in high technology, affirming Nvidia's continued activities in the country.

title: NVIDIA Enables Trustworthy, Safe, and Secure Large Language Model Conversational Systems
summary: NVIDIA has introduced NeMo Guardrails, an open-source toolkit designed to ensure the safety, security, and trustworthiness of large language model (LLM) conversational systems. This toolkit allows developers to define programmable rules, known as guardrails, that guide user interactions and prevent off-topic discussions, misinformation, toxicity, and malicious code execution. NeMo Guardrails integrates with existing LLMs and toolkits like LangChain, providing a robust framework for building reliable AI applications.

title: Right on Track: NVIDIA Open-Source Software Helps Developers Add Guardrails to AI Chatbots
summary: NVIDIA has released NeMo Guardrails, new open-source software designed to help developers ensure generative AI applications, particularly those powered by large language models, remain accurate, appropriate, on-topic, and secure. This software enables the creation of topical, safety, and security guardrails to prevent AI from veering off course, filtering unwanted language, and restricting connections to safe third-party applications. NeMo Guardrails is compatible with various developer tools like LangChain and Zapier, and is integrated into the NVIDIA NeMo framework, which can also be accessed as part of the NVIDIA AI Enterprise platform or as a service.

title: Alibaba Cloud cuts prices by up to 50% for core, storage products
summary: Alibaba Cloud, the cloud computing division of Alibaba Group, announced price cuts of up to 50% on its core products and services to increase its market share in China amidst growing competition. This move, effective Wednesday, includes reductions for services using Arm, Intel-based chips, and Nvidia GPUs. The company also revealed that over 200,000 enterprises have requested beta testing for its AI-powered large language model, Tongyi Qianwen.

title: Msci Kld 400 Social Index: Meaning, Example, FAQs
summary: The MSCI KLD 400 Social Index is a market capitalization-weighted stock index that focuses on large, mid, and small-cap U.S. companies with high environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards, excluding certain industries. Founded as the Domini 400 Social Index, it was a pioneer in socially responsible investing. The index is reviewed quarterly and tends to be dominated by large American technology companies like Microsoft, NVIDIA, Alphabet, Tesla, and Visa.

title: Super Micro stock surged more than 30% after it offered a bullish forecast based on AI demand
summary: Super Micro's stock rose over 30% after the company issued a positive forecast driven by high demand for AI-related technologies. CEO Charles Liang noted a boom in sales, particularly due to advancements like ChatGPT, leading investors to significantly increase their bids for the San Jose server maker's stock.

title: Meet the Maker: Software Developer Builds Fully Functional Superhero Helmet
summary: Kris Kersey, an embedded software developer and YouTuber, has created a fully functional Iron Man-style superhero helmet using the NVIDIA Jetson platform. The 3D-printed helmet features a heads-up display with computer-vision capabilities, providing real-time information such as temperature, GPS, and object detection. Kersey plans to make the project's code open source and continue developing a full suit, demonstrating the blend of art and engineering in bringing imaginative technology to life.

title: AMD to Make 'New' 2019-Era 3000G APUs to Sell Old Motherboards
summary: AMD is reportedly resuming production of its 2019-era 12nm Zen+ Ryzen 3000G APUs, specifically the Ryzen 3 3200G and Ryzen 5 3400G, with plans to produce 30,000 new chips. This move is believed to be an effort to stimulate sales of older AM4 motherboards, particularly in the Chinese market. The article notes that price adjustments will be necessary for these dated APUs to be competitive against newer, similarly priced options like the Ryzen 5 5600G.

title: Growing chip supply helps Baxter slash backlog
summary: Baxter International significantly reduced its backlog for Front Line Care products by half in Q1, thanks to an improved supply of electromechanical components, including semiconductor chips. This improved availability has allowed the healthcare company to reduce spot purchasing, lower costs, and enhance operational predictability, though an elevated backlog still remains which the company aims to address throughout the year. Despite facing continued pressure from rising supply chain costs from suppliers, Baxter expresses cautious optimism about turning the corner on component supply.

title: Notable Wins
summary: The I/O Fund has achieved significant returns since its inception, outperforming major indices with a cumulative return of 326%. In 2025 alone, the fund returned 37%, positioning it as a top-performing hedge fund. The article details specific successful investments in companies like Bloom Energy, Astera Labs, Nvidia, and various cryptocurrencies, highlighting the fund's strategic allocation to AI and early insights into market trends.

title: YieldMax™ Launches NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF
summary: YieldMax™ has launched NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF, which seeks to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). This actively managed ETF由ZEGA Financial aims to provide substantial monthly income to investors, joining other YieldMax™ ETFs like TSLY, OARK, and APLY, and adheres to a gross expense ratio of 0.99%.

title: Here's how AI is driving the rise of GPU cloud specialists
summary: The rise of AI is fueling a new class of cloud providers specializing in GPU compute, offering lower prices than generalized cloud competitors. Companies like CoreWeave, Lambda Labs, RunPod, and Arc Compute are catering to AI workloads that require substantial GPU resources. These niche providers differentiate themselves through competitive pricing, strategic locations, and ready access to scarce compute resources amidst supply chain challenges.

title: Best AI ETFs
summary: This article identifies and compares five top Artificial Intelligence (AI) focused ETFs for investors looking to capitalize on AI growth without betting on individual stocks. The author details each ETF's inception date, expense ratio, AUM, year-to-date performance, and top holdings, along with a discussion on the risks and benefits of investing in AI. The article highlights that these thematic ETFs offer global exposure to companies benefiting from advancements in robotics, automation, and AI.

title: ServiceNow and NVIDIA Announce Partnership to Build Generative AI Across Enterprise IT
summary: ServiceNow and NVIDIA have partnered to develop enterprise-grade generative AI capabilities, leveraging NVIDIA software and infrastructure to enhance workflow automation on the ServiceNow Platform. This collaboration aims to create custom large language models trained on specific data to boost productivity across IT departments, customer service, employees, and developers. NVIDIA will also use these generative AI tools to streamline its own IT operations.

title: Roundhill Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF With CHAT
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT), an actively managed ETF focusing on global companies leading in generative AI technology. The fund aims to offer precise exposure to generative AI, distinguishing itself from other AI-themed ETFs that may lack such specific focus. Top holdings include Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet, with expectations for the basket to expand as more companies genuinely contribute to the generative AI space.

title: Roundhill Investments Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT)
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT), providing investors with exposure to companies at the forefront of generative AI technology. The ETF aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing generative AI market, which Roundhill estimates to have a total addressable market of over $120 billion for enterprise software. CHAT is an actively-managed ETF with top holdings including Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Salesforce, offering investors a way to participate in this innovative and transformative technology.

title: NVIDIA, Rolls-Royce and Classiq Announce Quantum Computing Breakthrough for Computational Fluid Dynamics in Jet Engines
summary: NVIDIA, Rolls-Royce, and quantum software company Classiq have achieved a breakthrough in quantum computing for computational fluid dynamics (CFD) in jet engines. They designed and simulated the world's largest quantum computing circuit for industrial CFD, measuring 10 million layers deep with 39 qubits, using NVIDIA's quantum computing platform and A100 Tensor Core GPUs. This collaboration aims to accelerate the design process and enable more sophisticated calculations for developing efficient and sustainable jet engines, utilizing a hybrid approach combining classical and quantum computing methods.

title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
summary: Supermicro has launched the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment and increase performance for AI and HPC workloads, while also lowering operating expenses and improving Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) by up to 86% in direct cooling costs. The systems offer up to 30x performance improvement over previous generations for large transformer models and boast advanced features like PCIe 5.0 and NVIDA H100 SXM5 Tensor Core GPUs.

title: Applied Materials plans $4B Silicon Valley R&D facility
summary: Applied Materials announced plans to invest up to $4 billion in a new Silicon Valley research and development facility. This "EPIC Center" will focus on advancing semiconductor manufacturing and is expected to house over $25 billion in research work within its first decade. The investment is contingent on receiving funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to accelerate the industry's ability to bring new technologies to market.

title: Supermicro Launches Industry's First NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU H100 Servers with Liquid Cooling -- Reduces Data Center Power Costs by Up to 40%
summary: Supermicro has introduced the industry's first NVIDIA HGX H100 8 and 4-GPU servers featuring liquid cooling, designed to significantly reduce data center power costs by up to 40%. These new rack-scale solutions accelerate deployment, enhance performance, and lower operating expenses, also offering an estimated 86% reduction in direct cooling costs compared to air-cooled data centers. The servers support high-performance AI workloads with NVIDIA HGX H100 Tensor Core GPUs and are integrated with advanced liquid cooling systems to optimize Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) and Total Cost to Environment (TCE).

title: Dell Technologies and NVIDIA Introduce Project Helix for Secure, On-Premises Generative AI
summary: Dell Technologies and NVIDIA have launched "Project Helix," a joint initiative to simplify the deployment of secure, on-premises generative AI models for businesses. This project provides full-stack solutions with optimized hardware and software to help enterprises leverage their proprietary data for AI applications. It aims to support the entire generative AI lifecycle, from infrastructure provisioning to deployment, allowing companies to quickly build customized AI solutions while maintaining data privacy.

title: NVIDIA Collaborates With Microsoft to Accelerate Enterprise-Ready Generative AI
summary: NVIDIA announced the integration of its NVIDIA AI Enterprise software with Microsoft Azure Machine Learning to provide an end-to-end cloud platform for enterprise-ready generative AI. This collaboration offers Azure customers tools to build, deploy, and manage customized AI applications using over 100 NVIDIA AI frameworks, enhancing development speed and security for large language models. The integration leverages high-performance NVIDIA accelerated computing for AI model training and inference and also makes NVIDIA Omniverse Cloud available on Microsoft Azure.

title: 'Extremely regrettable': SoftBank hits back after S&P cuts credit rating further into junk
summary: SoftBank has strongly criticized S&P Global Ratings for downgrading its credit rating further into "junk" status, deeming the assessment "extremely regrettable." S&P cited SoftBank's high exposure to volatile unlisted company shares through its Vision Funds and reduced listed assets after selling Alibaba stock. SoftBank, however, argues that S&P failed to properly account for its significantly strengthened cash position and the upcoming IPO of Arm.

title: How to Invest in Gaming
summary: The gaming industry, projected to reach US$211.2 billion in revenues by 2025, offers numerous investment opportunities, including mobile and cloud gaming which are experiencing significant growth. Investors can engage through individual stocks of hardware and software companies, or by using exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for broader industry exposure. Esports, particularly, is set for rapid expansion with a compound annual growth rate of 17.8 percent due to increasing smart device adoption and internet connectivity.

title: Nvidia stock price is surging thanks to ChatGPT and an expected boom in generative AI
summary: Nvidia's stock price surged over 26% after forecasting Q2 2024 revenue of $11 billion, significantly above Wall Street estimates, due to the increasing demand for its powerful GPU chips needed for generative AI technologies like ChatGPT. Despite a slight decrease in Q1 2024 revenue compared to the previous year, the company's strong future outlook based on the generative AI boom is driving investor confidence, also positively impacting other chipmakers like AMD. Nvidia's CEO stated that the company is greatly increasing its supply to meet the surging demand for its data center products.

title: Why Intel’s stock is falling as Nvidia leads the rest of the semiconductor sector on a massive surge
summary: While Nvidia and other semiconductor stocks saw a massive surge following Nvidia's optimistic outlook on AI-fueled demand, Intel's stock moved in the opposite direction, declining over 5%. An analyst suggested that Intel is a "major loser and share donor" in the competitive artificial intelligence chip market.

title: Cadence Cerebrus garners Singapore Business Review's tech award for AI-semiconductor services
summary: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. received the Singapore Business Review's Technology Excellence Award in the AI-semiconductor services category for its AI-driven Cadence® Cerebrus™ Intelligent Chip Explorer. This technology automates and scales digital chip design, optimizing power, performance, and area (PPA) while significantly reducing engineering effort and time to tape out. The Cadence Cerebrus has been widely adopted by top semiconductor companies and hyperscalers, revolutionizing chip design productivity.

title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

title: NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchips Designed for Accelerated Generative AI Enter Full Production
summary: NVIDIA announced that its GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip is now in full production, intended to power systems globally for complex AI and HPC workloads. This superchip combines the Arm-based NVIDIA Grace CPU and Hopper GPU architectures using NVLink-C2C interconnect technology, offering significantly higher bandwidth for demanding generative AI applications. Global hyperscalers, supercomputing centers, and numerous system manufacturers are deploying GH200-powered systems, which are expected to be available later this year.

title: WPP shakes up creative process with AI-powered content engine
summary: WPP has partnered with Nvidia to launch a new AI-powered content engine aimed at transforming the creative process for clients. This engine, built on Nvidia's Omniverse Cloud, will leverage generative AI tools from Adobe and Getty Images to create more realistic and brand-accurate imagery and videos efficiently. The initiative positions WPP as a leader in AI adoption within the advertising industry, addressing the fast-paced demands of digital marketing and potential economic pressures.

title: For the First Time Ever, Single-Stock ETFs Will Close Soon
summary: Seven single-stock ETFs, primarily offering leveraged or inverse exposure to individual stocks, are set to close in June, marking the first time such products will cease trading. Despite initial high expectations when they launched in July 2022, most single-stock ETFs have struggled, with minimal assets under management for those slated for closure. AXS will close five of its single-stock ETFs tied to companies like Nike, Pfizer, and PayPal, while GraniteShares is closing a Tesla-related fund, and AXS will also close two other leveraged/inverse ETFs targeting China internet and de-SPAC companies.

title: A.I. ETF Sold Out Of Monster Energy, Three Ways To Look At It
summary: The Artificial Intelligence driven ETF (AMOM) unexpectedly sold its entire position in Monster Beverage (MNST), despite Monster's strong performance and growth. The article explores three perspectives on this move, comparing Monster's valuation and performance against Starbucks (SBUX), PepsiCo (PEP), and Coca-Cola (KO), highlighting Monster's aggressive growth trajectory versus the more predictable cash flows and dividends of its peers. The analysis suggests Monster still has significant room for outperformance.

title: Opinion: Nvidia created an AI bubble, and software stocks are already paying the price
summary: Nvidia's optimistic projections for AI-driven revenue created a surge of excitement and investment in software stocks. However, despite this initial boost, many software companies are now experiencing declines as their recent earnings reports and forecasts fail to match Nvidia's high expectations for AI-related revenue, suggesting that the broader tech sector might not immediately benefit as much from AI as anticipated by some. This has led to speculation that Nvidia's impact has created an "AI bubble" in the software sector, with valuations running ahead of reality.

title: High Beta ETF 'SPHB' Lifted by Heavy Tech Sector Tilt
summary: The Invesco S&P 500 High Beta ETF (SPHB) performed strongly in May, driven by its significant allocation to the information technology sector. While the broader S&P 500 saw modest gains, the S&P 500 High Beta index climbed 2.4% due to the tech sector's 9.5% rally. SPHB has also demonstrated strong long-term performance, outpacing the S&P 500 over a three-year period.

title: Cramer's Lightning Round: Gold is great, stick with Franco-Nevada
summary: Jim Cramer shares his quick takes on several stocks during the "Lightning Round" segment of "Mad Money." He expressed caution about MP Materials due to insider selling, noted American Express's benefit from increased leisure spending, and strongly recommended Franco-Nevada as a gold investment, suggesting 10% portfolio allocation to gold. Cramer also advised against Clearfield in a competitive market, preferring Nvidia.

title: Broadcom Stock Pulls into the Buy Zone: Analysts Raise Targets
summary: Broadcom's stock experienced a correction after rallying on AI-related news, despite reporting strong Q2 revenue and higher guidance. Analysts have responded by raising price targets, suggesting the company's uptrend is likely to continue. The company's performance was driven by a 9% increase in semiconductor solutions and robust free cash flow, with expectations for continued growth into Q3.

title: Microsoft Inks Deal With NVIDIA-Backed CoreWeave
summary: Microsoft has signed a deal with NVIDIA-backed cloud-computing startup CoreWeave, planning to invest billions to bolster its cloud computing infrastructure for AI operations. This partnership will provide Microsoft easier access to NVIDIA’s GPUs, which are crucial for AI-based applications like ChatGPT, and aims to give Microsoft a competitive edge against rivals like Alphabet Inc. and Amazon Web Services. CoreWeave’s access to over 45,000 GPUs and its status as an elite CSP in the NVIDIA Partner Network positions it as a key enabler for Microsoft's AI endeavors.

title: Cathie Wood Dodged Nvidia Stock Before Its Enormous Surge: Is This a Warning for Investors?
summary: The article discusses Cathie Wood's Ark Invest firm selling a significant portion of its Nvidia stock before the chipmaker's massive surge, costing shareholders over $200 million in potential gains. It questions whether this decision is a warning for investors considering Ark Invest ETFs, especially given the funds' performance compared to Nvidia. While Wood defended the sales due to Nvidia's valuation and the presence of other AI plays, the article points out inconsistencies in her valuation arguments and suggests investors may want to explore other options.

title: Intel Reveals Fastest Arc Pro Workstation GPUs Yet
summary: Intel has launched its fastest Arc Pro workstation GPUs to date, the A60 for desktops and A60M for laptops, significantly increasing specifications over previous models. These new GPUs are designed for applications like CAD, AI inferencing, and media processing, aiming to challenge the market dominance of Nvidia and AMD. While Intel hopes to attract workstation users with improved performance and quarterly driver updates, some partners note a lack of demand and suggest Intel needs a more comprehensive solution to compete effectively.

title: Applied Materials Announces $4 Billion Investment In EPIC Center For R&D Collaboration
summary: Applied Materials is investing $4 billion into its new Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, a collaborative R&D project designed to accelerate semiconductor production techniques. This initiative aims to reduce the time from foundational research to mass commercialization by 30% through overlapping R&D phases and fostering direct collaboration between universities, equipment makers, and chip companies. The EPIC Center will strengthen Applied Materials' position in the industry, benefit chip makers with faster product roadmaps, and provide invaluable learning opportunities for university students.

title: Nvidia and Oracle back $270M funding round for generative AI startup Cohere
summary: Generative AI startup Cohere Inc. has secured $270 million in a Series C funding round with significant backing from Nvidia Corp., Oracle Corp., Salesforce.com Inc.'s venture capital arm, and SentinelOne Inc., led by Inovia Capital. Cohere, founded by former Google AI researcher Aidan Gomez (co-author of the Transformers paper), offers AI models via cloud APIs for tasks like text generation, summarization, and search, and is currently valued at $2.2 billion.

title: Big Tech Is Outpacing Small-Caps Amid Market Rally
summary: Big Tech is significantly outperforming small-cap stocks in the current market rally, with the Direxion Daily Technology Bull 3X ETF (TECL) up nearly 120% compared to the Direxion Daily Small Cap Bull 3X Shares (TNA) 3.7% gain year-to-date. This outperformance, particularly by companies like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, is despite lingering recession concerns. The article questions the sustainability of Big Tech's rally while suggesting that small-cap funds like TNA could offer future upside.

title: A breakdown of AI ETFs as tech names burn bright
summary: This article breaks down various AI-focused ETFs available in Europe, examining their methodologies, holdings, and performance. It highlights the differences in how these ETFs define and capture the AI theme, from market-cap weighted exposure to companies involved in big data and NLP to equally-weighted exposure based on AI-related revenue or IP. The piece also compares their costs, liquidity, and correlation of returns, noting that while some are dominated by large tech names, others offer differentiated exposure to companies leveraging machine learning.

title: AMD launches new Instinct MI300X AI chip to take on Nvidia’s GPUs
summary: AMD has launched its new Instinct MI300X AI accelerator chip, designed to compete with Nvidia's GPUs in the generative AI market. The company also introduced its Infinity Architecture Platform, powered by eight MI300X chips, and its ROCm software ecosystem. Despite these announcements, AMD's stock fell due to a lack of major customer endorsements for the new chips and details on pricing.

title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

title: Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FDTX)
summary: The Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF (FDTX) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in innovative businesses with the potential for market disruption, including global disruptive technology companies in areas like machine learning and AI. The fund launched in April 2020 and is managed by Fidelity, with assets under management of $191.07 million. FDTX holdings include major tech companies such as NVIDIA, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

title: The A.I. Race Is Now Between Two Chip Giants Led by Taiwanese American CEOs
summary: The generative A.I. boom has intensified competition between semiconductor giants Nvidia and AMD, who are racing to produce advanced A.I. chips. AMD recently announced the MI300X, a direct competitor to Nvidia's H100, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominant market share in the A.I. chip sector. Both companies are led by Taiwanese American CEOs, Jensen Huang of Nvidia and Lisa Su of AMD, who share similar backgrounds and have played pivotal roles in their companies' success in the semiconductor industry.

title: Invesco Semiconductors ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (PSI) $PSI
summary: MarketBeat has assigned the Invesco Semiconductors ETF (PSI) an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $95.38. This rating is derived from analyzing 505 analyst ratings over the past year covering 25 companies within the ETF, which represent 89.4% of its portfolio. The report provides a detailed breakdown of consensus ratings and price targets for the ETF's top 25 holdings, alongside their individual share prices, market rank, and potential upside.

title: Democratic Large Cap Core ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DEMZ)
summary: The Democratic Large Cap Core ETF (DEMZ) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with a price target of $41.72, suggesting a 0.31% potential upside. This rating is based on 651 analyst ratings covering 25 of the ETF's constituent companies. Holdings with strong ratings include NVIDIA and Microsoft, while Loews has a lower rating score.

title: Fidelity Disruptive Automation ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FBOT) $FBOT
summary: This article provides an aggregate rating and price target for the Fidelity Disruptive Automation ETF (FBOT). Based on analyst ratings for its holdings, FBOT has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $35.26. The report details the individual ratings and price targets for the top 12 holdings within the ETF, including companies like NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Amazon.

title: JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (JUSA)
summary: This article provides a detailed review and aggregate rating for the JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA). MarketBeat calculates the ETF's "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating and a price target of $61.93 by analyzing the weight and individual analyst ratings of its underlying stock holdings. The review also lists the top 25 holdings with their respective analyst ratings, price targets, and potential upside.

title: Best Gaming Stocks
summary: The article highlights the growing potential of the video game industry for investment portfolios, spurred by trends like eSports and NFTs. It identifies Benzinga's top five gaming stocks for 2022, including Roblox, Scientific Games, NVIDIA, Electronic Arts, Take-Two Interactive, and Sony, categorizing them into hardware and software providers. The author also discusses the pros and cons of investing in gaming stocks, acknowledging the industry's growth prospects but also its volatility and sensitivity to economic conditions.

title: Comparing the Expense Ratios of ETFs Investing in AI: Which One Is Right for You?
summary: This article compares the expense ratios and year-to-date returns of four ETFs that invest in artificial intelligence, focusing on options with lower fees. It discusses broad-based tech ETFs like FTEC, IETC, and IYW, and a new economies composite ETF, KOMP, highlighting their investment strategies and top holdings. The piece advises investors to consider expense ratios, YTD returns, investment goals, risk tolerance, and portfolio diversification when choosing an AI-focused ETF.

title: Intel pairs its hardware with HPE solutions to drive enterprise AI success
summary: Intel Corp. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. have partnered for 30 years and are now collaborating to deliver AI-focused solutions for enterprises. Intel's strategy involves powerful hardware like its GPU Max Series with 100 billion transistors and a comprehensive software suite called OpenVINO for training video-based AI use cases. This partnership aims to make advanced AI technology accessible to various sectors, including government, cloud companies, and general enterprises.

title: KT to invest $5.4 bn by 2027 to develop AI platforms
summary: South Korea’s telecom service provider KT Corp. plans to invest 7 trillion won ($5.4 billion) by 2027 to become the country’s leading AI service provider, focusing on the robot, healthcare, and education markets. The investment will be allocated to securing super AI source technologies, upgrading related infrastructure, and developing AI service models. KT aims to generate 1.3 trillion won ($1 billion) in revenue from five AI-related service areas by 2025: contact centers, logistics, robots, healthcare, and education.

title: TGRW Doubling Returns of SPY
summary: The T. Rowe Price Growth Stock ETF (TGRW) has demonstrated strong performance in 2023, doubling the returns of the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) year-to-date. TGRW's strategy of investing in growth firms with strong financial characteristics and a competitive expense ratio has contributed to its success, even as it approaches its three-year milestone. The ETF has also shown significant asset under management growth and strong technical indicators suggesting continued momentum.

title: Active Growth ETF TGRW Doubling Returns of SPY
summary: The T. Rowe Price Growth Stock ETF (TGRW) has demonstrated strong performance in the first half of 2023, doubling the year-to-date returns of the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) with 32.1% compared to SPY's 15.8%. TGRW, which focuses on firms with strong cash flow and above-average earnings growth, is approaching its three-year anniversary and has seen substantial AUM growth. This active growth ETF is outperforming its benchmark thanks to its investment strategy and a competitive expense ratio of 52 basis points.

title: Artificial Intelligence Opens a Generational Opportunity for Chipmakers
summary: The article discusses how the expansion of AI applications, especially large language models, is creating a "generational opportunity" for chipmakers. It highlights the projected growth of AI chip spending to nearly $165 billion by the end of the decade, primarily benefiting GPU-based chip providers like Nvidia, and notes that the demand for specialized AI hardware is leading to significant investments, partnerships, and a potential "long capital investment cycle" for the semiconductor industry. The article also examines how this shift will impact traditional CPU providers and accelerate the replacement of legacy computing infrastructure with AI-first hardware.

title: Nvidia and AMD shares fall on report of new U.S. ban on AI chip exports to China
summary: Shares of Nvidia Corp. and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. dropped following a report that the Biden administration is considering a new ban on sales of AI chips to China. This potential ban comes despite Nvidia managing to avoid $400 million in lost sales last year after a previous ban on some chip sales to China. The news impacted both companies with Nvidia shares falling 3% after hours and AMD shares also decreasing by 3%.

title: Nvidia stock falls after CFO says no material impact from prospective wider ban on AI chip sales to China
summary: Nvidia's stock dropped after its CFO, Collette Kress, stated that an expanded U.S. ban on AI chip sales to China would likely have no immediate material impact on the company. However, Kress expressed concern about the long-term effects, suggesting it could lead to a "permanent loss of opportunities" globally. This statement followed reports that the Biden administration was considering widening export bans on AI technology to China.

title: Oracle is no longer buying Intel processors for its data centers
summary: Oracle, one of the largest data center operators globally, has stopped purchasing Intel processors for its data centers. This decision was confirmed by Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison. Instead of Intel, Oracle is now buying billions of dollars worth of Nvidia and AMD chips.

title: Stock of the week - Advanced Micro Devices (29.06.2023)
summary: This article discusses the potential impact of new US chip export restrictions to China on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). While such restrictions negatively affected semiconductor stocks, AMD's exposure may be limited as China's share of its revenue has significantly decreased. The article also compares AMD's stock performance to Nvidia and Intel, noting Nvidia's stronger recovery from the 2022 sell-off.

title: The Best QQQ Stocks To Buy Now
summary: The article identifies nine top-performing tech and tech-adjacent stocks within the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF, highlighting their growth, innovation, and involvement in artificial intelligence. It details each company's industry, market value, and key factors contributing to its strong performance and future potential, especially in the context of AI advancements. The QQQ ETF itself is noted for its strong returns compared to the S&P 500 over various timeframes.

title: Apple’s market value ends above $3 trillion for the first time
summary: Apple Inc.'s market value surpassed $3 trillion for the first time at the close of a trading session, driven by positive inflation data and confidence in its market expansion strategies. This milestone reflects a 49 percent gain in Apple shares for 2023, amid a tech stock rebound fueled by expectations of slower interest rate hikes from the US Federal Reserve and buzz around artificial intelligence. The company's financial resilience, strong balance sheet, and a recent augmented-reality headset launch contribute to its success, making it one of only five US companies with a valuation exceeding $1 trillion.

title: Q3 Stock Market Outlook: Time to Batten Down the Hatches or Raise the Sail?
summary: Morningstar's Q3 2023 stock market outlook suggests that while the U.S. market is still undervalued, the margin of safety has decreased, and the rally in the first half of the year has been disproportionately driven by a few "Magnificent Seven" stocks. Investors are advised to take profits from overextended growth and technology stocks and reallocate to undervalued sectors like communications and real estate, and to value, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks. The outlook anticipates slower market gains due to economic slowdown, tight monetary policy, and reduced credit availability, but expects any future pullbacks to be relatively shallow.

title: Apple makes history as first $3 trillion company amid tech stock surge
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first company to reach a $3 trillion market value, driven by investor optimism about its expansion into new markets and a projected easing of interest rate hikes. This accomplishment is set against a backdrop of a broader tech stock surge, with other companies like Tesla and Nvidia also seeing significant gains due to advancements in AI and strategic partnerships. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance and its reputation as a safe investment, alongside the success of other tech giants.

title: Growth-Stock Funds Fly High but Fall Short in 2023′s First Half
summary: Growth-stock funds experienced a strong rebound in the first half of 2023 after significant losses in 2022, with large-growth funds doubling small-growth funds' gains. However, two-thirds of U.S.-centric active managers underperformed stylistically relevant indexes and passive rivals due to untimely sector allocations and light stakes in mega-cap stocks that drove market performance. International and emerging markets showed more modest gains, with some regions outperforming others, leading to a mixed bag for global stocks.

title: ASE, Amkor, UMC and Samsung Getting a Slice of the CoWoS Market from AI Chips, Challenging TSMC
summary: The surging demand for AI chips is overwhelming TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity, leading chipmakers like Nvidia to diversify their supply chains. This shift creates opportunities for other foundries and OSATs such as UMC, ASE (including SPIL), and Samsung, which are developing their own advanced packaging solutions to meet the growing market needs. Samsung, with its turnkey service and significant R&D investments in 2.5D packaging, is actively pursuing a larger share of the AI chip market.

title: Nvidia AI Tech Ramps Up Carbon Capture & Storage Predictions 700,000x
summary: Nvidia has introduced an AI-powered approach to carbon capture and storage (CCS) modeling that accelerates predictions by 700,000 times, significantly reducing the time and cost associated with traditional simulators. Utilizing Fourier Neural Operators (FNO) architecture, this technology allows scientists and engineers to rapidly identify optimal carbon injection sites, pressures, and rates. This innovation is crucial for scaling up CCS technologies and helping industries meet net-zero targets by making carbon sequestration processes more efficient and reliable.

title: 4 questions for Danny Shapiro, Nvidia's head of automotive marketing
summary: This article features an interview with Danny Shapiro, Nvidia's vice president of automotive, discussing how the company became a significant player in automotive technology. It highlights Nvidia's role in the automotive industry and mentions related articles about AI and tech funding in the Bay Area.

title: FIAX ETF Stock Price History
summary: This article provides comprehensive historical stock price data for the Nicholas Fixed Income Alternative ETF (FIAX), including daily closing, opening, high, low, volume, and percentage change. It also offers key statistics over selected date ranges and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF's performance, such as its 52-week range and daily trading fluctuations. The data is presented in a table format covering February 23, 2026, to March 20, 2026, highlighting recent price movements.

title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DFAC)
summary: This article provides a review and aggregate rating for the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC). Based on 801 analyst ratings across 25 companies (34% of the portfolio), DFAC has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $40.95. The report details the consensus ratings for individual holdings within the ETF, including top components like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. – More market share gains expected
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is expected to gain further market share in the data center CPU market and capitalize on opportunities to expand its presence in the data center GPU market due to increased generative AI adoption. The company's margins are projected to grow post-acquisition of Xilinx and Pensando, driven by increasing revenue contributions from its Data Centre and Embedded segments. Phillip Securities Research initiates coverage with an Accumulate recommendation and a target price of US$125.00, anticipating marginal revenue growth in FY23e and re-acceleration in FY24e.

title: Semiconductor Stocks Are Hot -- So Is Their Long-Term Outlook
summary: Semiconductor stocks and related ETFs, like the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH), have experienced significant growth this year, driven largely by enthusiasm for artificial intelligence, particularly due to companies like Nvidia. While concerns about valuation exist, analysts believe the long-term outlook remains attractive, bolstered by a rebounding PC and smartphone market, and the massive potential of AI development across industries.

title: HIVE Rebrands, Announces Expansion Into Cloud Computing Services
summary: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd., formerly HIVE Blockchain, has rebranded and announced its expansion into cloud computing services, utilizing its 38,000 Nvidia GPUs. The company plans to launch "HIVE Cloud" to offer rental services for GPU server clusters, aiming to tap into the high demand for AI-driven computational power. While expanding into a potentially high-margin business, HIVE will also continue its existing bitcoin mining operations.

title: NVIDIA Drives Performance Gap Between AI Funds
summary: The article analyzes the performance gap between two major AI-focused ETFs, Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF (BOTZ) and Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF (ROBO). It highlights how BOTZ's significant outperformance is largely attributed to its heavy weighting in NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA), which has seen exceptional growth. The comparison provides insights for investors considering different approaches to AI sector investments, acknowledging NVIDIA's dominance while also pointing out the risks associated with such a concentrated bet.

title: Aehr Reports Record Revenue and Profit for Fiscal 2023 and Guides for Over 50% Increase in Revenue for Fiscal 2024 on Strength from Semiconductor Wafer Level Test and Burn-in Demand
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced record financial results for fiscal 2023, with net sales reaching $65.0 million and GAAP net income of $14.6 million, driven by demand for semiconductor wafer level test and burn-in products. The company also provided strong guidance for fiscal 2024, projecting revenue of at least $100 million, a 50% increase, and GAAP net income of at least $28 million, reflecting over 90% growth. This growth is attributed to increased adoption of their FOX systems and WaferPak Contactors for silicon carbide in electric vehicles, as well as new opportunities in silicon photonics and gallium nitride markets.

title: Fortinet’s stock nabs record closing high, while Microsoft, Nvidia, Broadcom bids all fall short
summary: On Friday, July 14, 2023, several AI-related stocks, including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Broadcom, aimed for record closing highs, but only Fortinet succeeded. Nvidia and Broadcom shares initially rose following positive analyst feedback on their AI chip market potential but ultimately closed down. This illustrates the fluctuating nature of the tech rally driven by artificial intelligence.

title: Nvidia’s stock could have a pathway to $600, Citi says
summary: Citi Research analyst Atif Malik has raised his price target for Nvidia Corp. stock to $520 from $420, citing the company's dominance in the rapidly growing artificial intelligence accelerator market. Malik also outlined a bull-case scenario where Nvidia's stock could reach $600. He anticipates blistering growth in the AI accelerator market, with Nvidia poised to outperform competitors.

title: Why the Nasdaq 100 may not capture the full growth potential of AI
summary: This article argues that while the Nasdaq-100 has seen significant gains from AI-driven tech stocks like Nvidia, it may not offer investors the most comprehensive or effective exposure to the long-term growth potential of artificial intelligence. The authors suggest that dedicated AI thematic strategies can provide broader diversification into pure-play AI companies, capture the growth of future mega-caps earlier, and improve risk-adjusted returns compared to a core allocation in the Nasdaq-100. They emphasize that while the Nasdaq-100 taps into AI through a few large companies, a targeted AI strategy offers more comprehensive exposure across the evolving AI landscape.

title: Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows
summary: Microsoft and Meta are expanding their AI partnership, bringing Meta's Llama 2 large language models to Azure and Windows. This collaboration aims to democratize AI by providing developers with open models, enabling them to build generative AI tools and experiences. Azure customers can now fine-tune and deploy Llama 2 models, while Windows developers can run Llama locally, integrating AI safety features from both companies.

title: Samsung primed for graphics chip leadership with GDDR7
summary: Samsung Electronics has unveiled the industry's first GDDR7 DRAM, which is faster and more energy-efficient than existing models. This new graphics memory product boasts a data transfer speed of 32 Gbps, making it able to process 50 ultra-high definition movies in one second, and consumes 20% less power than its predecessor. Samsung expects this technology to elevate user experiences in areas requiring outstanding graphics performance like AI, high-performance computing, and autonomous vehicles, positioning them to lead the graphics chip market.

title: Nasdaq Is Reducing the Influence of Giant Tech Firms in a Popular Stock Market Index
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a "special rebalance" of its Nasdaq 100 index to reduce the dominance of giant tech firms like Apple and Microsoft. This move addresses the historic rally of a few mega-cap tech stocks, which have caused overconcentration in the index and potentially increased diversification risk for investors. The rebalance, effective July 24, will prompt funds tracking the index to adjust their portfolios, potentially leading to trading costs and minor market volatility.

title: Startup Cerebras Takes on Nvidia With Chain of AI Supercomputers
summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. has launched the first of nine AI supercomputers, "Condor Galaxy 1," in partnership with Abu Dhabi's G42, aiming to challenge Nvidia's dominance in AI technology. The supercomputer, located in Santa Clara, California, is valued at over $100 million and will expand, with further systems planned for Austin, Asheville, and international locations. This initiative provides an alternative for AI workloads.

title: Applied Materials: Excessive AI Hype Drives Semiconductor Stock Overvaluation
summary: This article argues that the semiconductor industry, including Applied Materials (AMAT), is currently overvalued due to excessive AI hype, despite significant sales declines from major chipmakers. While AI will eventually benefit AMAT, the necessary capital investments may delay FCF boosts until the 2030s, and AI-related demand growth may be offset by falling demand in other sectors. The author believes AMAT's stock could lose over half its value as investors re-evaluate its immediate fundamentals.

title: Nvidia is ‘dominating’ and could unlock $300 billion in AI revenue by 2027, analyst says
summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh is increasingly optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, projecting the company could tap into $300 billion in AI-related revenue by 2027. Rakesh believes Nvidia will maintain a significant share in the AI server market despite potential future competition. This outlook comes as Nvidia's stock has already seen a 200% increase this year.

title: NVIDIA Names Melissa Lora to Board of Directors
summary: NVIDIA announced the appointment of Melissa Lora, former president of Taco Bell International, to its board of directors and Audit Committee. CEO Jensen Huang highlighted her extensive senior management, operating experience, and finance expertise as immense benefits to the board. Lora's appointment expands NVIDIA's board to 14 members.

title: New YieldMax ETF Seeks Synthetic Covered Call Strategy on Amazon
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) on the NYSE, which utilizes a synthetic covered call strategy on Amazon (AMZN) to generate monthly income. This actively managed fund, advised by Tidal Financial Group and subadvised by ZEGA Financial, aims for compelling yields with capped participation in Amazon’s gains without directly investing in AMZN. AMZY follows a similar model to the previously launched YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NVDY), offering investors enhanced income through options.

title: Micron Delivers Industry's Fastest, Highest-Capacity HBM to Advance Generative AI Innovation
summary: Micron Technology announced it has begun sampling the industry's first 8-high 24 GB HBM3 Gen2 memory, boasting bandwidth greater than 1.2 TB/s and pin speed over 9.2 Gb/s, a significant improvement over existing HBM3 solutions. This new memory offers a 2.5 times performance per watt improvement, setting new benchmarks for AI data center metrics and reducing training times for large language models like GPT-4. Micron's 12-high stack with 36 GB capacity is expected to sample in early 2024, further enhancing capacity and efficiency for generative AI and HPC applications.

title: ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott: Generative AI Could Boost Global GDP By Almost $7 Trillion
summary: ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott believes generative AI could boost global GDP by nearly $7 trillion, seeing it as a transformative moment comparable to the internet or iPhone. ServiceNow is integrating generative AI into all its workflow offerings and is partnering with Nvidia to accelerate customer value realization from AI. The company also reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations, driven by its role in digital transformation.

title: No Better Time Than Now to Work in Biotech, Amgen CEO Tells Summer Interns
summary: Amgen CEO Bob Bradway addressed the company's summer interns, emphasizing the exciting opportunities in biotechnology due to advancements in science, technology, and AI. He highlighted Amgen's mission of serving patients as its core strength and encouraged interns to make an impact, challenge the status quo, and leverage their unique talents in their careers. Interns praised Amgen's culture and leadership's commitment to mentorship and patient-centricity.

title: One in ten retail investors using Chat GPT-style AI to help pick and manage investments
summary: A study by eToro reveals that 11% of retail investors are currently using AI tools like ChatGPT for investment decisions, with this figure rising to 19% among 34-44-year-olds. An additional 35% are open to using AI, citing benefits like time savings and improved decision-making, while 40% remain against it. The findings also indicate strong retail investor confidence in the technology sector, with 30% planning to increase investment in tech for the rest of 2023, specifically highlighting AI.

title: U.S. chipmaker AMD to invest $400 million in India by 2028
summary: U.S. chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD.O) announced a significant investment of $400 million in India over the next five years, including the establishment of its largest design center in Bengaluru, creating 3,000 new engineering jobs. This move aligns with India's efforts to become a chipmaking hub, attracting other major players like Applied Materials and Micron with its incentive programs. AMD's expansion underscores the country's growing importance in the global semiconductor industry.

title: Pixar, Apple and Nvidia are teaming up to promote a 3D file format that could help boost Apple's upcoming Vision Pro
summary: Pixar, Apple, Nvidia, Adobe, and Autodesk are collaborating to promote OpenUSD, a 3D file format. This initiative aims to streamline the creation of 3D content, potentially benefiting game development based on Pixar movies and boosting Apple's upcoming Vision Pro mixed-reality headset by making 3D environments more accessible.

title: Hesai Technology Advances Autonomous Vehicle Lidar Integration with NVIDIA DRIVE and Omniverse
summary: Hesai Technology has announced a collaboration with NVIDIA to integrate its lidar sensors into the NVIDIA DRIVE and NVIDIA Omniverse ecosystems. This partnership aims to enhance autonomous driving development by providing developers with tools to seamlessly integrate Hesai's lidar sensors, such as the AT128, into their systems and utilize NVIDIA's simulation platforms for robust testing. The collaboration is expected to accelerate the deployment of safe and efficient autonomous driving solutions.

title: Beating the benchmark? How AI-driven ETFs stack up
summary: The article discusses the performance of AI-driven ETFs and whether they offer an information advantage in stock selection compared to traditional benchmarks. It highlights examples like the QRAFT AI-Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Momentum ETF (AMOM) and the AI Powered Equity ETF (AIEQ), noting AMOM's outperformance against a momentum benchmark while AIEQ trails the S&P 500. The piece also explores the potential benefits of AI in portfolio management, such as a lack of emotion and more frequent rebalancing.

title: Qualcomm's fiscal 3Q results fall short as slowing smartphone demand takes its toll
summary: Qualcomm's fiscal third-quarter results missed sales expectations and guidance due to softening smartphone demand, causing its stock to dip. The company, a major processor supplier for Android devices, reported adjusted earnings above Street expectations but significantly down year-over-year. CEO Cristiano Amon is banking on Qualcomm's artificial intelligence strategy to drive future growth.

title: CoreWeave Secures $2.3 Billion Debt Financing Facility led by Magnetar Capital and Blackstone to Meet Surging Demand and Ongoing Expansion of Specialized Cloud Infrastructure to Power AI
summary: CoreWeave has secured a $2.3 billion debt financing facility led by Magnetar Capital and Blackstone to fuel its expansion of specialized cloud infrastructure for AI. This funding will enable CoreWeave to purchase more compute resources, open new data centers, and grow its staff to meet the surging demand for large-scale GPU-accelerated workloads in the generative AI space. The company plans to have 14 data centers by the end of 2023 and recently unveiled the world's fastest AI supercomputer.

title: Gen Digital earnings beat, revenue was in line with estimates
summary: Gen Digital (NASDAQ: GEN) reported its first-quarter earnings, with an EPS of $0.47, surpassing analyst estimates of $0.46. The company's revenue for the quarter was $946 million, matching the consensus estimate. Gen Digital's stock has seen an 8.57% increase in the last three months, though it is down 30.00% over the last 12 months.

title: AMD's stock breaks with chip selloff to finish week higher as analyst cites 'important buying opportunity'
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares unexpectedly rose last week, up 2.6%, despite a broader chip sector selloff that saw Nvidia and the PHLX Semiconductor Index decline significantly. An analyst pointed to this performance as an "important buying opportunity" for AMD. Nvidia's earnings report is anticipated on August 23rd.

title: AI as an investment theme
summary: The article discusses Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a significant investment theme due to its potential to revolutionize industries and create new opportunities. It highlights the booming AI sector, strong investor interest, and the impact of AI on technology stocks and the broader market. The author also touches upon various ways to invest in AI, including specific ETFs, and emphasizes the importance of careful research despite the vast potential.

title: Nvidia teams with Accenture and ServiceNow for AI program
summary: Nvidia, Accenture, and ServiceNow have partnered to launch "AI Lighthouse," a program aimed at helping ServiceNow customers quickly integrate generative AI tools. The initiative combines ServiceNow's automation platform, Nvidia's AI supercomputing and software resources, and Accenture's consulting expertise to develop and deploy custom generative AI large language models and applications. The program focuses on enhancing customer service, promoting self-service options, automating content generation, and boosting developer productivity.

title: 3 AI Stocks You’ll Regret Not Buying Soon
summary: This article highlights three AI stocks that show significant potential in the medical industry: Lantern Pharma (LTRN), Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX), and AbCellera Biologics (ABCL). These companies are leveraging AI to accelerate drug discovery, improve treatment development, and form strategic partnerships, addressing the high costs and failure rates of traditional drug development. Investors are encouraged to consider these stocks due to their innovative approaches and recent positive developments.

title: The Case For Intel: An Illustration Of The ‘Value’ Investing Mindset
summary: This article argues for Intel as a "value investment" due to its current undervaluation despite strong long-term prospects. It delves into strategic market trends, Intel's strong industry position, favorable political and geopolitical factors, and a revived company culture under CEO Pat Gelsinger. The author emphasizes that true "value investing" focuses on a company's ability to correct its problems and adapt to strategic environmental shifts over a multi-year horizon, rather than short-term financial details.

title: Opinion: Why Nvidia investors are rattled by Supermicro’s AI chip supply constraints
summary: Nvidia investors are concerned by AI chip supply constraints reported by Supermicro, a Silicon Valley computer and server maker. Supermicro's executives stated they need more AI chips from Nvidia, confirming existing supply limitations for Nvidia's popular graphics chips. This suggests that the high demand for AI applications may face hardware availability issues.

title: 3 Growth ETFs To Avoid in 2023
summary: This article identifies three growth ETFs—Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), and AI Powered Equity ETF (AIEQ)—that investors should consider avoiding in 2023. The author, Bryan Armour, CFA, discusses concerns such as high concentration, performance chasing, high turnover, and valuation issues for these funds, despite growth stocks performing well year-to-date. The piece advises investors to focus on long-term strategies rather than chasing past performance in a market with stretched valuations and continued uncertainty.

title: SK Telecom to invest $100M in AI company Anthropic
summary: SK Telecom announced a $100 million investment in U.S. AI company Anthropic to expand its presence in the AI sector and collaborate on developing an AI platform and large language model. This partnership is expected to accelerate the development of the global Telco AI Platform, aligning with SK Telecom's broader strategy to foster an AI ecosystem with international telecommunications companies. Anthropic's chief scientist will lead the development of a new LLM supporting multiple languages, including Korean, English, German, and Japanese.

title: 4 AI-powered ETFs: Pros and cons of AI stockpicking funds
summary: This article explores AI-powered Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs), discussing how they use machine learning to select stocks and the potential benefits and drawbacks. It details four specific AI-driven ETFs, highlighting their assets under management and expense ratios. The piece emphasizes that while AI stock-picking is thrilling, it's still in its early stages and investors should consider their needs and costs.

title: Kneron unveils lower-cost, energy-efficient AI chip
summary: Kneron Inc, an AI chip start-up, has introduced its new KL730 AI chip as a more cost-effective and energy-efficient alternative to Nvidia's GPUs for generative AI solutions. The KL730, which uses TSMC's 12-nanometer process, is designed to reduce operational costs significantly and is 150-200% more energy-efficient than competitors' products. Kneron expects autonomous vehicles and servers to be major applications for its NPU technology, with customer sampling for the KL730 beginning by year-end.

title: Hesai Reports Record Lidar Sales, Revenue
summary: Hesai Technology (NASDAQ: HSAI) reported record-breaking unaudited financial results for Q2 2023, with quarterly revenue reaching RMB440.3 million ($60.7 million), a 108.5% YoY increase. The company shipped 52,106 lidar units, a 946.5% YoY increase, significantly driven by ADAS lidar growth. Hesai also solidified its position as the No. 1 global automotive lidar supplier, secured new design wins with major OEMs, and deepened its collaboration with NVIDIA.

title: LX International liquidates Yantai LCD component warehouse in China
summary: LX International has liquidated its Yantai LCD component warehouse in China due to decreased demand for LCD panels, particularly from key customer LG Display. This move follows LG Display's reduction in LCD module production at its Yantai plant as the market shifts from LCD to OLED technology for small panels. The liquidation reflects the evolving display market where OLED is expected to significantly increase its share in IT devices.

title: Congress Large Cap Growth ETF Holdings
summary: The Congress Large Cap Growth ETF (CAML), established on August 22, 2023, is an actively managed fund investing in large-cap companies for long-term capital growth. With $333.49 million in assets under management and 41 holdings, its top investments include NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet. The ETF focuses on companies anticipated to experience earnings growth.

title: TSMC’s CoWoS Dominance: Amkor, ASE, JCET’s Response
summary: The article discusses how the increasing demand for high-performance computing, AI, and 5G has led to a shift towards advanced packaging techniques like 2.5D and 3D, exemplified by TSMC's CoWoS technology. Despite TSMC's significant role, traditional assembly and test firms such as ASE, Amkor, and JCET are actively developing their own advanced packaging solutions to remain competitive. These firms are securing partial orders and maintaining market share by catering to products not in the highest-end category, showcasing their continued relevance alongside wafer foundries.

title: GraniteShares' Single Stock ETFs Will Follow Nvidia, Tesla, AMD
summary: GraniteShares has launched four new single-stock ETFs to capitalize on the trading momentum of Nvidia, Tesla, and Advanced Micro Devices. These new ETFs offer unique leveraged and short exposures, with the GraniteShares 1.5x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD) launching just before Nvidia's earnings report. The asset manager plans to roll out almost three dozen more such filings, emphasizing being first to market with these trading-focused products.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Second Quarter Fiscal 2024
summary: NVIDIA reported record revenue of $13.51 billion for the second quarter of fiscal 2024, a 101% increase from a year ago, driven by strong growth in Data Center revenue at $10.32 billion. The company also announced a new $25.00 billion share repurchase authorization and provided a positive outlook for the third quarter, expecting revenue of $16.00 billion. The CEO highlighted the ongoing transition to accelerated computing and generative AI as key drivers for NVIDIA's success.

title: Technical Analysis of GraniteShares 2x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NASDAQ:NVD)
summary: This article provides a technical analysis of the GraniteShares 2x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD) using various indicators. It summarizes signals from moving averages, oscillators, and pivots across different timeframes, offering insights into potential buy, sell, or neutral trends. The analysis also includes an FAQ section explaining key technical analysis concepts and their application to NVD.

title: Bullish on Nvidia? This single-stock ETF could offer 1.5 times leverage on the popular chip stock — but be careful what you wish for.
summary: This article discusses single-stock ETFs, focusing on those linked to Nvidia following its strong earnings report. It highlights how these ETFs offer leveraged exposure to the chipmaker, which is a key player in the AI industry, while also cautioning potential investors about the inherent risks.

title: AMD debuts two new graphics cards for the consumer market
summary: AMD has introduced two new graphics cards, the Radeon RX 7800 XT and RX 7700 XT, to compete with Nvidia in the consumer chip market. These cards, priced at $499 and $449 respectively, are positioned as more affordable alternatives to high-end GPUs while offering significant performance per dollar. Based on AMD's latest RDNA 3 architecture, they include features like upgraded machine learning accelerators, ray tracing optimization, and FSR 3 technology for improved gaming visuals.

title: Nvidia Stock Is a Top Holding in These Tech ETFs
summary: Nvidia Corporation (NVDA) is a top holding in many tech, AI, and semiconductor ETFs due to its crucial role in manufacturing GPUs that power AI applications. Despite potential challenges like competition and geopolitical factors, Nvidia's innovative technology and market dominance make it a key component for investors seeking exposure to the rapidly growing AI market. ETFs like the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) and the Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF (SHOC) have significant allocations to NVDA, reflecting its importance in the sector.

title: Hedge fund exposure to 7 biggest tech stocks at record high, Goldman Sachs says
summary: Hedge funds have reached record exposure to the seven largest tech stocks by market capitalization, according to Goldman Sachs data, driven by strong performance and the artificial intelligence theme. These seven stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, now constitute about 20% of the total net market value held by hedge funds tracked by Goldman Sachs. Despite the concentration risk, investment managers rationalize these allocations due to the significant returns generated by these companies this year.

title: Institute for National Defense and Security Research-Newsletter
summary: This article analyzes the impact of the Matsu undersea cable interruption on Taiwan's national defense and security, emphasizing the critical role of submarine cables for communication and military operations. It highlights how the CCP's grey zone tactics, including cable damage, undermine Taiwan's cyber resilience and calls for establishing comprehensive backup mechanisms, strengthening network infrastructure, and developing low-orbit satellites, drawing lessons from the Russo-Ukrainian War, to enhance Taiwan's communication system. The author stresses the need for integration between military and civilian technological capabilities and international cooperation to secure national communication.

title: How space traffic in orbit could spell trouble on Earth
summary: The increasing number of satellites in Earth's orbit, particularly large constellations like Starlink, significantly raises the risk of catastrophic collisions and the generation of more space debris. Such collisions could disrupt vital services on Earth, including GPS, communication, weather forecasting, and intelligence gathering. The current patchwork of international regulations is insufficient to manage this growing traffic, highlighting the urgent need for a formal, coordinated global system to prevent disasters and maintain the functionality of orbital infrastructure.

title: VinFast market cap reaches $191B, comparable to Tesla’s valuation in 2020
summary: Vietnamese EV maker VinFast (NASDAQ:VFS) achieved a market capitalization of $190 billion, making it the third most valuable carmaker globally, a valuation Tesla reached in 2020. This milestone is notable given VinFast's relatively low sales of 24,000 cars in 2022 compared to Tesla's established leadership when it hit a similar valuation. The high market cap is partly attributed to a limited float, with founder Pham Nhat Vuong controlling 99% of shares, which causes significant stock volatility.

title: Why Is Ambarella (AMBA) Stock Down 20% Today?
summary: Ambarella (AMBA) stock dropped nearly 20% after the company reported a disappointing quarter and cut its sales outlook. The chipmaker, which specializes in AI vision processors for applications like police body cameras and self-driving cars, cited aggressive inventory cutting by customers and weak end-market demand. Despite the CEO's anticipation of a return to growth next fiscal year, the company remains unprofitable and its market capitalization is significantly higher than its annual sales.

title: Cathie Wood Just Sold Nvidia -- and Bought These 5 Potentially Explosive Growth Stocks Again
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest firm sold 3,952 shares of Nvidia from its Ark Genomic Revolution ETF for approximately $1.9 million despite Nvidia's strong Q2 earnings. Concurrent with this sale, Ark Invest purchased shares in five biotech growth stocks: Intellia Therapeutics, Invitae, Accolade, CRISPR Therapeutics, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals, signaling a strategic shift towards the biotech sector for potential explosive growth. Wood has been increasing her firm's biotech holdings in both the Genomic Revolution ETF and the flagship Ark Innovation ETF, suggesting a belief that biotech offers the best return potential for growth investors currently.

title: Meet Five Generative AI Innovators in Africa and the Middle East
summary: This article highlights five generative AI innovators across Africa and the Middle East who are customizing Large Language Models (LLMs) for their respective markets. It features startups like MazzumaGPT in Ghana, which helps developers draft smart contracts, and MetaDialog in Dubai, which built the first LLM to support both Arabic and English. Beyond Limits in Abu Dhabi is also noted for training and fine-tuning LLMs for corporations, with all three companies benefiting from NVIDIA's Inception program.

title: AI In Medical Devices Global Market Report 2023: Unveiling
summary: The "AI In Medical Devices Global Market Report 2023" indicates significant growth in the AI in medical devices market, projected to reach $66.35 billion by 2027 from $10.64 billion in 2022. Key drivers include technological advancements like Nvidia's Clara Holoscan MGX platform and increasing demand for AI-integrated wearables for health monitoring. Major players are also expanding through acquisitions to enhance their AI capabilities in healthcare.

title: XOMO - YieldMax XOM Option Income Strategy ETF Stock Price and Quote
summary: This article provides a detailed quote and financial data for the XOMO - YieldMax XOM Option Income Strategy ETF. It includes current stock price, performance metrics, dividend information, fund characteristics, and recent news announcements related to YieldMax ETFs. The fund aims for current income and indirect exposure to Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) through a synthetic covered call strategy.

title: Coherent to set up centre of excellence at IIT-Madras Research Park
