summary: American firm Coherent has signed a tripartite memorandum of understanding with Guidance Tamil Nadu and IIT-Madras Research Park (IITMRP) to establish its first global Centre of Excellence (CoE) in India on the IITMRP campus. This CoE will focus on R&D for lasers, optical networking components, EV batteries, and compound semiconductor devices. The company plans to hire technical talent and start with an applications engineering lab for laser materials processing, leveraging Tamil Nadu's innovation ecosystem and engineering talent.

title: Qualcomm CEO says AI may breathe new life into smartphones: 'It could create a new upgrade cycle'
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon believes artificial intelligence could spark a new upgrade cycle for smartphones, revitalizing a market that saw a significant sales decline in 2022. Amon indicated that the company's upcoming Snapdragon Summit in October would showcase new AI-driven use cases from smartphone manufacturers. Unlike Nvidia's focus on data centers, Qualcomm aims to bring AI capabilities directly to devices, enhancing user experience and driving demand for new models.

title: SOXQ ETF: Huawei's Latest Phone And Implication For Semis Stocks
summary: Huawei has launched the Mate 60, a new 5G smartphone, signaling its recovery from past sanctions and strengthening China's semiconductor industry. This development poses competitive risks for holdings within the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ). Given these implications for the semiconductor market, the author maintains a "Hold" position on SOXQ.

title: Nvidia Says It Will Invest In Arm’s IPO After Failed Acquisition
summary: Nvidia has confirmed its intention to invest in Arm's initial public offering, despite its failed acquisition attempt of the company. The GPU giant was named as a potential "cornerstone investor" in Arm's updated IPO filing, alongside other tech companies like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Google. This investment highlights the continued importance of Arm's architecture to Nvidia's products and the broader tech ecosystem.

title: Seagate And Western Digital: Benefiting From AI And Generative AI Storage Demand (STX)
summary: The article discusses how Seagate and Western Digital are poised to benefit from the increasing demand for data storage driven by Artificial Intelligence (AI) and generative AI. It highlights that AI applications require extensive memory and storage, positioning Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) from these companies to dominate data centers for the next decade. Advanced technologies like HAMR from Seagate and MAMR from Western Digital are expected to keep HDDs competitive against SSDs through 2030.

title: Building safe, secure and trustworthy AI: Adobe’s commitments to our customers and community
summary: Adobe outlines its commitment to safe, secure, and trustworthy AI development, aligning with the White House Voluntary AI Commitments. The company emphasizes rigorous testing, security measures, and the development of Content Credentials to enhance transparency and combat misinformation in AI-generated content. Furthermore, Adobe pledges to empower creators by enabling "Do Not Train" tags and advocating for federal anti-impersonation rights to protect artists in the AI era.

title: AIoT Platforms Are on the Rise, But What Are They?
summary: This article explains the concept of AIoT (Artificial Intelligence of Things) platforms, which integrate AI programming into IoT devices to enhance functionality and efficiency. It details real-world applications across smart homes, edge computing, healthcare, and smart cities, highlighting the projected rapid growth of the AIoT market. The piece emphasizes the transformative potential of AIoT for industries, resource optimization, and decision-making.

title: Chipmaker Arm is valued at $55 billion after a blockbuster IPO that is this year's biggest by far
summary: Arm Holdings successfully priced its IPO at the top of its range, raising $4.87 billion and achieving a valuation of approximately $54.5 billion. This marks the largest listing of the year globally and is expected to invigorate the equity markets and a pipeline of other tech IPOs. SoftBank will retain about 90% ownership after the offering, which saw significant investment from major tech companies like Intel, Apple, and Nvidia.

title: Check out Direxion Daily Nvda Bull 2X Shares's stock price (NVDU) in real time
summary: This article provides real-time stock price information for Direxion Daily Nvda Bull 2X Shares (NVDU), including current trading data, key statistics, and historical performance. It details the stock's last price, volume, 52-week range, and dividend information. The page also offers a summary of events, but notes there is no recent news for the security.

title: Arm climbs 25% in Nasdaq debut after pricing IPO at $51 a share
summary: Arm Holdings saw its stock jump nearly 25% on its first day of trading on the Nasdaq after pricing its IPO at $51 per share, valuing the company at almost $60 billion. SoftBank, which took Arm private in 2016, retains about 90% of the company's outstanding shares. Despite its high valuation relative to its current growth, Arm's unique royalty-based business model, including revenue from decades-old designs, and its strategic importance in AI and chip design, are key factors in investor interest.

title: Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS), including its current stock price, key statistics like PE Ratio and Dividend Yield, and its top holdings as of August 31, 2025. The ETF has a portfolio value of $8.44 billion with top holdings including NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. It also presents historical performance data and breakdowns of asset allocation and sector exposure.

title: Chip Stocks Drop After Report Says TSMC Is Worried About Demand
summary: Chip stocks dropped following a report that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), the world's largest third-party semiconductor manufacturer, has expressed concerns about slowing chip demand. TSMC reportedly told its suppliers to delay some deliveries, indicating potential weakness in the broader chip market. This news directly impacted investor sentiment towards the technology sector.

title: JPMorgan Global Select Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:JGLO) $JGLO
summary: This article provides details on the JPMorgan Global Select Equity ETF (JGLO), including its current price, holdings, and recent news. The ETF aims for long-term capital appreciation by investing in global equities across various market capitalizations, integrating ESG factors. Key statistics, expense ratios, major shareholders, and top holdings like NVIDIA and Microsoft are highlighted.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF
summary: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) offers investors global equity exposure to large and mid-capitalization developed and emerging markets. It tracks the MSCI All Country World Index, providing diversification across numerous countries and sectors efficiently. The ETF is marketed as a low-cost investment vehicle with potential for long-term growth, though it acknowledges inherent investment risks.

title: Intel pitches the 'AI PC' at software developer event
summary: Intel announced a new "Meteor Lake" chip, expected in December, that will enable generative AI chatbots to run directly on laptops without needing cloud data centers. This development, showcased at a software developer conference, aims to offer businesses and consumers greater privacy and faster AI responses for ChatGPT-style technologies. Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger positioned this as a "sea change moment" for tech innovation, with the company also announcing plans for a successor chip and partnerships in the AI space.

title: Infosys and NVIDIA expand partnership to build generative AI for businesses
summary: Infosys and NVIDIA have expanded their partnership to create a generative AI platform aimed at boosting enterprise productivity. This collaboration will leverage computer vision and NVIDIA's NeMo framework to address various industry challenges, including retail inventory management and customer service, while integrating Infosys’ Responsible AI Toolkit to mitigate risks. Infosys also plans to establish a NVIDIA Centre of Excellence to train 50,000 staff in generative AI technologies.

title: AMDY Stock Price | YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF (AMDY), including its current stock price, performance metrics, key statistics, and holdings. It also covers recent news regarding its distributions and offers technical analysis insights. The fund, launched in September 2023, aims to generate income through options strategies on AMD stock and invests in short-term U.S. treasury securities.

title: Silicon Hegemon: Could China Take Over Taiwan’s Semiconductor Industry Without Invading?
summary: This article explores the high-impact, low-probability scenario of China taking control of Taiwan's semiconductor industry, particularly TSMC, without a full-scale military invasion. It outlines hypothetical coercion tactics short of war, such as blockades and economic pressure, that could force Taiwan into ceding control. The authors propose military, regulatory, and policy responses for Taiwan and its allies to prevent such an outcome, emphasizing deterrence and preparedness to defend free passage to and from Taiwan.

title: Supermicro At 30: From Designing AI Chips To Selling AI Systems
summary: Supermicro, founded 30 years ago, began with Charles Liang's vision to improve server motherboards and power supplies after working on an AI processor design. The company has grown into a $10 billion systems business with global manufacturing facilities and aims for $20 billion in the next three years, continuously expanding its operations and exploring new factory locations.

title: Nvidia's French offices raided in cloud-computing competition inquiry, Wall Street Journal reports
summary: French competition authorities raided Nvidia's offices on suspicion of anticompetitive practices related to cloud computing and graphics cards. The investigation, part of a broader inquiry into potential market dominance in cloud computing, follows Nvidia's significant growth partly due to rising demand for its AI chips. This action aligns with France's recent aggressive stance against large tech companies.

title: CHPS ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NEO:CHPS
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the CHPS ETF, Global X Artificial Intelligence Semiconductor Index ETF Class A. It covers key statistics such as Assets Under Management, expense ratio, dividend yield, and issuer information. The article also breaks down the fund's holdings by sector and region, discusses its performance, and answers frequently asked questions regarding its investments, dividends, and AUM.

title: Palantir: Implications Of The $28B Mega Cisco-Splunk Deal (NYSE:PLTR)
summary: Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk underscores the increasing value of AI capabilities in the industry, raising questions about potential takeover targets. Palantir Technologies, with its aggressive focus on AI and growth in Business Intelligence, is identified as a possible acquisition candidate. This deal highlights the broader trend of large corporations seeking to acquire advanced AI capabilities for long-term revenue growth.

title: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article analyzes NVIDIA (NVDA) using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation model to determine if the stock is undervalued. Based on the DCF analysis, NVIDIA's stock, with a DCF value of $75.03 per share against a market price of $440.41, is considered significantly overvalued, showing a negative margin of safety. The author emphasizes that this is a simplified analysis and advises readers to conduct their own thorough research before making investment decisions.

title: Tech spared as bond selloff buffets stocks
summary: Treasury yields surged to their highest levels in over a decade as traders anticipated prolonged high borrowing costs from the Federal Reserve, impacting most US stocks. However, the Nasdaq 100, driven by major technology firms like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, saw gains. This market movement occurred despite a last-minute deal to avert a government shutdown, highlighting the market's primary focus on interest rate policy.

title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: The REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund launched on October 9, 2023. It aims to mirror the returns of the Solactive FANG Innovation Index while generating monthly income by investing in index securities and writing call options. The fund currently has $584.04 million in assets under management and a dividend yield of 27.49%.

title: NVIDIA Omniverse is Changing Filmmaking and Exxact is Helping It Happen
summary: The article highlights how NVIDIA Omniverse and Exxact Corporation are revolutionizing filmmaking, especially in animation and VFX, by enabling real-time virtual production. It showcases how companies like Sony Pictures Animation, disguise, and Versatile Media are using Omniverse to improve workflows, accelerate production, and facilitate remote collaboration. Exxact Corporation provides integrated, high-performance computing solutions to help these companies adopt Omniverse Enterprise.

title: IDC report: AI and DL storage infrastructure
summary: This article highlights an IDC report on AI and deep learning (DL) storage infrastructure, emphasizing the growing need for enterprise-class storage solutions to support AI applications. The report discusses key considerations for AI storage training infrastructure, enterprise deployment models, and why scaling beyond NFS is crucial. It also positions NetApp as a strong contender for AI-driven workloads due to its performance, scalability, and partnerships with hyperscalers.

title: Amkor's Largest Global Semiconductor Factory Opens in Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park developed by Viglacera
summary: Amkor Technology Vietnam celebrated the grand opening of its largest global semiconductor factory in the Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park, developed by Viglacera Corporation. This advanced factory, with a $1.6 billion investment, will serve leading electronics companies and is expected to boost Vietnam's semiconductor industry. Viglacera, with over 20 years of experience in industrial real estate, continues to attract significant foreign direct investment, especially in high-tech sectors, and plans further expansion of its industrial park network.

title: Supermicro Accelerates Everything at ISC23
summary: Supermicro showcased its latest High-Performance Computing (HPC) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions at ISC23 in Hamburg, Germany, emphasizing its leadership in AI infrastructure. Key highlights included liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX H100 GPU servers, an AI development platform with NVIDIA, and an 8U PVC system featuring Intel Data Series GPUs. The company also presented high-performance storage solutions optimized for AI workloads, demonstrating its commitment to accelerating AI adoption.

title: Beamr Issues Q3-2023 CEO Letter to Shareholders
summary: Beamr's CEO Sharon Carmel issued a letter to shareholders detailing Q3 2023 activities and progress, highlighting reaching $1.4M in revenues from Q1-Q3 2023 and $5.4M in cash and cash equivalents. The company also announced signing another top-five media company as a client and is on track to launch its new Beamr SaaS in Q1 2024, expecting revenue ramp-up in 2024. Collaboration with Nvidia continues, and Beamr is expanding sales and marketing capabilities in preparation for the SaaS launch.

title: BigBear.ai (BBAI) Enhances Military Planning Through AFRL
summary: BigBear.ai (BBAI) has expanded its commitment to the Air Force Research Laboratory’s (AFRL) Composable Collaborative Planning BAA, significantly advancing military planning processes by delivering resource-informed plans in under a month. The company's AI-driven solutions are gaining prominence, with partnerships like Autodesk integration and substantial contracts, demonstrating its impact in military technology. BigBear.ai's stock has outperformed the Zacks Computer and Technology sector, reflecting strong growth and promising future revenue expectations.

title: BlackLine (BL) Strengthens Portfolio With Microsoft Teams Integration
summary: BlackLine (BL) has integrated Microsoft Teams and introduced enhanced capabilities for its Microsoft Dynamics 365 Connector to streamline finance and accounting processes. This partnership offers seamless compatibility with Microsoft ERPs, providing real-time notifications and improved data access. BlackLine also highlighted its robust portfolio, including recent acquisitions, AI-enabled solutions, and strong customer growth, expecting significant revenue for fiscal 2023.

title: Supermicro Starts Shipments of NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip-Based Servers, the Industry's First Family of NVIDIA MGX Systems
summary: Supermicro announces the shipment of new GPU systems based on the NVIDIA reference architecture, featuring NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper and NVIDIA Grace CPU Superchip. These systems, designed with a modular architecture for AI infrastructure, are the industry's first family of NVIDIA MGX Systems. Supermicro highlights their advanced liquid-cooling technology and ability to deliver thousands of rack-scale AI servers per month, accelerating time to market for AI-enabled applications.

title: REX Shares Partners with Tuttle Capital Management to Launch T-REX Single-Stock ETFs
summary: REX Shares, in partnership with Tuttle Capital Management, has launched T-REX Single-Stock ETFs, offering targeted 200% and -200% daily exposure to Tesla and NVIDIA. This new suite of ETFs is designed for sophisticated traders seeking to enhance potential returns through pinpoint positioning in highly traded stocks. The launch follows REX Shares' recent introduction of the REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF, which focuses on generating income from top technology stocks using a covered call strategy.

title: Experience real-time quotes, in-depth charts, and analyst ratings
summary: This page provides stock quotes and detailed information for the NVDX T-REX 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF, including its current trading performance, historical data, and recent weekly reports. It also outlines Webull's trading platforms, advisory services, and important disclosures regarding investment risks and account protections.

title: Cathie Wood explains how the artificial intelligence boom could propel Ark's Innovation ETF
summary: Cathie Wood believes the artificial intelligence boom could significantly boost Ark Invest's flagship Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK). While the fund has gained 17% this year, Wood emphasized that AI represents a reality, not just a dream, and that its disruptive nature will create new industry leaders. She cautioned investors to be discerning with their AI bets, highlighting Tesla as a key holding while expressing reservations about some other "Magnificent Seven" stocks.

title: Bitcoin Miner Bit Digital Diversifies Into AI for 'Substantially Higher Margin' Than Mining
summary: Bitcoin miner Bit Digital (BTBT) is diversifying its revenue streams by expanding into the artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure business, anticipating "substantially higher margins" than its traditional bitcoin mining and ether staking operations. The company signed a deal with an AI firm to provide cloud-based GPU resources, expected to generate over $250 million in additional revenue over three years, funded by a $35 million investment in GPUs. This strategic move aims to create a reliable income stream uncorrelated to bitcoin prices, helping the company weather potential downturns in the crypto market and upcoming halving events.

title: Nvidia and AMD reportedly developing Arm-based PC processors
summary: Nvidia and AMD are reportedly developing new Arm-based PC processors, aiming to enter the Windows computer market as early as 2025. This move aligns with Microsoft's efforts to foster competition and introduce more energy-efficient CPUs with advanced AI features, similar to Apple's Arm-based Macs. Qualcomm currently holds an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft for Arm-based Windows chips, which is set to expire by the end of the year, potentially opening the door for Nvidia and AMD.

title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

title: Will Super Micro Computer Be a Trillion-Dollar Stock by 2050?
summary: This article examines the potential for Super Micro Computer (Supermicro) to reach a $1 trillion valuation by 2050, driven by the booming AI market. It outlines a mathematical case for growth based on revenue projections and market valuations, acknowledging Supermicro's strong performance as an AI stock. However, the author cautions against over-optimistic long-term predictions, citing potential risks like acquisitions, new competitors, geopolitical tensions, and economic downturns, suggesting investors should instead focus on the company's more foreseeable future.

title: Google, Nvidia Back AI Startup That Helps Combat Chip Shortage
summary: CentML, an AI startup focused on improving the efficiency of machine learning systems, has raised $27 million in a seed funding round. The investment, backed by Google's Gradient Ventures, Nvidia Corp., Radical Ventures, Deloitte Ventures, and Thomson Reuters Ventures, aims to help developers optimize computing power for AI training, which could alleviate chip shortages.

title: Shutterstock Integrates AI Into Its Library of 700 Million Images
summary: Shutterstock has launched Creative AI, a new generative AI tool integrated into its vast library of over 700 million images. This tool, powered by OpenAI's Dall-E, allows users to generate, edit, and expand images, making customized content easier to produce. Shutterstock emphasizes that its AI is trained on licensed content and that contributors are compensated, addressing concerns about unauthorized AI image generation.

title: Microsoft will hold a 27% stake in OpenAI and gain access to its AI models.
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have finalized a new agreement where Microsoft will acquire a 27% stake in OpenAI, valued at $135 billion. This agreement grants Microsoft access to OpenAI's AI technology, including AGI models, until 2032. OpenAI has also committed to investing an additional $250 billion in purchasing Azure services.

title: Bargain Hunting in Semiconductor Stocks: Down 25%, Texas Instruments Is a Top Stock to Buy
summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) shares have underperformed recently due to a cyclical downturn in the semiconductor industry, while competitors like Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor have risen. Despite this, Motley Fool contributors Jason Hall and Jeff Santoro argue that the company remains a strong long-term investment. They believe that looking beyond current weaknesses reveals significant future growth potential for Texas Instruments.

title: Jidu launches AI-based robocar in China
summary: Jidu Auto, a joint venture between Geely and Baidu, has launched its first AI-powered battery electric vehicle, the Ji Yue 01, in China. This "robocar" features Baidu's Apollo Level 4 autonomous driving technology and advanced intelligent cockpit systems. Despite cutting-edge technology, the CEO acknowledges the need to build brand awareness to gain significant market share.

title: Insight 2023: NetApp Enhanced Data Pipeline for Hybrid Multi-Cloud AI and Advancements on Secure Storage Vs. Ransomware
summary: NetApp announced significant portfolio updates at Insight 2023, focusing on enhancements to its data pipeline for hybrid multi-cloud AI and advancements in secure storage against ransomware. The company is simplifying AI data pipeline deployment across hybrid multi-cloud environments, while also strengthening data protection with features like autonomous ransomware detection and an extended Ransomware Recovery Guarantee. These innovations aim to address the dual challenges of leveraging AI opportunities and mitigating ransomware threats for customers.

title: AMD soars as AI chip sales prediction bodes well for rivalry with Nvidia
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares jumped 8% after CEO Lisa Su provided an upbeat 2024 sales forecast of over $2 billion for its MI300 AI chips, positioning AMD as a clear second source to Nvidia in the AI accelerator market. This prediction helped alleviate concerns about a disappointing fourth-quarter forecast despite at least 18 analysts cutting price targets due to conservative forecasts and weakness in other AMD markets. The MI300 is set to become AMD's fastest product to reach $1 billion in sales, having secured commitments from multiple large hyperscale customers.

title: Super Micro stock has surged this year, but investors may be overlooking a risk
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc., a partner of Nvidia, has seen its stock surge over 200% this year, fueled by AI-ready server sales. The company reported fiscal first-quarter revenue of $2.12 billion and raised its annual guidance to between $10 billion and $11 billion. However, investors are cautioned that the company's increasing reliance on AI-related server sales (now over half its revenue) could pose a risk.

title: Raspberry Pi Receives Strategic Investment from Arm, Further Extending Long-Term Partnership
summary: Arm Holdings plc has made a strategic minority investment in Raspberry Pi Ltd, deepening their partnership that began in 2008. This investment aims to further enable the Internet of Things (IoT) developer community with low-cost, high-performance computing solutions built on Arm technology, particularly as edge compute and AI applications grow. Both companies share a vision to democratize technology and remove barriers to entry for innovation across various user groups.

title: Jabil takes on Intel's silicon photonics transceiver module business
summary: Jabil has acquired Intel's silicon photonics pluggable transceiver module business, integrating it into Jabil Photonics to expand its presence in the data center supply chain. This move allows Intel to focus on higher-value silicon photonics components for AI infrastructure, as part of its broader cost-cutting and portfolio optimization strategy. The acquisition aims to better position Jabil to address evolving data center demands, particularly those driven by artificial intelligence.

title: NVDY ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:NVDY
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NVDY), highlighting its dividends, returns, and operational strategy. NVDY aims to generate monthly income by utilizing a synthetic covered call strategy on Nvidia stock, involving synthetic long exposure, covered call writing, and US Treasuries for collateral. Key financial metrics such as AUM, dividend yield, expense ratio, and recent performance are detailed, along with information on its top holdings and issuer.

title: Fortinet (FTNT) Q3 Earnings Beat Expectations, Sales Miss
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) reported mixed third-quarter 2023 results, with non-GAAP earnings per share of 41 cents surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 37 cents, an improvement of 41.4% year-over-year. However, total revenues of $1.33 billion missed the consensus mark by 1% despite a 16.1% year-over-year increase, driven by strong demand from small enterprise customers. The company provided guidance for Q4 and the full year 2023, while several analyst recommendations were mentioned.

title: Intel CEO Admits to and Details The Company's Three Biggest Mistakes
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger openly discussed what he considers Intel's three biggest missteps: the failed smartphone business, the cancellation of the early AI-oriented GPU Larrabee, and the historical lack of focus on building a robust foundry. He believes these errors led to a disadvantage in the current AI market and impacted the company's overall direction. Gelsinger is now driving Intel's IDM 2.0 initiative, opening its fabs to external customers to generate revenue and invest in manufacturing and R&D.

title: Investing in Semiconductor ETFs & Stocks
summary: This article explores investing in semiconductor ETFs and stocks, detailing the sector's evolution, market dynamics, and future outlook, especially concerning AI. It highlights semiconductors' crucial role in modern technology, the economic powerhouse of the industry, and the benefits of investing via ETFs for diversification and accessibility. The piece also discusses factors influencing the sector, including geopolitical efforts to secure supply chains and the profound impact of AI on semiconductor demand and innovation.

title: Invest in the Market’s Momentum – and NVIDIA’s – With SPMO
summary: The article discusses the market's recent strong momentum, highlighted by the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite's best week since November 2022, and NVIDIA's significant gains. It suggests the Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO) as a way for investors to capitalize on this momentum, noting that NVIDIA is currently SPMO's top holding.

title: Amdocs and NVIDIA to Accelerate Adoption of Generative AI for $1.7 Trillion Telecom Industry
summary: Amdocs and NVIDIA announced a collaboration to optimize large language models (LLMs) to accelerate the adoption of generative AI applications and services across the $1.7 trillion telecommunications and media industries. This partnership will see Amdocs customize enterprise-grade LLMs on NVIDIA accelerated computing, leveraging NVIDIA DGX Cloud AI supercomputing and NVIDIA AI Enterprise software within the Amdocs amAIz framework. The initiative aims to enable communications service providers to efficiently deploy generative AI for customer experiences, network provisioning, and other operational enhancements, building on Amdocs' existing partnership with Microsoft.

title: The Evolution Of AI: From IBM And AWS To OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: This article traces the extensive history of Artificial Intelligence, starting from its foundational concepts in the 1950s, through periods of significant advancement and slowdowns like the "AI Winter." It highlights the pivotal contributions of major tech companies such as IBM with Watson, Amazon and Apple with virtual assistants, Google with deep learning breakthroughs, and OpenAI and Anthropic with generative AI and safety-focused development. The piece also discusses the critical role of hardware companies like Intel and NVIDIA, and looks ahead to future directions including responsible AI, spatial AI, and customer-centric applications.

title: After Earnings, Is AMD Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses AMD's stock post-Q3 earnings, evaluating its fair value, economic moat, and future prospects, particularly in the AI accelerator market. The company's target of over $2 billion in AI accelerator revenue for 2024 positions it as a significant player. While fair value is estimated at $125 per share, high uncertainty exists due to competition from Nvidia and potential shifts in the x86 architecture dominance.

title: 10월 하락장 맞춘 AI ETF…엔비디아보다 '이것' 담았다 [글로벌마켓나우 플러스]
summary: LG AI연구원과 인공지능 전문 자산운용사 크래프트 테크놀로지스가 협력하여 'LG 크래프트 AI 파워드 US 라지캡 코어 상장지수펀드'(LQAI ETF)를 뉴욕증권거래소에 상장했습니다. 이 ETF는 엔비디아보다 구글과 마이크로소프트 비중을 높이고, 크래프트 테크놀로지스의 AI 위험 예측 지표를 통해 10월 하락장과 11월 초 반등을 예측하는 정확도를 보였습니다. 내년 시장 전망에서는 넷플릭스를 최선호 종목으로 꼽고, 기술주와 헬스케어 비중을 높이며 부동산 섹터는 편입하지 않고 있습니다.

title: New AMD Radeon PRO Workstation Graphics Card to Power Next-Generation Professional Content Creation, CAD and AI Applications
summary: AMD has launched the Radeon PRO W7700, a new workstation graphics card designed for professional content creation, CAD, and AI applications. Priced under $1,000, it offers significant performance increases in key professional software compared to competitors and features 16GB of VRAM and DisplayPort 2.1. The card also includes hardware-based AI accelerators to speed up AI workloads in various applications.

title: Arm Announces Appointment of Ami Badani as Chief Marketing Officer
summary: Arm Holdings plc has announced the appointment of Ami Badani as its new Chief Marketing Officer (CMO). Badani, who previously served as Vice President of Marketing and Developer Products at NVIDIA, will lead Arm's global marketing organization and report directly to CEO Rene Haas. Her expertise in AI and developer ecosystems is expected to be crucial as Arm focuses on expanding its compute platform for the AI era.

title: FMR LLC Acquires New Stake in Magenta Therapeutics Inc
summary: FMR LLC, through its flagship company Fidelity Investments, has acquired a new stake in Magenta Therapeutics Inc (MGTA) by purchasing 2,218,212 shares at $0.6996 per share. This transaction gives FMR LLC a 14.97% ownership stake in the clinical-stage biotechnology company, indicating a strategic investment aligning with the firm's focus on technology and healthcare sectors. Despite Magenta Therapeutics currently operating at a loss, FMR LLC's move suggests a belief in the company's long-term value and transformative potential in stem cell and gene therapy.

title: Sudden release of the Nvidia H200: double the capacity, the bandwidth is booming
summary: NVIDIA has unexpectedly released the HGX H200, an advanced Tensor Core GPU based on the Hopper architecture, boasting significantly increased memory capacity and bandwidth for AI and HPC workloads. The H200, featuring HBM3e memory, doubles capacity and offers 2.4 times the bandwidth of its predecessor, the A100. It also claims a 2x inference speed increase over the H100 GPU for LLMs and will be deployed by major cloud service providers and used in the new Jupiter supercomputer.

title: Why TSLL Became the First Billion-Dollar Single-Stock ETF
summary: The Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 1.5X Shares (TSLL) became the first single-stock ETF to reach $1 billion earlier this year, driven by traders making aggressive bets on Tesla. Ed Egilinsky, Managing Director at Direxion, attributes TSLL's success to Tesla's highly polarizing nature and constant presence in headlines. He also discusses the growing interest in other single-stock ETFs and how traders utilize these leveraged products.

title: Amdocs to create telco-specific GenAI models using Nvidia service
summary: Amdocs will utilize Nvidia's AI foundry service to develop custom large language models (LLMs) specifically for the global telecommunications industry. This collaboration aims to provide telcos with enhanced performance and optimized resource utilization through specialized GenAI capabilities, building on a prior partnership between Amdocs and Microsoft. The development emphasizes the growing industry need for tailored AI solutions to address sector-specific challenges, as highlighted by other major telecom players developing their own GenAI models.

title: 3 Long-Term Growth Stocks Analysts Are Bullish On
summary: The article highlights three long-term growth stocks—Nvidia (NVDA), Block (SQ), and Editas Medicine (EDIT)—that analysts are bullish on amidst market volatility. Nvidia is recognized for its leadership in AI chips, Block for its evolving ecosystem including Bitcoin and BNPL, and Editas Medicine for its promising gene editing treatment EDIT-301 for sickle cell disease. The piece encourages investors to consider these stocks for long-term portfolio growth.

title: AMD Is Powering the New Steam Machine. Will It Move the Needle for AMD Stock?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) will power Valve's new Steam Machine with its RDNA 3 GPU and Zen 4 CPU, a development that reinforces AMD's reputation as an innovator and aids in brand visibility and diversification, despite being unlikely to significantly impact AMD stock revenue immediately. The article discusses AMD's strong Q3 earnings, high valuation multiples compared to competitors like Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments, and analyst price targets. While AI dominates current chipmaker focus, the gaming industry remains crucial, showcasing AMD's continued innovation in the sector.

title: Dropbox and NVIDIA Team to Bring Personalized Generative AI to Millions of Customers
summary: Dropbox and NVIDIA announced a partnership to integrate personalized generative AI into Dropbox's services, aiming to enhance productivity for millions of users. Dropbox will leverage NVIDIA's AI foundry, including AI Foundation Models and AI Enterprise software, to improve features like Dropbox Dash for universal search and Dropbox AI for summarizing large files. This collaboration focuses on boosting search accuracy, organization, and workflow efficiency by building custom, fine-tuned large language models with NVIDIA's technology.

title: 3 Best Long-Term Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 10 Years
