title: Oracle debuts cloud generative AI service to help enterprises deploy and fine-tune language models
summary: Oracle has launched its Cloud Infrastructure Generative AI service, enabling enterprises to leverage and fine-tune large language models like Meta's Llama 2 and Cohere's algorithms. This service supports over 100 languages, offers GPU cluster management, and provides flexible fine-tuning options, allowing businesses to integrate generative AI into their existing systems for tasks such as text generation and summarization. While analysts note the service's current limitations in model support compared to competitors, its integration with Oracle's business applications and database portfolio, alongside its cloud and on-premise deployment flexibility, could make it an attractive solution for many enterprises.

title: Top 10 Semiconductor IP Companies | A Reusable Technology
summary: The article identifies and profiles the top 10 semiconductor IP companies, detailing their overviews, key offerings, financial data, and recent developments. It also provides an overview of the semiconductor IP market, including its drivers and projected growth from $6.4 billion in 2023 to $11.3 billion by 2032. The content highlights the importance of semiconductor IP in integrated circuit design, emphasizing benefits like cost reduction and accelerated development.

title: The Best Desktop PCs: Early 2024
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to the best pre-built desktop PCs available in early 2024, categorizing them for different needs such as gaming, all-in-one solutions, and mini PCs. It offers detailed recommendations including specific models like the Corsair Vengeance for gaming, HP Envy 34 AIO for creative work, and Apple Mac Mini M2 for compactness. The guide also discusses considerations between building a PC and buying pre-built, and factors like value, upgradeability, and space constraints.

title: Nvidia billionaire Jensen Huang dines with TSMC execs in Taiwan to discuss the great AI chip shortage
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met with TSMC executives in Taiwan to discuss the ongoing AI chip supply constraints, a significant hurdle for the booming artificial intelligence industry. Huang emphasized TSMC's pivotal role in scaling AI capacity and expressed confidence that 2024 would be a crucial year for the sector. He also touched upon the broader implications for the computer industry, highlighting Taiwan's central position in this new era of computing.

title: Intel stock sinks more than 10% as early 2024 outlook comes up short
summary: Intel's stock dropped over 10% after the company issued a first-quarter 2024 outlook that fell short of analyst estimates, despite beating Wall Street expectations for the latest quarter. While CEO Pat Gelsinger stated the core PC and server chip businesses are healthy, overall sales are impacted by weakness in subsidiaries like Mobileye and its programmable chip unit. The company also broke a seven-quarter streak of declining revenue, with a 10% sales growth in Q4 2023.

title: AI & The Dragon: What Trends Will Dominate 2024?
summary: In 2024, Emerging Markets (EM) are expected to rebound, driven by potential Fed rate cuts and a weakening US dollar, with China and Brazil presenting more attractive valuations than the "Magnificent Seven" and other EM ex-China countries. The article suggests focusing on long-term trends and strategic allocations to specific growth sectors within EM. China's "Year of the Dragon" is anticipated to bring a gradual recovery in consumer sentiment and real estate, supported by government policies and significant expected EPS growth.

title: AMD earnings are around the corner: This number could be the ‘swing factor’
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings, with significant attention on its MI300 AI accelerator and data-center GPU forecasts. The company is looking to gain a larger share of the AI chip market, currently dominated by Nvidia. This outlook was reinforced by Elon Musk's recent statement about Tesla's plans to purchase AMD chips.

title: Opinion: Supermicro is heading toward an Nvidia-like second half — but is it sustainable?
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. is experiencing growth rates similar to Nvidia due to its success in selling servers equipped with Nvidia's AI chips. This surge has led investors to question the sustainability of the AI boom, particularly for Supermicro, given its position in the more commoditized server business compared to Nvidia's proprietary chip design.

title: 2 Unstoppable Technology ETFs That Could Turn $100,000 Into $1 Million Over the Next 30 Years
summary: This article highlights two technology-focused ETFs, the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) and the iShares Robotics and Artificial Intelligence Multisector ETF (IRBO), as prime investment opportunities for long-term growth. It suggests that an initial investment of $100,000 split between these two ETFs could grow to over $1.6 million in 30 years, capitalizing on the booming artificial intelligence sector.

title: 15 semiconductor companies that have followed Nvidia's lead in two key areas
summary: This article identifies 15 semiconductor companies that have demonstrated strong pricing power and improved efficiency, following Nvidia's successful lead. This combination of operational strengths is highlighted as a positive indicator for long-term investors in the stock market. The piece notes Nvidia's dominance in AI-supporting GPUs and anticipates its continued sales growth through 2025.

title: Fund selectors flock to AI and semiconductor ETFs in January
summary: Investors dramatically increased their allocation to semiconductor and artificial intelligence ETFs in January, betting on the continued strong performance of these themes throughout 2024. The Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence & Big Data UCITS ETF (XAIX) and the iShares MSCI Global Semiconductors UCITS ETF (SEMI) saw significant inflows, indicating sustained investor appetite for AI and chip-related investments despite a decrease in overall thematic ETF inflows in 2023.

title: US Accuses More Chinese Tech Firms of ‘Helping Beijing’s Military’
summary: The US Defence Department has added over a dozen Chinese tech firms, including YMTC, Megvii, Hesai Technology, and NetPosa, to a list of companies allegedly aiding Beijing's military. This action is part of Washington's ongoing efforts to prevent American technology from strengthening the People's Liberation Army, despite China's opposition, which calls it an abuse of state power. While inclusion on the list doesn't lead to immediate bans, it damages reputations and warns US entities about engaging with these firms.

title: Pixels to Profits: The Changing Economics of Gaming in 2024
summary: This article examines the evolving economics of the video gaming industry in 2024, highlighting its massive revenue and attraction for investors. It discusses key trends, how to gain exposure, and strategies for finding valuable opportunities within this diverse sector. The piece also provides a brief market summary covering Evergrande's liquidation, LVMH's record revenue, UK profit warnings, Meta's dividend, US Fed rates, job openings, and Australia's inflation, before diving into gaming company types and future outlooks.

title: Exclusive | Intel Delays $20 Billion Ohio Project, Citing Slow Chip Market
summary: Intel is delaying its $20 billion chip-manufacturing project in Ohio, pushing the completion of its factories to late 2026. This delay is attributed to challenging market conditions and the slow distribution of U.S. government grants intended to boost the domestic semiconductor industry. The company's stock symbol is INTC.

title: How NVIDIA’s Failure Analysis Lab Solves Silicon and Board-Level Mysteries
summary: NVIDIA's dedicated failure analysis team plays a critical role in identifying the root causes of malfunctions in their complex hardware products, from silicon-level defects to board-level issues. Employing sophisticated techniques and collaborating across departments, this team ensures product quality and accelerates time to market by quickly resolving engineering challenges. Their work highlights the importance of in-house expertise in maintaining the high standards of NVIDIA's technology.

title: FDA Hits Blockbuster Amgen Drug
summary: The FDA issued a "boxed warning" for Amgen's blockbuster drug Prolia due to the potential for dangerously low calcium levels in patients with advanced kidney disease, especially those on dialysis. This news comes as Amgen also announced a collaboration with Nvidia to use generative AI for biomarker discovery and precision medicine development. Prolia, Amgen's second-highest selling drug, faces patent expiration next year, which could lead to generic alternatives.

title: Analyzing all the security and AI news from Cisco Live EMEA 2024
summary: Cisco Live EMEA 2024 showcased significant innovations in security and AI, with a strong emphasis on addressing key IT pain points. The company introduced Cisco Identity Intelligence and AI Assistant for Secure Access to combat identity-based attacks and streamline security administration. Additionally, Cisco unveiled new Validated Designs for AI infrastructure and expanded its partnership with Nvidia to provide diverse networking solutions for AI deployments.

title: ¿Cuáles son los CEDEAR más elegidos por los argentinos?
summary: Certificados de Depósito Argentinos (CEDEAR) are a popular investment option in Argentina for diversifying portfolios and hedging against peso devaluation by investing in foreign companies. In January, Coca-Cola (KO) was the most chosen CEDEAR, followed by Apple (AAPL) and Tesla (TSLA). Recent changes by Banco Comafi regarding conversion ratios aim to make CEDEARs more accessible to a wider range of investors by lowering entry prices.

title: ITOT ETF: Investing Well Doesn’t Have to Be Complicated
summary: The iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT) offers a straightforward, low-cost way to invest in a broad range of U.S. companies, providing exposure to 90% of the total domestic stock market. With an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03% and a track record of strong double-digit annualized returns over the past decade, ITOT is presented as an effective long-term portfolio building block due to its wide diversification across 2,589 stocks. Despite its simplicity, it has delivered significant wealth building for investors since its inception in 2004.

title: Morgan Stanley hikes Nvidia price target ahead of earnings: 'AI demand continues to surge'
summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for Nvidia to $750 from $603, reiterating an overweight rating, citing strong near-term demand for AI processors. This new target suggests a 10% upside for the AI chipmaker, which has seen its stock surge significantly recently. Analyst Joseph Moore notes that while there are concerns about sustainability, the current AI demand remains robust.

title: Protein Analyzer Market revenue to hit USD 6 Billion by 2033, says Research Nester
summary: The global protein analyzer market is projected to reach USD 6 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of over 8% from 2023, driven by changing lifestyles, increased consumption of specific beverages, and a rise in diagnostic tests. Key growth factors include the surging demand for protein supplements globally, particularly in North America, and the enhanced production of animal feed and fertilizers in the Asia Pacific region. The DUMAS technique is expected to hold the largest market share due to its efficiency and safety, while protein analysis services and kits will also see significant growth.

title: Nvidia Overtakes Amazon in Market Value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon in market value, making it the fourth most valuable US-listed company earlier in the day. The chipmaker's rally, driven by exploding demand for AI chips, has seen its shares jump 46% this year. While Nvidia closed slightly below Amazon's valuation, the event marks a significant milestone fueled by the AI boom.

title: AI frenzy puts Nvidia briefly ahead of Amazon in market value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon.com in market capitalization on Monday, driven by the ongoing euphoria around artificial intelligence. The chipmaker reached a record high of $734.96, giving it a market value of $1.82 trillion, slightly ahead of Amazon's $1.81 trillion. This event marks Nvidia's highest valuation relative to Amazon since 2002, with investors keenly awaiting its upcoming quarterly results on February 21st.

title: This Leveraged ETF Is Up Almost 50% the Past 3 Months
summary: The Direxion Daily Robotics, Artificial Intelligence & Automation Index Bull 2X ETF (UBOT) has seen a nearly 50% increase over the past three months, driven by strong AI-related revenue and positive future guidance from major tech companies. Nvidia, a key player in AI, is also experiencing significant growth, with its stock up about 50% year-to-date and threatening to overtake Amazon in market capitalization. Single-stock ETFs like the Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 1.5X Shares (NVDU) offer traders leveraged exposure to Nvidia's bullish performance.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) ahead of its Q4 earnings report, highlighting the dominance of its data center segment and the impact of AI growth. The company maintains a "Wide" economic moat due to intangible assets and switching costs, but its stock is considered overvalued with a "Very High" uncertainty rating and a fair value estimate of $480. Key factors to watch include supply constraints, U.S. restrictions on sales to China, and competition from hyperscalers developing in-house AI chips.

title: Nvidia replaces Alphabet as Wall St's third most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Google-parent Alphabet to become the third most valuable U.S. company, with a market capitalization of $1.825 trillion. This milestone follows its recent overtaking of Amazon, driven by strong investor interest in the dominant AI chipmaker ahead of its upcoming quarterly report. Nvidia's stock has surged due to its control of about 80% of the high-end AI chip market and strong demand for its products.

title: February 2024 The AI race makes first meaningful impact on the suppliers of optics
summary: The AI race significantly impacted optics suppliers in Q4 2023, with Coherent and Innolight reporting substantial revenue growth driven by Nvidia's demand for 800G transceivers. While Nvidia initially handles much of the optics in-house, it allocates larger volumes to these suppliers later. Major tech companies show mixed spending on infrastructure, although overall market momentum is positive with AI leading the recovery.

title: Relx Share Price Spiked Up 3% but Later Fell on FY 2023 Results
summary: Relx PLC announced strong FY 2023 financial results, reporting an 8% organic revenue growth to £9,161 million and a 13% organic adjusted operating profit increase to £3,030 million. The company proposed an 8% higher full-year dividend of 58.8p per share. Despite an initial 3% spike in its share price, it later fell, trading at 3430.5p.

title: Nano-X Imaging (NNOX) Stock Soars 50% on Nvidia Investment
summary: Nano-X Imaging (NNOX) stock surged over 50% today after Nvidia disclosed its investment in the medical imaging firm. This investment, though small, has provided a significant catalyst for NNOX, which had been struggling and previously categorized as a penny stock. The news has thrust Nano-X into the spotlight, making it a top performer among the companies Nvidia recently invested in, despite prior bearish expert assessments.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $7,000 in the "Magnificent Seven" in 2017
summary: This article examines the investment returns if one had invested $7,000 across the "Magnificent Seven" stocks (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) in 2017. It highlights the significant growth these companies experienced, with Nvidia and Tesla showing the highest returns, and compares this performance to an investment in the S&P 500 over the same period. The author concludes that these established growth stocks can still offer good long-term returns despite their current size.

title: Iris Energy Triples AI Cloud Services Business to 816 NVIDIA H100 GPUs
summary: Iris Energy, a leader in next-generation data centers powered by 100% renewable energy, is expanding its AI cloud services by purchasing an additional 568 NVIDIA H100 GPUs for $22 million. This expansion will triple its current capacity to 816 NVIDIA H100 GPUs to meet growing customer demand for training advanced AI and large language model workloads. The delivery and commissioning of these new GPUs are anticipated in Q2 2024, with financing workstreams in progress to support further growth in this sector.

title: 25 Most Valuable Semiconductor Companies In The World
summary: The semiconductor industry faced a 9.4% sales decline in 2023 but is projected to rebound in 2024, exceeding 2022 record revenues. Key players like NVIDIA, TSMC, and Broadcom are highlighted, with NVIDIA focusing on AI infrastructure, TSMC expanding manufacturing in Japan, and Broadcom reporting strong Q4 2023 earnings. The article lists the 25 most valuable semiconductor companies globally, ranked by market capitalization as of February 15, 2024.

title: 英伟达一出手，资金跟着涌! “英伟达炒股板块”彻底火了
summary: NVIDIA's strategic investments in AI-related technology companies have sparked significant interest and capital influx into a "NVIDIA stock-picking sector." Companies like SoundHound AI and Arm have seen substantial stock surges following NVIDIA's disclosed holdings, highlighting the chip giant's influential "brand effect" and its early leadership in the AI revolution. Analysts believe these investments are just the beginning of NVIDIA's broader strategy to support the AI ecosystem.

title: NVIDIA Corp. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: NVIDIA Corp. shares fell 4.35% on Tuesday, closing at $694.52, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declined. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for NVIDIA's stock.

title: Nvidia CEO Wears Fuchsia Leather Jacket At Insight Sales Event
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wore a fuchsia leather jacket, Insight's signature color, at the company's Amplify sales event, signaling a strong partnership in generative AI. An Insight executive stated that Huang's virtual presence and choice of attire underscored the significance of their collaboration, especially as Nvidia expands its AI solutions. The event also featured Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and focused on generative AI use cases, infrastructure upgrades, and the growing demand for AI PCs amidst a broader PC refresh cycle.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2024
summary: NVIDIA reported record-breaking financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024, driven by surging demand for accelerated computing and generative AI. The company achieved record quarterly revenue of $22.1 billion and full-year revenue of $60.9 billion, with significant growth in its Data Center segment. NVIDIA also announced a quarterly cash dividend and provided an optimistic outlook for the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

title: Nvidia gives upbeat forecast as AI reaches ‘tipping point’
summary: Nvidia Corp. has announced an upbeat forecast, predicting approximately $24 billion in revenue for the current quarter, surpassing analyst estimates. This optimistic outlook follows strong fourth-quarter results, driven by surging demand for its AI accelerators, which CEO Jensen Huang states indicates AI has reached a "tipping point." The company's stock jumped significantly, reflecting its continued position as a primary beneficiary of the AI computing boom.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $10,000 Into the Ark Innovation ETF's Top 5 Stocks in 2019
summary: This article examines the hypothetical returns of investing $10,000 into the top five holdings of the Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) as of January 2019, compared to investing the same amount directly into the ETF. It reveals that direct investment in the individual top stocks, particularly Nvidia and Tesla, would have yielded significantly higher returns despite some underperforming assets like Invitae. The author suggests that individual stock selection from the ETF's holdings can lead to better outcomes than investing in the entire fund, though the ETF offers diversification.

title: Keysight and NVIDIA to Demo 6G Neural Receiver Design Flow at MWC 2024
summary: Keysight and NVIDIA are partnering to demonstrate a complete design flow for training and validating neural receivers for 6G wireless technology at MWC 2024. This collaboration focuses on developing AI-native wireless solutions, where neural receivers, trained using NVIDIA Sionna and Keysight's testing equipment, will replace traditional human-designed receive chains. The demonstration will showcase how these solutions allow for site-specific optimization and validation of neural receiver performance in an Open RAN testbed.

title: AI Theme 'Skyrockets', ETFs 'Attracting a Gold Rush': Who Will Be the Next NVIDIA?
summary: NVIDIA's recent exceptional earnings, with a 265% year-over-year Q4 revenue increase and 765% earnings per share surge, have driven its stock to new highs and significantly boosted its market value. This success has also fueled a "gold rush" into semiconductor and AI-related ETFs, with funds like the VanEck Vectors Semiconductor ETF and leveraged NVIDIA-focused ETFs experiencing substantial capital inflows and impressive gains. The article highlights that the AI dividend is far from over, with projections for significant growth in the semiconductor industry and AI-related applications globally.

title: SPY ETF Reaches Record High Amid Nvidia-Fueled AI Rally—Key Chart Pattern to Watch
summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) reached a record high, mirroring the S&P 500's surge driven by mega-cap technology stocks, particularly after Nvidia's strong earnings report. Nvidia, along with other AI-related companies like Microsoft and Broadcom, contributed significantly to the rally, highlighting the outsized influence of Magnificent Seven stocks within the SPY's portfolio. Investors should watch the SPY's rising wedge pattern for potential breakouts or breakdowns, which could signal future price movements.

title: How Nvidia grew to a trillion-dollar company fueling the AI revolution
summary: Nvidia has grown into a nearly $3 trillion company by specializing in Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), which have become crucial for the artificial intelligence revolution. Initially used for gaming, GPUs' ability to perform concurrent calculations makes them ideal for AI, giving Nvidia a significant first-mover advantage and market dominance. The company's unique technology and supply chain have led to widespread adoption by major tech firms, with discussions emerging about potential shortages and the need for continued investment in chip manufacturing.

title: TWN CEF: No FOMO, Ride The 'AI 5' Tailwind With This Hated Fund (NYSE:TWN)
summary: The Taiwan Fund Inc (TWN) is a closed-end fund focused on long-term capital growth through actively managed investments in stocks listed on the Taiwan Stock Exchange. With approximately $330 million in assets, the fund primarily invests in companies like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), which has significantly contributed to its recent performance. The article suggests this fund, despite being "hated," offers a way to capitalize on the "AI 5" tailwind.

title: ServiceNow and NVIDIA Expand Partnership With Introduction of Telco-Specific GenAI Solutions to Elevate Service Experiences
summary: ServiceNow and NVIDIA have expanded their partnership to introduce telco-specific generative AI solutions aimed at improving service experiences. The first solution, Now Assist for Telecommunications Service Management (TSM), built on the Now Platform and leveraging NVIDIA AI, is designed to boost agent productivity, speed resolution times, and enhance customer experiences for telecommunications service providers. This collaboration focuses on using GenAI for critical use cases like customer care and service assurance to drive business transformation in the telco industry.

title: What's Cathie Wood been selling?
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Investment Management has sold shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. and Nvidia Corp., diversifying holdings amidst the AI frenzy. This marks Wood's first sale of TSMC stock in over two years and follows previous sales of Nvidia. The move comes as both TSMC and Nvidia have seen significant gains, with Wood's funds betting on growth potential in less-talked-about software companies like UiPath and Twilio, despite earlier predictions of AI's transformative impact.

title: Micron begins production of a 'better' HBM3E memory
summary: Micron Technology has started volume production of HBM3E DRAM for AI datacenters, claiming its offering consumes 30 percent less power than competitors. Its 24Gbyte 8-high HBM3E components will be featured in Nvidia's H200 tensor core GPUs shipping in Q2 2024, potentially positioning Micron ahead of rival SK Hynix in HBM3E production. The company is also preparing a 12-high 36Gbyte HBM3E component for introduction in March 2024, with its stock opening almost 5 percent higher on the news.

title: Nvidia's Rally Casts Spotlight on 2 Leveraged ETFs
summary: Nvidia's strong financial performance, including 265% year-over-year revenue growth, has propelled its stock, highlighting the potential for leveraged ETFs invested in the company. For bullish investors, the Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 1.5X Shares (NVDU) offers increased exposure, while the Direxion Daily NVDA Bear 1X Shares (NVDD) provides an option for short-term profit-taking or hedging. Additionally, the Direxion Daily Robotics, Artificial Intelligence & Automation Index Bull 2X ETF (UBOT) benefits from Nvidia's AI-driven growth as Nvidia comprises a significant portion of its holdings.

title: After Earnings and a Massive Rally, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Following a strong fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report and a significant stock rally, Morningstar assesses Nvidia's valuation. While the company's AI dominance and strong demand are sustainable, its stock is currently rated as fairly valued, with a 3-star Morningstar Rating and a fair value estimate of $730 per share. The "Very High" Uncertainty Rating reflects potential competition and the volatile nature of some of its markets.

title: ServiceNow, Hugging Face, and NVIDIA Release New Open-Access LLMs to Help Developers Tap Generative AI to Build Enterprise Applications
summary: ServiceNow, Hugging Face, and NVIDIA have released StarCoder2, a new family of open-access large language models (LLMs) for code generation. These models, developed by the BigCode community, aim to help developers build enterprise applications using generative AI with improved performance, transparency, and cost-effectiveness. StarCoder2 offers three model sizes, including a 3 billion-parameter model by ServiceNow and a 15 billion-parameter model by NVIDIA, and is trained on 619 programming languages for various specialized tasks.

title: NANC ETF Tracking Democrat Stock Trades Beating S&P 500 on Tech Bets
summary: The Unusual Whales Democratic ETF (NANC), which tracks stock trades by Democrats in Congress, has outperformed the S&P 500 since its launch in February 2023, returning 30% compared to the S&P 500's 24%. This outperformance is largely attributed to its significant holdings in mega-cap tech stocks like Microsoft, Amazon, Apple, and Nvidia. In contrast, a similar ETF tracking Republican trades (KRUZ) has only gained about 15% over the same period.

title: VONG ETF: A Great Way to Invest in Top Growth Stocks
summary: The Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) offers a straightforward and effective way to invest in a diverse range of top growth stocks. With a strong track record of impressive returns, a low expense ratio of 0.08%, and a portfolio that includes leading mega-cap technology companies and other high-growth firms, VONG is considered a compelling option for investors seeking exposure to large-cap growth in the U.S. market. The ETF's holdings are highly rated, with many featuring Outperform-equivalent Smart Scores.

title: Nvidia skyrocketed over the past 5 years, but 3 stocks performed even better—and none is Apple or Tesla
summary: While Nvidia has achieved an impressive 1,904% return over the past five years, three mid-cap stocks have significantly outpaced it: e.l.f. Beauty (2,491%), Super Micro Computer (4,175%), and Celsius Holdings (over 5,300%). The article highlights that smaller companies (mid-caps) often show greater growth potential and less media coverage than large-cap stocks like Nvidia, suggesting diversification into these smaller companies can boost long-term returns. Investors are advised to use diversified funds (ETFs and mutual funds) to mitigate volatility and risk associated with individual small-cap stock picks.

title: Citadel's Ken Griffin Oversees $500 Billion in Managed Assets: These Are His Top 5 Holdings
summary: Ken Griffin's Citadel Advisors manages $500 billion in assets and is a top-performing hedge fund. SEC filings reveal its top non-derivative holdings, which include the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, and Advanced Micro Devices. These investments highlight a strategy of betting on market indexes and major players in tech and AI while acknowledging potential risks and sector trends.

title: Dow's nearly 125-point rally led by gains for shares of NVIDIA Corp., Cisco
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by nearly 125 points, primarily driven by strong performances from NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc. NVIDIA's shares surged after an analyst upgrade, while Cisco remained relatively flat despite an analyst reiteration of an overweight rating. The overall market saw NVIDIA and Cisco among the top gainers, contrasting with losses from other Dow components.

title: Magnificent Seven vs. the ‘Granolas’: How does Europe’s version stack up?
summary: This article compares the U.S. "Magnificent Seven" stocks with Europe's "Granolas," highlighting their differences in market value, sector diversity, and profitability. While the Magnificent Seven are largely tech-driven and exceptionally profitable, the Granolas offer broader sector representation and solid returns, often trading at a discount despite their strong performance. The piece examines historical returns, valuation ratios, and individual stock assessments for both groups.

title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights: BigBear.ai, Palantir, Super Micro Computer, SoundHound and Nvidia
summary: Zacks Investment Ideas highlights five AI-related stocks: BigBear.ai (BBAI), Palantir (PLTR), Super Micro Computer (SMCI), SoundHound AI (SOUN), and Nvidia (NVDA). The article focuses on BBAI and SOUN as lesser-known AI plays with significant partnerships—BBAI with Palantir, and SOUN receiving an investment from Nvidia—suggesting potential for substantial growth and profitability. Both companies have shown promising financial indicators, including BBAI's first profitable quarter and SOUN's consistent EPS beats.

title: Sprott Copper Miners ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:COPP)
summary: The Sprott Copper Miners ETF (COPP) tracks the Nasdaq Sprott Copper Miners index, investing in global companies involved in copper mining, exploration, development, and production. Launched on March 5, 2024, the ETF has $309.11 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.65%. Its top holdings include Freeport-McMoRan, Teck Resources, and Antofagasta plc.

title: NetApp Turbocharges AI Innovation with Intelligent Data Infrastructure
summary: NetApp has introduced new capabilities for its intelligent data infrastructure, aiming to enhance generative AI projects and provide a competitive advantage. These updates include AIPod for AI-optimized converged infrastructure, new FlexPod for AI reference architectures, and validation for NVIDIA OVX systems. NetApp also announced Autonomous Ransomware Protection with AI (ARP/AI) embedded in storage, leveraging machine learning to combat sophisticated cyber threats.

title: NetApp Turbocharges AI Innovation with Intelligent Data Infrastructure
summary: NetApp announced new capabilities for its intelligent data infrastructure, integrating with NVIDIA's high-performance compute, networking, and software, to maximize the potential of generative AI (Gen AI) projects. These updates include NetApp AIPod™ certifications for NVIDIA DGX BasePOD, new FlexPod for AI reference architectures, and validation for NVIDIA OVX systems. Additionally, NetApp is introducing Autonomous Ransomware Protection with AI (ARP/AI) embedded in storage to combat sophisticated cyber threats.

title: Here’s the One Chinese EV Stock Worth Buying and Holding
summary: Li Auto (NASDAQ: LI) is the only profitable Chinese EV manufacturer among its peers like Nio and Xpeng, primarily due to its focus on premium hybrid electric SUVs that appeal to families and mitigate range anxiety. The company reported significant net income growth of 2,068% year-over-year in Q4 2023, driven by a 184.6% increase in vehicle deliveries. While it offers premium features and innovative technology, Li Auto issued downside guidance for Q1 2024.

title: NVIDIA and HP Supercharge Data Science and Generative AI on Workstations
summary: NVIDIA and HP Inc. announced the integration of NVIDIA CUDA-X data processing libraries with HP AI workstation solutions to accelerate data preparation for generative AI development. These libraries, including NVIDIA RAPIDS cuDF, can speed up tasks for data scientists using pandas software by up to 110x on NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada Generation GPUs. This collaboration aims to provide a full-stack development solution that significantly enhances data science workflows on HP AI workstations.

title: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article analyzes NVIDIA Corp (NVDA) using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation model. Based on the model's inputs and calculations, including forecasted free cash flows, a 12% discount rate, and a 2% terminal growth rate, NVDA is found to be significantly overvalued. The calculated DCF value of $104.71 per share is substantially lower than its current market price of $925.99, indicating a negative margin of safety.

title: SCHX: As Cheap As It Gets For Large-Cap Exposure
summary: This article recommends the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap ETF (SCHX) for investors seeking exposure to large-cap stocks, highlighting its diversification, low costs, and tax efficiency. Key holdings include tech giants like Microsoft and Apple, leading to significant exposure to the Information Technology sector. The author, Michael A. Gayed, CFA, discusses market efficiency and offers insights from his Lead-Lag Report service.

title: TSMC to Win More Than $5 Billion in Grants for US Chip Plant
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is expected to receive over $5 billion in federal grants for its chipmaking project in Arizona, a significant move for President Biden's bid to revitalize American semiconductor manufacturing. This award, part of the 2022 Chips and Science Act, is not yet finalized but marks a major step in distributing funds to leading chipmakers like Intel, Micron Technology, and Samsung. TSMC is investing $40 billion in two Arizona fabrication facilities, though the project has faced delays due to labor shortages and technological considerations.

title: Megacap tech stocks dominate many ESG funds. Here’s why
summary: Megacap tech stocks frequently dominate ESG funds because these funds still aim for top performers within economic groups, and the tech sector is considered "cleaner" regarding environmental impact. While ESG funds saw net outflows in Q4 2023 due to advisors pulling back, individual investor interest remains strong, viewing ESG as a long-term allocation strategy rather than a momentum play. Despite misconceptions, ESG funds can and do invest in energy companies, as explained by experts from DWS Group and former VettaFi.

title: HP's 6 Biggest Announcements At Amplify Partner Conference 2024
summary: At its Amplify Partner Conference 2024, HP unveiled several major advancements focusing on hybrid work and AI integration. Key announcements included new business laptops and mobile workstations featuring AI capabilities, an expanded partnership with Nvidia for AI Studio software, and a revamped Workplace Experience Platform (WEX) with AI-powered management. HP also introduced new conferencing solutions, an AI MasterClass program for partners, and new Color LaserJet Pro 3000 printers for SMBs.

title: Happy 25th QQQ
summary: The Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) celebrated its 25th anniversary, showcasing its significant growth and position as the fifth-largest ETF. The article discusses QQQ's composition, performance history, and highlights alternatives like QQQM for lower costs and QQQE for an equal-weighted approach, emphasizing QQQ's role in innovative company investments.

title: Aclara joins Nvidia and Utilidata in bringing AI to the smart meter
summary: Aclara Technologies has partnered with Utilidata and Nvidia to embed AI modules directly into smart meters, marking the first hardware collaboration for Utilidata's Nvidia-powered distributed AI platform, Karman. This initiative aims to provide utilities with real-time grid insights and significantly more processing power than traditional solutions, accelerating the adoption of AI-driven applications at scale. The partnership expands on earlier efforts to bring AI into smart meters and addresses the need to replace aging meters across the U.S.

title: AMD CEO Lisa Su Talks the Invisible Role of A.I. Chips in Oscar-Winning Films
summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su highlighted the significant role of A.I. chips in the production of Oscar-winning animated films at SXSW, noting how they drastically accelerate rendering times. She explained that AMD's hardware is used by partners like Pixar and Wētā FX, enabling sophisticated visual effects that were previously far more time-consuming. The executive VFX producer David Conley also emphasized the critical partnership with companies like AMD for the future of filmmaking, especially with the integration of real-time processing and A.I.

title: These Bad Habits Hold Investors Back From Peak Performance
summary: This article discusses how common investor bad habits, such as poorly timed buying and selling decisions driven by emotion and excessive activity, lead to underperformance compared to funds. It highlights the "behavior gap" and advises investors to focus on good habits and long-term goals rather than reacting to short-term market noise, political turbulence, or fear of missing out on high-performing stocks.

title: EDGAR Filing Documents for 0001493152-24-003271
summary: This SEC filing details Form 497 definitive materials submitted by GraniteShares ETF Trust, with the accession number 0001493152-24-003271 and a filing date of January 22, 2024. The document lists various GraniteShares leveraged daily ETFs, including those tracking META, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA, AMD, UBER, BABA, GOOGL, AMZN, AAPL, COIN, DIS, and F. It also provides contact and address information for the GraniteShares ETF Trust.

title: Democratic ETF outpaces Republican peer on Big Tech bets
summary: An exchange-traded fund tracking Democratic lawmakers' stock trades has significantly outperformed a Republican-oriented fund since its launch last year, driven by strategic investments in megacap technology companies like Nvidia and Microsoft. The Democratic ETF, called NANC, has surged nearly 35%, benefiting from its tilt towards growth stocks, while the Republican ETF, KRUZ, which favors energy and value stocks, gained 18.1%. This performance gap highlights the differing investment strategies based on political affiliations and coincides with growing scrutiny over congressional stock trading.

title: Applied Digital Wins Contract with Together AI
summary: Applied Digital announced a contract with Together AI, including an $18 million prepayment as part of a $75 million deal. Applied Digital will provide H100 GPUs and high-speed InfiniBand fabric to support Together AI's demanding generative AI model training and deployment. This partnership aims to leverage Applied Digital's robust infrastructure to advance AI innovation and capitalize on the growing HPC market.

title: Intel’s chief people officer: Humans need to be at the center of AI
summary: Intel's chief people officer, Christy Pambianchi, emphasizes the importance of keeping humans at the center of artificial intelligence development and integration. The company, an early pioneer in microprocessors, is now deeply invested in AI through initiatives like its AI Inside program, proprietary iGPT, and a significant AI upskilling program. Pambianchi explains how Intel plans to responsibly incorporate AI into its workforce, encourage employee adoption, and prepare staff for future AI-driven roles.

title: Meet the Tech Company That Had a Better Year Than Nvidia
summary: Super Micro Computer, a server maker and Nvidia customer, has seen its value and revenue grow significantly, outperforming even Nvidia in the past year. The company's runaway sales of servers equipped with Nvidia's AI chips are projected to double its revenue this year, leading to its inclusion in the S&P 500 and a market cap of $60 billion.

title: NVIDIA Launches Blackwell-Powered DGX SuperPOD for Generative AI Supercomputing at Trillion-Parameter Scale
summary: NVIDIA has announced its next-generation AI supercomputer, the NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, powered by GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchips. This new system is designed for processing trillion-parameter models with constant uptime for generative AI training and inference, featuring liquid cooling, 11.5 exaflops of AI supercomputing, and up to 576 Blackwell GPUs. Additionally, NVIDIA unveiled the DGX B200 system, an air-cooled platform for AI model training, fine-tuning, and inference, providing 15x faster real-time inference for trillion-parameter models.

title: Blackwell: Nvidia's GPU architecture to power new generation of trillion-parameter generative AI models
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its new Blackwell GPU architecture, designed to power trillion-parameter generative AI models with significantly reduced cost and power consumption compared to previous generations. This architecture introduces six transformative technologies, including a custom manufacturing process and enhanced networking capabilities, promising breakthroughs across various advanced computing domains. Blackwell will be available through cloud providers and third-party server manufacturers, enabling a new era of large-scale AI deployment.

title: NVIDIA, Huang Win Top Honors in Innovation, Engineering
summary: NVIDIA has been named the world's most innovative company by Fast Company magazine, recognizing its transformative work in putting AI to use across various industries. This accolade follows NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang's induction into the U.S. National Academy of Engineering for his contributions to high-powered graphics processing units and fueling the AI revolution. Leaders from Microsoft, Stanford University, and TSMC have praised Huang's visionary leadership and enduring impact on computing and AI.

title: BNY Mellon becomes first global bank to deploy Nvidia-powered AI supercomputer
summary: BNY Mellon has become the first global bank to implement an AI supercomputer powered by an Nvidia DGX SuperPOD system. This system, equipped with Nvidia DGX H100 systems and InfiniBand networking, will support various AI applications such as deposit forecasting, payment automation, and predictive trade analytics. The bank aims to accelerate processing capacity and innovation in AI-enabled capabilities to manage client assets.

title: NVIDIA’s AI models to be deployed on NXP’s edge processing devices portfolio
summary: NXP Semiconductors has announced a collaboration with NVIDIA to enable the deployment of NVIDIA's trained AI models on NXP's edge processing devices through the eIQ machine learning development environment. This integration simplifies the training and deployment of AI models for developers by incorporating the NVIDIA TAO Toolkit into NXP's eIQ environment. The partnership aims to accelerate AI development for industrial and IoT edge applications, providing customers with pre-tested AI models and faster market entry.

title: SAP and NVIDIA to Accelerate Generative AI Adoption Across Enterprise Applications Powering Global Industries
summary: SAP and NVIDIA have expanded their partnership to accelerate the adoption of generative AI across SAP's cloud solutions and applications. This collaboration aims to integrate business-specific generative AI capabilities into SAP's Joule copilot and other cloud offerings, leveraging NVIDIA's generative AI foundry services and NIM microservices to fine-tune large language models (LLMs) and enhance data insights. The new integrated capabilities are expected to be available by the end of 2024, enabling customers to automate tasks, improve human resources, and optimize business processes using AI.

title: Lenovo Vehicle Computing, WeRide Forge Partnership For Autonomous Driving Solutions
summary: Lenovo Vehicle Computing and WeRide have partnered to develop Level 4 autonomous driving solutions for commercial applications. The collaboration will leverage Lenovo's AD1 domain controller, based on the NVIDIA DRIVE Thor platform, and integrate it into WeRide One, a versatile autonomous driving solution. This partnership aims to deliver enhanced autonomous driving products and services globally, focusing on safe and reliable commercial autonomous vehicles.

title: Nvidia, Teradyne Robotics Launch AI-Powered Industrial Robots
summary: Teradyne Robotics is partnering with Nvidia to integrate AI capabilities into its industrial robots, aiming to improve performance and efficiency. This collaboration involves incorporating Nvidia's AI computing technology into collaborative robots (cobots) to significantly speed up path-planning and launching new AI-powered solutions like the MiR1200 Pallet Jack for enhanced precision in material handling. The partnership seeks to establish Teradyne's Universal Robots and Mobile Industrial Robots as preferred platforms for AI applications in industrial automation, combining their expertise with Nvidia's advanced AI platform and simulation tools.

title: Seeking a Growth ETF with Strong Returns? Check Out ILCG
summary: The iShares Morningstar Growth ETF (ILCG) is highlighted as a strong option for investors seeking growth, offering diversified exposure to 389 top U.S. large- and mid-cap growth stocks. The article emphasizes its long track record of strong returns, low expense ratio of 0.04%, and consistent double-digit annualized returns since its inception in 2004. Despite slightly underperforming the S&P 500 over three years, ILCG has outperformed it over five and ten-year periods, making it a reliable, cost-effective long-term investment.

title: Inside Micron's most advanced memory fab - we visited the colossal control room and newest A3 fab in Taiwan
summary: This article details a visit to Micron's highly advanced DRAM manufacturing facilities in Taiwan, specifically highlighting the scale of operations at Fab 16, the cutting-edge A3 facility, and the Remote Operations Center. It touches upon Micron's sustainability efforts, diverse memory product portfolio, and the company's optimistic outlook amid the AI boom, contrasting it with previous industry downturns. The article also provides a profile of Dr. Donghui Lu, Head of Micron Taiwan, emphasizing his passion for PC DIY and background in the tech industry.

title: What’s Inside Direxion’s Daily Semiconductor Bull and Bear 3x shares?
summary: This article describes the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull (SOXL) and Bear 3X Shares (SOXS) ETFs. It explains that these funds aim for daily investment results of 300%, or 300% of the inverse, of the performance of the NYSE Semiconductor Index, while cautioning that investment objectives are not guaranteed. The article also provides a link for more information about Direxion.

title: How to invest in ChatGPT stock?
summary: This article explains that direct investment in ChatGPT stock is not possible as its developer, OpenAI, is not publicly traded. Instead, it suggests investing in publicly traded companies heavily involved in AI technology, such as Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), Perion Network (PERI), and C3.ai (AI). The article details each company's relevance to AI and provides an investment strategy for AI stocks, emphasizing diversification and monitoring financial health.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 03/24/2024
summary: This article highlights the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending March 24, 2024, showcasing funds that gained significantly due to market movements in sectors like cannabis, housing, technology, and financials. It details the performance of the top 10 ETFs, providing context for their gains influenced by specific company news, economic indicators, and sector trends. Investors are cautioned about the inherent volatility of such leveraged products.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 03/24/2024
summary: This article details the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week of March 24, 2024, highlighting their weekly returns and the underlying factors contributing to their performance. It covers various sectors, including cannabis, homebuilders, technology (Nvidia, META, FANG+), travel, banking, auto industry, and semiconductors, explaining the market drivers behind each ETF's gains. The article emphasizes the rapid movement characteristic of leveraged funds and the importance of due diligence.

title: Faith-Based ETF Aims to Compete With Secular Funds
summary: The Inspire 500 ETF (PTL) is launching with an expense ratio below 10 basis points, aiming to compete with secular funds on fees and performance. Robert Netzly, CEO of Inspire ETFs, believes investors shouldn't pay more for values-aligned investing and plans to offer more low-cost faith-based ETFs. This move challenges the traditional higher expense ratios seen in ESG and faith-based funds, potentially attracting a broader range of investors and advisors concerned with both values and costs.

title: 5 Best Momentum ETFs To Buy
summary: This article discusses momentum investing and lists five top momentum ETFs to consider buying, including the JPMorgan U.S. Momentum Factor ETF (JMOM). It highlights JMOM's structure, holdings, and performance, featuring NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) as a key holding. The article also touches on analyst insights and hedge fund sentiment regarding NVIDIA.

title: Best-Performing Stocks for the Quarter
summary: The article identifies the best-performing stocks in Q1, highlighting that while Nvidia is prominent due to AI, a broader range of companies including retailers, utilities, and packaged food companies also performed strongly. It categorizes top performers into overall, undervalued, and moat stocks, providing detailed analysis, Morningstar ratings, and fair value estimates for several key companies.

title: These Growth Funds Continued to Shine in Q1 2024
summary: Growth equity funds, especially those with exposure to AI-related stocks, continued their strong performance in Q1 2024, significantly outperforming value funds. While large-cap growth led the way, small- and mid-cap growth funds also fared well. International growth funds also saw gains, although they lagged behind their US counterparts.

title: NVIDIA’s Rapid Rise Fueled by Chip Giants TSMC and SK Hynix
summary: The article highlights that NVIDIA's significant market valuation and AI leadership are heavily reliant on its partnerships with semiconductor giants TSMC and SK Hynix. Despite their indispensable roles in manufacturing and developing critical components like advanced GPUs and HBM chips, TSMC and SK Hynix have significantly lower valuations compared to NVIDIA. The KraneShares Emerging Markets Consumer Technology ETF (KEMQ) is presented as a way for U.S. investors to gain exposure to these key enablers of AI technology.

title: Lowe’s looks to Nvidia and AI to improve its retail operations
summary: Lowe's is partnering with Nvidia to integrate AI across its retail operations, including digital businesses, supply chain, and in-store experiences. The collaboration aims to enhance customer interactions, empower associates, and optimize store management through technologies such as AI-powered visualizations, digital twins in Nvidia Omniverse, and computer vision for checkout efficiency. Lowe's has been leveraging AI for years and sees ripe applications for the technology in improving both business processes and customer satisfaction.

title: 3 AI Voice Stocks to Capitalize on the Next Big Trend
summary: This article highlights three AI voice stocks poised for growth: SoundHound AI (SOUN), LivePerson (LPSN), and Nvidia (NVDA). It explains the difference between generative AI and AI voice, predicting AI voice to be the next significant trend in the AI sector. The author details each company's strengths, particularly SoundHound AI's market dominance and Nvidia's strategic investments in the AI voice space.

title: Leading chipmaker TSMC evacuates staff and pauses some machinery after Taiwan's largest earthquake in a quarter-century
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), the world's largest advanced chipmaker, and its rival United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) halted some machinery and evacuated staff after Taiwan experienced its strongest earthquake in 25 years. The 7.4 magnitude temblor raised concerns about the semiconductor supply chain's vulnerability, given Taiwan's critical role in producing high-end chips. TSMC shares slid about 1.5% and UMC less than 1% in early trading following the event.

title: Should You Invest in the Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT)?
summary: The Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT) offers broad exposure to the Technology - Software segment, particularly in AI and next-gen software development. Launched in 2005, it is passively managed with an expense ratio of 0.60% and currently holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 1 (Strong Buy). Its top holdings include Meta Platforms, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices, and it has shown strong year-to-date performance.

title: Alger Launches 2 Transparent Actively Managed ETFs
summary: Fred Alger Management has launched two new transparent actively managed ETFs: the Alger AI Enablers & Adopters ETF (ALAI) and the Alger Concentrated Equity ETF (CNEQ). ALAI focuses on companies involved with artificial intelligence, while CNEQ invests in large-cap growth companies, with a significant allocation to technology. These additions bring Alger's ETF family to five funds, building on their earlier entry into the ETF market in 2021.

title: Alger Concentrated Equity ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Alger Concentrated Equity ETF (CNEQ), which was created on April 5, 2024. The ETF focuses on large-cap companies with high growth potential, maintaining a concentrated portfolio of no more than 30 stocks. The reported holdings include major technology companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Amazon, alongside others across various sectors.

title: AMD Advances Open Source Initiative with MES Firmware Release for Radeon GPUs
summary: AMD announced its plan to open-source the Micro Engine Scheduler (MES) firmware for its Radeon GPUs, enhancing user control and accessibility. This move directly addresses lobbying efforts from companies like Tiny Corp and could facilitate the integration of third-party chiplets. The company also anticipates significant growth from its MI300s, projecting substantial revenue increases in the coming fiscal years.

title: TSMC Arizona and U.S. Department of Commerce Announce up to US$6.6 Billion in Proposed CHIPS Act Direct Funding, the Company Plans Third Leading-Edge Fab in Phoenix
summary: TSMC Arizona and the U.S. Department of Commerce have signed a preliminary memorandum for up to US$6.6 billion in CHIPS Act direct funding. TSMC plans to build a third advanced semiconductor fab in Phoenix, Arizona, bringing its total investment in the site to over US$65 billion. This expansion aims to enhance U.S. semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and create thousands of high-tech jobs, with production from the three fabs utilizing 4nm, 2nm, and more advanced processes.

title: Intel challenges Nvidia with new Gaudi 3 AI chip as AMD expands processor lineup
summary: Intel has launched its new Gaudi 3 AI chip, promising significantly improved performance over its predecessor and competitive speeds against Nvidia's H100 and H200 chips for training and inference of large language models. Simultaneously, AMD expanded its AI processor lineup with new Versal AI Edge Series Gen 2 and Prime Series Gen 2 chips, targeting edge computing devices and leveraging customizable FPGA technology. Both companies are pushing boundaries in the AI hardware space, with Intel also focusing on an open platform for enterprise AI and AMD enhancing its offerings for connected devices.

title: NVIDIA gives mobile robots all-around sight with Nova Carter
summary: NVIDIA has introduced Nova Carter, a platform designed to accelerate the development of commercial-grade mobile robots. This system provides surround vision for autonomous navigation, allowing developers to focus on other AI applications in robotics. Nova Carter offers out-of-the-box support for capabilities like 3D mapping, vSLAM, and object detection, powered by the Nova Orin reference architecture and equipped with a suite of sensors for 360-degree perception.

title: 20 Chip Manufacturing Companies in India to Know
summary: This article highlights 20 key chip manufacturing companies in India, emphasizing the country's growing importance in the semiconductor industry across design, fabrication, and materials development. It details how the rise of AI has heightened the demand for advanced chipsets, positioning India as a crucial hub for global semiconductor firms. The listed companies include major players like NVIDIA, AMD, Samsung, and Intel, alongside specialized Indian firms contributing to various aspects of semiconductor technology.

title: Better Chip Stock: Marvell Technology vs. Qualcomm
summary: This article compares Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Qualcomm (QCOM) as semiconductor stock investments. Marvell, despite a near-term slowdown, is driven by AI-oriented chips and acquisitions, while Qualcomm is recovering from a smartphone market downturn with diversification into automotive and IoT, alongside new AI mobile chips. The author concludes that Qualcomm is the better buy due to its lower valuation, higher dividend yield, and anticipated market recovery.

title: Nvidia drives top-performing ETFs in Q1 as clean energy woes continue
summary: Semiconductor ETFs, heavily weighted with Nvidia, were the top performers in Q1 2024, continuing their momentum from the previous year. Japanese equity ETFs also performed strongly due to a weak yen and rising Nikkei 225. Conversely, clean energy ETFs continued to struggle, facing challenges from high-interest rates and potential policy changes.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE), which was established on April 11, 2024. The fund actively manages a portfolio primarily focused on large-cap equities, similar to the S&P 500 and Russell 1000, and sells call options to generate income, aiming for current income and potential capital growth. The list provided shows its top holdings include major technology and financial companies such as NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.

title: Akamai expands cloud services with dedicated GPUs for media workloads
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. has launched a new industry-optimized cloud service utilizing Nvidia RTX 4000 Ada generation GPUs, specifically designed for media workloads to enhance video encoding, decoding, and processing. This service aims to provide faster and more cost-effective solutions for media providers compared to standard virtual machines, leveraging Akamai's global edge network. Although optimized for media, the service is also capable of supporting other demanding tasks like generative AI training and inference.

title: Like Nvidia Stock but Prefer ETFs? This Is the Best Semiconductor ETF to Invest In Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Other Megatrends.
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) is highlighted as an excellent option for investors seeking exposure to the semiconductor industry and megatrends like AI, vehicle electrification, and autonomous driving, while mitigating the risk of investing in individual stocks like Nvidia (NVDA). The ETF has Nvidia as its largest holding, accounting for 20% of its portfolio, and has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over various timeframes due to its focus on the robustly growing chip market. It provides diversification across the semiconductor value chain, including chip producers, foundries, and equipment manufacturers.

title: AMD’s stock can surge 40%, this new bull says — even as Nvidia still dominates
summary: HSBC analyst Frank Lee has upgraded AMD shares to buy from hold, setting a new price target of $225, which suggests a 40% upside. This comes despite Nvidia's dominance in the AI GPU market, as Lee believes there will be multiple winners in this sector. The upgrade reflects a positive outlook for AMD's performance in the burgeoning AI chip industry.

title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

title: Cadence Supercharges Chip Debug And Prototyping With Palladium Z3 And Protium X3 Systems
summary: Cadence Design Systems has launched its new Palladium Z3 and Protium X3 systems, designed to significantly enhance chip debugging and advanced prototyping. The Palladium Z3 offers 1.5 times the performance and double the capacity of its predecessor for pre-silicon debugging, while the Protium X3, built with AMD Versal Premium VP1902 adaptive SoCs, provides 1.5 times faster performance and triple the capacity for advanced physical prototyping. These systems are crucial for accelerating the development of complex chips in the AI era, with general availability expected in Q3 2024.

title: Steering Clients Through Unstable Markets
summary: Paul Schatz, president of Heritage Capital, discussed with etf.com his strategies for navigating client portfolios through current market instability characterized by higher inflation and interest rates. He has reduced exposure to "hot" AI and tech stocks, finding new opportunities in "unloved" sectors like pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and staples. Schatz also shared his market outlook, anticipating a first-quarter peak followed by a minor pullback and potential for fresh highs over the summer, while also discussing his cautious approach to bonds.

title: 4 Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks
summary: Amidst the AI boom led by Nvidia, Morningstar analysts identify Infineon Technologies, STMicroelectronics, MediaTek, and Skyworks Solutions as undervalued semiconductor stocks. The article discusses the varying valuations between digital and analog chipmakers, highlighting the robust demand for AI-related semiconductors versus the mixed recovery in non-AI sectors like automotive, industrial, and personal electronics. It provides detailed reasons for each company's valuation and market position.

title: Cisco Reimagines Security for Data Centers and Clouds in Era of AI
summary: Cisco has unveiled Hypershield, a new security architecture designed to protect data centers and clouds in the era of AI. This innovation allows for security placement anywhere it's needed, providing AI-native security capabilities such as autonomous network segmentation and distributed exploit protection. Cisco Hypershield aims to tip the scales in favor of defenders against sophisticated AI-driven cyber threats, leveraging hardware acceleration and cloud-native technologies like eBPF.

title: How to Find Cloud Software Company Stocks to Trade and Invest
summary: This article provides guidance on investing in cloud computing stocks, detailing different types of cloud services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) and key factors influencing stock performance like market demand and financial health. It offers investment strategies, including individual stocks (e.g., ServiceNow, Zoom) and ETFs (e.g., Global X Cloud Computing ETF), and lists major players like Amazon, Microsoft, and Adobe in the cloud software industry.

title: Super Micro Computer stock crashes 23%, drags down AI darling Nvidia after it doesn't preannounce earnings
summary: Super Micro Computer stock plunged 23% after the company announced its Q3 earnings date without a preannouncement, unlike last year. This lack of a positive preannouncement sparked investor concerns about the strength of its upcoming results. The decline also impacted Nvidia, whose chips are used in Super Micro's servers, causing its stock to fall by about 10%.

title: Nvidia and 3 More AI Stocks With New 'Outperform' Ratings
summary: The article highlights Nvidia and three other AI chip stocks—Microchip Technology, NXP Semiconductors, and Impinj—that have recently received "Outperform" ratings from Evercore ISI Group. It delves into their financial performance, market positions, analyst ratings, and future growth prospects, particularly in the context of the burgeoning AI and IoT markets. The analyst Mark Lipacis projects significant growth and margin expansion for these companies.

title: Akamai Launches Cloud Infrastructure and Services Powered by NVIDIA for Enhanced Video Processing
summary: Akamai Technologies has launched a new media-optimized cloud offering leveraging NVIDIA RTX 4000 Ada Generation GPUs to enhance video processing for the media and entertainment industry. This service aims to provide significantly faster encoding and transcoding compared to traditional CPU-based methods, offering scalable and reliable architectures for various demanding workflows. Beyond media, the GPUs are also applicable to generative AI/ML, data analysis, scientific computing, gaming, and high-performance computing.

title: Big Monthly Dividends From Big Tech: JEPQ Vs. JEPI
summary: The article compares two popular high-yield monthly dividend ETFs, JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI) and JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ). Both ETFs offer exposure to big tech companies and aim to provide income investors with attractive yields. The author intends to compare them to help investors decide which is better for a diversified income portfolio.

title: US chip giant Nvidia snaps up Israeli AI workload management startup
summary: Nvidia has acquired Run:ai, an Israeli startup specializing in AI workload management software, for an estimated $600-$700 million. This acquisition marks Nvidia's largest in Israel since its 2020 purchase of Mellanox Technologies. Run:ai's co-founders and employees will join Nvidia's expanding R&D operations in Israel, aiming to enhance the efficiency of AI computing resources for customers across various infrastructure environments.

title: Nvidia acquires GPU cluster optimization startup Run:ai for reported $700M
summary: Nvidia Corp. has acquired Run:ai, a startup specializing in software that optimizes the performance of graphics card clusters for AI workloads. Although the terms were not officially disclosed, the deal was reportedly valued at $700 million, significantly higher than Run:ai's prior funding. Run:ai's technology aims to boost AI performance by resolving common inefficiencies in GPU-powered servers, such as data exchange delays and memory collisions, allowing for more efficient utilization of GPU resources.

title: 2024 Beijing Auto Show: WeRide's four major products officially debut.
summary: WeRide, a leader in autonomous driving technology, unveiled four core products at the 2024 Beijing Auto Show, including a L2++ mass-produced car, Robotaxi, Robobus, and an autonomous road sweeper. The company showcased these products and offered test rides, highlighting its advanced development capabilities and contributions to the intelligent driving industry. WeRide also emphasized its collaboration with Bosch and the commercial operation success of its autonomous taxi and minibus in various cities.

title: Chiefs of SK Group, Nvidia talk about partnership
summary: SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won met with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang in Silicon Valley to discuss their partnership. SK hynix, an SK Group affiliate, supplies high bandwidth memory (HBM) chips for Nvidia's AI processors. Huang presented Chey with a handwritten note emphasizing their collaboration in creating the future of AI.

title: Conduent and Voya Financial adopt Oracle Database@Azure for cloud migration
summary: Conduent and Voya Financial have successfully migrated their database operations to Oracle Database@Azure, a service co-locating Oracle's Exadata servers within Microsoft Azure data centers. This move has enabled improved application performance, real-time transaction processing, and streamlined IT infrastructure management for both companies. Additionally, Oracle announced the availability of Nvidia H100 and A100 GPUs in its US Government Cloud region, offering advanced AI capabilities for government agencies.

title: CEOs of Microsoft, Nvidia and other tech giants join federal AI advisory board
summary: A federal AI advisory board has been established, comprising prominent tech executives, academics, and civil rights leaders, including CEOs from Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. Led by Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, the board will advise the Department of Homeland Security on the safe application of AI in critical infrastructure and issue recommendations for organizations like power grid operators and manufacturers. This initiative stems from President Biden's executive order on AI, which also mandates data sharing on new AI models' safety with the government.

title: An AI-focused ETF from a hedge fund veteran is gaining attention by soaring 60% over the past year
summary: Ivana Delevska's Spear Alpha ETF (SPRX), an AI-focused fund, has gained attention by soaring over 60% in the past year, significantly outperforming Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF. Delevska, a hedge fund veteran, credits her early identification of the AI boom through extensive industrial sector research. The ETF, with $70 million in assets and about 26 holdings, has an almost 11% weighting in Nvidia, which contributed significantly to its outperformance.

title: Why I Just Added This Ultra-High-Yield Dividend ETF to My Retirement Account
summary: The author recently added the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) to their retirement account, aiming for consistent passive income and price appreciation with lower volatility. JEPQ offers a high dividend yield (9.2% over 30 days, 10.8% over 12 months) through a strategy of holding a high-quality equity portfolio and selling out-of-the-money call options on the Nasdaq-100 index. This approach is designed to generate monthly income and provide relatively steady returns, making it an attractive investment for retirement planning.

title: How To Buy Arm shares from the UK (NASDAQ:ARM)

The company anticipates its new data center will be fully booked within three years, driving discussions to acquire approximately 10,000 Nvidia GPUs annually to meet rising demand for compute services. This expansion positions Sakura Internet as a key alternative to major international cloud providers in Japan.

summary: This article details the holdings of the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC), which launched on October 21, 2013, focusing on information technology equity. The ETF manages $15.298 billion in assets across 286 holdings, tracking a market-cap-weighted index of US information technology stocks. It lists top holdings like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, and provides a comprehensive log of recent changes in its portfolio.

title: Dimensional US High Profitability ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Dimensional US High Profitability ETF (DUHP), which focuses on large-cap US stocks selected for high profitability. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Eli Lilly, and Microsoft, with a section also listing recent changes in its portfolio.

title: iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF Holdings
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the holdings for the iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT), which was established in 2004 and focuses on total market equity.
