title: NVIDIA and Sherwin-Williams Set to Join Dow Jones Industrial Average; Vistra to Join Dow Jones Utility Average
summary: NVIDIA Corp. and The Sherwin-Williams Co. are set to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Intel Corp. and Dow Inc., respectively. This change aims to provide more representative exposure to the semiconductor and materials sectors. Additionally, Vistra Corp. will replace The AES Corp. in the Dow Jones Utility Average to better represent the Independent Power Producers and Renewable Electricity Producers industry.

title: Nvidia to take Intel's spot on Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: Nvidia, a leading force in AI chips, is set to replace Intel on the Dow Jones Industrial Average after Intel's 25-year run, reflecting a significant shift in the chipmaking market. This change comes as Intel's shares have declined significantly and Nvidia's valuation has soared due to its pivotal role in generative AI. Sherwin-Williams will also join the index, replacing Dow, effective next week.

title: AI is now designing chips for AI
summary: AI is increasingly being used to design microchips, a process historically performed by highly skilled engineers. This AI-assisted approach, particularly using reinforcement learning and generative AI, is accelerating chip design, optimizing performance, and improving energy efficiency, which is crucial given the high power consumption of AI. This development could democratize chip design and enable smaller companies and innovators to create custom chips more affordably and quickly.

title: Tech Shake-Up: NVIDIA Supplants Intel In The Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: NVIDIA is replacing Intel in the Dow Jones Industrial Average after Intel's 25-year tenure on the index. This change comes as NVIDIA's shares have soared due to the AI boom, while Intel recently reported a significant loss. The move aims to increase the index's exposure to the semiconductor industry, with NVIDIA's market cap now second only to Apple among publicly traded companies.

title: Earnings call: Silvercrest reports growth amid strategic expansions
summary: Silvercrest Asset Management Group Inc. (NASDAQ: SAMG) reported a 13% year-over-year increase in assets under management (AUM), reaching $35.1 billion in Q3 2024, driven by supportive markets and strategic expansions like new operations in Atlanta and Singapore. Despite a 12% rise in expenses mainly due to compensation, the company is optimistic about future organic net flows and aims to restore adjusted EBITDA margins to the upper 20s. Silvercrest emphasizes its commitment to growth investments and shareholder returns through share buybacks, while maintaining financial flexibility.

title: Arm Stock Price Prediction: 2025, 2030
summary: This article provides a price prediction for Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) stock for 2025 and 2030, highlighting its strong performance due to AI trends but also noting its high valuation. It discusses the methodologies used for stock price prediction, including fundamental and technical analysis, and advises cautious investment due to volatility and analyst concerns about downside risk.

title: Sherwin-Williams will join Dow Jones Industrial Average on Nov. 8
summary: The Sherwin-Williams Co. (NYSE: SHW) is set to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) on November 8, replacing Dow Inc. (NYSE:DOW). This change, along with NVIDIA Corp. replacing Intel Corp., aims to better represent the chemicals/materials and semiconductor industries within the 30-stock index. Sherwin-Williams' inclusion highlights its significant market capitalization and reputation.

title: Nvidia briefly overtakes Apple as world's most valuable company—it began in a breakfast booth at Denny's
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Apple as the world's most valuable company with a market cap of $3.38 trillion driven by its advanced AI chips. The company's journey began with its co-founders at a Denny's in 1993, overcoming early failures to become a dominant force in AI and computing. CEO Jensen Huang attributes their success to a combination of vision, luck, and 61 years of hard work.

title: Nvidia passes Apple as world's most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has once again surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable publicly traded company, reaching a market cap of $3.43 trillion. This comes as Nvidia's stock has nearly tripled in 2024 due to high demand for its GPUs in the AI market, while Apple's shares are up about 17% this year. Nvidia is also set to join the Dow Industrial Average, replacing Intel.

title: Rackspace Brings GPU-as-a-Service to Spot Cloud Platform
summary: Rackspace Technology is introducing a GPU-as-a-service offering to its Rackspace Spot platform, an online auction for cloud servers, to address the growing demand for compute power in AI. This new service, including access to Nvidia GPUs, is designed to provide cost-effective and scalable cloud infrastructure, particularly for AI, machine learning, and data analytics workloads. Rackspace is also expanding its physical presence with a new location in San Jose, California, to support this initiative.

title: Rackspace Technology launches GPU as a Service offering
summary: Rackspace Technology has launched a new GPU as a Service (GPUaaS) offering, leveraging Nvidia H100 processors, as an expansion of its Rackspace Spot platform. This service, housed in a new SJC3 data center in Silicon Valley, provides on-demand access to high-performance GPUs for AI, machine learning, and data analytics without significant upfront hardware investment. The expansion also adds the SJC3 data center as the seventh global location for the Rackspace Spot platform, improving latency and scalability for West Coast customers.

title: Is Apple Inc. (AAPL) a Good Stock to Buy Now According to Redditors?
summary: This article examines Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) as a stock to buy according to Redditors, ranking it 5th on a list of top trending stocks. It discusses recent market trends, the impact of AI on mega-cap stocks, and strategies for hedging risks. The article highlights Apple's strong fundamentals, cash reserves, and growth potential, particularly with its new Apple Intelligence platform, citing positive analyst sentiments and a record-breaking September quarter.

title: NVIDIA Names Ellen Ochoa to Board of Directors
summary: NVIDIA has appointed Ellen Ochoa, former director of NASA's Johnson Space Center and the first Latina astronaut, to its board of directors. Ochoa's extensive experience adds to NVIDIA's mission in computing and AI. Her appointment expands the board to 13 members.

title: A.I. Investor Day & AGIX Bell Ringing at Nasdaq
summary: KraneShares is hosting an A.I. Investor Day and AGIX Bell Ringing ceremony at Nasdaq on November 8, 2024. The event will celebrate the launch of the KraneShares Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AGIX) and feature leading investment strategists, institutional allocators, and executives from top AI firms to discuss investment opportunities in the evolving AI landscape. The discussion will cover the rapid transformation of industries by AI and its impact on the global economy.

title: 3 Ways Stock-Split Stocks Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams Are Modernizing the Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: This article discusses how the additions of Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams to the Dow Jones Industrial Average are modernizing the index. It highlights that stock splits are crucial for inclusion in the price-weighted Dow, unlike market-cap-weighted indexes. The changes signify a shift towards a more tech-focused and growth-oriented Dow, moving away from its traditional emphasis on blue-chip dividend stocks.

title: VCI Global secures $30m investment for AI data center in Malaysia
summary: VCI Global has secured an additional $30 million investment from Alumni Capital for its AI data center in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, bringing the total investment from Alumni Capital to $35 million. The facility, known as the AI Computing Center (AICC), is set to house 512 Nvidia H200 Tensor Core GPUs, with the first phase expected to be completed in Q1 2025. This investment will bolster VCI Global's strategic growth in AI cloud computing and enable it to serve customers globally, especially in Southeast Asia.

title: NVDX Stock Fund Price and Chart — CBOE:NVDX
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the T-Rex 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF (NVDX), including its price, chart, key statistics like AUM and expense ratio, and investment strategy. NVDX is designed for short-term, bullish bets on NVIDIA, offering 200% daily leveraged exposure and involves high risk due to daily rebalancing and potential for total loss if NVIDIA stock declines significantly. The fund invests mainly in cash and targets the information technology sector, specifically semiconductors.

title: NVDX ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:NVDX
summary: This article provides an analysis of the T-Rex 2X Long NVIDIA Daily Target ETF (NVDX), highlighting its objective to offer daily leveraged exposure to NVIDIA's stock. The analysis also covers key statistics such as AUM, expense ratio, dividend history, and price performance.

title: NVIDIA and SoftBank Corp. Accelerate Japan’s Journey to Global AI Powerhouse
summary: NVIDIA and SoftBank Corp. are collaborating to accelerate Japan's sovereign AI initiatives, with SoftBank building Japan's most powerful AI supercomputer using NVIDIA's Blackwell platform and planning for the Grace Blackwell platform. They have also successfully piloted the world's first combined AI and 5G telecom network using NVIDIA AI Aerial, creating new revenue opportunities for telecom operators. SoftBank is also developing an AI marketplace with NVIDIA AI Enterprise to meet Japan's demand for local and secure AI computing.

title: ‘Every Industry, Every Company, Every Country Must Produce a New Industrial Revolution,’ NVIDIA CEO Says
summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang and SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son discussed Japan's pivotal role in the AI revolution at NVIDIA's AI Summit Japan. Huang emphasized that every industry and country needs a new industrial revolution driven by AI, with Japan uniquely positioned to lead in both digital (agentic AI) and physical (robotics AI) domains. A major announcement included NVIDIA's collaboration with SoftBank to build Japan's most powerful AI supercomputer using the NVIDIA Blackwell platform to support sovereign AI initiatives.

title: Are marketers prepared for the oncoming world of AI regulation?
summary: The article discusses the evolving landscape of AI regulation and its implications for marketers. It highlights the challenge for businesses to balance innovation with protecting consumer rights and maintaining trust, especially in the UK where regulatory approaches are still being defined. Key aspects covered include the need for robust AI infrastructure, data privacy, transparency, and the integration of brand values into AI strategies, emphasizing that self-governance and clear guidelines are crucial for navigating this new regulatory environment.

title: Supreme Court weighs Nvidia challenge to securities fraud claims
summary: The Supreme Court is deliberating a challenge from Nvidia to a securities fraud lawsuit alleging the company misled investors about its sales dependence on volatile cryptocurrency miners. Justices appeared hesitant to set a broad rule, focusing instead on whether the plaintiffs met the legal bar for proceeding with a class action under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act. The outcome could significantly impact legal standards for similar lawsuits against corporations.

title: Amdocs sees record revenues, expands gen AI capabilities
summary: Amdocs reported record revenues of $5 billion for fiscal 2024, a 2.4% year-over-year increase, driven by strong performance across all global regions. The company is focusing on enhancing profitability by phasing out less profitable business segments and expanding its generative AI platform, amAIz, with new capabilities developed in collaboration with NVIDIA AI and AWS for improved customer experience and operational efficiency. Amdocs secured wins with major customers like AT&T, T-Mobile US, Charter Communications, and Rogers, highlighting its commitment to innovation in cloud, monetization platforms, and AI.

title: Microchip Releases PolarFire® FPGA Ethernet Sensor Bridge to Accelerate Real-time Edge AI with NVIDIA Holoscan
summary: Microchip has launched its PolarFire FPGA Ethernet Sensor Bridge, designed to help developers create AI-driven sensor processing systems compatible with the NVIDIA Holoscan platform. This solution leverages PolarFire FPGAs for energy-efficient, low-latency connectivity and multi-protocol sensor compatibility, initially supporting MIPI CSI-2 and D-PHY. It aims to accelerate real-time edge AI in various sectors by enabling seamless integration of sensor data with NVIDIA Holoscan, NVIDIA IGX, and NVIDIA Jetson platforms.

title: AGNC Investment Stock: A Top 15% Yielder To Buy For 2025 (Rating Upgrade) (NASDAQ:AGNC)
summary: AGNC Investment is poised to benefit from recent Federal Reserve rate cuts, which are expected to reduce interest costs and enhance profitability. This could lead to an upward repricing of its book value and a higher valuation. Despite potential risks related to inflation or stronger economic growth affecting future rate decisions, AGNC's attractive 15% yield and low price-to-book ratio make it a promising investment for 2025.

The article highlights Alphabet's YTD price performance and current market capitalization.

summary: Nvidia's upcoming earnings report is anticipated to cause significant volatility in the S&P 500, with options markets predicting a larger impact than typical economic data releases. Given Nvidia's substantial contribution to the S&P 500's returns and EPS growth, its performance is seen as a key indicator for overall market sentiment. Traders are considering hedging strategies, such as put spreads on the Nasdaq-100 ETF, in anticipation of potential market turbulence.

title: Wall Street’s hottest stock, Palantir, only employs 3,892 people
summary: Palantir (PLTR) has become the S&P 500's best-performing stock, reaching a $150 billion market capitalization despite having fewer than 4,000 employees. The company's shares surged after strong third-quarter earnings, driven by demand for its AI platform, and a planned move to Nasdaq. This remarkable valuation highlights a growing trend in AI-adjacent stocks where market cap per employee is a significant metric.

title: Nvidia Corp. (NVDA)-Makes Big Beat on Earnings, But Stock May Need Santa to Propel to New All Time High
summary: Nvidia Corp. (NVDA) reported strong third-quarter earnings, surpassing Wall Street expectations and easing concerns about AI-related valuations, with CEO Jensen Huang emphasizing a foundational transformation in computing. While the beat indicates strong momentum, the stock might remain range-bound in the near term between $190 and $212. A "Santa Claus rally" or positive macro data, alongside sustained demand and successful platform rollouts, could propel the stock to new highs.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Third Quarter Fiscal 2025
summary: NVIDIA reported record quarterly revenue of $35.1 billion for the third quarter of fiscal 2025, a 94% increase year-over-year, driven by strong growth in Data Center revenue which reached $30.8 billion. The company highlighted the ongoing impact of AI across industries and revised its outlook for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 with expected revenue of $37.5 billion.

title: To understand Nvidia, take a look at its CEO and culture
summary: A new book, "The Nvidia Way," by Tae Kim, explores the critical role of CEO Jensen Huang and the company's unique work culture in its significant success, particularly in the AI industry. The excerpt highlights Huang's intense work ethic, his use of "fear and anxiety" as motivational tools, and the demanding environment that shaped Nvidia's early years, including a fierce competitive challenge from Intel. The article emphasizes that beyond technology, the distinct culture fostered by Huang has been a key driver of Nvidia's dominance.

title: Under the Hood of Fidelity’s Blue Chip Growth ETF
summary: The Fidelity Blue Chip Growth ETF (FBCG) is an actively managed U.S. equity growth strategy that has significantly outperformed its benchmark, the Russel 1000 Growth index. The fund employs bottom-up fundamental analysis to identify blue chip companies with sustainable business models, high returns on equity, and strong earnings growth, including both mega-cap and emerging companies. FBCG's active management and investment strategy have led to attractive returns for investors.

title: Under the Hood of Fidelity’s Blue Chip Growth

FBCG's portfolio includes both mega-cap companies and smaller emerging growers, aiming for consistent +10% annual EPS growth.

The outlook also details the evolving impact of AI on both investment portfolios and financial advisor practices. It aims to help advisors navigate challenges and opportunities for their clients in 2025.

title: Palantir and Nvidia rank high on list of companies with rising sales estimates

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Invests $4B in Anthropic: A Strategic Move to Challenge NVIDIA in AI
summary: Amazon.com (AMZN) has invested $4 billion in AI startup Anthropic, aiming to challenge NVIDIA's dominance in the AI chip market. This strategic move is intended to make Amazon's Neuron SDK more appealing by having Anthropic teams work directly with AWS personnel to facilitate customer transitions from NVIDIA. The article also touches upon the broader adoption of AI in manufacturing and its observed benefits like increased efficiency and productivity.

title: CHIPS Act funding released to Intel for Ohio plants, jobs
summary: The Biden administration has awarded Intel nearly $8 billion in CHIPS Act funding, with $1.5 billion specifically designated for its semiconductor fabrication plants in Ohio. This funding supports Intel's anticipated $90 billion investment in various states, including Ohio, by the end of the decade, as part of a larger $100+ billion expansion strategy. Ohio leaders, including Gov. Mike DeWine, welcomed the announcement, emphasizing its importance for economic and national security and for making advanced computer chips in America.

title: Three Reasons to Avoid Corning and One Stock to Buy Instead
summary: This article analyzes why investors should be cautious with Corning (GLW) despite its recent stock performance. The author highlights three main reasons: sluggish long-term revenue growth, stalled EPS growth, and low return on invested capital. Instead, the article recommends considering ServiceNow and other "Strong Momentum Stocks" for the upcoming bull run.

title: The S&P 500 (SPY) Is Ready for a Sell-Off
summary: The S&P 500 has gained 25% year-to-date, but this growth is largely driven by a small group of "Magnificent 7" stocks, pushing valuations above historical averages. While the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) is generally recommended for long-term investors due to the market's historical resilience, the current concentration of gains suggests a potential sell-off is due. Despite this, experts advise against shorting the market and suggest that consistently investing in SPY can still lead to long-term gains, as bear markets are typically short-lived and followed by stronger bull markets.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Faces Competition as Chinese Chips from Zhaoxin Close the Gap in AI Race
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is facing increasing competition in the AI chip market from Chinese manufacturers like Zhaoxin, despite US export restrictions. While Chinese chips are improving, they still lag behind American technology. The article notes that AMD ranks 6th on a list of AI stocks, but suggests other AI stocks may offer higher returns.

title: Yext, Inc. (YEXT) Positioned for Stronger Growth with Expansion into Social Media and Usage-Based Pricing
summary: Yext, Inc. (YEXT) is poised for stronger growth through its recent acquisition of Hearsay Systems, expansion into social media with "Yext Social," and adoption of usage-based pricing for resellers. These strategic moves aim to accelerate innovation and create long-term value, enhancing Yext's position in the cloud-based digital knowledge management and search solutions market. The company is leveraging AI to drive cloud computing growth and focusing on sustainability within the cloud industry.

title: nVent collaborates with Nvidia on AI-ready liquid cooling solutions
summary: nVent announced its collaboration with Nvidia to deploy liquid cooling solutions for hyperscale and high-performance computing data centers, specifically for the Nvidia GB200 NVL72 and next-generation platforms. This partnership aims to enhance performance and energy efficiency using nVent's customized cooling technology, which includes coolant distribution units, liquid-to-air heat exchangers, and manifold products. The collaboration also extends to delivering solutions for the Open Compute Project (OCP) community to address demand and improve reliability in data centers.

title: What’s up with… KT, Swisscom, MTN
summary: KT Corp. is reorganizing its corporate structure, merging its Enterprise Division with its Strategy and New Business Division to become an AICT company, with the goal of leading the B2B IT market through global cooperation. Swisscom has launched its Swiss AI Platform, providing sovereign data storage and processing for enterprises, with initial applications including GPU-as-a-service and an AI Work Hub, and a GenAI Studio launching in 2025. MTN Group has renewed its focus on Open RAN by collaborating with the ORAN Development Company (ODC) to develop and test innovative Open RAN solutions for the African market.

title: European AI infra company Nebius nabs $700M from Nvidia, Accel, others
summary: Nebius, a European AI infrastructure company formerly known as Yandex N.V., has raised $700 million from investors including Nvidia, Accel, and Orbis, to fund its U.S. expansion. The company plans to use the capital to build out its data center footprint, employing a hybrid approach with co-location facilities and greenfield sites. This funding, combined with existing capital, brings Nebius's total available funds to build infrastructure to around $3 billion, with further capital raises anticipated, and the company projects an annualized run rate of $750 million to $1 billion by the end of 2025.

title: Nvidia leads $700M investment in ex-Yandex CEO Arkady Volozh’s Nebius Group to fuel AI infrastructure growth
summary: Nebius Group, founded by former Yandex CEO Arkady Volozh, has secured $700 million in a private placement led by Nvidia, Accel, and Orbis Investments to expand its AI infrastructure. This funding will enable Nebius to scale GPU clusters and cloud platforms to meet increasing AI demand. The company plans to invest $1 billion by mid-2025 and has significantly raised its annualized run-rate revenue forecast.

title: ZJK Industrial up 172% on NVIDIA cooling components deal, enhanced collaboration
summary: ZJK Industrial's shares surged 172% after announcing an expanded collaboration with NVIDIA Corp to provide liquid cooling systems for NVIDIA's AI infrastructure. ZJK, which previously supplied metal fasteners to NVIDIA, is creating a specialized team for this new endeavor. The Chinese company, which recently completed its IPO, also counts BYD, DJI, GoerTek, Samsung, and Garmin among its clients.

title: Why Momentum Stocks & ETFs Are Crushing the Market
summary: Momentum stocks and ETFs are significantly outperforming the broader market this year, with factor investing in momentum demonstrating strong returns over the past decade. The Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO) and iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF (MTUM) have seen substantial gains due to the continued investor interest in high-performing stocks. Companies like Amazon, NVIDIA, Meta Platforms, Berkshire Hathaway, and Eli Lilly are key holdings driving the performance of these momentum-focused ETFs.

title: ASX November Tech Winners: NoviqTech leads as AI boom lifts ASX technology stocks
summary: The ASX technology sector experienced a significant boom in November, largely driven by the global enthusiasm for Artificial Intelligence (AI). The Info Tech (XIJ) index rose by 10%, outperforming the S&P/ASX 200 index, with NoviqTech leading the charge with a 423% increase in stock price. Other major beneficiaries this year include Appen and Life360, while the article also details the top 50 ASX tech winners and several losers for the month.

title: REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF (CEPI), which was created on December 4, 2024, by REX Shares. The ETF has $78.72 million in assets under management with 28 holdings, focusing on crypto-related and digital payment solutions stocks, and aims to generate income by writing covered call options. The list includes top holdings such as Micron Technology, Applied Digital Corp, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd ADR, along with various options contracts.

title: ZJK Stock Skyrockets Following NVIDIA Partnership News
summary: ZJK Industrial’s stock surged over 170% after announcing a collaboration with NVIDIA to produce liquid cooling components for next-generation AI servers. This partnership positions ZJK as a key supplier for NVIDIA, highlighting its engineering capabilities and commitment to innovation. However, the stock’s limited float, lack of analyst coverage, and speculative valuation warrant caution for investors despite the positive development.

title: Nvidia tangos with an activist hedge fund: An excerpt from Tae Kim's new book on the tech giant
summary: This article is an excerpt from Tae Kim's book "The Nvidia Way," detailing Nvidia's encounter with activist hedge fund Starboard Value in 2013 and its subsequent acquisition of Mellanox. Starboard initially pushed Nvidia for a stock buyback program and divestment from non-GPU projects, a strategy that led to significant returns for Starboard. Later, Starboard's activist stake in Mellanox indirectly led to Nvidia's multi-billion dollar acquisition of the company, which proved crucial for Nvidia's dominance in AI and data center computing.

title: Nvidia Could See Competition From Magnificent Seven Peer
summary: Nvidia, a leading AI chipmaker, could face significant competition from Amazon, another Magnificent Seven peer, which is developing its own AI chip called "Trainium." This new chip, expected in 2025, aims to challenge Nvidia's market dominance in AI model training. The article discusses how Amazon's entry could provide a viable alternative to Nvidia's offerings and its potential impact on the chip market.

title: Thailand and Vietnam Embrace Sovereign AI to Drive Economic Growth
summary: Thailand and Vietnam are actively pursuing sovereign AI initiatives to boost their economies. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang met with prime ministers in both countries to discuss collaborations, including an AI R&D center in Vietnam and the expansion of computing infrastructure in Thailand, emphasizing the importance of utilizing domestic data and workforces for AI development.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Challenges NVIDIA with Faster, Cheaper AI Chip Alternatives
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is emerging as a credible alternative to NVIDIA in the AI chip market, offering faster speeds at lower prices, according to industry analysts. This development comes amid new U.S. government curbs on chip exports to China, aiming to limit Chinese access to advanced AI technology. While AMD shows promise, the article suggests other AI stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

title: AI's growth is just getting started, BlackRock's thematic ETF head says
summary: BlackRock's U.S. head of thematic and active ETFs, Jay Jacobs, believes the AI boom is still in its early stages, highlighting infrastructure and cybersecurity as key areas for growth in 2025. He emphasizes that the theme extends beyond megacap tech to include various semiconductor, data center, and software companies. Jacobs also points out the need for physical infrastructure, including energy, materials, and real estate, to support AI's expansion.

title: NoTraffic Reduces Road Delays, Carbon Emissions With NVIDIA AI and Accelerated Computing
summary: NoTraffic, a member of the NVIDIA Inception program, utilizes NVIDIA AI and accelerated computing to reduce traffic congestion, mitigate accidents, and cut carbon emissions. Their AI Mobility platform, deployed in cities like Tucson and Vancouver, predicts road scenarios and optimizes traffic signals, leading to significant reductions in delays, economic benefits, and improved air quality. The company estimates potential annual economic savings of $14 billion across the U.S. if its technology were widely adopted.

title: People are buying the wrong stock thinking it's part of OpenAI's Stargate Project
summary: A biotechnology company named Metagenomi (MGX) saw its stock price surge by as much as 33% due to investor confusion, as many believed it was involved in OpenAI and SoftBank's new "Stargate Project." The actual MGX involved is a technology fund from the UAE, not the biotech firm. The Stargate Project aims to invest $500 billion in AI infrastructure, with figures like Donald Trump, Sam Altman, and Masayoshi Son backing it, though some, like Elon Musk and Gavin Baker, have expressed skepticism about the funding claims.

title: Oracle surges, chip stocks rise as Trump unveils $500 bln AI plan
summary: Oracle's shares jumped nearly 9% after former President Donald Trump announced a $500 billion AI infrastructure investment plan, "Stargate," involving Oracle, OpenAI, and SoftBank to keep the U.S. competitive in the global AI race. Other technology partners and AI-related companies, including Nvidia, Microsoft, Arm, Vistra, Dell Technologies, Super Micro Computer, and Palantir Technologies, also saw their stock prices increase following the news. The plan includes an immediate $100 billion investment, with the remaining over the next four years, and construction of data centers already underway in Texas.

title: NVDY: Its 6-Month Outperformance Could Be Overturned Soon, A Complete Analysis
summary: This article analyzes the YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NVDY), noting its current option portfolio distribution across bull puts, call credit spreads, and long naked calls. The analysis suggests that NVDY could start to underperform Nvidia stock (NVDA) if NVDA appreciates above $152 before February 21, 2025, and no additional profits are captured unless NVDY surpasses $163 by that date. Given analyst ratings and short-term market outlook predicting a continued upward trend for NVDA, NVDY's recent 6-month outperformance might soon be reversed.

title: NYSEARCA:NVDY: High-Yield Income ETF or Missed NVIDIA Gains?
summary: The YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:NVDY) offers an attractive 84% dividend yield through a covered call strategy on NVIDIA stock. While it provides high monthly income, this approach caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to NVIDIA's direct stock appreciation. Investors must weigh NVDY's income generation against its limited total return, dividend volatility, and specific risks like single-stock dependence and tax inefficiency.

title: XLK vs. QQQ: Which Tech ETF Should You Buy for Your Portfolio?
summary: This article analyzes two popular tech-heavy ETFs, XLK and QQQ, comparing their long-term performance, expense ratios, dividends, and portfolio allocations. While both have outperformed the S&P 500, XLK focuses purely on tech, making it more concentrated in top tech stocks like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. QQQ offers broader diversification by including communication services and consumer cyclicals, which may appeal to investors seeking less concentration in the top three tech giants.

title: Nvidia calls China’s DeepSeek R1 model ‘an excellent AI advancement’
summary: Nvidia praised China's DeepSeek R1 model as "an excellent AI advancement" despite concerns that it contributed to a 17% drop in Nvidia's stock. DeepSeek's open-source reasoning model reportedly outperformed leading U.S. models like OpenAI's, with significantly lower training costs. Nvidia views this breakthrough as a driver for more work for its GPUs, emphasizing "Test Time Scaling" as a new form of scaling law in AI development.

title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

title: One reason Nvidia looks like a bargain stock following the DeepSeek news
summary: Following a 17% decline in Nvidia's stock and investor concerns about DeepSeek's low-cost generative AI development, the article suggests that Nvidia might be considered a bargain stock. Despite fears that the hardware build-out driving Nvidia's growth may slow, the author emphasizes that it is still very early in the analysis of the DeepSeek news. Other technology stocks like Micron, Super Micro, and Workday are also mentioned as trading at relatively low valuations.

title: How a more focused growth index can cut your risk in the stock market
summary: The S&P 500 has become a riskier investment due to its high concentration in "Big Tech" growth stocks, as evidenced by Nvidia's recent stock drop after news of a competitor's cheaper AI technology. An alternative growth strategy, such as one offered by the Invesco S&P 500 Pure Growth ETF, can help investors mitigate this risk while maintaining exposure to large-cap growth stocks in a low-cost index fund.

title: DeepSeek Panic Live Updates: Nvidia Stock Drops 4% As Trump Reportedly Targets China Exports
summary: Nvidia's stock experienced a turbulent week, dropping 4% on Wednesday, amid concerns over a less capital-intensive AI model from China's DeepSeek and reports that Donald Trump is considering stricter export controls on semiconductor chip sales to China. The market volatility, driven by DeepSeek's competitive AI model and potential geopolitical shifts, prompted analysts to reassess the AI investment landscape, though many still view Nvidia's selloff as an overreaction. DeepSeek's emergence has led to significant shifts in market values for major tech companies and ignited debates about the future of AI development and U.S. competitiveness.

title: NVDU: Providing Leverage To Nvidia, A Hot Tech Stock That Has Corrected (NASDAQ:NVDU)
summary: Nvidia's rapid growth has led to its stock outperforming the NASDAQ and Apple, despite a high P/E ratio. The Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 2X Shares ETF (NVDU) amplifies NVDA's performance but carries significant risk due to its leveraged nature. A recent correction in NVDA's stock could present a buying opportunity, though its valuation remains high.

title: These ETFs can help investors protect themselves from selloffs like the DeepSeek panic — but here’s the catch
summary: This article discusses how buffer ETFs can help investors protect against market selloffs, such as the recent DeepSeek panic that affected technology stocks. It highlights that these ETFs have outperformed the broader market recently but advises investors to understand the trade-offs involved. The article also mentions the allure of leveraged ETFs tied to Nvidia despite recent market volatility.

title: Trump Pauses Tariffs on Mexico; DJIA Closes Down Only 122 Pts; Nasdaq Still Lags with 1%+ Drop
summary: US President Donald Trump postponed new tariffs on Mexico for a month after reaching an agreement on border troops, which slowed but didn't halt a stock market decline. The DJIA closed down 122 points, while the S&P 500 slipped 0.8% and the Nasdaq saw the steepest decline at 1.2%. The iShares MSCI Mexico ETF (EWW.US) rebounded, and major tech stocks like Tesla, Nvidia, and Apple saw losses narrow slightly.

title: Western Digital Corporation (WDC): Jim Cramer Calls It ‘Way Too Cheap’
summary: Jim Cramer has called Western Digital Corporation (NASDAQ:WDC) stock "way too cheap," citing numerous analyst upgrades. The article also discusses Cramer's broader market observations, including the chaotic earnings season and the stir caused by DeepSeek's claim of training an AI model for a fraction of the typical cost, which Cramer and SemiAnalysis question. Western Digital ranks third on a list of stocks Cramer recently analyzed, with 66 hedge funds holding stakes, though the article suggests AI stocks may offer higher and quicker returns.

title: AI to drive 165% increase in data center power demand by 2030
summary: Goldman Sachs Research anticipates a significant surge in global data center power demand, projecting a 50% increase by 2027 and up to 165% by 2030 compared to 2023, primarily driven by the expansion of artificial intelligence. This growth will strain infrastructure, necessitating substantial investment in the electric grid and potentially affecting supply-demand balance for data centers, with occupancy rates expected to peak around 95% in late 2026 before moderating. The market expansion presents diverse investment opportunities across hyperscalers, asset managers, utilities, and data center operators.

title: GA NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 Instances
summary: CoreWeave announces it is the first cloud provider to offer generally available NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-based instances, establishing a new bar for AI performance and scalability. This move reinforces CoreWeave's leadership in providing cutting-edge NVIDIA GPUs and managed cloud services for generative AI, agentic AI, and HPC workloads. The GB200 NVL72 instances combine 36 NVIDIA Grace CPUs and 72 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs in a liquid-cooled, rack-scale design, offering significant performance improvements and cost reductions for advanced AI tasks.

title: Supermicro Ramps Full Production of NVIDIA Blackwell Rack-Scale Solutions with NVIDIA HGX B200
summary: Supermicro has announced the full production availability of its end-to-end AI data center Building Block Solutions accelerated by the NVIDIA Blackwell platform, offering both air-cooled and liquid-cooled options. These new systems, featuring the NVIDIA HGX B200, provide significantly enhanced cooling capacities and increased GPU densities for demanding AI workloads. Supermicro emphasizes its commitment to delivering sustainable, cutting-edge solutions for AI innovation, including comprehensive rack-level integration and deployment services.

title: 1 Unstoppable BlackRock ETF With 40.6% of Its Portfolio Invested in Broadcom, Amazon, Nvidia, Microsoft, and Palantir
summary: The iShares U.S. Tech Independence Focused ETF (IETC), managed by BlackRock, reportedly has 40.6% of its portfolio invested in five major tech companies: Broadcom, Amazon, Nvidia, Microsoft, and Palantir. This ETF focuses on U.S.-based tech companies that develop most of their technology and generate most of their revenue domestically, often coinciding with the booming AI sector. The ETF significantly outperformed the S&P 500 in 2024, with a 37.4% return, driven by the strong performance of its top holdings, and has shown consistent outperformance since its inception in 2018.

title: The Nvidia Way: How Nvidia Became the Most Valuable Company in History
summary: This article reviews "The Nvidia Way: Jensen Huang and the Making of a Tech Giant" by Tae Kim, detailing Nvidia's journey to becoming the world's most valuable company. It highlights CEO Jensen Huang's leadership, the company's commitment to excellence, strategic hiring, and generous stock programs as key factors. The book emphasizes that Nvidia's success was not a fluke but the result of foresight, hard work, and perseverance through early struggles, particularly in anticipating and capitalizing on the AI revolution.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 02/02/2025
summary: This article identifies the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending February 2, 2025, detailing their returns and the key market drivers behind their performance. Highlights include ETFs inverse to natural gas and semiconductors, and leveraged ETFs tracking Alibaba, Meta, and Apple, driven by factors like weather forecasts, economic stimulus, AI advancements, and company earnings.

title: 3 Reasons Vanguard’s VGT ETF Belongs in Your Portfolio
summary: The Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT) is presented as a strong option for investors seeking aggressive tech exposure, especially in the growing AI boom. The article highlights three key advantages: its superior focus on tech compared to the Nasdaq 100, its heavy concentration in leading tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, and its competitive low expense ratio of 0.09%. These factors position VGT as a viable choice for future-proofing portfolios despite concerns about an overextended AI trade.

title: NXP buys Kinara for $307M to power AI workloads at the network edge
summary: Dutch chipmaker NXP Semiconductors N.V. has acquired California-based startup Kinara Inc. for $307 million to bolster its presence in AI workloads at the network edge. This acquisition, following two other recent deals, aims to strengthen NXP's Industrial & IoT group and expand its AI capabilities, especially after a period of declining revenues. Kinara's neural processing units, like the Ara-1 and Ara-2 NPUs, are expected to provide NXP with viable edge AI products and help diversify its chip business.

title: Earnings call transcript: Astera Labs Q4 2024 sees strong growth but stock dips
summary: Astera Labs (ALAB) reported record Q4 2024 revenue of $141.1 million, a 179% year-over-year increase, and EPS of $0.37, meeting analyst expectations. Despite strong financial performance and a 147% stock return over six months, shares dipped 3.79% in aftermarket trading. The company forecasts continued growth with Q1 2025 revenue guidance of $151-$155 million, driven by demand in AI and cloud infrastructure, and anticipates 2025 to be a "breakout year."

title: CEO of Canada’s Celestica sells $130-million of shares, cashing in on Nvidia-style gains during AI boom
summary: Celestica's CEO, Rob Mionis, sold $130-million in company shares amidst an AI boom that saw the company's stock price surge over 1,000 percent, akin to Nvidia's growth. Other top executives also sold shares, totaling $212-million, as Celestica's profits jumped significantly due to its critical role in providing storage and network switches for AI-driven data centers, making hyperscalers a major portion of its revenue. This financial success comes after decades of languishing stock prices, with the company finding new life through its high-end manufacturing for the AI sector.

title: Supermicro forecasts crazy revenue growth for fiscal 2026, boosting its stock
summary: Super Micro Computer Inc. saw its stock jump 9% after forecasting at least $40 billion in revenue for fiscal 2026, driven by demand for its AI hardware and direct-liquid cooling technology. This ambitious projection significantly exceeded Wall Street expectations, despite the company also revising down its fiscal 2025 guidance and facing scrutiny over financial irregularities and an ongoing Justice Department probe. The company is actively working to finalize its financial results to meet a critical February 25 deadline set by financial regulators.

title: DeepSeek Shows There Is No Longer One “AI Trade,” but Many
summary: Recent developments from Chinese company DeepSeek, particularly its R1 model which uses fewer expensive chips, are signaling a new phase in AI investing. Fund managers now anticipate greater divergence among AI players, with potential benefits for companies like Broadcom and Marvell due to a shift towards more efficient chips. While efficiency gains could impact demand for some hardware, the overall growth in AI applications is expected to compensate, benefiting key players like Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor.

title: The top 6 chip producers in the world by revenue share
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) leads the global chip foundry market with a 62% revenue share in 2024, followed by Samsung at 10%. The article details the top six chip producers by revenue, highlighting that Taiwanese companies collectively hold 70% of the total global chip foundry revenue, which reached $131.7 billion in 2024.

title: How Fidelity Growth Company’s Big Nvidia Bet Influences Its Rating
summary: Fidelity Growth Company's performance is heavily influenced by its significant investment in Nvidia, which constitutes about 16% of its assets. While lead manager Steve Wymer has an excellent track record and the fund has consistently outperformed, the high concentration in Nvidia introduces risk due to the stock's volatile history and reliance on the nascent AI GPU market. Despite the fund's large size limiting agility, its exceptional leadership and long-term performance continue to earn it a Silver Morningstar Medalist Rating.

title: Is the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) still a brilliant buy after storming higher?
summary: The iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) has delivered impressive returns, averaging 16.1% per year over the past decade, driven by its investment in major US companies. While its current price-to-earnings ratio of 29x is high, the author believes the ETF still has a good future, despite not being a "brilliant buy" at its current valuation. The fund provides exposure to leading global businesses in sectors like cloud computing, AI, and e-commerce.

title: After Earnings, Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter results with growth in client and data center revenue, though its AI GPU growth forecast was disappointing compared to competitor Nvidia. Morningstar has cut its fair value estimate for AMD to $140 per share from $160 due to less optimism about AI GPU growth, but still considers the stock undervalued. The company holds a narrow economic moat based on intangible assets in chip design and expects significant long-term growth, particularly in the data center segment for AI applications.

title: SoundHound AI, Serve, Nano-X in focus as Nvidia exits stakes; cuts Arm investment (NVDA:NASDAQ)
summary: Nvidia has reportedly exited its stakes in SoundHound AI, Serve Robotics, and Nano-X, causing shares of these companies to fall sharply in premarket trading. The Jensen Huang-led chipmaker also reduced its investment in Arm, according to its latest 13-F filing. This move indicates a shift in Nvidia's investment portfolio.

title: LI's Broadridge, Verint testing new Chinese chatbot DeepSeek for automation, efficiency
summary: Long Island-based technology companies Broadridge Financial Solutions and Verint Systems are testing DeepSeek, a Chinese artificial intelligence app, to improve efficiency and automate processes. Despite potential security risks and bans on government devices, both companies are evaluating DeepSeek in closed systems, seeing it as a cost-effective alternative to other AI tools like ChatGPT. Experts note DeepSeek's ability to train on smaller datasets as an advantage.

title: This Innovative ETF Lets You Follow Republican Politicians’ Trades | KRUZ ETF
summary: The Unusual Whales Subversive Republican Trading ETF (KRUZ) allows investors to track the stock trades of Republican members of Congress and their spouses, capitalizing on their statistically uncanny market outperformance. This innovative ETF, which has outperformed the S&P 500 by over 50% since its inception, suggests that politicians may be trading on insider knowledge despite denials. While there are ethical concerns and reporting lags, the KRUZ ETF provides a legal method for investors to potentially benefit from these informed trades, with top holdings including JPMorgan Chase, Comfort Systems USA, and Nvidia.

title: 2 High-Powered ETFs That Have Doubled in 5 Years. Here's Why It's Not Too Late to Invest.
summary: This article highlights two growth-focused ETFs, the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) and the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG), both of which have doubled in value over the past five years. It explains why these ETFs, concentrating on large and mid-cap growth stocks, particularly in tech and consumer discretionary sectors, remain attractive investments. The article suggests that despite past gains, continued growth in areas like AI and strong underlying companies make them suitable for long-term investors.

title: JEPQ vs JEPI: Are these boomer candy ETFs good buys in 2025?
summary: The article compares the JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI) and the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ), which are known for their high dividend yields. While these ETFs offer attractive monthly income, their total returns have historically underperformed passive S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 index funds. The author suggests that for long-term investors, the vanilla S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices remain superior due to their better historical total returns despite lower yields.

title: NVDW ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:NVDW
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Roundhill NVDA WeeklyPay ETF (NVDW), highlighting its objective to deliver weekly income and 1.2x leveraged exposure to NVIDIA (NVDA) stock performance. It details the fund's structure, inclusion of total return swap agreements, and investments in short-term US Treasurys for collateral. The analysis also covers key statistics such as AUM, dividend yield, expense ratio, and mentions the inherent risks due to leverage and lack of diversification.

title: Want to be a millionaire by 2040? Here's how $200K today could grow to $1 million tomorrow with this BlackRock Tech ETF
summary: BlackRock's iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (ETN) offers a potential path to turn $200,000 into $1 million by 2040, primarily by investing in major technology companies with strong AI exposure like Meta, Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. The ETF has shown impressive historical returns, averaging 11% yearly since 2001 and 20.3% over the last decade, far outpacing the S&P 500. However, investors are advised to diversify their portfolios due to potential risks if AI growth fails to meet expectations.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report, maintaining a "fairly valued" rating with a long-term fair value estimate of $130 per share. The analysis highlights the critical role of Nvidia's data center business, its wide economic moat due to GPU technology and CUDA software, and the "Very High" uncertainty rating given competitive pressures and market dynamics. Despite potential slowdown concerns and competition, Morningstar projects robust growth for Nvidia's data center revenue in the near-to-medium term.

title: Why Leveraged ETFs Are for the Birds
summary: Leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have seen significant inflows, but analysis reveals investors typically gained only slightly more than holding unleveraged assets directly, while incurring much higher fees and volatility. Even when held for their intended single-day period, these ETFs did not fully capture the leveraged return of their reference assets, and their performance deteriorated over longer holding periods, suggesting they are unsuitable for both speculation and long-term investment. The article concludes that investors should generally avoid these products due to their high costs, increased volatility, and failure to consistently deliver their promised leveraged returns.

title: STMicroelectronics unveils new data center photonics chip for AI workload interconnects
summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced the PIC100, a new data center photonics chip designed in collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to support 800Gbps and 1.6Tbps optical interconnects for AI workloads. This chip, the first in STMicroelectronics' new PIC product family, combines silicon photonics and BiCMOS technologies on a 300mm silicon platform. It aims to provide ultra-high speed and low-power optical technology, essential for sustainable AI growth, and will be manufactured in France.

title: 8 Best Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy in 2025
summary: Quantum computing, while still largely in an R&D phase, holds immense potential to revolutionize technology with its ability to accelerate computing speeds far beyond current capabilities. Despite a projected commercialization timeline closer to the mid-2040s, advancements in cloud computing are increasing accessibility for developers, making it a promising, albeit speculative, investment area. The article highlights key companies such as IonQ, Microsoft, Nvidia, Alphabet, Intel, Honeywell, IBM, and Rigetti Computing, suggesting that investors can gain exposure through these tech giants or specialized quantum computing ETFs.

title: Chip design software maker Cadence revenue skyrockets with AI demand
summary: Cadence Design Systems reported strong Q4 2024 revenues, up 27% to $1.36 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-driven chip design software and expanded partnerships with major chipmakers like Nvidia and Intel. Despite robust growth and a 2024 fiscal year revenue of $4.6 billion, the company's 2025 financial outlook fell below Wall Street expectations due to uncertainties in its China business amid evolving trade restrictions. Cadence expects flat growth in China for 2025 and plans to be prudent with its guidance given the market's unpredictability.

title: Sudden Ascent: Is Recursion Pharmaceuticals NVIDIA’s AI Favorite?
summary: Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) stock surged following an SEC filing that revealed NVIDIA maintained its stake in the AI-driven drug discovery company, interpreting this as a strong endorsement. While the market views Recursion as a key AI biotech investment, analysts suggest the stock might be overvalued given its lack of profitability and high valuation metrics. The article advises investors to conduct thorough due diligence, weighing the high-risk, high-reward nature of the investment against the company's significant potential in AI-powered drug discovery.

title: KULR Technology Group, Inc. (KULR): Among the Hot Penny Stocks to Buy Now
summary: KULR Technology Group, Inc. (NYSE:KULR) is highlighted as one of the top penny stocks to buy, trading at $2.02 with an 815.91% six-month performance. The company specializes in advanced thermal management technologies for battery safety and efficiency, expanding into the AI infrastructure market through strategic partnerships. KULR reported record revenue of $3.19 million in Q3 2024, driven by growth in paying customers and engineering services, positioning it as a promising investment in the evolving AI ecosystem.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FYEE)
summary: MarketBeat has provided a review and aggregate rating for the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE). Based on analyst ratings for its 24 top holdings, FYEE has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $28.80. The article details the consensus ratings and price targets for the ETF's main components, highlighting key companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: NVIDIA Earth-2 Features First Gen AI to Power Weather Super-Resolution for Continental US
summary: NVIDIA's Earth-2 platform introduces CorrDiff, a generative AI weather model that significantly enhances forecast precision for the continental U.S., down to a kilometer scale. This model, optimized to be 10,000 times more energy-efficient than traditional methods, aims to improve disaster preparedness and save lives by providing high-resolution predictions of extreme weather events. It is already being adopted by various agencies and companies globally, demonstrating its potential for applications in regional forecasting, renewable energy, and disaster management.

title: Palantir, Super Micro, Nvidia, Alibaba, Berkshire Hathaway, Rivian, Apple, and More Stock Movers
summary: A slide in technology stocks pulled down the S&P 500 on Monday, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 1.2%. This decline followed a report that Microsoft had cancelled data-center leases, leading to a broader sell-off in the tech sector. Key movers included Palantir, Super Micro, Nvidia, Alibaba, Berkshire Hathaway, Rivian, and Apple.

title: Nvidia to report earnings amid infrastructure spending, DeepSeek concerns
summary: Nvidia is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, capping off a remarkable year of growth driven by demand for its data center GPUs essential for AI. Despite its success, investors are cautious due to concerns about major customers potentially tightening capital expenditure and the emergence of more efficient AI models from startups like DeepSeek, which could reduce the need for extensive GPU infrastructure. CEO Jensen Huang is expected to address these concerns, emphasizing the continued need for GPUs in AI development and deployment, particularly for the inference phase.

title: Investors Cautious on Nvidia Stock Before Earnings
summary: Nvidia (NVDA) stock sees investor caution ahead of its fourth-quarter earnings report, despite a historical trend of post-earnings gains. Concerns about China's AI advancements and potential slowing demand from customers have led to the stock sliding, with Wolfe Research calling the report a "make or break" moment. Options traders are pricing in a significant post-earnings move of 15.2%, higher than its average swing, while volatility expectations remain low.

title: Nvidia sales grow 78% on AI demand, company gives strong guidance
summary: Nvidia reported strong fiscal fourth-quarter earnings, beating Wall Street expectations, with revenue up 78% year-over-year due to high demand for its AI-accelerating GPUs. The company also issued robust guidance for the first quarter, projecting $43 billion in revenue, driven by the anticipated "significant ramp" of its next-generation Blackwell AI chips. While growth rates are moderating as Nvidia scales, its data center business, now 91% of total sales, continues to surge, with CEO Jensen Huang noting "amazing" demand for Blackwell.

title: After waiting with bated breath, Wall Street absorbs Nvidia's latest earnings
summary: Nvidia's latest earnings report reveals $39.3 billion in revenue, a 78% increase for the quarter ending in January, surpassing analyst expectations. The chipmaker's performance is crucial for Wall Street due to its significant market valuation and central role in the AI sector. Despite strong revenue, Nvidia's forward-looking statements signaled slowing profit-margin growth, and concerns about sustaining the rapid pace of AI investment and recent economic slowdowns have impacted its stock.

title: Bull of the Day: Celestica Inc. (CLS)
summary: Celestica Inc. (CLS) is highlighted as a "Bull of the Day" due to its role in providing technology infrastructure for AI, energy transition, and other megatrends. Despite a recent market selloff, CLS stock has significantly outperformed Nvidia and the broader tech sector, with strong projected revenue and earnings growth for 2025 and 2026. The company also trades at a discount compared to the Zacks Tech Sector based on forward earnings and sales, making it an attractive buy on the dip.

title: Tencent fires up AI race with model it says outdoes DeepSeek
summary: Tencent Holdings Ltd. has launched its new AI model, Hunyuan Turbo S, which it claims outperforms DeepSeek, intensifying the AI race particularly in convergence economies. The model is designed for instant responses and will be integrated into Tencent's consumer chatbot, Yuanbao, and made available to developers. This move follows a series of AI model rollouts from tech giants like Alibaba and Baidu, all aiming to compete with the influential DeepSeek.

title: What’s Happening With Palantir Stock?
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) experienced a significant rally followed by a sharp decline, raising questions about its current valuation. Morningstar equity analyst Mark Giarelli views Palantir as having strong growth potential, especially given its leadership in AI technologies, and currently sees the stock as fairly valued despite its high volatility. Investors are cautioned to consider the stock's wide swings, the impact of potential government spending cutbacks, and the large but uncertain total addressable market for its analytical software.

title: Focus on Execution as Synopsys Reports Solid 1Q FY 2025 Financial Results
summary: Synopsys reported solid 1Q FY 2025 financial results, with revenue of $1.455 billion andGAAP EPS of $1.89, demonstrating strong performance driven by design activity and its قیادت in various technology sectors. The company's focus on hardware-assisted verification, EDA software, and AI innovations, particularly for 2nm projects, positions it for continued growth. Synopsys expects 2Q FY 2025 revenue between $1.585 billion and $1.615 billion, anticipating a 10.0% year-over-year growth.

title: Astera Labs CFO Michael Truett sells $10.38m in common stock
summary: Astera Labs (NASDAQ: ALAB) CFO Michael Truett sold 125,800 shares of common stock for approximately $10.38 million on February 26, 2025, through a charitable remainder unitrust. This transaction follows a 13% stock decline in the past week, though the company still boasts a 73% gain over six months. Despite strong Q4 2024 revenue growth and optimistic Q1 2025 guidance, investor concerns about future growth have caused recent stock dips, though analysts maintain "Buy" ratings with high price targets.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FYEE)
summary: The Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE) holds an aggregate "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $29.02. This rating is based on 758 analyst ratings for 24 companies, which constitute 52.7% of the ETF's portfolio. Top holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, with NVIDIA showing a potential upside of 53.4%.

title: 2 ‘Strong Buy’ Stocks to Snap Up If You Want to Invest in Elon Musk’s DOGE
summary: Elon Musk, in his role heading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), is focused on cutting federal spending and expanding his economic influence through AI and other ventures. For investors looking to capitalize on this, Nvidia (NVDA) and Modine Manufacturing (MOD) are highlighted as "Strong Buy" stocks. Nvidia is crucial to Musk's AI efforts, powering xAI's supercomputers, while Modine, a longtime Tesla supplier, provides essential thermal management solutions for data centers and EVs, positioning both companies for significant growth.

title: ServiceNow’s newest AI agents bring intelligent automation to telecommunications firms
summary: ServiceNow has introduced new AI agents at MWC25, developed with Nvidia, to bring intelligent automation to telecommunications firms. These agents aim to boost productivity for Communication Service Providers by autonomously solving problems in customer service and network operations, building upon the existing Now Assist for Telecommunications Service Management platform. The integrated system promises to enhance customer experiences, streamline billing, and free up human engineers for higher-level work.

title: Is Advanced Micro Devices Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock has significantly underperformed the Nasdaq Composite, falling 26.7% over the past three months and 43.4% over 52 weeks. This underperformance is attributed to competition, particularly from Chinese AI models and NVIDIA, and concerns about its AI market share. Despite beating Q4 revenue and EPS estimates, analysts are moderately bullish with a "Moderate Buy" rating, projecting a 47.8% potential upside.

title: JFrog advances AI security with Hugging Face partnership, Nvidia NIM and new MLOps platform
summary: JFrog has announced new initiatives to enhance AI security and deployment, including a partnership with Hugging Face for advanced security scanning of machine learning models. The company is also integrating its platform with Nvidia Inference Microservices (NIM) for secure deployment of GPU-optimized models and launching JFrog ML, an MLOps solution to unify ML development with DevSecOps practices. These efforts aim to bring greater trust, transparency, and security to the AI supply chain, addressing concerns about malicious models and simplifying generative AI application delivery at scale.

title: Anthropic Stake for the Masses Is Latest ETF Industry Gambit
summary: KraneShares has acquired a direct stake in Anthropic, a privately held AI company and competitor to OpenAI, through one of its exchange-traded funds. This move combines the surging interest in artificial intelligence with the growing trend of offering private assets to a broader investment audience. Anthropic recently secured funding at a valuation exceeding $60 billion.

title: Semrush CEO Oleg Shchegolev sells $368,509 in company stock
summary: Oleg Shchegolev, CEO of SEMrush Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:SEMR), sold 32,932 shares totaling $368,509 to cover tax obligations from restricted stock units vesting. This was not a discretionary trade, and Shchegolev retains significant direct and indirect ownership in the company. Semrush reported strong Q4 2024 earnings and positive Q1 2025 guidance, and announced leadership changes with Bill Wagner becoming CEO and Shlagle transitioning to CTO.

title: Malaysia to pay Arm Holdings $250 million for chip design blueprints
summary: Malaysia will pay Arm Holdings $250 million over 10 years for chip design blueprints to enable local manufacturers to produce their own chips within the next decade, driven by the AI boom. Arm will also establish its first Southeast Asian office in Kuala Lumpur, and the deal includes training 10,000 engineers in Malaysia. This initiative aims to build a complete supply chain in advanced industries like AI data servers, autonomous vehicles, and robotics, and to foster 10 local chip companies with significant yearly revenue.

title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, remains committed to Juniper buy, faces tariff issues
summary: HPE announced it will lay off 2,500 employees over the next 18 months as part of a cost-cutting program, despite reporting a 16% increase in Q1 revenue for fiscal year 2025. The company also faces challenges including a higher-than-normal AI server inventory, a U.S. Justice Department lawsuit delaying its $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, and potential impacts from U.S. government tariffs. HPE remains committed to the Juniper acquisition and plans to mitigate tariff effects through supply chain adjustments and pricing.

title: What is CoreWeave, the cloud-services provider expected to go public next week?
summary: CoreWeave, an AI cloud services provider, is preparing for an initial public offering slated for next week. The company operates 32 data centers housing over 250,000 Nvidia GPUs, with Nvidia itself holding a 6% stake and acting as a key partner. CoreWeave's customer concentration could be a concern for potential investors as it enters the public market.

title: Chipmakers are finding that good earnings aren't enough to please Wall Street
summary: Despite the ongoing AI boom, chipmakers like Marvell Technology, Nvidia, and Advanced Micro Devices are struggling to impress Wall Street, as investor expectations have reached historically high levels. Even with strong earnings and revenue, many stocks have plunged because guidance or performance fell short of optimistic projections. This trend highlights the immense pressure on semiconductor companies amidst the AI buildout and increasing scrutiny from investors.

title: Signs of AI Slowdown Could Be Evident in Nvidia, Palantir
summary: Recent price weakness in Nvidia and Palantir may indicate a potential slowdown in the AI market, despite continued integration of AI technology in various industries. This suggests that the initial AI hype might have already been priced into these stocks, leading to muted responses even to positive earnings reports. The article provides trading options for investors looking to capitalize on either bullish or bearish movements in these AI-related stocks.

title: Applied Digital: A High-Risk Lottery Ticket In The AI Infrastructure Gold Rush (NASDAQ:APLD)
summary: Despite a 23% stock drop since November and concerning fundamentals, Applied Digital is shifting from crypto mining to AI cloud services and HPC hosting. The company faces significant financial challenges, evidenced by a 12.75% preferred equity deal and a monthly cash burn of $21 million, without securing major clients for its North Dakota facilities. While its 400MW AI infrastructure has the potential for $2 billion in annual revenue, Applied Digital remains a speculative investment due to strong competition and unfavorable financing terms.

title: LAYS Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:LAYS
summary: The LAYS ETF, named "STKd 100% NVDA & 100% AMD ETF," is an actively managed fund seeking long-term capital appreciation through a double-stacked stock strategy involving NVIDIA and Advanced Micro Devices. It utilizes swap agreements and/or listed options to provide leveraged exposure to these semiconductor companies. title: Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) Extends Collapse as Tech Stock Carnage Exposes Giant Valuation Gap
summary: Palantir (PLTR) stock has experienced a significant collapse, losing over a third of its market cap in three weeks, despite its upcoming inclusion in the S&P 100. This downturn is attributed to a broader tech stock selloff, concerns over potential defense spending cuts by the U.S. government—a major client for Palantir—and the exposure of previously lofty valuations in the tech sector. Analysts currently rate PLTR as a Hold, with an average price target suggesting a 20% upside.

title: Is It Time to Buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: After a recent selloff in the US stock market, the valuations of the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap tech stocks have decreased. Morningstar analysts now consider Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet to be undervalued, while Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Despite these changes, Morningstar strategists continue to recommend underweighting growth stocks, favoring a rotation into value stocks.

title: Is Nvidia’s Favorite Autonomous Vehicle Stock a Buy?
summary: Nvidia's recent $25 million investment in Chinese autonomous vehicle (AV) company WeRide has sparked interest, but the stock's initial surge has vanished. While WeRide is a global AV leader operating in several countries, it faces significant risks including regulatory hurdles, intense competition from companies like Waymo and Tesla, and substantial financial losses. Nvidia's investment is primarily seen as a strategic move to expand its GPU market in China's AV sector.

title: Is it time to buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks have experienced a significant selloff, making some of them appear undervalued according to Morningstar's fair value metrics. While Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet are now rated as buys, Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Morningstar strategists still recommend underweighting growth stocks overall, favoring value stocks due to market volatility and slowing economic growth.

title: Block Becomes First Company in North America to Deploy the Latest NVIDIA GB200 Systems for Frontier Models
summary: Block, Inc. announced it will be the first company in North America to deploy the NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with DGX GB200 systems, housed in an Equinix data center. This infrastructure will support Block's research and training of open-source generative AI models, aiming to advance AI in underexplored areas. The initiative underscores Block's commitment to open-source innovation and solving real-world problems for its customers and community, building on its previous work in deepfake detection and hyper-realistic generated audio.

title: Cerence Inc. Launches Cerence xUI, Innovative AI Assistant Platform for Next-Generation In-Vehicle User Experiences
summary: Cerence Inc. has launched Cerence xUI™, an AI assistant platform designed to enhance in-vehicle user experiences through conversational AI, leveraging both edge and cloud technologies. This platform, developed in collaboration with NVIDIA and Microsoft, utilizes the CaLLM™ family of large language models to offer personalized and engaging automotive voice interactions. Cerence xUI allows automakers flexible integration options for new and existing vehicles, promising advanced performance, reduced latency, enhanced privacy, and cost efficiency.

title: SoundHound AI to Spotlight Voice AI Innovation at 2025 NVIDIA GTC
summary: SoundHound AI will participate in the 2025 NVIDIA GPU Technology Conference (GTC) to showcase its latest voice AI innovations. The company will demonstrate next-gen in-vehicle voice assistants leveraging generative AI on the edge with NVIDIA DRIVE AGX, and introduce a groundbreaking in-car voice commerce ecosystem. SoundHound AI aims to redefine in-vehicle experiences and unlock new revenue streams for automotive partners by integrating advanced AI solutions.

summary: The article suggests that BlackRock Science and Technology Trust (BST) presents a buying opportunity after a 13% decline, trading at a discount to its Net Asset Value (NAV). summary: The NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on analysis of its holdings.
