title: OSRH Stock Plummets to 52-Week Low at $1.31 Amid Market Challenges
summary: Bellevue Life Sciences Acquisition Corp. (OSRH) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $1.31, reflecting tumultuous market conditions and an oversold RSI. The company’s market capitalization is now $25.6 million, with a one-year return of -86.51% and a YTD return of -87.55%. In other news, OSR Holdings secured an equity financing agreement with White Lion GBM Innovation Fund, allowing it to sell up to $80 million in common stock to White Lion to enhance its financial flexibility in the medical device industry.

title: China’s Leapmotor says it can weather an expansion of chip curbs
summary: Leapmotor, a Chinese electric vehicle maker, believes it can withstand an expansion of US chip curbs despite using chips from Qualcomm and Nvidia. The company's co-President, Michael Wu, stated they would be "fully-prepared" for any restrictions, foreseeing a small chance of the EV sector being directly affected. Leapmotor is also exploring a potential collaboration with Ferrari and is making progress with its current joint venture with Stellantis.

title: NVIDIA Launches Family of Open Reasoning AI Models for Developers and Enterprises to Build Agentic AI Platforms
summary: NVIDIA has introduced the Llama Nemotron family of open reasoning AI models, designed to help developers and enterprises build advanced AI agents. These models, enhanced through post-training, show significant improvements in accuracy and inference speed for complex reasoning tasks. Major companies like Microsoft, SAP, and ServiceNow are already integrating these models into their platforms to enhance AI-driven solutions and productivity across various industries.

title: Storage vendors rally behind Nvidia at GTC 2025
summary: At GTC 2025, twelve major storage vendors, including Cloudian, Cohesity, Dell, HPE, Hitachi Vantara, NetApp, VAST Data, and WEKA, announced support for Nvidia's AI stack, integrating their products with Nvidia's Blackwell Ultra GPUs and AI Data Platform. These integrations focus on providing high-performance storage, data management, and AI infrastructure solutions to accelerate AI training and inferencing workloads for enterprise customers. The announcements feature new reference architectures, software enhancements for AI data processing, and optimized systems leveraging Nvidia's GPU, networking, and software technologies.

title: NVIDIA Honors Americas Partners Advancing Agentic and Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA recognized 14 Americas partners with its 2025 NVIDIA Partner Network awards at the GTC 2025 conference for their leadership in advancing agentic and physical AI across various industries. New award categories like 'Trailblazer,' 'Rising Star Consulting,' and 'Innovation' were introduced to highlight partners driving AI adoption, growth, and creative solutions. These partners have been instrumental in helping customers integrate NVIDIA-accelerated AI technologies, including large language models and generative AI chatbots, to solve complex challenges and transform business operations.

title: NVIDIA Teams Up with GE, Google, IBM, And CrowdStrike To Redefine AI And Quantum Technology
summary: NVIDIA is establishing the NVIDIA Accelerated Quantum Research Center (NVAQC) in Boston and announced multiple collaborations to advance AI and quantum computing. Partnerships include Google for AI initiatives across various industries, GE HealthCare for autonomous medical imaging, IBM for enterprise AI capabilities, and CrowdStrike for AI-driven cybersecurity. Additionally, NVIDIA is working with General Motors on AI-powered vehicles and Yum! Brands to revolutionize AI in the restaurant industry.

title: GM, Nvidia to partner on AI tech for factories and next-gen vehicles
summary: General Motors and Nvidia are expanding their partnership to integrate AI-powered technology into GM's next-generation vehicles and across its production facilities. This collaboration will see GM using Nvidia's Drive AGX platform for advanced autonomous driving and in-cabin experiences, and Nvidia's Omniverse platform to create digital twins of factories for enhanced efficiency and virtual testing. The initiative aims to leverage AI to revolutionize vehicle technology and manufacturing processes, addressing the growing demand for software-defined vehicles and personalized customer experiences.

title: Jensen Huang Clarifies on His Explosive Comment on Quantum Computers at Nvidia GTC
summary: During Quantum Day at Nvidia GTC 2025, CEO Jensen Huang clarified his earlier statement that practical quantum computers were 15 to 30 years away, which had caused stock prices of quantum computing companies to fall. Huang announced Nvidia's investment in a new Boston-based research facility, the Nvidia Accelerated Quantum Research Center (NVAQC), to accelerate quantum computing innovations, focusing on integrating A.I. supercomputing with quantum systems and error correction. While acknowledging significant progress, panelists agreed that full-scale practicality for quantum computing is still under development, and Huang challenged the industry to prove its real-world value.

title: Nvidia unveils next generation of DGX SuperPOD in partnership with Equinix
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its next generation of DGX SuperPOD, the DGX GB300 SuperPOD, designed to enhance AI inferencing with Grace Blackwell Architecture. This release comes as Nvidia partners with Equinix Inc., which will manage Nvidia's Instant AI Factory services, providing ready-made, AI-ready data centers globally. The collaboration aims to democratize AI deployment, enabling organizations to quickly scale and operate advanced AI infrastructure.

title: NVDY: Usually Not A Fan, But The Huge Distribution Yield Is Currently Tempting
summary: The YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NVDY) offers a very high distribution yield, currently 121.82%, by selling call options on Nvidia stock. While tempting due to increased implied volatility, the author notes concerns such as high expense ratios and potential for suboptimal call-selling prices, especially in volatile markets. Despite these drawbacks, the author considers NVDY an attractive option over NVDA in the short term, particularly given Nvidia's historical April performance and upcoming May earnings.

title: Lumentum (LITE) Joins NVIDIA’s AI Photonics Ecosystem for Faster Data Centers
summary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE) has joined NVIDIA's AI Photonics Ecosystem, leveraging its high-power, high-efficiency lasers for deployment in NVIDIA Spectrum X Photonics networking switches. This collaboration aims to enhance system resiliency, reduce power consumption, and enable faster AI infrastructure deployments, showcasing the potential of photonic technologies to replace traditional copper interconnects in large-scale AI operations. The article also touches on the growing threat of AI-driven cybercrime and its significant financial impact.

title: 4 Best Leveraged ETFs of Last Week
summary: Last week, the stock market saw moderate gains across major indices, driven by the Fed's decision to hold interest rates steady, NVIDIA's GTC event, and mixed retail sales data. Against this backdrop, several leveraged ETFs performed well, including those focused on energy, uranium, Brazilian stocks, and financials. These ETFs capitalized on the week's market movements, with MicroSectors Energy 3X Leveraged ETNs, Defiance Daily Target 2x Long Uranium ETF, ProShares Ultra MSCI Brazil Capped, and Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares showing significant gains.

title: Why Nano Dimension Stock Crashed on Tuesday
summary: Nano Dimension stock dropped 16.5% after a Delaware court ruled in favor of Desktop Metal, forcing Nano Dimension to go through with its acquisition of Desktop Metal. Nano Dimension had attempted to back out of the $183 million deal announced nine months prior, leading to legal action from Desktop Metal. The ruling mandates Nano Dimension to execute a national security agreement with CFIUS and close the purchase by March 31, or extend the deadline.

title: Accelerating the Future of Transportation with SES AI’s NVIDIA-Powered Innovation for Electric Vehicles
summary: SES AI is utilizing NVIDIA hardware and software, including domain-adapted large language models (LLMs) and GPU-accelerated simulations, to rapidly discover new battery materials for electric vehicles. This collaboration has led to significant advancements, such as a 1.6Kx acceleration in simulation workflows, a 25% increase in LLM accuracy, and the identification of 17 candidate materials, resulting in a 20% improvement in battery cycle life. Their approach aims to reduce the time for battery electrolyte discovery from five years to six months, driving sustainable transportation.

title: Advanced Micro Devices Can Double in Price: Here’s Why
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is projected to double in price due to its increasing market share against NVIDIA in GPU and CPU markets, driven by lower-cost, better-performing products. Insider transactions, strong institutional buying, and an undervalued stock price relative to its growth potential support this optimistic outlook. The company is actively addressing its software infrastructure gap to further compete with NVIDIA.

title: S&P 500 Gains and Losses Today: Supermicro, Arista Networks, and Nvidia Lead Tech Selloff
summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.1% on Wednesday, March 26, 2025, as a tech selloff led by Super Micro Computer, Arista Networks, and Nvidia, combined with news of potential tariffs on car imports, impacted the market. Super Micro Computer shares plunged after a Goldman Sachs downgrade, while Nvidia stock slipped amid concerns over AI chip sales in China. In contrast, Cintas shares surged after better-than-expected earnings, with Paychex also gaining from its quarterly report, and Dollar Tree rose after announcing the sale of its Family Dollar brand.

title: What does fast food want with Nvidia?
summary: Yum Brands, parent company of Taco Bell and KFC, has announced a development deal with Nvidia to integrate AI technology into its 61,000 restaurants. This partnership aims to give Yum Brands full control over its data and intellectual property, diverging from its previous reliance on third-party tech partners. The goal is to use Nvidia's tech to process store-level performance, generate personalized action plans for managers, and ultimately enhance efficiency without replacing human workers.

title: Is This the Best Artificial Intelligence ETF On the Market You Should Be Buying?
summary: This article evaluates the Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AIQ) as a top investment option for gaining diversified exposure to the AI megatrend. It highlights AIQ's broad reach across the AI ecosystem, its 15.8% annualized return since 2018, and its holdings beyond just chip manufacturers to include software and data companies like ServiceNow and Snowflake. The ETF is presented as a calculated play on AI's future growth, offering opportunities with less individual stock risk.

title: CoreWeave Stock Treads Water After Scaled-Back IPO
summary: CoreWeave, an AI-focused cloud computing company, had a lukewarm IPO, with its stock ending the day at its IPO price of $40 per share, despite initial stumbling. The future of the CHIPS Act also remains a wildcard, despite bipartisan support.

summary: This article discusses the transformative impact of "AI²" — artificial intelligence (AI) and artificial incretins (GLP-1s) — on the technology and healthcare sectors. The authors emphasize that both innovations are multiyear developments with vast, evolving investment potential beyond their initial applications, making them crucial for active managers to monitor.

title: Enlight wins tender to develop solar-powered data center in southern Israel

The company plans to invest up to $1.1 billion in the project, which will include a solar and energy storage facility integrated within the data center complex. This development aims to leverage existing high-voltage transmission and communication networks in the region, which is already home to the Ashalim Solar Complex.

Pichai's remaining direct holdings in Alphabet amount to over 2.7 million shares.

title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta’s latest Llama models put open-source AI in the driving seat
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has unveiled new additions to its Llama family of large language models, including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which Zuckerberg claims will put open-source AI in the driving seat. These models are available through Meta AI assistant and are designed for enhanced reasoning skills and various tasks. Meta is also working on a larger model, Llama 4 Behemoth, intended to be the "highest performing base model in the world."

title: SOUN Stock Down 63.1% YTD: Buying Opportunity for AI Investors?
summary: SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock has fallen significantly by 63.1% YTD and 25% in March, raising questions about a potential buying opportunity for investors. The decline is attributed to a global trade war and a class-action lawsuit alleging financial misstatements. Despite current challenges, SoundHound's diversification, strategic partnerships with major tech and automotive companies, and large addressable markets suggest strong future revenue growth, although its current valuation remains high.

title: Quantum Computing Stocks Climb as Rigetti, IonQ Tapped for Department of Defense Program
summary: Shares of Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and IonQ (IONQ) gained significantly after being selected for a Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) program aimed at developing a "useful" quantum computer by 2033. Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) also saw its shares rise as part of the initiative, contributing to a broader rally in quantum computing and AI stocks despite general market concerns over new tariffs.

title: Aehr Test Systems Reports Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Revenue Growth and Solid Bookings and Backlog
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced its financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2025, reporting a significant increase in net revenue to $18.3 million, up from $7.6 million in the prior year. The company highlighted strong bookings of $24.1 million and effective backlog of $21.8 million, driven by expansion into new markets like AI processors, gallium nitride (GaN) semiconductors, and hard disk drive components, diversifying revenue beyond silicon carbide. Despite strong performance and new market penetration, Aehr temporarily withdrew its fiscal 2025 guidance due to uncertainty regarding tariff announcements.

title: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest buys $13M tumbling tech stock, dumps Bitcoin ETFs
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has been strategically buying shares of Coinbase, Amazon, and Nvidia, amounting to over $26 million, following President Donald Trump’s "reciprocal tariffs" announcement. Concurrently, the firm has divested $12 million from its ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB). This move comes as tech stocks have stumbled and Bitcoin ETFs have experienced significant outflows.

title: Nvidia, NetApp, Palo Alto, Lumen Team with Google Cloud
summary: At Google Cloud Next, several major technology companies announced new or expanded partnerships with Google Cloud. Nvidia is collaborating on agentic AI with Blackwell-powered infrastructure and Gemini LLMs, while Palo Alto Networks announced surpassing $1.5 billion in sales on the Google Cloud Marketplace. Lumen plans to integrate Google Cloud WAN with its connectivity services, and NetApp has added new capabilities to Google Cloud NetApp Volumes to simplify high-performance workloads.

summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) saw its stock rebound on Wednesday, surging 12.3% to close at 158.75 after a four-day losing streak. The prior decline of 17% was attributed to news of Trump tariffs. Despite facing various challenges, the world's top chip foundry managed to snap its recent slide.

The piece emphasizes diversification and the potential to mitigate market volatility through income-generating investments.

title: Tigress Financial raises Intel stock price target to $52 on AI growth

The acquisition builds on an existing infrastructure hosting deal between the two companies, with CoreWeave utilizing Core Scientific's data centers to power its AI-optimized public cloud, which provides access to over 250,000 Nvidia graphics cards.

The company's stock touched $164.42 intraday but ended the day at $162.88, slightly below the $163.93 needed to close above the $4 trillion threshold.

title: Stock Market Today: Nvidia Slips Nearly 1% on Tariff Jitters Despite ARK Fund Confidence
summary: Nvidia's stock declined by almost 1% due to concerns over potential new tariffs on semiconductor imports, impacting the broader chip sector. Despite this dip, institutional investors like Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs showed continued confidence by purchasing additional Nvidia shares. Analysts at Bank of America maintained a positive outlook with a $220 price target, citing strong demand for Nvidia's AI infrastructure products.

title: China fears Nvidia chips could track, trace and shut down its AIs
summary: China has raised concerns that Nvidia's H20 AI chips could contain backdoors allowing the US to track, locate, and remotely shut down its artificial intelligence systems. The Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) summoned Nvidia for an explanation and proof, citing allegations that US lawmakers have called for "tracking and positioning" functions in advanced chips exported abroad. While Nvidia denies these claims, the incident reflects China's growing apprehensions about potential vulnerabilities in foreign-made technology, reminiscent of previous US suspicions regarding Huawei.

title: Super Micro Computer stock falls as much as 16% after quarterly results, profit forecast miss estimates

The company reported adjusted EPS of $0.41 and revenue of $5.76 billion, both below analyst estimates, attributing the shortfall to capital constraints and supply chain issues with Nvidia. Despite the recent dip, shares were still up over 50% year-to-date as investors continue to flock to AI-themed stocks.

title: Cyngn Reports Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results
summary: Cyngn Inc. announced its second quarter 2025 financial results, highlighting a $32 million capital raise to extend its cash runway through 2027 and a relocation to an expanded headquarters in Mountain View, CA. The company reported continued expansion of DriveMod deployments across various industries and significant advancements in its technology roadmap through collaboration with NVIDIA and the adoption of generative AI. Despite sales cycles for emerging technologies often delaying immediate revenue recognition, the company remains focused on scaling deployments and converting commercial momentum into long-term growth.

title: Trump demands resignation of Intel CEO over China ties
summary: Former President Donald Trump has demanded the immediate resignation of Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, citing his alleged ties to China and calling him "highly conflicted." This demand comes amid Trump's broader pressure on the semiconductor industry, including a threat of a 100% tariff on imported chips, and follows concerns raised by Sen. Tom Cotton regarding Tan's investments in Chinese companies with links to the People's Liberation Army. Intel's stock dropped over 2% following the news, further complicating the company's efforts to catch up with competitors in the AI race.

summary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. announced a significant expansion of its U.S.-based semiconductor facility to boost manufacturing of ultra-high-power (UHP) lasers, essential for AI-driven Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) platforms. This investment aims to create high-skilled jobs and strengthen the U.S. position in the global AI supply chain, partnering with companies like NVIDIA to support next-generation AI infrastructure with energy-efficient optical interconnects.

The piece emphasizes the importance of dividend reinvestment for boosting total returns.

title: What Is NVIDIA’s Three-Computer Solution for Robotics?

This solution includes NVIDIA DGX for AI training, NVIDIA Omniverse with Cosmos on RTX PRO Servers for simulation, and NVIDIA Jetson AGX Thor for on-robot inference, forming a complete development architecture. This approach addresses the data gap in robotics by leveraging synthetic data generation and digital twins for robust robot learning and deployment.

title: Why Clean Energy Fuels Stock Raced Nearly 13% Higher Today
summary: Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE) stock surged nearly 13% after the company reported better-than-expected second-quarter earnings. The renewable natural gas specialist beat analyst estimates on both revenue and non-GAAP net income, driven by strong demand and beneficial government policies like the clean fuel production tax credit. The company is also expanding its reach by securing new supply arrangements with large municipal transit fleets, indicating a promising outlook for its niche market.

title: Under new, unusual agreement, U.S. will get a 15% cut of Nvidia and AMD chip sales to China
summary: The U.S. government has reached an unusual agreement with Nvidia and AMD, securing a 15% cut of their revenues from chip sales to China in exchange for export licenses. This deal follows the Trump administration's earlier halt on advanced chip sales to China due to national security concerns. While President Trump confirmed the terms, the agreement has sparked controversy among lawmakers and experts regarding its legality and potential implications for national security and the future of export controls.

title: NVIDIA Opens Portals to World of Robotics With New Omniverse Libraries, Cosmos Physical AI Models and AI Computing Infrastructure
summary: NVIDIA has announced new Omniverse libraries and Cosmos world foundation models to advance robotics development and deployment. These tools are powered by new RTX PRO Servers and DGX Cloud, enabling developers to create physically accurate digital twins, reconstruct the real world in simulation, and generate synthetic data for training physical AI models. Key developments include Omniverse NuRec 3D Gaussian Splatting for world reconstruction, Cosmos WFMs for diverse data generation, and Cosmos Reason, a 7-billion-parameter reasoning VLM for physical AI.

title: Nvidia, Ansys Partner To Integrate Omniverse Into Simulation Tools: Here’s More
summary: Ansys, now part of Synopsys Inc., has partnered with Nvidia Corp. to integrate Nvidia's Omniverse technology into its engineering simulation products. This collaboration aims to streamline simulation workflows for complex applications like autonomous systems and fluid dynamics. By embedding Omniverse capabilities directly into Ansys software, engineers can develop more accurate models, test virtual scenarios, and accelerate product development.

title: Trump’s Political Tax on Nvidia Chips to China
summary: The Trump Administration controversially reversed its ban on Nvidia selling H20 chips to China, a decision driven by an agreement for Nvidia to pay 15 percent of its revenue from these sales to the U.S. government. This move, similar to past actions regarding TikTok and Nippon Steel, raises concerns that the administration is leveraging national security for political or financial gain. This trend could undermine the credibility of future national security arguments and make policymakers hesitant to address legitimate threats.

Despite the short-term issues, CEO Gary Dickerson expressed optimism about long-term demand for computing power and the new administration's focus on increasing semiconductor supply in the U.S.

summary: NVIDIA has released a new open dataset called Granary, featuring approximately 1 million hours of audio, to address the scarcity of data for multilingual speech AI. This dataset was used to train high-accuracy and high-throughput AI models, NVIDIA Canary-1b-v2 and NVIDIA Parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v3, for audio transcription and translation across 25 European languages. These tools aim to help developers scale AI applications, support global users, and foster innovation in speech AI by providing essential resources, especially for underrepresented languages.

This collaboration introduces data intelligence into the storage platform, enabling context-rich, AI-ready data integration and enhancing object storage capabilities for AI workloads. The initiative also seeks to drive operational efficiency with remote direct memory access transfers, significantly boosting performance for AI applications.

(EVLV) and raised its price target from $7 to $9, citing the company's strong 29% year-over-year revenue growth. Evolv Technologies, known for its AI-based weapons detection systems, also issued upward guidance, leading to increased investor optimism.

The government's stake could increase to 15% if Intel's foundry business underperforms.

Despite these mixed results and ongoing China trade policy headwinds, analysts remain overwhelmingly bullish on Nvidia's long-term prospects due to strong AI demand.

The company expects continued rapid growth in the global AI market, with management projecting cumulative data center spending of up to $4 trillion over the decade, supporting Morningstar's view that AI spending will not decline soon.

summary: NVIDIA is collaborating with U.S. government labs and leading companies to construct America’s AI infrastructure, aiming to accelerate scientific discovery and economic growth. This initiative includes significant GPU deployments at national laboratories (Argonne and Los Alamos) and the establishment of the NVIDIA AI Factory Research Center in Virginia, which will serve as a blueprint for large-scale AI factory build-outs. summary: NVIDIA is collaborating with the South Korean government and industrial leaders such as Samsung, SK Group, and Hyundai Motor Group to significantly expand the nation's AI infrastructure with over 260,000 NVIDIA GPUs. Despite concerns about cash burn, the company, which integrates Bitcoin mining into its business, plans to continue its Bitcoin-anchored balance sheet strategy.

summary: Citi analyst Atif Malik raised the price target for Lam Research (LRCX) stock to $190 from $175, maintaining a "Buy" rating, due to increasing AI investments and strong company performance. Despite acknowledging LRCX's potential, the article suggests that other AI stocks might offer greater upside and less downside risk.

title: Peter Thiel dumps entire Nvidia stake, slashes Tesla holdings amid bubble fears
summary: Billionaire investor Peter Thiel has sold his entire stake in Nvidia and significantly reduced his holdings in Tesla, citing growing concerns over an AI-fueled bubble in technology valuations. This divestment follows similar moves by SoftBank Group Corp. and Michael Burry, and comes after Thiel's previous warnings about stretched valuations in the tech sector, likening the current environment to the 1999-2000 Dotcom bubble. The company is expected to report strong Q3 revenue, and Wall Street may still be underestimating its future growth despite lofty expectations.

This investor anxiety is impacting the entire chip sector and other AI-exposed tech companies.

The company is also focused on expanding U.S.-based manufacturing and addresses operational challenges related to product transitions and complex system configurations.

Backed by Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, Humain aims to supply 6% of the world's AI computing power by 2034 and has secured significant deals with Nvidia, Amazon, and xAI, as well as with competitors like AMD and Qualcomm, despite some U.S. national security concerns. This action is part of a broader US government effort to curb the smuggling of advanced AI chips to China, which authorities fear could be used for military, surveillance, disinformation, and cybersecurity applications.

The Department of Justice thwarted some shipments and highlighted China's goal of using AI for military modernization.

OpenAI, despite challenges, is positioned to disrupt online experiences by focusing on trusted information over ads, maintaining a strong position alongside Nvidia in the AI era.

This request comes after President Donald Trump's decision to allow these sales with a 25% fee, a policy shift from the Biden administration's ban due to national security concerns. Despite concerns about an AI bubble, Moore believes the strong growth in AI semiconductors, memory, and fab utilization will continue. He provides bullish ratings and price targets for all three companies, emphasizing their individual strengths in high-performance processing, custom ASICs, and specialized connectivity solutions for AI infrastructure.

title: Exclusive: Nvidia buying AI chip startup Groq for about $20 billion in its largest acquisition on record
summary: Nvidia is making its largest acquisition to date, purchasing assets from AI chip startup Groq for approximately $20 billion. Groq, founded by creators of Google's tensor processing unit, will enter a "non-exclusive licensing agreement" with Nvidia, with Groq's CEO and other senior leaders joining Nvidia while parts of Groq will continue as an independent company. This deal significantly bolsters Nvidia's position in the competitive AI chip market.

title: Nvidia to license AI chip challenger Groq’s tech and hire its CEO

Although not an acquisition of the entire company, reports suggest Nvidia is acquiring assets worth $20 billion, which would be its largest purchase yet. This move aims to further solidify Nvidia's dominance in chip manufacturing by integrating Groq's LPU technology, known for its speed and energy efficiency in running LLMs.

Elizabeth Warren, are raising alarms over the Trump administration's alleged decision to greenlight the sale of Nvidia's H200 AI chips to China. Utilizing direct-to-chip liquid cooling for optimal performance and power efficiency, the initiative aims to provide essential computational resources for Generative AI developers and has been certified by Japan’s METI as a "Cloud Program."

This move, which has not been officially announced by Nvidia, is seen by analysts as a strategic way to widen its competitive moat in the AI market, particularly in the inference segment, preventing Groq's assets from falling into competitors' hands. The deal highlights a growing trend among tech giants to prioritize talent and technology acquisition through licensing to avoid regulatory hurdles associated with traditional mergers.
